title: Multisite synergism-induced electron regulation of high-entropy alloy nanowires for electrocatalytic reforming of polylactic acid plastic hydrolysate
abstract: Transforming waste PLA into chemical feedstocks for high-value products offers sustainable advantages, reducing emissions while advancing circular production.

title: Recent advances, challenges, and future prospects in the selective thermal reduction of carbon dioxide to methane
abstract: Mechanism-based catalyst tuning and design, with discussion on typical reactors.

title: Molecularly engineered Li compensation agent-integrated separator enabling regeneration of degraded LiFePO 4
abstract: A lithium replenishment separator that generates neither gas nor solid residues during pre-lithiation enables the regeneration of degraded LFP cathodes to their original capacity, offering a sustainable and green strategy for lithium compensation.

title: Roles of alkaline-earth metals and electron density of Al 3+ in octanol dehydration for linear alpha-olefin production
abstract: Among AEM-impregnated Al 2 O 3 catalysts, Ba–Al 2 O 3 achieved the highest 1-octene purity by suppressing isomerization via increased Al 3+ electron density and reduced re-adsorption energy.

title: Enabling high-temperature processing of thin film Li-ion batteries using a LISICON based solid-state electrolyte
abstract: In situ impedance measurements during pulsed laser deposition show that Li 4− x Ge 1− x P x O 4 (LGPO) thin-film crystallinity, morphology and composition influence ionic transport, with ∼10 −5 S cm −1 at room temperature.

title: Tetragonal symmetric-shaped {VIV4O 8 } cubane-encasing vanado-phosphate derivative: prototype liquid-configured device-grade supercapacitor with enhanced performance
abstract: Superior device-grade supercapacitor performance using dumb-bell shaped capsular oxo-vanadates functionalized with diphosphonate ligands.

title: A spontaneous water-detaching hydrophobic coating resistant to calendar aging based on non-equilibrium wetting
abstract: This study classifies wetting behavior into equilibrium and non-equilibrium types. A new water-based coating is developed, providing a self-cleaning surface that resists aging and maintains hydrophobicity in varying outdoor conditions.

title: In situ hard templating-assisted, facet-engineered, two-dimensional non-van der Waals CuInS 2 for efficient CO 2 reduction reaction
abstract: In two-dimensional (2D) materials, selective facet exposure is highly sought after but can be typically achieved only through sophisticated methods like chemical vapour deposition (CVD) or molecular beam epitaxy (MBE).

title: Tailoring Pt-loaded MoS 2 /SnO 2 heterostructures for high-sensitivity room-temperature ammonia detection: a DFT and COMSOL analysis
abstract: Metal oxide semiconductor (MOS) nanostructures have been explored intensively for gas detection; however, they often need high operating temperatures, consume high power, and demonstrate poor selectivity.

title: Structure–activity correlation in layered double hydroxides: facilitating oxygen evolution through the lattice oxygen mechanism
abstract: A fundamental methodology for the sustainable generation of hydrogen is electrochemical water-splitting; however, the protracted kinetics of the oxygen evolution reaction (OER) diminish its efficacy.

title: Insights into electrolyte-dependent interfacial chemistry in a high-voltage Na 3 VFe(PO 4 ) 3 cathode through combined experimental and theoretical studies
abstract: Na 3 FeV(PO 4 ) 3 exhibits 86.11% capacity retention after 2000 cycles at 3C in 1 M NaClO 4 (EC/DMC + 2% FEC), attributed to a stable CEI (NaF, Na 2 CO 3 , and organics) and a low Na + migration barrier (∼0.18 eV) enabling fast ion transport.

title: Air-processed FA–Cs perovskite scalable films without Pb 0 defects for efficient inverted photovoltaic modules
abstract: (CH 3 ) 3 SI is incorporated into blade-coated FACs perovskite films to reduce harmful Pb 0 defects and enhance the stability and efficiency of photovoltaic modules (10 × 10 cm 2 substrate) from 18.25% to 19.98%.

title: Unveiling γ′ phase creep damage in multi-component Ni-based superalloys by crystal plasticity phase-field simulation
abstract: A phase-field model combined with crystal plasticity theory is developed to study the precipitation kinetics and creep behavior of multi-component Ni-based superalloys.

title: High-performance alkaline water electrolysis: a membrane–catalyst–device integrated paradigm
abstract: Alkaline exchange membrane water electrolysis (AEMWE) is critical for advancing next-generation water electrolysis technologies.

title: Highly efficient electron transport layer with weak pinning and low barrier contact for solar cells: monolayer GaN on a 2D lead-free perovskite
abstract: A vdW Cs 3 YSr 3 I 12 /monolayer GaN dual-semiconductor heterojunction is designed, where the NEA at the Y/GaN(0001) interface suppresses gap states, achieving zero-barrier quasi-ohmic contact and reaching a tunneling efficiency of 97.1% ( S = 0.74).

title: Role mechanisms, design principles and engineering strategies of bimetallic and multi-metallic catalysts for the eCO 2 RR
abstract: Guided by eCO 2 RR mechanisms, bimetallic and multi-metallic catalysts engineered with diverse strategies exploit synergy to achieve high activity and selectivity for varied products, advancing the development of the eCO 2 RR.

title: Hierarchical micro-tile growth of monoclinic tungsten oxide nucleated on MWCNTs hexagonal skeletons: wide-potential solid-state supercapacitor with a mechanical bendable design
abstract: Material mutualism between W 25 O 73 and MWCNTs yielding a high capacitnace of 1301.46 F g −1 . A flexible symmetric solid-state supercapacitor delivers 10.10 Wh kg −1 energy density, 601.03 W kg −1 power density, and 82.78 % retention after 10 000 cycles.

title: Entanglement assembly of graphite worms: a direct strategy to achieve highly crystalline graphene foam for superior electromagnetic interference shielding
abstract: Highly crystalline graphene foam is fabricated via confined expansion-induced entanglement of graphite worms. The 3D conductive network with hierarchical pores enables superior EMI shielding, achieving specific effectiveness of 19302.3 dB cm 2 g −1 .

title: A long term highly reversible zinc metal anode regulated by polar molecular interface adsorption
abstract: Methyl acetate (MA), as an efficient additive, reconfigures the anode/electrolyte interface in situ , enhancing the reversibility of plating/stripping through interface coordination chemistry.

title: The thermal transport in a twin superlattice: from phonon coherence phenomenon to anomalous excitation of high-energy phonons at twin boundaries
abstract: Although ∑5 and ∑9 twin superlattices cannot enhance thermal conductivity through phonon coherence effects like the ∑3 twin structure, they can positively influence thermal transport by anomalously exciting high-frequency phonons at the interface.

title: Flux-assisted synthesis of a visible-light-active La 2 FeTiO 6 double perovskite with intrinsic bifunctional activity for photocatalytic water reduction and oxidation
abstract: Flux-assisted synthesis yields a visible-light-responsive La 2 FeTiO 6 double perovskite with intrinsic bifunctional activity for H 2 and O 2 evolution, establishing LFTO as a promising material for solar-hydrogen production.

title: Enhanced energy density of polymer dielectrics at high temperature by building a B–N coordinated supramolecular network
abstract: Electrostatic capacitors are considered the preferred candidates for high-power energy storage.

title: Correction: Multiporous structure formation in three-way catalyst particles for enhanced catalytic performance
abstract: Correction for “Multiporous structure formation in three-way catalyst particles for enhanced catalytic performance” by Duhaul Biqal Kautsar et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2026, 14 , 1110–1122, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TA04974A.

title: Tailoring the gas sensing properties of ZrS 2 monolayers through Ni and Pd modifications: a DFT study
abstract: DFT reveals that Ni and Pd modifications enhance ZrS 2 sensing toward hazardous gases. Doped systems show high sensitivity, while decorated surfaces offer ultrafast recovery for environmental monitoring.

title: Melt processed perovskite glass-ceramic films for large-area X-ray detection and imaging
abstract: A melt-processed perovskite glass-ceramic (GC) film allows for robust integration with TFT backplanes, making it suitable for high-performance X-ray imaging applications.

title: Porosity meets robustness: design of ultralight MXene/PVA composite foams for high-performance flexible supercapacitors
abstract: High strength porous MXene/PVA foams are prepared, where hydrogen bonding enhances mechanical properties of the foams and porous structure enables rapid ion transport throughout the network, leading to superior flexible supercapacitor performance.

title: Dual-carbon and chalcogenide engineered Ni 3 Se 4 /delaminated V 2 C MXene /reduced graphene oxide composite as the bifunctional electrode for asymmetric supercapacitor and hydrogen evolution applications
abstract: The rational design of multifunctional materials with superior electrochemical properties is vital for advancing energy storage and conversion technologies.

title: Breath-figure-derived porous hybrid layers for safety-enhanced separators in lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Breath-figure self-assembly enables porous poly(vinylidene fluoride- co -hexafluoropropylene)/boehmite hybrid coating layers for safety-enhanced separators in lithium-ion batteries.

title: Polarization field engineering through doping-induced anisotropic distortion for efficient piezo-photocatalysis
abstract: La 3+ -induced anisotropic distortion and defect engineering synergistically tailor the electronic structure and piezoelectric polarization field of Bi 4 Ti 3 O 12 , constructing a well-balanced piezo–photocatalytic system with markedly enhanced activity.

title: Research progress of anode/electrolyte interface modulation for aqueous aluminum metal batteries
abstract: Aqueous aluminum metal batteries (AAMBs) have recently emerged as promising candidates for next-generation energy storage systems.

title: HOF-Ni-GDY dual-ohmic-junction engineering: inducing photogenerated electron–hole dual channel separation for boosted hydrogen evolution
abstract: This study integrates Ni and HOF nanorods onto GDY, utilizing its acetylene bonds for substrate adsorption, Ni for electron capture, and HOF for an induced fit microenvironment—a novel strategy for high-performance non-noble-metal photocatalysts.

title: A bionic stem-shaped copper-doped aniline–pyrrole copolymer composite photothermal sponge evaporator for seawater desalination and sewage treatment
abstract: A bionic stem-shaped copper-doped aniline–pyrrole copolymer composite photothermal sponge evaporator is used for high-efficiency solar-driven interfacial evaporation, featuring excellent evaporation performance.

title: Resolving conflicting interpretations of ionic conductivity in halogen-doped Li 7 P 3 S 11 solid electrolytes: atomistic insights from Li and polyhedral dynamics
abstract: Contrasting ionic conductivity trends reported for halogen-doped Li 7 P 3 S 11 to date were interpreted in terms of halogen dopant migration behavior and associated PS x structural transformation phenomena, which vary with the amount of Li interstitials.

title: Thermo-adaptive gelatin-based hydrogel skin patch with switchable mechanics and adhesion for on-demand wearable sensing
abstract: We engineered a thermoresponsive gelatin-based hydrogel patch (Gel/PAA/PDA@Ag/CTAB) via synergistic molecular design to address core trade-offs of adhesion-detachment and mechanics-multifunctionality in electronic skins.

title: Tuning the electrochemical properties of tungsten oxide nanoplates via Sn doping and mixed-phase formation for superior quasi-solid-state asymmetric supercapacitors
abstract: Sn-doped tungsten oxide nanoplates with mixed phases show enhanced electrochemical performance, enabling high-efficiency quasi-solid-state asymmetric supercapacitors with improved energy storage, stability, and overall device performance.

title: Ge-Induced Cation Ordering for Enhanced Thermoelectric Performance in AgSbTe 2
abstract: Waste heat recovery through thermoelectric conversion is a promising route toward sustainable energy utilization. AgSbTe 2 is a notable mid-temperature thermoelectric material owing to its inherently low thermal conductivity. However, its...

title: Exploring the multifunctionality of Co and Ni selenospinels: unveiling Co 3 Se 4 as an efficient trifunctional electrocatalyst for glycerol, hydrazine, and water oxidation reactions
abstract: Co 3 Se 4 , as a multifunctional electrode material, efficiently catalyses glycerol, hydrazine, and water oxidations.

title: Precisely controlled low-valent nickel sites in planar polyphthalocyanine for enhanced urea oxidation
abstract: This work develops a 2D nickel-polyphthalocyanine catalyst (NiPPc/C) featuring confined atomic low-valent Ni sites, which lowers the potential for urea oxidation and enables energy-saving hydrogen production.

title: Novel soluble sulfonyl-containing conjugated polymers as highly efficient photocatalysts for CO 2 reduction reaction
abstract: Organic linear conjugated polymers, including P3HT-T, P3HTO-T, and PDBTOOC8-T, have set a new benchmark for conversion efficiency in photocatalytic CO 2 reduction.

title: Tuning porous Li 6.25 Ga 0.25 La 3 Zr 2 O 12 (LGLZO) frameworks for enhanced ion transport in semi-solid-state lithium metal batteries
abstract: Interconnected pore structures in quasi-solid-state batteries enable efficient ion transport, yielding high ionic conductivity, elevated critical current density, and prolonged cycling stability.

title: The mechanism of Li-ion transport in carbonate-based electrolytes
abstract: Molecular dynamics simulations were performed on 1 M LiPF 6 in the electrolyte composed of EC and DMC. As temperature decreases, the solvation of Li + shifts from EC-dominated to DMC-dominated coordination, which suppresses the solvent exchange of Li + .

title: Exploiting Zn and Zr synergy in co-doped Sr(Fe 1− x Mo x )O 3− δ – versatile Co-free electrode materials for solid oxide fuel cells
abstract: Zn and Zr co-doped Sr(Fe 1− x Mo x )O 3− δ (SFM) were used to enhance the cathodic SOFC performance by means of increased lattice basicity. The results indicate substantial improvement over the conventional SFM material.

title: Microstructure engineered multiphase tellurides with enhanced thermoelectric efficiency
abstract: This review highlights the progress in phase diagram engineering and microstructural design in Te based multiphase alloys.

title: Ir single atoms and nanoclusters anchored on Ni aerogels for enhanced alkaline water oxidation: unravelling the Ir coordination environment and the Ir–Ni synergistic effect
abstract: Ir single atoms and nanoclusters anchored on nickel aerogels serve as an efficient catalyst for alkaline water oxidation, with characterization and theoretical calculations revealing the atomic Ir coordination and Ir–Ni synergistic effect.

title: A simple and scalable fabrication of a high-performance flexible microsupercapacitor using hierarchical Ni–Mo–S nanostructures decorated on Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene
abstract: The assembled device delivers a broader voltage window, efficient ion movement, strong current response, and stable cycling. Architecture boosts charge storage, ensuring high performance for practical use.

title: Effect of binder viscosity on the rheology of cathode slurries and performance of lithium-ion battery half cells
abstract: PVDF moderates the structure of CB in cathode slurries, affecting cathode loading and battery cell performance.

title: A universal model for energy level alignment at interfaces of hole-collecting monolayers in p-i-n perovskite solar cells
abstract: A universal model is developed, which describes the energy level alignment at the interfaces of hole-collecting monolayers and perovskite layers.

title: Organic–inorganic interface engineering for integrated control of surface sites and hydrogen dynamics in chemoselective hydrogenation
abstract: Interfacial organic grafting enables simultaneous control of hydrogen dynamics and Pt surface-site accessibility, achieving high chemoselectivity and activity in structure-sensitive hydrogenation.

title: Cu 2 O@COF core–shell catalyst for electrochemical CO 2 reduction
abstract: Cu 2 O nanocubes coated with porous covalent organic framework (COF) layers enable efficient and selective electrochemical CO 2 reduction to C 2+ products. The Cu@COF core–shell catalysts show high activity, good current density and enhanced stability.

title: Development and performances analysis of eco-friendly pullulan/ polyvinyl alcohol composites based all-solid-state supercapacitors
abstract: Next-generation electronic devices, including embedded microsystems and wearable technologies, require the development of safe, and low-cost energy storage systems to meet the 21st-century society demands.

title: Polypyrrole-bridged vanadium oxide hierarchical microspheres for high-rate and durable aqueous Zn-ion storage
abstract: Polypyrrole incorporation into vanadium oxide enables the formation of hierarchical microspheres with improved structural stability and electrochemical kinetics, leading to enhanced rate capability and cycling durability in aqueous Zn-ion batteries.

title: PdNi nanosheets with modulated d-band centers for efficient ethanol electrooxidation via the C 2 pathway
abstract: Pd x Ni y alloy nanosheets with an optimized d-band center spacing enable efficient EOR via the C 2 pathway.

title: Engineering Ni/Ni 3 N/MoN interface with strain modulation toward sustainable high-current-density alkaline seawater hydrogen production via in situ ammonium formation
abstract: The NN/MN/NF exhibits an overpotential of 71 mV at 300 mA cm −2 , as well as notable stability over 100 h at 500 mA cm −2 in 1 M KOH natural seawater. Moreover, the in situ formation of NH 4 + inhibits precipitation-induced corrosion of the active sites.

title: One-step regulation of pore evolution in hard carbon from open to closed for high rate and high plateau capacity sodium-ion storage
abstract: Hard carbon rich in closed pores was successfully fabricated via one step pyrolysis of white dextrin, exhibiting a high reversible specific capacity of 248.4 mAh g −1 at 10C.

title: Chalcogen cathodes beyond sulfur: the rising role of selenium and tellurium in zinc-ion energy storage
abstract: Review of Se/Te chalcogen cathodes for aqueous Zn-ion batteries, highlighting design strategies, redox mechanisms, and prospects for high-performance, safe, durable energy storage.

title: Copolyesters with branched structures for simultaneous fluorescence and resilience
abstract: Branched PBST copolyesters balance chain flexibility/rigidity, suppresses crystallization, and endows >60% elastic recovery and enhanced fluorescence for ion sensing and fatigue monitoring, with good biocompatibility and biodegradability.

title: Tannic acid-modified polyvinyl alcohol hydrogel electrolyte enabling the development of dendrite-free and flexible aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: A tannic acid-modified PVA hydrogel electrolyte regulates Zn 2+ flux and suppresses dendrite growth via strengthened interfacial coordination, enabling stable cycling of aqueous zinc-ion batteries.

title: Revealing short- and long-range Li-ion diffusion in Li 2 MnO 3 from finite-temperature dynamical mean field theory
abstract: Li 2 MnO 3 is a key component of Li-excess layered cathodes of the form (1 − x ), LiMO 2 + x , Li 2 MnO 3 (M = Mn, Ni, Co, …), yet its role in setting Li-ion transport limitations remains under debate.

title: MOF-derived chalcogenides multifunctional heterostructure materials for high-performance supercapacitor and oxygen evolution reaction catalytic activity
abstract: The synthesized composites are investigated for supercapacitor and oxygen evolution reaction applications. The Cu 2 S/ZnS/CoS 2 @NPC shows the specific capacitance of 1206 F g −1 at 1 A g −1 , outperforming both the Cu/Zn/Co@NPC and Cu 2 Se/ZnSe/CoSe 2 @NPC.

title: Same but different: how permutation degeneracy enhances machine learning in single-atom catalyst discovery
abstract: Incorporating structural permutation degeneracy into machine learning descriptors significantly improves the accuracy and generalizability of catalytic activity predictions in single-atom catalysts.

title: Synergistic interaction of phosphorous-doped BiOI with functionalized carbon nanofibers for efficient electrochemical sensing of dimetridazole
abstract: This study reports a 2D phosphorus doped BiOI decorated 1D functionalized carbon nanofiber nanohybrid for electrochemical detection of dimetridazole, where synergistic interactions enhance active sites, conductivity, and selectivity.

title: A phenazine-based organic polymer for long-life sodium storage
abstract: PBDP, a phenazine-based organic polymer, served as the anode of a sodium ion battery, providing an excellent capacity of 385 mAh g −1 and retaining 169 mAh g −1 after 10000 cycles due to its high theoretical capacity and low solubility.

title: Voltage-driven evolution of lithium nanoparticle morphology and SEI precursors
abstract: Lithium metal anodes offer unmatched theoretical capacity amongst candidate battery materials, yet their commercial viability remains limited by uncontrolled dendrite growth and solid electrolyte interphase (SEI) formation.

title: Iodine-driven artificial SEI layer for high performance lithium metal batteries in an SO 2 -based electrolyte
abstract: An I 2 -treated LiAlCl 4 –3SO 2 electrolyte forms a Li 2 S/Li 2 O-rich SEI, suppressing dendrite formation and enhancing stability for durable lithium metal anodes.

title: Mechanisms of strength, thermal stability, and doping effects in metal-doped bilayer borophene for energy storage applications using a DFT-trained deep-learning potential
abstract: DFT-trained deep-learning potentials are used to uncover how Al and Ru dopants tune strength and structural transition temperatures in bilayer borophene, guiding design for robust 2D devices.

title: Window layer engineering of kesterite CZTSSe thin-film solar cells
abstract: We successfully fabricated i-ZnO-free CZTSSe-based TFSCs with efficiencies exceeding 10% by employing a simplified window structure at a low substrate temperature.

title: Predictive thermal safety of lithium-ion batteries through a unified kinetic-thermal framework
abstract: Thermal runaway remains a critical barrier to the safe deployment of high-energy lithium-ion batteries.

title: Defect-engineered MIL-101(Cr) via facile low-temperature calcination for efficient CO 2 cycloaddition under mild conditions
abstract: Defective metal organic frameworks have emerged as promising catalysts for CO 2 conversion owing to their tunable active sites and exceptional catalytic activities.

title: Electronegativity-driven d-band center shifts in Ru–Ni alloys for low methane hydrogenolysis of polyolefin plastics
abstract: Ru–Ni alloys enable selective C–C bond cleavage in plastic waste via d‑band modulation, converting it to valuable fuels/chemicals while minimizing methane formation.

title: Photocatalytic transfer hydrogenation of nitriles to primary amines over a Pd nanocube-modified poly(heptazine imide) catalyst
abstract: A Pd nanocube-modified PHI composite was successfully fabricated via electrostatic self-assembly and effectively promoted the visible-light-driven hydrogenation of nitriles to primary amines.

title: Perovskite oxides for electrochemical small-molecule oxidation: advances and mechanisms
abstract: This review highlights the application of perovskite oxides as electrocatalysts for small-molecule oxidation, focusing on their structure–performance relationships, defect engineering, and reaction mechanisms to guide efficient energy conversion.

title: Selective oxidation of methanol into value-added chemical over alkali etched silver nanowire-Ni-ZIF composite
abstract: Defect-rich D-Ag NWs-Ni-ZIF enables selective methanol-to-formate electrooxidation (1.313 V at 10 mA cm −2 ), replacing OER to cut water-splitting energy and boost H 2 production.

title: Weakening solvation via dipole interactions enables efficient and stable perovskite solar cells
abstract: Pentyl propionate (PAAC) weakens DMF–Pb 2+ coordination via dipole interactions, moderating nucleation and crystallization, enabling high-quality films and devices with a power conversion efficiency of 24.06%.

title: Surface Segregation and Reconstruction of Core-Shell Electrocatalysts for Water Splitting
abstract: Electrochemical water splitting to produce large-scale hydrogen is a leading sustainable energy development technique to mitigate carbon emissions. Among the most promising electrocatalysts, core-shell nanoarchitectures have garnered significant attention for...

title: Formation and activity mechanisms of a carbon-nitride-supported single-atom catalyst for photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: The formation mechanism and origin of high activity in SACs with 100% atom utilization were elucidated through integrated in situ XAFS and DFT calculations. The results establish new design guidelines for highly active single-atom catalysts.

title: Decoupling the crucial architectures of carbon supports governing Pt nanoparticle anchoring behaviour for electrocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: A rigorous and systematic investigation of engineered carbon supports with well-defined architectures is provided to precisely isolate and evaluate the crucial support structural factors governing Pt nanoparticle anchoring behavior.

title: Electric field-tuned band engineering and photocatalytic H 2 evolution mechanism in ZnS/SnS 2 and ZnSe/SnSe 2 heterojunctions
abstract: Study show electric fields control bandgap, and Z-scheme in ZnS/SnS 2 & ZnSe/SnSe 2 . Negative fields preserve Z-scheme; positive fields cause Type-II/I transitions. Optimal negative fields boost STH efficiency 1.8× (ZnS/SnS 2 ) and 3× (ZnSe/SnSe 2 ).

title: An Ab Initio Study and Machine Learning Framework to Capture the Motional Effects in Solid-State NMR of Lithium-Ion Conductors
abstract: Solid-state NMR spectroscopy, when combined with first-principles density functional theory (DFT) calculations, offers a highly sensitive probe of atomic-scale structure and dynamics in solid-state ion conductors, enabling the characterisation of...

title: Recent advances and artificial neural network-response surface methodology-based predictive modeling of CO 2 adsorption in 3D triptycene-based nanoporous materials
abstract: Triptycene-based materials combine rigid 3D geometry, intrinsic free volume, and tunable functionality for CO 2 capture and separation. ANN–RSM modeling links porosity and operating conditions to uptake and guides predictive materials design.

title: Stability challenges in non-aqueous Li–O 2 batteries and their protective strategies: a comprehensive review on electrode and electrolyte engineering
abstract: Anode, air-cathode, and electrolyte stabilization using electrolyte additives by forming a stable SEI enables the formation of toroidal Li 2 O 2 and offers stability against ROS species in Li–Air batteries.

title: Gold-bridged LaCo 1− x Au x O 3 perovskite nanocomposites for synergically enhanced electrochemical hydrogen storage
abstract: With a dual role, Au substitution into the Co site (material modification) and uniform Au nanoparticle decoration (bridging components), 0.5% Au:LCO/GO nanocomposite yields a high hydrogen storage capacity of 2230 mAh g −1 .

title: Platinum-group-metal high-entropy materials: emerging electrocatalysts for sustainable energy conversion
abstract: The review summarizes the classification of high entropy materials from thermodynamic aspects, different synthesis techniques, and specific focus on PGM based HEMs as electrocatalysts for various energy conversion applications.

title: High-entropy alloy electrocatalysts for oxygen reduction and hydrogen evolution reactions: recent advances and future perspectives
abstract: High-entropy alloys unlock tunable multi-site catalysis for oxygen reduction and hydrogen evolution reactions, revealed by core effects and bibliometric mapping of emerging trends.

title: MBenes : emerging two-dimensional metal borides for multifunctional energy storage and conversion technologies
abstract: The need for efficient, sustainable energy storage has increased research into novel two-dimensional materials obtained from MAB phases, where high metal-to-boron ratios make them strong candidates for energy applications.

title: Surface-Engineering strategies for chloride-resistant oxygen evolution electrocatalysts in direct seawater electrolysis
abstract: This review summarizes recent advances in surface engineering for OER catalysts in direct seawater electrolysis, focusing on how surface-modulation enhances catalytic activity, corrosion resistance, and selectivity in harsh marine environments.

title: Modulating spin states in dual active sites of CoFe electrocatalysts for energy harvesting applications
abstract: Illustration of crystal-field splitting of Co 3d orbitals and the resulting unpaired electron spin.

title: Surface termination engineering of MXene nanofluidic membranes for efficient osmotic energy harvesting
abstract: The correlation between ionic transport properties and surface terminal atoms was unveiled based on the surface termination tuning of MXenes. Due to Cl–Cl interactions, Ti 3 C 2 Cl 2 nanofluids delivered a superior osmotic power output of 17.84 W m −2 .

title: Unveiling enhanced hole transfer mechanism in perovskite solar cells with a self-assembled monolayer and [1]benzothieno[3,2-b][1]benzothiophene interlayer
abstract: C10-BTBT enhances the charge transfer between the perovskite and the MeO-2PACz hole transport layer, facilitating more efficient carrier extraction and improving interfacial electronic coupling.

title: Influence of precursor type on NMC811 cathode material properties: insights from spray pyrolysis synthesis and in situ XRD analysis
abstract: Spray pyrolysis yields spherical (nitrate) vs. wrinkled (acetate) NMC811 particles. In situ XRD identifies optimal annealing temperatures; subsequent annealing reveals faster ordering for nitrate (3–5 h) vs. acetate (10–15 h).

title: Ferrocenyl-dithiolane integrated Cu( i ) coordination polymers: framework engineering for synergistic redox activity towards supercapattery applications
abstract: Ligand-directed assembly of ferrocene–Cu( i ) CPs yields Fc-Cu1 (1D) and Fc-Cu2 (2D) with a similar Cu–I array. The 2D Fc-Cu2 exhibits enhanced conductivity and charge transport, enabling superior and stable supercapattery performance.

title: Co single-atom-anchored carbon nitride quantum dots activate peroxymonosulfate for efficient tetracycline degradation
abstract: Co single-atom/carbon nitride quantum dots activate peroxymonosulfate via Co–N sites, enabling rapid tetracycline degradation, ultralow Co leaching, and wide adaptability via O–O bond scission to generate singlet oxygen.

title: Chirality induced operando probing of analyte–surface hybrid states in heterogeneous redox processes
abstract: Polarity-resolved circularly polarized operando Raman spectroscopy helps identify spin transfer mechanisms at surface–analyte interfaces – a step towards real-time monitoring of surface quasi-states in sensing and catalysis.

title: Silver complex-modified tetravanadium-substituted polyoxomolybdate-containing ordered tunnels for supercapacitors and electrocatalytic CO 2 reduction
abstract: The substituted Keggin clusters were anchored between adjacent Ag-bipy layers, forming unique POMOFs with a variety of ordered tunnels, which show excellent capacitance characteristics and higher electrocatalytic activity for the CO 2 RR.

title: An operando spectroscopic examination of the influence of trace humidity on interdigital back contact metal halide perovskite solar cells
abstract: In operando investigations of trace humidity effects (5–35 ppm V ) on metal halide perovskite solar cells using back contact devices are revealing water migration, outgassing, charge-carrier quenching, and reversible photocurrent responses.

title: A urotropine-induced g-C 3 N 4 membrane enhancing CO 2 transition for robust and active urea-assisted water splitting
abstract: To address the kinetic limitations in UOR, we develop an urotropine-induced strategy to engineer NiS–Ni 3 S 2 @g-C 3 N 4 . This catalyst exhibits outstanding UOR performance with a 1.34 V to reach 100 mA cm −2 , and 90% activity retention over 40 hours.

title: Revealing the photochemical activity of fluorographene towards organic transformations: selective aerobic photooxidation of sulfides to sulfoxides
abstract: An effective, mild, and sustainable protocol has been developed, employing fluorographene as the photosensitizer for the chemoselective oxidation of sulfides to the corresponding sulfoxides.

title: Atmosphere-dependent crystallization of carbon nitride: balancing activity and stability for efficient photocatalysis
abstract: This work elucidates the K + -assisted crystallization of carbon nitride, showing how synthesis atmosphere balances photoactivity and stability. Optimal air calcination at 550 °C produces a durable, high-performance photocatalyst.

title: In situ photo-Fenton-like reaction and oxygen evolution through polaron-mediated WO 3
abstract: The polaron mediated photo-Fenton-like reaction of WO 3 has improved charge carrier utilization for efficient in situ waste-water removal and oxygen evolution reaction.

title: Synergistic tuning of CO 2 activation and *CHO adsorption enables high-rate electrocatalytic CO 2 reduction to CH 4 on a CuSn@N–C catalyst
abstract: Here, a “two-in-one” CuSn bimetallic nanoparticle-decorated nitrogen-doped carbon catalyst (CuSn@N–C) is reported, which synergistically promotes CO 2 activation and *CHO intermediate adsorption to boost the activity and selectivity of the CH 4 .

title: Accelerated electrochemical nitrate-to-ammonia conversion over bimetallic Ni 2 Mo 3 N with mechanistic insights
abstract: Nickel molybdenum nitride supported on nickel foam (Ni 2 Mo 3 N/NF) was used as a catalyst for nitrate reduction to ammonia (NO 3 − RR). Ni 2 Mo 3 N/NF achieved a high faradaic efficiency of 97.72% and a maximum ammonia yield of 5.55 mg h −1 cm −2 .

title: Unveiling the kinetic mechanism of oxygen vacancy and N-doping co-modified SrTiO 3 (100) for photocatalytic conversion of CO 2 and H 2 O into CH 4 via associative proton transfer: insights from ab initio molecular dynamics
abstract: The introduction of bifunctional active sites prolongs the carrier lifetime and accelerates the H 2 O-mediated proton transfer kinetics in the photocatalytic conversion from CO 2 and H 2 O to CH 4 .

title: Janus-structured MXene composite films with tannic acid-enhanced strength and environmentally stable electromagnetic interference shielding
abstract: The Janus-structured composite film, fabricated via layer-by-layer vacuum-assisted filtration using tannic acid-functionalized MXene, exhibited significantly superior overall performance compared to its directly blended counterpart.

title: PbI 2 interface reconstruction suppresses ion migration for stable planar perovskite solar cells
abstract: By redistributing PbI 2 from the buried interface to the surface, BBCA molecule enables multi-interface PbI 2 reconstruction and suppresses ion migration, achieving a champion efficiency of 25.14% in perovskite solar cells.

title: Rational design of multivariate covalent organic frameworks with enhanced light-harvesting for selective detection and photocatalytic reduction of Cr( vi )
abstract: A multivariate COF constructed via molecular engineering serves dual functions: enabling highly selective fluorescence detection of Cr( vi ) and exhibiting superior photocatalytic activity for reducing toxic Cr( vi ) to less harmful Cr( iii ).

title: Investigation of the interface structure and electronic state of nanocomposite La 0.6 Sr 0.4 Co 0.2 Fe 0.8 O 3− δ and Ce 0.9 Gd 0.1 O 2− δ electrodes for solid oxide fuel cells
abstract: Governing factors for the reactivity of nanocomposite electrodes for solid oxide fuel cells were revealed using scanning transmission electron microscopy (STEM) and soft X-ray absorption spectroscopy (XAS) analyses.

title: Precious-metal-free catalyst design strategies for stable anion-exchange membrane water electrolysis using seawater
abstract: This review highlights precious-metal-free catalyst designs, including heteroatom doping and heterojunction engineering, to stabilize seawater anion exchange membrane water electrolysis by mitigating scaling, surface passivation, and side reactions.

title: Tailoring electrolyte solvation for improved Na-based supercapacitor efficiency: an operando characterization approach
abstract: Balancing performance and cyclability is key for Na-based supercapacitors. The NaClO 4 : [C 3 mpip][NTf 2 ] IL delivers the highest capacitance, NaOTf : PEG400 DES is stable short-term, while 17 m NaClO 4 shows gas evolution and interface instability.

title: Tuning the MN 4 plane in π-conjugated metal-phthalocyanine networks toward efficient CO 2 electroreduction: a DFT-ML study
abstract: This study screened CuN 2 C 2 -Pc and FeN 2 O 2 -Pc as efficient CO 2 reduction catalysts via DFT and machine learning. With limiting potentials of 0.29 V and 0.28 V, they significantly outperform traditional precious metal catalysts.

title: Tuning dimensionality and linkage in metal–organic frameworks for enhanced electrochemical energy storage
abstract: The study reports the synthesis and comparative analysis of two MOFs with distinct dimensions: a 3D pillared-layered MOF (3D-PL-MOF) and a 2D layered MOF (2D-L-MOF).

title: Construction of a hollow Sb 2 S 3 @NC core-shell nanorod as an anode via a microstructure regulation strategy for high-performance Na + /K + storage
abstract: The dual synergistic strategies of internal void buffering and NC layer modification adopted by H-Sb 2 S 3 @NC can effectively mitigate the volume variation of Sb 2 S 3 and remarkably enhance its cycling stability and rate capability.

title: Developing a sharp cone war hammer-like MIL-88A/Ag/MIL-88B Z-scheme heterojunction by a controlled in situ semi-conversion strategy: adsorption–photodegradation of meloxicam and antibacterial activity
abstract: MIL-88A/Ag/MIL-88B was synthesized using in situ semi-transformation strategies for studying the adsorption–photodegradation of meloxicam and antibacterial activity.

title: A highly resilient, conductive, and anti-swelling hybrid-crosslinked hydrogel based on a semi-interpenetrating network for multimodal sensing and marine monitoring
abstract: A highly resilient, conductive, and anti-swelling hybrid-crosslinked hydrogel based on a semi-interpenetrating network for multimodal sensing and marine monitoring.

title: XPEAK: an XRD-driven machine learning platform for predicting the catalyst-enhanced dehydrogenation peak temperature of MgH 2
abstract: An XRD-AI platform enabling discovery of high-potential catalysts for MgH 2 dehydrogenation.

title: Polymer-intercalated vanadium oxide as a near-zero-strain dual-mechanism cathode for rechargeable magnesium-metal batteries
abstract: Polymer-intercalated V 2 O 5 with V–N coordination bonds stabilizes the framework and promotes Mg 2+ diffusion. Dual Mg 2+ intercalation/PANI binding delivers high rate capability and long cyclability in rechargeable magnesium-metal batteries.

title: Beyond the slurry: architecting binder-free tin anodes for high-energy lithium-ion batteries—a review
abstract: A comprehensive review of the transition from slurry-cast anode limitations to engineered binder-free Sn architectures, highlighting fabrication strategies, structural design, and performance principles for next-generation lithium-ion batteries.

title: Bent alkyne units as active sites: structure–activity correlation in graphdiyne-inspired polymers for photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: Bending alkyne bonds modulates the band structure, exposes active sites, and significantly boosts photocatalytic hydrogen evolution.

title: Phosphonitrile-based porous polymer interfaces for coupled homogenization of Zn 2+ flux and interfacial electric field distribution enabling the development of dendrite-free zinc anodes
abstract: PPOP binding sites facilitate Zn 2+ desolvation and selective transport. This regulates ionic flux and suppresses the local electric field concentration, lowering nucleation overpotential to enable uniform, dendrite-free zinc deposition.

title: Structure-dependent hydrogen sorption and cycling stability of MgH 2 enabled by Ti/Ni multiphase catalyst–CNT co-modification
abstract: A Ti/Ni multiphase catalyst–CNT co-modification strategy enables fast hydrogen sorption, reduced apparent activation energy, and enhanced cycling stability of MgH 2 through synergistic catalytic and carbon-layer effects.

title: Machine learning aided design of reversible MXene electrocatalysts for Li–air batteries
abstract: Suitable MXenes are proposed as reversible electrocatalyst cathodes for lithium–air batteries using a combined density functional theory-machine learning workflow.

title: Rapid electron/ion transport in a 3D holey graphene aerogel framework toward high-loading Na–Se batteries
abstract: A holey 3D graphene framework is engineered to achieve efficient electron conduction and rapid Na + diffusion in a self-supporting SePAN cathode, which delivers exceptional performance in Na–Se batteries at high mass loading.

title: Multifunctional Z-scheme Bi-MOF/g-C 3 N 4 photocatalyst for pharmaceutical degradation, hydrogen evolution, and electricity generation
abstract: Sunlight-driven photocatalyst for energy and environmental sustainablility.

title: Mitigating structural deterioration via partial substitution with Fe in Mn-based Prussian white cathodes for Na-ion batteries
abstract: Partial substitution of Mn by Fe in Prussian White impacts the electrochemical performance, as well as both redox and structural mechanisms.

title: The methanol electrooxidation activity and the CO-resilient surface of a La x Fe 1− y − z Cu y Pd z O 3 perovskite
abstract: The La x Fe 1− y − z Cu y Pd z O 3 shows activity towards the electrooxidation of methanol along with a CO-resilient surface.

title: Cerium-Infused Bismuth Ferrite: A Piezoelectric Powerhouse Igniting Fenton-like Catalysis to Shatter Microplastics
abstract: Microplastic (MP) pollution is a global environmental concern, and conventional advanced oxidation processes (AOPs) for MPs removal often require harsh conditions. Meanwhile, the environmental risks of degradation byproducts are overlooked....

title: Ion-exchange assisted charge storage via strategic intercalation of ZnCo 2 O 4 /V 2 O 5 heterostructures for high-energy hybrid supercapacitors
abstract: Oxygen-vacancy-enriched ZnCo 2 O 4 /V 2 O 5 heterostructures enable synergistic ion-exchange-assisted charge storage via surface redox and K + intercalation, delivering 2632 F g −1 and achieving 102 W h kg −1 in an asymmetric hybrid supercapacitor.

title: Robust CO 2 electrolysis via in situ interfacial engineering with exsolved Fe nanoparticles on a perovskite cathode
abstract: Schematic illustration of CO 2 adsorption and reduction process on the in situ exsolved cathode surface and comparison of mechanically composited and in situ exsolved nanoparticle morphologies and interfaces.

title: Modulating the M–S motifs of a metallic electrocatalyst with low overpotential for the hydrogen evolution reaction in acidic electrolytes
abstract: In situ S–Ni–M–S (M = Fe/Co) motifs in R-FeNiCoS optimize electronic structure, reducing energy barriers and enhancing HER activity comparable to commercial Pt catalysts.

title: Photocatalytic hydrogen evolution enabled by integrating stable C^N cyclometalated [Pt(C^N)(O^O)] motifs into covalent organic frameworks
abstract: Single-atom Pt-integrated MCOFs derived from [Pt(C^N)(O^O)] motifs enable cocatalyst-free photocatalytic hydrogen evolution, with the Pt–C motifs as the active sites, offering a strategy for designing single-atom-based MCOF photocatalysts.

title: Cyclic voltammetry driven Bi–Ag single-atom alloy electrocatalysts for enhanced CO 2 -to-formate conversion
abstract: Cyclic voltammetry drives the reconstruction of Bi–Ag single-atom alloys with atomically dispersed active sites, achieving up to a 1.9-fold higher faradaic efficiency and 3.5-fold enhanced selectivity for CO 2 -to-formate conversion.

title: Engineering hydrolytic stability and reversible O3/P3/OP2 transitions in O3-type sodium-ion battery cathodes through Cu 2+ doping
abstract: Cu doping enhances the hydrolytic stability of O3-type cathodes by suppressing Na + /H + exchange and surface degradation. It promotes Na + transport and reversible phase transitions, thereby improving electrochemical performance.

title: Photovoltaic-assisted water oxidation on MoCoO x -coupled multi-element-doped BiVO 4 photoanode enabling 6.4% unbiased solar-to-hydrogen efficiency under visible light
abstract: Ti/W/P co-doping and MoCoO x coupling enhance photovoltaic-assisted charge separation in BiVO 4 , boosting visible-light-driven water oxidation and achieving 6.4% solar-to-hydrogen efficiency in a tandem configuration.

title: Atomistic insights into the chemical stability and ionic transport at Li-metal/Li-argyrodite interfaces
abstract: Atomistic simulations of the Li-metal/Li-argyrodite interface reveal the formation of an amorphous solid-electrolyte interface (SEI) that then crystallises into an antifluorite phase of poor Li-ion conductivity.

title: Dual-functional CF 3 SO 3 − /amine additives synergistically construct an MgF 2 /Mg 3 N 2 -rich interphase toward high-performance magnesium–sulfur batteries
abstract: Dual OTf − /M4 additives created a non-nucleophilic electrolyte featuring mononuclear [Mg(M4) 3 (OTf)] + active species, enabling in situ formation of MgF 2 /Mg 3 N 2 -rich interphase that facilitated rapid Mg 2+ transport and high-performance Mg/S batteries.

title: Construction of an amorphous Fe-doped CoOOH coated crystalline Co 2 Mo 3 O 8 heterostructure for stable and efficient oxygen evolution
abstract: The amorphous Fe-doped CoOOH coated crystalline Co 2 Mo 3 O 8 heterostructure formed via electrochemical reconstruction exhibits high OER activity and excellent structural stability, achieving efficient and stable oxygen evolution.

title: Dual atom Cu–Fe ensemble sites for enhanced efficiency of green ammonia production from the tandem electrochemical nitrate reduction process
abstract: A Cu–Fe dual-atom catalyst on N-doped carbon enables tandem nitrate-to-ammonia conversion via bifunctional ensemble sites. Cu activates NO 3 − , Fe hydrogenates NO 2 − intermediates, achieving high efficiency, as confirmed by experiments and DFT.

title: In-situ synthesized oxygen-vacancy-rich CuO nanosheet arrays for low-temperature ppb-level NO2 sensing
abstract: Developing a strategy to tailor the morphology and sensing properties of CuO nanosheets array for low-temperature, ppb-level selective Nitrogen dioxide (NO2) detection is of great significance as it represents a...

title: Theoretical insights into the topotactic transformation of intermediate γ-Mo 2 N into α-Mo 2 C by carburization
abstract: The carburization process of γ-Mo 2 N to α-MoC 1− x under the atmosphere of CH 4 and H 2 has been investigated by using DFT calculations and ab initio molecular dynamics (AIMD) simulations.

title: Gradient inverse temperature crystallization for high-quality MAPbBr 3 perovskite single crystals and supreme-sensitivity X-ray detectors
abstract: Gradient inverse temperature crystallization under Br-rich/Pb-poor conditions yields high-quality MAPbBr 3 single crystals. These endow X-ray detectors with a supreme sensitivity of 4.36 × 10 5 µC Gy air −1 cm −1 and an ultralow detection limit.

title: Correction: From cluster halides to catalysts: nanostructured molybdenum carbides for efficient hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: Correction for ‘From cluster halides to catalysts: nanostructured molybdenum carbides for efficient hydrogen evolution reaction’ by Guillaume Dubois et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2026, 14 , 11332–11343, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TA09668E.

title: Carbon nanotube-grafted silicon–carbon composite as a highly durable anode material for lithium-ion batteries
abstract: SWCNT-embedded Si–C composites offer enhanced structural integrity and internal conductivity, enabling stable operation with high areal capacity and long-term cycling performance for practical lithium-ion battery applications.

title: Flexible and high-color-rendering electroluminescent device via self-adsorptive carbon quantum dots
abstract: Carbon quantum dots featuring highly coordinative surface groups largely simplify the fabrication of composite luminescent materials, enabling the development of flexible and high-color-rendering ACEL devices.

title: Multifunctional electrocatalysis on transition metal-doped biphenylene: a first-principles and machine learning study of single-atom catalysts for HER, OER, and ORR
abstract: Machine-learning-assisted DFT screening identifies high-performance TM single-atom catalysts on biphenylene for electrochemical HER/OER/ORR.

title: Characterizing the MnO 2 dissolution process via EQCM for rechargeable aqueous batteries
abstract: This work clarifies the pH-dependent deposition and dissolution of MnO 2 in aqueous electrolytes using in situ EQCM. It shows that proton-coupled redox processes and interfacial pH govern MnO 2 growth, stability, and dissolution behavior.

title: Cottonseed cake-derived hard carbon anode with modulated carbon layer spacing and pores for highly reversible and durable sodium-ion batteries
abstract: Herein, a novel cottonseed cake-derived hard carbon anode material was synthesized via pre-oxidization, acid–base treatment and carbonization, which provides a promising potential toward highly reversible and durable sodium-ion batteries.

title: Oxygen-deficient bimetallic oxide M 0.11 W 0.89 O 3− x for flexible energy storage and electrochromic applications
abstract: The development of multifunctional materials that combine energy storage and electrochromic properties is crucial for next-generation flexible electronics.

title: Molecular orientation–steric hindrance tradeoff of cyclohexylammonium passivators for high-performance perovskite solar cells
abstract: We investigate cyclohexylammonium salts with tailored alkyl chains in PSCs. Cyclohexylethylammonium iodide (CEAI) enables near-vertical orientation and strong defect interactions, yielding 25.58% efficiency (certified 25.30%) and enhanced stability.

title: Water-based room-temperature synthesis of Ce-BDC-MOFs using different additives: a comprehensive investigation on properties and tetracycline adsorption performance
abstract: The water-based room-temperature synthesis of Ce-BDC-MOFs as green adsorbents.

title: Origin of capacity rise in LiFePO 4 batteries: practical implications for reliable cell design
abstract: The origin of the unclear “capacity rise” phenomenon in LFP batteries was systematically revealed and validated across 79 prismatic cells with diverse designs, enabling deliberate control and practical guidelines for cell design.

title: Why do amorphous–crystalline heterostructures excel in the urea oxidation reaction over single phases?
abstract: Amorphous (red) are disordered with defects and active sites; crystalline (blue) are ordered, conductive, and stable; A–C heterostructures combine both for superior UOR performance.

title: Realizing a thermoelectric conversion efficiency of 10% with long-term stability in a kesterite Cu 2 ZnSn(S 1− x Se x ) 4 single-leg device
abstract: High and stable thermoelectric performance in a kesterite Cu 2 ZnSn(S 1− x Se x ) 4 single crystal.

title: Multi-scale metal composite electrocatalysts: synthesis, synergistic mechanisms and applications
abstract: This review highlights recent progress in metal single atom–nanoparticle composite electrocatalysts, focusing on their synthesis, structure–activity relationships, and synergistic mechanisms for energy conversion reactions.

title: Binder-free nickel–iron selenide catalyst arrays for coupling hydrogen production with polyethylene terephthalate plastic electro-upcycling
abstract: Simultaneous green H 2 production and PET plastic upcycling.

title: Halogenated additive-driven morphology optimization enables universal performance enhancement in multiple electron acceptor-based organic solar cells
abstract: Halogen-based solid additives (SAs) that utilize noncovalent interactions have demonstrated remarkable success in precisely modulating the morphology of active layers to enhance organic solar cell (OSC) performance.

title: Crystal structure of Cu 2 Zn(Ge x Si 1− x )Se 4 solid solution: the kesterite to wurtz–kesterite structural phase transition
abstract: Cu 2 Zn(Ge x Si 1− x )Se 4 exhibits a distortion-driven phase transition from tetragonal kesterite ( I 4̄) to monoclinic wurtz–kesterite ( Pn ), with phase coexistence at x = 0.45–0.55 and band gaps ranging from 1.31 to 2.22 eV.

title: Influence of europium ion doping on photoinduced properties of 2D cobalt hydroxide: photocatalytic degradation and negative photoconductivity studies
abstract: Europium-doped cobalt hydroxide nanosheets were successfully synthesized. The incoporation of Eu ions induced a rise in thermal stabililty and negative photoconductivity of the material.

title: Tungsten oxide electron transport layer enables unprecedented operational stability in MAPbI 3 p–i–n perovskite solar cells
abstract: By using tungsten oxide (WO x ) as an electron transport layer in inverted perovskite solar cells, long-term operational stability is achieved attributed to interface stabilization between MAPbI 3 and WO x .

title: Influence of Li-site dopants on phase stabilization, lithium distribution, and ionic conductivity in high-entropy Li-garnet solid electrolytes
abstract: Li-site dopants regulate the topology of active and dopant-adjacent vacancies in high-entropy garnets, governing Li-ion transport and phase stability.

title: Confining the FeSe/NiSe heterostructure in in situ formed carbon microspheres for high-efficiency sodium storage
abstract: A hybrid comprising an FeSe/NiSe heterostructure and an in situ derived carbon layer (FeNiSe@C) is fabricated. FeNiSe@C delivers high initial coulombic efficiency and excellent sodium storage performance.

title: Multifunctional phase change composites enabled by structurally engineered carbon microtubule aerogels for efficient thermal energy conversion and storage
abstract: Fabrication schematic of CKF@CoNS aerogel and LA/CKF@CoNS PCC; application in thermal management of electronics, personal wearables, and batteries; photothermal and electrothermal conversion and storage.

title: Diffusion–reaction competition governs zinc electrodeposition in three-dimensional carbon scaffolds
abstract: We identify diffusion–reaction competition governing Zn deposition in 3D carbon scaffolds. Nanoporous, high specific surface area carbons enhance ion transport, shifting deposition to a diffusion-dominated regime and enabling bottom-up Zn growth.

title: Oxygen-rich g-C 3 N 4 quantum dot engineered graphene cathode enabling highly selective 2e − oxygen reduction reaction in a value-added reaction assisted Zn–air battery
abstract: Oxygen-rich g-C 3 N 4 quantum dots on graphene regulate active sites via C–O–C groups and π–π coupling, stabilizing OOH* for highly selective 2e − ORR, while enabling value-added reaction assisted Zn–air batteries with high capacity and H 2 O 2 yield.

title: Impact of metal and active site configurations in hydrogenation reactions with N-doped graphene single atom catalysts
abstract: A computational study of graphene SACs (Fe, Co, Mn, Ru) shows that the active-site configuration dictates hydrogenation: pyrrolic NHNH is the most preferred, except for Ru. NBO/NEDA rationalize trends, guiding design beyond metal choice.

title: Charge gradient separator regulating ion transport towards stabilizing Zn metal anodes
abstract: The charge gradient separator facilitates Zn 2+ diffusion and simultaneously restricts SO 4 2− from approaching the Zn anode surface, thus effectively promoting uniform Zn deposition and suppressing side reactions.

title: Unraveling the interfacial degradation mechanism of a metal oxide electrocatalyst/gas diffusion layer in Zn–air batteries through FIB-SEM analysis
abstract: FIB-SEM probed the GDL/electrocatalyst/electrolyte interface in Fresh and Used Zn–air batteries. Tomography reveals decompaction, pore formation, catalyst permeation and La segregation, reducing conductivity and O 2 diffusion.

title: Bioderived and all-solution-processed tribolayer component enables adaptive design of flexible nanocellulosic triboelectric nanogenerators
abstract: All-solution-processed TENG made of ultrathin nanocellulose and graphene (<10 µm) enables high-output, flexible, and biodegradable energy harvesting and self-powered sensing for sustainable, adaptive wearable electronics.

title: Synergistic ion–electron coupling in thermoelectric hydrogels for self-powered sensing
abstract: A synergistic ion-electron coupling strategy is proposed by integrating ionic and electronic conduction pathways within a single hydrogel system.

title: Grafting self-assembled monolayers on polymeric substrates toward Li 3 N-stabilized solid electrolyte interphases
abstract: Self-assembled monolayers (SAMs) provide a powerful molecular-level strategy for constructing stable solid electrolyte interphase (SEI) of lithium (Li) metal batteries, yet their application is generally limited to inorganic substrates.

title: Generating green hydrogen via nickel sulfide modified titania thin film photocatalysts
abstract: We prepared NiS@TiO 2 thin films using a simple solution processing method, and investigated the hydrogen evolution efficiency using methanol as sacrificial electron donor.

title: High-precision synthesis and electrochemistry of Pt nanoparticles
abstract: The effect of catalyst particle size on electrochemical reactions remains underestimated. Here, we investigate size-dependent activity and durability using high-precision size-controlled Pt nanoparticle synthesis via decoupled nucleation and growth.

title: Development of high-performance deicing coatings through engineered weak interfacial domains
abstract: A polymer coating with engineered weak interfacial domains reduces ice adhesion by 90% and interfacial toughness by 96%, enabling ice shedding from UAV propellers via a validated stress-focusing mechanism.

title: Morphology-controlled Fe 3 O 4 @CNF nanocomposites for sustainable paper-based energy storage with recyclability
abstract: This work presents a biodegradable Fe 3 O 4 @CNF flexible electrode for paper-based supercapacitors, combining efficient energy storage and photocatalytic pollutant degradation with waste-free, sustainable, and scalable processing.

title: Synergistic Zn 2+ -doping and in situ alkali conversion toward high-voltage and air-stable O3-type sodium-ion battery cathodes
abstract: A synergistic Zn 2+ -doping and in situ alkali conversion strategy stabilizes the O3-type cathode, achieving exceptional high-voltage cycling stability and enhanced air stability through structural and surface regulation.

title: Interfacial In–S V –N bond mediated d/p-band center up-shift in an S-scheme heterojunction for boosting photocatalytic H 2 evolution
abstract: A 2D/2D S-scheme S V -ZIS/CN heterojunction with interfacial In–S V –N bonds serves as atomic charge-transfer bridges, mediating d/p-band up-shift for boosted photocarrier separation and superior H 2 evolution.

title: LAPONITE ® nanoclay as a solid-state charge transfer mediator for piezocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: Robust Si–O–Zn interfacial bonds in LAPONITE ® /ZnO composites facilitate rapid electron–hole transfer and inhibit recombination, enabling superior photo/piezocatalytic hydrogen evolution with outstanding activity and stability.

title: Surface engineering of stainless-steel mesh with superwettability toward efficient water treatment through electroflotation
abstract: A superaerophobic electrode engineered by femtosecond laser ablation achieved high electroflotation performance by generating fine bubbles and thereby promoting bubble–pollutant interactions.

title: Unlocking lattice oxygen mobility via acid etching in Co 3 O 4 /CeZrO 2 for efficient passive NO x adsorption
abstract: Enhancing active oxygen species mobility and replenishment to develop high-performance passive NO x adsorbers (PNAs) for effective NO x removal during the cold-start period (<200 °C) of diesel engines is crucial to meeting stringent emission standards.

title: Low-Voltage Paired Electrolysis via MOF-Derived Hierarchical Pt-Cu Electrocatalysts for Integrated Hydrogen Production and Chemical Upgrading
abstract: Integrating value-added chemical upgrading with energy-efficient hydrogen production represents a promising strategy for sustainable electrochemical systems. Herein, we report MOF-derived Pt-incorporated Cu catalysts directly grown on Cu foam (Pt-Cu/CF) as...

title: Moderate hydrogen bond network via in situ dissolved interfacial cation solvation for highly active aqueous zinc–manganese batteries
abstract: According to synergistic effects of cation and hybrid electrolytes, water molecules were constrained by in situ dissolved cations at cathode interface to construct moderate hydrogen bond network for promoting MnO 2 /Mn 2+ dissolution/deposition.

title: High cyclic reversibility in eutectic lithium calcium anodes characterised by electron microscopy
abstract: Microscopy shows Li leaving and re-entering a 3D Li–Ca eutectic electrode structure, thus suppressing dendrite formation. Asymmetric cycling routines highlight the differences in the anodic and cathodic behaviour of the electrode during a half-cycle.

title: Novel silicoaluminophosphate molecular sieves with CHA/GME intergrowth structures: synthesis, structural elucidation and catalytic application
abstract: Novel SAPO molecular sieves with CHA/GME intergrowth show outstanding catalytic performance in methanol amination.

title: Hierarchical chemical bonding and multi-valley band edge-induced high performance in layered Bi 6 Ag 2 O 6 Se 4 : a theoretical study
abstract: Strong anharmonicity and multi-valley band edges lead to promising thermoelectric performance in layered p-type Bi 6 Ag 2 O 6 Se 4 along the in-plane (IP) direction.

title: Defect passivation by annealing enables stable transport in Li-doped Mg 2 Sn epitaxial films for microfabricated thermoelectric devices
abstract: Post-growth annealing suppresses defect-driven instability in Li-doped Mg 2 Sn epitaxial thin films, increasing mobility and power factor. Stable p-type transport translates to improved micro-TE device output.

title: Significantly enhanced energy storage performance in multi-layer SrTiO 3 -based films via introducing a PZT polarization layer
abstract: SrTiO 3 paraelectrics, characterised by low electrical hysteresis loss, have been extensively employed in capacitor dielectric energy storage films.

title: Versatile roles of elemental doping in the optimization of the thermoelectric performance of R-GeTe studied by first principles calculations
abstract: The intrinsic mechanism of the effects of eight dopants on the electrical and phonon properties of R-GeTe was comprehensively studied, including defect formation energy, band structure and lattice thermal conductivity.

title: Composition-driven design of solid polymer electrolytes: effects of non-coordinating and coordinating polymers on ionic transport in PVDF-HFP/PEG blends
abstract: This study performs an in-depth investigation on how the interplay between coordinating and non-coordinating polymers governs ionic transport in solid polymer blend electrolytes (SPBEs).

title: Synergistic Enhancement of Strength and Ductility in Crosslinked Polymer Binders for High-Stability Silicon Anodes in Lithium-Ion Batteries
abstract: Crosslinking of the polymer binder is a powerful approach to suppress volume changes of Si-anode in lithium-ion batteries. However, this curing system is predominantly effective for soft-type flexible polymeric backbones...

title: Cu cluster-anchored bismuthene promoting electrocatalytic reduction of CO 2 into C 2 products: a theoretical study
abstract: Cu cluster doped bismuthene towards CO 2 RR to C 2 products was investigated via DFT. The results reveal that the selectivity depends on the size of Cu clusters, where no more Cu 3 is identified.

title: Dispersion-mediated active-layer interfacial regulation using halogenated thioanisole additives achieves high-efficiency organic solar cells
abstract: Halogenated thioanisole additives modulate donor–acceptor intermolecular interactions by tuning dispersion-dominated π–π stacking and short-range contacts, enabling compact molecular packing and 20.21% efficiency for organic solar cells.

title: Synthesis of visible-light-responsive YbTaO 4− x N y via different work function metal-assisted nitridation for photocatalytic overall water splitting
abstract: YbTaO 4− x N y (M) synthesized via nitridation assisted by three different work function metal powders (Mg, Zr, Al) exhibits varied absorption band edges and visible-light-driven water splitting performance.

title: Moisture-driven electric power generation using graphene-based hydrogels for sustained power output and self-powered sensing
abstract: Moist electric generators (MEGs) produce electric power from hydration gradients and have been identified as a promising sustainable technology for advanced energy systems.

title: Flexible zinc-ion hybrid supercapacitors with high energy density and long cycling life enabled by the microfluidic assembly of MXene composite fibers
abstract: Well-aligned MXene/MnO 2 battery cathode and rGO/MXene capacitor anode were prepared via microfluidic wet-spinning technology. The flexible ZIHSCs assembled with these electrodes exhibited high energy density and excellent cycling stability.

title: Broadband solar absorber and high-temperature thermal emitter based on deep-learning optimization and design
abstract: A deep-learning-designed metamaterial absorber achieves >90% absorptivity over a 2078 nm bandwidth. Its thermal radiation efficiency stays high and even improves as temperature rises, promising for thermal energy conversion.

title: Synergistic redox enhancement in ternary Mn–ZnFeOOH oxyhydroxides for high-performance supercapacitor applications
abstract: Illustration of ion transport and charge storage mechanism in the engineered electrode for supercapacitor application.

title: Insight into multivalent iron complex-bound oxygen vacancy-rich BiOBr nanodiscs for photocatalytic ammonia synthesis
abstract: Multivalent Fe atoms attached to OV-rich 2D-BiOBr nanodiscs have been fabricated and utilized for photocatalytic NH 3 production from N 2 . The role of active sites and OVs has been clearly investigated by experimental and computational studies.

title: The coulombic efficiency trough in silicon anodes for lithium-ion batteries: mechanisms, challenges, and stabilization strategies
abstract: Silicon anodes exhibit a coulombic efficiency trough associated with volume expansion, repeated SEI rupture, and irreversible lithium loss, which can be mitigated by LiF-rich interphases, adaptive binders, and voltage window optimization.

title: Computational design of GeC 4 and SnC 4 monolayers as high-capacity fast-kinetics anodes for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: DFT-predicted GeC 4 and SnC 4 monolayers deliver 944 and 683 mAh g −1 capacities with 0.18–0.22 eV diffusion barriers, ultra-low voltages, minimal expansion, and metallic conductivity for high-performance sodium-ion battery anodes.

title: Microwave-derived Fe 2 O 3 /rGO electrodes for photo-assisted supercapacitors with enhanced charge storage
abstract: Fe 2 O 3 /rGO combines photoresponsivity with pseudocapacitive charge storage. Under illumination, rGO promotes carrier separation and charge transfer, enabling faster Fe 3+ /Fe 2+ redox reactions and a 19% increase in discharge capacitance.

title: Ultrasonic exfoliation of a Cd-based metal–organic framework into ultrathin nanosheets for visible-light-initiated trifluoromethylation and sequential oxidation–cyclisation reactions
abstract: A 2D Cd( ii )-MOF (XAIU-5) photocatalyses trifluoromethylation and oxidation–cyclisation under visible light. Ultrasonic exfoliation yields nanosheets with enhanced activity via exposed active sites and improved charge separation.

title: Dopant-induced strain modulation regulates the water oxidation performance of hematite photoanodes
abstract: We demonstrate the deep interconnection between the dopant-induced changes in crystal size and strain, and their effect on the electrochemical behavior of hematite photoanodes for solar water splitting.

title: Conductive natural fibers as dual-functional conductive agents: high conductivity and stress relief in silicon-based anodes
abstract: A novel conductive fiber derived from nature was synthesized to construct a highly conductive network and relieve the electrode stress.

title: Supercapacitors for a sustainable energy future: from materials design to multifunctional energy systems
abstract: Guest editors Chandra Sekhar Rout, Yusuke Yamauchi and Subrata Kundu introduce this themed issue on supercapacitors for a sustainable energy future.

title: Orchestrating dual-interface stabilization via a threefold mechanistic synergy of aluminum dihydrogen phosphate for ultrastable aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: Aqueous zinc-ion batteries (AZIBs) are plagued by dendrite proliferation, parasitic hydrogen evolution, and cathode degradation, which stem intrinsically from high water activity and the inherent instabilities of the electrode interfaces....

title: In situ grown cobalt-doped nickel–molybdenum bimetallic phosphide nanonet on carbon cloth for high cycling stability flexible supercapacitors
abstract: The Co-NiMoP/CC electrode shows a net-like structure on carbon cloth fibers, providing high electrochemical activity. The FASC retains 70.34% capacitance after 100 000 cycles and can power an LED and an electronic clock even under bending.

title: Harnessing the synergistic effects between a polymorphic phase boundary and second-phase hardening for enhanced electromechanical compatibility in KNN-based ceramics
abstract: A synergistic coupling of tetragonal phase enrichment and second-phase hardening breaks the d 33 – Q m trade-off in KNN-based ceramics.

title: AI-driven discovery of high-performance metal–organic frameworks for next-gen atmospheric water harvesting
abstract: Metal–organic frameworks (MOFs) offer unique advantages for atmospheric water harvesting (AWH) due to their high porosity and tuneable water adsorption behavior.

title: Integrated overview of solvents and materials for reactive carbon capture and utilization
abstract: This review links CO 2 capture solvents and materials to practical processes and contactors, explores integrated capture-conversion routes for upgrading CO 2 , and assesses techno-economic feasibility and the current status of capture technologies.

title: Failure mechanisms of aluminum alloy foil anodes in lithium-ion batteries governed by composition and cell design
abstract: The work demonstrates that both alloying and the negative to positive (N/P) capacity ratio dictate the cycle life performance and dominant mode of failure in aluminum alloy foil anodes in lithium-ion batteries.

title: Preparation of gradient heterogeneous hydrogels by Pickering emulsion templating method and its trifunctional coupling of photothermal–evaporation–power generation
abstract: Solar-driven interfacial evaporation holds great promise: vapor-driven hydropower turns ambient heat into clean electricity while producing freshwater, tackling both the energy crisis and water scarcity in one stroke.

title: Biomimetic hierarchical scattering coatings via interfacial molecular stitching for durable radiative cooling of outdoor power facilities
abstract: Inverse Kirkendall-tailored biomimetic hierarchical scatterers with multi-scale nanoporosity enable efficient radiative cooling for power infrastructure through optimized light management.

title: Molecular fusion in covalent organic frameworks promotes oxygen reduction and water oxidation for efficient photocatalytic H 2 O 2 production
abstract: Molecular fusion strategy is applied in covalent organic framework (COF) photocatalysts for efficient hydrogen peroxide (H 2 O 2 ) production.

title: Controlling the ratio of Ni and Fe co-doping in a Prussian-white cathode to improve the structural stability and redox performance of advanced sodium-ion batteries
abstract: This study proposes co-doping of Ni and Fe, along with its optimized ratios (Mn : Ni : Fe = 6 : 3 : 1), to secure the cycling and structural stability of manganese hexacyanoferrate (Prussian white) cathode in the sodium-ion batteries (SIBs).

title: Boron-modulated amorphous cobalt layer on Cu foam enabling efficient and stable hydrolysis of ammonia borane
abstract: The B-CoCu foam enables rapid hydrogen production with a high turnover frequency (TOF) of 161.5 (H 2 ) mol/((cat-metal)mol min −1 ) and an excellent stability for over 40 cycles without obvious degradation.

title: Interfacial chemistry in aqueous rechargeable batteries: critical insights into solvation control, stability limits, and design challenges
abstract: Aqueous batteries are analysed from an interfacial-centric perspective, where electrolyte solvation, local proton activity, and electric double-layer organization collectively determine electrochemical stability and device performance.

title: 3D-printed Bi 2 Te 3 -based thermoelectric devices with phase-change heat sinks: toward efficient energy harvesting and ultra-fast temperature sensing
abstract: A DIW-printed out-of-plane Bi 2 Te 3 -based device with a 3D-printed PCM heat sink increased the thermal gradient and boosted output power by 150%. It was integrated into a wireless temperature alarm as a self-powered IoT solution.

title: Size-dependent amorphous–crystalline phase transitions in ultra-small gold colloids
abstract: We combined liquid phase high resolution TEM and atomistic simulations to demonstrate the size effect on the atomic structure of ligand-free gold colloids.

title: Orchestrating explainable AI, ChatGPT, and human expertise: a framework for extracting polymer design guidelines
abstract: Orchestrating XAI, ChatGPT, and expert knowledge to extract actionable and chemically intuitive guidelines for designing high-performance functional polymers.

title: Bio-based superaerophobic hydrogels to regulate bubble dynamics in gas-evolving electrocatalysis
abstract: Fully bio-based chitosan hydrogels impart superaerophobicity, enabling bubble-tolerant HER electrodes.

title: High- k elastic composite dielectrics and microstructural engineering: high-sensitivity, ultra-wide linear range, and robust flexible capacitive pressure sensors for wearable electronics
abstract: Synergistic integration of high- k TPU/MWCNT composites and rational microstructure design achieved a capacitive pressure sensor with exceptional pressure resolution across an ultra-broad dynamic range.

title: Dual-iron source engineering for optimised solid-state reaction kinetics towards high-performance Na 4 Fe 3 (PO 4 ) 2 P 2 O 7 cathodes
abstract: A scalable kilogram-scale synthesis of NFPP was achieved through a FePO 4 –FeC 2 O 4 synergistic strategy, enabling the fabricated NFPP//HC pouch cell to deliver exceptional structural integrity and prolonged cycling durability.

title: Electrochemical reduction conditioning modified Fe-based catalysts with structural disorders for efficient ammonium production from nitrite reduction
abstract: Electrochemical reduction of NO2 -to NH4 + offers a direct, energy-efficient pathway for sustainable ammonia production by circumventing the rate-determining NO3 --to-NO2 -conversion that constrains traditional NO3 -reduction (NO3RR). Herein,...

title: Heterodimensional “nebula-cluster” hybrids from MOFs and BP nanosheets: towards multifunctional, flame retardant, and closed-loop recyclable flexible foam
abstract: The escalating demand for multispectral stealth and multifunctional integration in lightweight platforms, such as unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), presents a formidable challenge for conventional single-band materials.

title: Boron-engineered interfacial electronic states orchestrate layered metal–π–carbon nitride coupling for intensified nonradical reactivity
abstract: Boron-engineered LDH@BCN delivers durable, nonradical PMS activation for efficient MMH degradation with negligible metal leaching.

title: Synergistic mechanisms of boron-doped heptazine/triazine homojunction in polymeric carbon nitride for efficient photocatalytic H 2 O 2 evolution
abstract: The incorporation of B atoms assists in forming an appropriate heptazine/triazine heterojunction ratio within MCN- x B–LiK, facilitating rapid ORR for H 2 O 2 .

title: Heteroatom engineering of ortho -fluorinated triarylamine based hole transport materials for enhanced performance in perovskite solar cells
abstract: A series of low-cost donor–π–donor structured hole-transport materials have been developed. Among them, DTP-FOS exhibits superior performance to spiro-OMeTAD, achieving higher power conversion efficiency and enhanced operational stability.

title: Stabilizing layered Sr-intercalated CoO 2 nanotubes for bifunctional energy applications: enhanced OER catalysis and energy storage
abstract: Synthesis of phase-pure Sr-intercalated CoO 2 nanotubes with tunable electronic structure for OER and supercapacitors.

title: Graphdiyne-supported metal alloy at the atomic level for the efficient cycloaddition reaction of CO 2
abstract: This work realized the successful anchoring of Ir and Cu atoms in pairs on graphdiyne. The iodide ligand Ir–Cu dual atomic site facilitated the efficient cycloaddition of CO 2 and epoxide.

title: ZIF-8-based biocomposites via reactive extrusion: towards industrial-scale manufacturing
abstract: Twin-screw extrusion, a continuous mechanochemical approach, enables one-step solid-state production and shaping of ZIF-8-based biocomposites for biomedical applications.

title: Heterocyclic aramid-based phase-change films fabricated via the oil-in-oil emulsion technology with multiple energy conversion, thermal regulation, and electromagnetic shielding properties
abstract: Octadecane (OD) was encapsulated within a composite matrix composed of heterocyclic aramid (HA), carbon fibers and carbon nanotubes to form an OD-in-HA/C non-aqueous emulsion, which was then coated to yield flexible HA/C/OD phase change films.

title: Theoretical evaluation of pristine, single B- and N-doped, and BN co-doped graphenylene as metal-free cathode catalysts for nonaqueous Li–O 2 batteries
