abstract: BN co-doped GP, with low ORR/OER overpotentials, favorable Li 4 O 4 formation, fast kinetics, and reduced Li 2 CO 3 formation, is a stable, metal-free, environmentally benign bifunctional cathode electrocatalyst for rechargeable non-aqueous Li–O 2 batteries

title: In situ investigation of Li permeation through grain boundaries in garnet-based solid electrolytes
abstract: Garnet-type solid electrolytes, such as Li 7 La 3 Zr 2 O 12 (LLZO), are promising candidates for next-generation solid-state batteries due to their high ionic conductivity, mechanical stability, and excellent compatibility with lithium metal anodes.

title: Elucidating the structure–performance relationship in single-particle NCM cathodes via controlled precursor synthesis
abstract: Well-faceted single-particle Ni-rich NCM(LiNi 0.92 Co 0.03 Mn 0.05 O 2 ) cathodes were synthesized via molten-salt calcination. This study correlates precursor morphology-driven particle shape, size, and facets directly to electrochemical characteristics.

title: Photothermal halloysite aerogel encapsulating phase change materials for solar-driven seawater desalination
abstract: The halloysite-based aerogel exhibits a high encapsulation rate for phase change materials, and the solar-driven seawater desalination device based on it demonstrates excellent seawater desalination rate.

title: Structural and catalytic insights into Pd-UiO-67 frameworks for CO 2 hydrogenation to methanol
abstract: This study investigates the catalytic performance of palladium nanoparticles supported on UiO-67(Zr) MOF for CO 2 hydrogenation to methanol, emphasising the influence of the size and location of Pd particles in relation to the MOF matrix.

title: Formaldehyde electrolysis in a membrane-free electrolyzer: low-energy hydrogen and formate co-production with Cu-based boride electrocatalysts
abstract: B/Cu x O/CF electrocatalyst drives formaldehyde oxidation at 0.06 V (100 mA cm −2 ), achieving ∼97% formate and reducing energy demand by ∼1.64 V while doubling H 2 output.

title: Janus NiN 4 –CuN 4 catalyst supported by a double-layered ZIF-8 structure for CO 2 electrocatalytic reduction to C 2+ products
abstract: The inner NiN 4 layer adsorbs CO 2 and catalyzes its conversion to CO, while the CO diffuses outward. The outer CuN 4 layer reduces CO 2 (CO 2 RR) at high CO concentration, producing 24.4% CH 3 CH 2 CH 2 OH.

title: Discovery of energetic ionic cocrystals via high-throughput virtual screening
abstract: High-throughput computational screening guided by empirical design rules addresses resource-heavy trial-and-error for ADN cocrystals, producing the first example that realizes the balance between hygroscopicity and energy.

title: Proton-coupled electroreduction of nitrate on α-SnWO 4 and photon-assisted enhancement of ammonia formation
abstract: A cooperative redox participation of the dual metal sites in α-SnWO 4 makes it a photo-cathode for ammonia production from aqueous nitrate solution through proton-assisted electron-transfer.

title: Dynamic hydrogen bond evolution in thermosensitive hydrogels for self-adaptive passive cooling
abstract: The dynamic hydrogen-bonded thermosentive hydrogel with a hierarchical synergistic dissipative pattern is developed based on AI-assisted optimization, overcoming the kinetic-thermodynamic mismatch in traditional hydrogels.

title: Hydroxylamine intermediate governs selectivity in nitrite hydrogenation on Pd-based catalysts for sustainable water treatment
abstract: Catalytic hydrogenation of nitrate (NO 3 − ) and nitrite (NO 2 − ) is a promising route for drinking water purification and rebalancing the global nitrogen cycle.

title: Multifunctional sulfamic acid additive for synergistic electrolyte regulation toward long-cycling aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: Aminosulfamic acid enables bulk-interface synergy by restructuring the hydrogen-bond network, optimizing Zn 2+ solvation, forming a water-poor adsorption layer, and promoting uniform (002)-plane deposition to suppress dendrites and side reactions.

title: Bifunctional Ni-TiO 2 catalyst for the efficient glycolysis of polylactic acid: a selective route to obtain 2-hydroxyethyl lactate
abstract: This study explores PLA depolymerization over Ni-TiO 2 catalyst which selectively produce 2-hydroxyethyl lactate under mild conditions and enabling sustainable plastic waste valorization.

title: Friction-triggered self-reactive deep eutectic solvents for tunable lubrication under an ultra-heavy load through in situ formation of an ultra-thick multi-gradient tribofilm
abstract: To ensure the reliable operation of mechanical systems under increasingly severe conditions, developing intelligent lubricants with harsh condition self-adaptability, tunable interface behavior and enduring lubrication is a promising solution.

title: Salt-responsive evaporation behavior of chitosan-based hydrogels: driven by dual-mechanism synergy for efficient seawater desalination
abstract: A salt-induced water activation strategy is proposed to develop a highly efficient marine bio-derived chitosan-based hydrogel evaporator featuring reduced crystallinity and anomalous salinity-induced evaporation enhancement.

title: Interface engineering of graphdiyne (g-C n H 2 n −2 ) coupled with NiCoP to construct a barrier-free electron channel for photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: An efficient NiCoP/GDY Ohmic junction photocatalyst was developed for H 2 evolution. Enhanced activity was achieved via barrier-free electron transfer, providing a new design paradigm for GDY-based photocatalysts.

title: Tin-promoted platinum supported on CeO x nanocluster catalysts for CO 2 -oxidative dehydrogenation of propane
abstract: The oxidative dehydrogenation of propane with CO 2 technology is a promising route for propylene production. This study demonstrates the synergistic activation of propane and CO 2 over supported Pt–Sn bimetallic catalysts on CeO x -SiO 2 .

title: A comparative study of nickel–silica catalyst architectures for CO 2 methanation: insights into structure–performance relationships
abstract: Ni–SiO 2 catalysts with distinct architectures influence methane formation during CO 2 methanation.

title: Orbital modulation and coupled redox cycling in Fe–Mn dual-atom catalysts for efficient Fenton-like water purification
abstract: A Fe–Mn dual-atom catalyst with coupled redox cycling enables selective singlet oxygen generation for efficient water purification.

title: Eutectic cleavage of lignocellulosic biomass to incubate pseudo-graphitic carbon crystallites for high-efficiency sodium energy storage
abstract: A lignocellulosic cleavage strategy is proposed to incubate pseudographitic carbon crystallites by eutectic chemistry. The as-modified hard carbon achieves an impressive enhancement in sodiation capacity and initial coulombic efficiency.

title: Tuning polymorphism, molecular orientation and crystallinity in P(NDI2OD-T2) thin films informed by Hansen solubility analysis
abstract: A Hansen solubility parameter-guided framework is introduced for decoupling side chain–solvent and backbone–solvent interactions for control of polymorphism and orientation in conjugated polymer thin films.

title: Lattice plainification and high symmetry enhance the thermoelectric performance of n-type Bi 2 Te 3 -based materials
abstract: Interstitial In doping suppresses the formation of anion vacancies, alters local bond angles, enhances symmetry, and improves µ . It introduces resonant energy levels, increases m * , synergistically optimizing S and σ , and improving PF and ZT .

title: Dual-site synergistic catalysis of a single atomic Ru and tungsten compound heterojunction for enhanced alkaline hydrogen evolution
abstract: Ru-W dual sites enable interfacial electronic synergy, promoting H–OH cleavage and H-bond formation via electron transfer. This lowers the energy barrier of the rate-determining step, enhancing alkaline HER kinetics.

title: Enhanced high-temperature energy storage performance in a PEI-based dielectric composite via constructing dual charge carrier transport barriers
abstract: This study constructed a sandwich structure that enhances the high-temperature energy storage properties of a PEI-based composite dielectric by introducing “valley type traps” and “peak-shaped barriers”.

title: Revealing the optimal pre-lithiation threshold of a LiNi 0.8 Co 0.1 Mn 0.1 O 2 cathode for stable anode-free lithium metal batteries
abstract: This work identifies the optimal pre-lithiation threshold for NCM811 in AFLMBs by resolving the trade-off between Li-compensation and structural degradation.

title: CuO-multimetal ferrites for synergically boosting peroxydisulfate activation and decontamination by adsorption and oxidation mechanisms
abstract: The MMFC exhibited high recyclability and stability with a removal efficiency of 89.5% after five consecutive cycles, demonstrating the MMFC/PDS process has potential application prospects for eliminating antibiotic residues in real contaminated water samples.

title: Anti-freezing, high-voltage, and ultra-stable micro-supercapacitors enabled by phase engineering of 2D SiP 2 in LiCl–DMSO electrolyte
abstract: This work develops cryogenic-tolerant, high-voltage micro-supercapacitors with long cycling stability via synergistic electrode–electrolyte optimization for advanced flexible and micro-electronics.

title: Separate and combined effects of Gua + and SCN − ions on charge carrier dynamics in mixed Sn–Pb perovskites
abstract: Guanidinium and thiocyanate ions show a synergistic effect in mixed tin-lead perovskites, passivating crystal defects, enhancing carrier transport, and yielding pinhole-free films with large grains.

title: Plasmon-enhanced colloidal silver telluride quantum dots for boosted near-infrared photodetection performance
abstract: We design a novel NIR nanostructure for high-efficiency emission enhanced by LSPR effects (CuE@SiO 2 @Ag 2 Te), which exhibits remarkable fluorescence EFs, PL QY, and long PL lifetime. Additionally, it demonstrates excellent photodetection performance.

title: Cost-effective hydrotalcite-derived Fe-based catalysts for stable and continuous catalytic methane decomposition
abstract: Here we report a hydrotalcite-derived Fe-based catalyst with favorable CH 4 decomposition performance, and the resulting carbon materials as conductive additives in sodium-ion batteries exhibit excellent performance.

title: Non-covalent interaction of hydroxyl-capped layered double hydroxide oligomers for high-performance P recovery
abstract: TEA-capped LDHs tune the balance between adsorption and desorption, preventing excessively strong binding and enabling fully recoverable phosphorus capture with high regeneration efficiency in water.

title: Aluminum chloride-based catholytes for stable high-voltage solid-state sodium batteries
abstract: High-voltage cycling is critical for high cathode-level energy density in sodium solid-state batteries. We present interactions between NaAlCl 4 catholytes and oxide cathodes and investigate fluorination strategies to enhance high-voltage stability.

title: Mechanistic insights into CO 2 capture and electrochemical conversion in nonaqueous Na–CO 2 batteries
abstract: A schematic illustration of amine-assisted CO 2 capture and its subsequent utilization in a Na–CO 2 battery.

title: Efficient synthesis strategy of near-zero volume change materials for all-solid-state batteries operable under minimal stack pressure
abstract: All-solid-state batteries using solid electrolytes with high ionic conductivity and operable under minimal stack pressure are promising for energy storage applications.

title: Fluorine-free microporous membranes with high wetting resistance for membrane distillation
abstract: A simple method has been developed for fabricating fluorine-free microporous membranes that possess high wetting resistance for membrane distillation.

title: One-pot nanocasting of 3D ordered bicontinuous mesoporous Pt-based multimetallic alloys for efficient hydrogen evolution
abstract: A one-pot strategy yields ordered gyroidal Pt-based multimetallic alloy catalysts with electron-rich Pt sites, enabling enhanced alkaline hydrogen evolution activity and durability.

title: Cr( vi ) reduction mediated by hydrogen radicals generated from formic acid dehydrogenation on tailored Ni-based catalysts: mechanisms, progress, and perspectives
abstract: A review on the construction of a reaction system based on Ni-based catalysts for the dehydrogenation of HCOOH to generate hydrogen radicals, and its application in the efficient reduction of Cr( vi ).

title: Surface modification strategies for direct methane and direct ammonia solid oxide fuel cell anodes: current approaches and future directions
abstract: This review highlights how infiltration, exsolution, and atomic layer deposition modify SOFC anodes to improve direct methane/ammonia conversion, enhance electrochemical performance, and combat degradation via coking and nitridation.

title: Flexible and lightweight PVDF–CsPbBr 2 I piezoelectric nanogenerator with enhanced interfacial coupling for versatile high-output sensing applications
abstract: Strong interfacial interaction at the PVDF–CsPbBr 2 I interface induces self-polarization and effectively stabilizes the electroactive β-phase.

title: Fabrication and applications of ionic covalent organic framework membranes
abstract: ICOFMs, with high ion selectivity, continuous mass transport/conduction channels and strong electrostatic interactions, show great application potential in conduction, membrane separation, biomedicine, sensing and energy conversion.

title: Chelating additive enabled dual-action hydrogel polymer electrolyte: suppressing dendrite formation and crosstalk in aqueous rechargeable zinc metal batteries
abstract: A dual-function PVA–PAA hydrogel electrolyte with an EDTA chelating additive suppresses zinc dendrites and Mn 2+ cross-talk in the aqueous Zn–MnO 2 batteries, enabling stable long-term cycling via coordinated ion regulation and interfacial control.

title: Synergistic effects of Li 3 InCl 6 /VGCF composite coating on the electrochemical performance of single-crystalline LiNi 0.6 Co 0.1 Mn 0.3 O 2 cathodes for all-solid-state lithium batteries
abstract: Li 3 InCl 6 /VGCF composite coating on SC-NCM613 cathode targets simultaneous enhancement of ionic and electronic transport.

title: Entropy-mediated lattice strain in a Ruddlesden–Popper perovskite oxide for highly active and bifunctional oxygen electrocatalysis
abstract: This study introduces an innovative high entropy RP perovskite cathode (HELNO) for SOFCs/SOECs. The novel structure of HELNO reduces the oxygen adsorption resistance and boosts the ORR/OER efficiency.

title: Molecular packing modulated photocatalytic degradation using cocrystal catalysts
abstract: Asymmetric stacking in triclinic cocrystals promotes charge separation and directional transport, enabling efficient exciton dissociation and a ROS-synergistic degradation pathway, leading to complete phenol mineralization.

title: Structural incommensurability drives ultralow lattice thermal conductivity in misfit layered (BiSe) 1.23 CrSe 2
abstract: Misfit layered (BiSe) 1.23 CrSe 2 exhibits intrinsically ultralow lattice thermal conductivity from anharmonic bonding, incommensurate structure, and cationic defects, further suppressed to the theoretical limit via Pb doping.

title: Tuning the local environment of atomic Pt shells of Pt 3 Fe@Pt nanoparticles enables energy-saving hydrogen production
abstract: Energy-saving hydrogen production is realized via coupling with the methanol oxidation reaction endowed with superior CO tolerance.

title: Substrate-dependence of the Ru active unit anchored on metal oxides for the oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: Incorporating Ru active sites into rutile-, spinel-, and pyrochlore-type metal oxides overcomes their intrinsic constraints, leading to improved catalytic activity and enhanced stability.

title: Heteroatom doping strategies in single-atom catalysts: tuning electronic structure for selective peroxymonosulfate activation
abstract: Heteroatom doping with P, B, and S serves as a key strategy for tailoring the electronic structure of SACs, thereby enabling selective PMS activation for effective wastewater treatment.

title: Phonon and charge-carrier transport decoupling via amorphous carbon coating promotes high thermoelectric cooling performance of p-type Bi 0.5 Sb 1.5 Te 3
abstract: An amorphous carbon layer, majorly composed of ordered sp 2 CC bonds and with a thickness of ca. 5 nm, effectively decouples phonon and charge-carrier transport in p-type Bi 0.5 Sb 1.5 Te 3 , underpinning high thermoelectric cooling performances.

title: Tungsten-modified MXene-integrated spinel oxides as high-performance and stable catalysts for water/seawater oxidation
abstract: This work presents an MXene-WO 3 integrated MnCo 2 O 4 spinel catalyst enabling highly selective, chloride-tolerant oxygen evolution for alkaline seawater electrolysis.

title: p-Block Ge Dual-Atom Catalysts with Enhanced Sulfur Hybridization for Bidirectional Catalysis in High-Performance Li-S Batteries
abstract: Single-atom catalysts (SACs) have been extensively utilized in lithiumsulfur (Li-S) batteries through d-p orbital hybridization; however, they still face challenges related to sluggish reaction kinetics and unresolved polysulfide shuttle effects....

title: Conductivity mechanism of lithium argyrodite solid-state electrolytes
abstract: This work demonstrates that the ionic potential isotropy between 4a and 4d Wyckoff sites is the primary descriptor of argyrodite conductivity.

title: A critical review on minimizing non-radiative recombination losses in antimony selenosulfide solar cells
abstract: This review highlights recent advancements in antimony-based solar cells, focusing on non-radiative recombination mechanisms. It discusses strategies to mitigate these losses and provides critical insights into future research directions.

title: Probing electrochemical phenomena in commercial pouch cells using a multimodal magnetic resonance approach
abstract: Further advances in portable electrochemical energy storage will increasingly depend on multifunctional in situ diagnostic tools capable of probing materials at the molecular level under realistic operating conditions.

title: Chirality engineering in layered metal hydroxides for enhanced electrocatalytic oxygen evolution and reaction mechanism revelation
abstract: This work provides a new approach to designing chiral nanomaterials for electrocatalytic oxygen evolution reaction (OER) and deepens the understanding of the relationship between chiral structure and electrocatalytic OER performance.

title: Divergent A-site regulation mechanisms in rare-earth and alkaline-earth nickelates for unlocking bifunctional oxygen electrocatalysis
abstract: A-site ORR/OER activity regulation in ANiO₃ nickelates: alkaline-earth systems follow a linear correlation, while rare-earth systems show f-electron modulated volcano, with EuNiO 3 as optimal bifunctional O 2 electrocatalyst.

title: Mesoporous platinum coating on quartz fibres by soft-templated electroless deposition
abstract: We report a soft-templated electroless deposition strategy for the fabrication of mesoporous platinum films on quartz fibres (mPt@QF).

title: Giant electrocaloric response in thiourea under a low electric field in the cryogenic region
abstract: Almost 70 years after the discovery of ferroelectricity of thiourea – one of the earliest organic crystal structures to be studied – this work unveils new electrocaloric effects in the cryogenic region that can be enhanced by mechanical pressure.

title: Superaerophobic hydrogels for diaphragm modification to suppress gas crossover in alkaline water electrolyzers
abstract: Interfacial engineering with superaerophobic PVA hydrogels provides a robust shield against gas crossover in alkaline water electrolysis, ensuring operational safety.

title: Surface-engineered Ni–Pt alloys as robust and cost-effective electrocatalysts for high-performance proton exchange membrane water electrolysis
abstract: A robust, cost-effective Pt-enriched Ni–Pt surface alloy electrocatalyst is developed for PEMWE. Optimized via thermal reconstruction and elucidated by DFT, it delivers enhanced stability and tuned hydrogen adsorption.

title: Biomass pyrolysis with a Fe-Ni-CaO char-based catalyst for efficient green hydrogen generation and bio-oil upgrading via coupled –H/O–H activation
abstract: This study explores low-temperature (600 °C) biomass pyrolysis to produce H 2 , and a tandem catalytic pyrolysis-phenol adsorption strategy is proposed for efficient catalyst use.

title: Enhancing electrocatalytic nitrogen reduction on Ce-WO 3 catalysts through the atomically local electric field effect: accelerated electron transfer and enhanced nitrogen activation
abstract: An atomic-level local electric field in Ce-WO 3 accelerates electron transfer through the Ce–O–W pathway, which enhances the activation of N 2 on the catalyst surface and improves electrocatalytic nitrogen reduction reaction performance.

title: Interface engineering of self-supported Ni 3 S 2 /MoS 2 @MXene heterostructure on nickel foam for advanced asymmetric supercapacitor with superior energy density
abstract: A self-supported Ni 3 S 2 /MoS 2 @MXene/NF electrode delivers high capacitance and rapid charge transport, enabling an asymmetric supercapacitor with high energy and power density for advanced energy-storage applications.

title: Engineering charge transfer channels in D–π–A structured multicomponent COFs for efficient photocatalytic uranium extraction coupled with organic degradation
abstract: A novel D–π–A covalent organic framework with cyano-grafted π bridges for efficient photocatalytic uranium extraction and simultaneous organics degradation.

title: Fluorine migration regulation for direct regeneration of high-nickel cathode scraps
abstract: In this work, a calcium citrate-assisted strategy is proposed, in which preferential reaction with HF suppresses fluorination side reactions arising from PVDF pyrolysis, thereby enabling efficient and stable regeneration of cathode materials.

title: Hierarchical NMO@NiFeP reconstructed by step-current electrolysis for durable oxygen evolution in alkaline freshwater and seawater
abstract: Step-current electrolysis induces the in situ deep reconstruction of NMO@NiFeP into hierarchical nanoflowers, balancing active site exposure and structural integrity for high-performance and corrosion-resistant seawater electrolysis.

title: Benefits and yields of simultaneous free chlorine and hydrogen generation in side-emitting optical fiber photocatalytic reactors
abstract: A side-emitting optical fiber photocatalytic reactor enables tunable co-generation of hydrogen fuel and free chlorine, decoupling optical and chemical limits to deliver on-demand disinfection and energy in decentralized water systems.

title: Synergistic tailoring of ion–dipole interactions and segmental dynamics in fluorinated ionogels for low-temperature micro-supercapacitors
abstract: Fluorinated side-chain modulation synergistically enhances ion carrier and ion mobility, enabling micro-supercapacitors operative at low temperatures.

title: Lead-optimized design of metal–organic frameworks for trace SF 6 capture
abstract: A lead-optimized strategy identified an amine functionalized MOF for SF 6 capture at trace concentrations. Density functional theory studies show that the amino group on the linker lowers the pore binding site energy for effective SF 6 capture.

title: Atomic-layer-deposited Ru–RuO x mixed protective layer for improving ionic–electronic conductivity of sintered LiCoO 2 cathodes in Li-ion batteries
abstract: Ru–RuO x mixed protective layers were deposited by atomic layer deposition without post-treatment, enabling improved interfacial conduction in conducting-agent-free sintered cathodes.

title: Site-specific attachment of a H 2 -evolving artificial metalloenzyme onto carbon nanotubes via electrografting of a protected thiophenolate diazonium salt
abstract: Electrografted thiophenolates at carbon nanotubes afford the attachment of a H 2 -evolving artificial metalloenzyme obtained from combined site-directed mutagenesis and nickel insertion.

title: Bioinspired Porous Heterogeneous Aerogel Fibers with Woven Architecture for Ultra‑Broadband Microwave Absorption and Thermal Regulation
abstract: With the rapid development of smart wearable technology, there is an urgent need for smart textiles that integrate efficient microwave absorption and thermal management functions. Inspired by the porous structure...

title: A high-entropy iron-based perovskite rich in oxygen vacancies for efficient oxygen reduction in intermediate-temperature SOFCs
abstract: Perovskite-type La 0.8 Sr 0.2 FeO 3-δ (LSF) is a competitive cobalt-free cathode candidate in solid oxide fuel cells (SOFCs), but its sluggish oxygen reduction reaction (ORR) kinetics at intermediate temperatures severely hinders practical application....

title: Fast hydride-ion conduction in complex hydride Ba 2 MgH 6
abstract: Defect engineering in Ba 2− δ MgH 6−2 δ enhances hydride-ion conductivity, evidenced by electrochemical measurements and molecular dynamics simulations, demonstrating defect control for high-performance hydride-ion solid electrolytes.

title: From electrode saturation to structural collapse: unraveling the rollover failure in a lithium-rich manganese/graphite pouch cell at elevated temperature
abstract: The failure mechanism of medium-voltage Li-rich batteries at elevated temperature is unveiled: electrode saturation triggers Li plating and transition-metal crosstalk, forming a degradation cascade that leads to catastrophic capacity rollover.

title: Elucidating the effect of mesopores on the conversion of green furans to aromatics over hierarchical Ga-MFI, Ga-MFI/MCM-41 composites, and Ga-SPP
abstract: Designing the optimal hierarchical Ga-MFI zeotype is shown to be a delicate balancing act where specific surface area, mesoporous volume, acid site strength and density, all need to be weighed together and tested for the target reaction at hand.

title: Mapping the catalytic landscape of doped Pd(111) for formic acid synthesis via CO 2 hydrogenation using first-principles, microkinetics, and SISSO descriptors
abstract: The DFT–microkinetics–SISSO framework reveals dopant-dependent activity and selectivity trends in Pd(111)-catalyzed CO 2 hydrogenation.

title: Investigating the asymmetric electrochemical, structural and electronic properties of Mn-rich Li(Mn,Fe)PO 4 electrode materials
abstract: Mn-rich Li(Mn,Fe)PO 4 cathode materials show considerable asymmetric behaviour in their electrochemical, structural and electronic properties, with important implications for commercial battery applications.

title: High-stability cathode materials for sodium-ion batteries: optimization strategies and promising future applications
abstract: This review includes: structural design approaches, performance-enhancing modifications, and emerging application prospects of advanced Na-ion cathodes; optimization strategies and future applications of stable Na-ion cathodes.

title: Enhanced catalytic performance of Ni/Al 2 O 3 for the carbon dioxide reforming reaction by controlling the pore structure and Ni addition in catalyst design
abstract: This study emphasizes the importance of the pore structure and Ni addition in designing highly active and stable Ni/Al 2 O 3 catalysts for carbon dioxide reforming.

title: Photothermal chemical mechanical polishing: a synergistic mechanism for fast and atomic level surface perfection
abstract: A photothermal chemical mechanical polishing (PTCMP) technique is developed with defect-engineered black nano-titanium dioxide (TiO 2 ) as the photothermal catalyst, achieving high material removal rate and atomic-level surface roughness.

title: Irreversible phase transitions in BiFeO 3 –SrTiO 3 lead-free piezoceramics
abstract: For the first time in BF-ST-Mn solid solutions, an irreversible phase transition from pseudo-cubic to rhombohedral induced by electric fields was observed via in situ poling synchrotron XRD, confirming their non-routine relaxor (NR) characteristics.

title: Mass transport behaviour of caesium and strontium in geopolymer cements
abstract: Geopolymers are a promising alternative to conventional Portland cement-based wasteforms for immobilising hazardous radioactive fission products such as caesium-137 and strontium-90, offering superior durability and lower leach rates.

title: Metal–organic framework derived hierarchical hollow materials for high-performance zinc–air batteries
abstract: This work critically evaluates recent advances in MOF-derived hollow materials and their transformative potential in advancing zinc–air battery (ZAB) technology.

title: New nanostructured, ion-conductive, bent-core liquid crystals containing lithium and sodium salts as soft electrolyte candidates
abstract: New TEG-based bent-core soft-electrolytes incorporating Li + and Na + salts form tuneable mesophases and supramolecular gels with ionic conductivities up to 10 −3 S cm −1 , optimised by composition control and annealing.

title: Altering the deposition behavior and enhancing the interface stability via a gradient-potassiophilic scaffold achieve a highly efficient and dendrite-free potassium metal anode
abstract: A 3D gradient-potassiophilic free-standing host was constructed to inhibit the K dendrite growth and volume variation, achieving a K metal anode with extraordinary cycling stability.

title: Micronuclear batteries for nuclear waste transmutation and sustainable energy: a comprehensive review and future perspectives
abstract: This review explores the applications of nanotechnology to address the challenges of radioactive wastes by transforming it into sustainable nuclear batteries, thereby enhancing the viability of nuclear energy as a clean alternative to fossil fuels.

title: Interfacial modulation of BiVO 4 photoanodes with SnO 2 quantum dots and FeOOH for efficient solar-driven water oxidation
abstract: This work presents a BiVO 4 -based photoanode co-modified with SnO 2 quantum dots and a FeOOH cocatalyst, enhancing the photocurrent, charge separation, and oxygen evolution for efficient solar-to-hydrogen conversion.

title: “Pseudo-Haber–Bosch” process for the production of green ammonia using a defect-rich and active Bi 2 MoO 6 /Cu 8 S 5 electrocatalyst via an interrupted chemical reaction
abstract: The Bi 2 MoO 6 /Cu 8 S 5 catalyst achieved an NH 3 yield rate of 6757 µg h −1 cm −2 FE of 57.9%. The unique synergistic oxide–sulfide heterostructure facilitates a “pseudo-Haber–Bosch” pathway via a dipole-assisted trapping mechanism.

title: Unraveling the deactivation mechanism and stability enhancement of cobalt-based heterogeneous Co 3 O 4 /g-C 3 N 4 catalysts in CO 2 hydrogenation
abstract: Sintering was identified as the primary factor responsible for the deactivation of the catalyst, while the active Co phases governed the product selectivity—HCP-Co favored CH 4 production, whereas FCC-Co facilitated CO formation.

title: Decoupled water splitting technologies: from redox mediators to renewable energy integration
abstract: Traditional water electrolysis is hindered by challenges: explosion risks from gas crossover, which persists despite membrane separation; high costs for membranes and noble metal catalysts; and limited adaptability to renewable energy fluctuations.

title: Photoreforming of plastic waste into value-added chemicals with hydrogen production
abstract: Plastics threaten ecosystems but possess untapped hydrocarbon potential. This review summarizes the mechanisms, diverse photocatalysts, typical conversion examples, challenges, and prospects of photocatalytic plastic conversion.

title: H2-SCR at high water concentrations with in-situ generated NH3 -SCR for efficient removal of NOx from H2 engines
abstract: The two main challenges of application of Pd-based catalysts in H2-SCR are catalytic performance and water resistance. In this study, oxide supports (TiO2 and ZrO2) were employed and experimentally demonstrated...

title: Embedding antiperovskite nanophases into polymer hosts boosts ionic conductivity and interfacial compliance for all-solid-state lithium metal batteries
abstract: Incorporating Li 3 OCl into the PEO–LiTFSI matrix reduces interfacial resistance and enhances ionic conductivity by suppressing PEO crystallinity and creating additional Li + transport pathways within the composite.

title: Regulating manganese dioxide polymorphism by epitaxial electrodeposition for reversible aqueous Zn metal batteries
abstract: Seed-layer-guided epitaxial electrodeposition under high temperatures enables the room-temperature formation of conductive γ-MnO 2 , unlocking the development of high-performance cathode-free aqueous Zn batteries.

title: Efficient Fe-N 4 site activated by Te doping with polarity engineering for enhanced nitrate electroreduction reaction
abstract: The Fe-N 4 /Te-CN catalyst achieves 2.31-fold higher activity than its non-polar Fe-N 4 /CN counterpart, underscoring the role of induced polarity as a pivotal lever for NRA performance.

title: Deciphering the dynamic behavior of geminal-Cu sites for high-efficiency reduction of nitrate to ammonia
abstract: Dynamic change of the Cu–Cu distance on polymeric carbon nitride facilitates a remarkably low potential-determining step for efficient NO 3 RR performance.

title: Enhanced green-light harvesting and PS II loading in CdTe QD-embedded IO-SnO 2 bio-photoanodes for photocurrent generation
abstract: The self-assembled IO-SnO 2 /CdTe QDs/PS II inverse-opal bioelectrodes show high PEC photocurrent, as CdTe QDs convert green to red light for PS II reabsorption and the macroporous network promotes PS II loading and interfacial electron transfer.

title: Catenated imide-linked porous organic polymers for solar energy harvesting
abstract: Porous organic polymers (POPs) have attracted significant attention for solar energy applications due to their high surface areas, chemical stability, and tunable structures.

title: Isosorbide dimethyl ether enables interfacial regulation to boost the aqueous zinc–sulfur battery performance
abstract: Air-stable isosorbide dimethyl ether is introduced into the aqueous electrolyte, which restructures Zn 2+ solvation, suppresses hydrogen evolution, enables uniform Zn deposition, and enhances I 3 − /I − redox mediation, boosting capacity retention.

title: Revisiting the K–Mn–O system for green cathode materials in potassium-ion batteries: from K 2 MnO 4 to K 3 MnO 4
abstract: This study reports the synthesis, structural characterization, and electrochemical evaluation of α-K 3 MnO 4 and K 2 MnO 4 as potential cathode materials for potassium-ion batteries (KIBs).

title: Decoupling structure–performance relationships in hard carbon anodes: a comparative study of slope- and plateau-dominated sodium storage mechanisms
abstract: Zinc gluconate enables controllable pore evolution in hard carbon via an in situ ZnO templating pathway, tuning slope- and plateau-dominated sodium storage involving adsorption, diffusion and pore-related processes.

title: Recent developments in manganese chalcogenide electrodes for sodium-ion hybrid capacitors
abstract: This review systematically summarizes recent advancements in efficient strategies for the exploration and design of advanced manganese chalcogenide-based anodes for sodium-ion hybrid capacitors.

title: Harnessing unidirectional deformation driven by light and temperature: towards an untethered soft microgripper in diverse environments
abstract: This work presents a multi-responsive unidirectional soft actuator demonstrating microgripping capabilities in response to visible light and temperature in both dry and wet environments respectively and has the potential for biomedical applications.

title: Alkali metal hydroxide treatment enhances the selectivity of CdWO 4 for oxygen reduction to hydrogen peroxide
abstract: Alkali metal hydroxide treatment enhances the selectivity of CdWO 4 for oxygen reduction to hydrogen peroxide, with the CsOH-treated CdWO 4 exhibiting the highest H 2 O 2 selectivity.

title: Polymer dots enabling ultrahigh capacitive energy storage in cellulose-based all-organic nanocomposites
abstract: The cyanoethyl cellulose-based nanocomposites, reinforced by polyethyleneimine-based polymer dots (PEPDs), achieve a high discharged energy density of 35.2 J cm −3 .

title: Efficient through-space charge transfer in heavy-atom-free photosensitizers via conformational preorganization for photocatalytic polymerization
abstract: Conformational preorganization enables efficient through-space charge transfer in heavy-atom-free photosensitizers. This strategy facilitates effective photocatalytic polymerization without requiring heavy atoms.

title: Self-powered flexible Janus-like metal–organic framework membrane for sustainable moisture-enabled electrokinetic energy harvesting
abstract: A self-powered, flexible, durable, and adaptable Janus MOFs-based membrane generates electricity from ambient humidity for next-generation sustainable wearable energy harvesters.

title: Domain engineering via AlN compositing in high-entropy ferroelectric ceramics for synergistic electro-thermal regulation
abstract: Our work elucidates an effective pathway for domain engineering design via secondary phase compositing. The introduction of AlN enables the synergistic regulation of ferroelectric stability and thermal transport properties.

title: Dopant-induced interfacial strain enables bifunctional water splitting in Ni-doped SnS/MoS 2 heterostructures: data-driven insights
abstract: Single-atom Ni doping transforms SnS/MoS 2 NHSs into an efficient bifunctional catalyst by simultaneously enhancing HER and OER activities through interfacial strain engineering.

title: Toward unified interphase engineering: the solid-electrolyte interphase in batteries and supercapacitors
abstract: SEI formation is a universal electrochemical phenomenon governing both batteries and supercapacitors. Battery-derived design principles are translated into predictive strategies enabling high energy, high power, and long lifetime.

title: Joule heating-assisted preparation of a highly efficient, long-term-stable FeNiMoO hydrogen evolution reaction catalyst
abstract: Our study demonstrates that Joule heating-assisted Mo-doping is effective in enhancing the HER performance of NFF. NiFe–Mo 1.5h -HTS exhibits excellent HER activity and stability over 3000 h at 400 mA cm −2 .

title: Multiscale thermoelectric transport: bridging quantum mechanics to macroscopic systems through the Landauer–Boltzmann paradigm
abstract: Thermoelectric energy conversion is a promising pathway toward sustainable energy technologies, enabling the direct conversion of temperature gradients into electrical power as well as solid-state cooling without moving parts.

title: Boosting the thermoelectric performance of SnTe through localized van der Waals gap construction and configurational entropy engineering
abstract: The combination of localized van der Waals gaps and configurational entropy engineering results in an exceptional average ZT avg of ∼0.96 in the SnTe-based thermoelectric material over a testing temperature range of 323–823 K.

title: Superior cycling stability of Zr–Hf–Co–Fe–Ni hydrogen isotope storage alloys achieved by hydrogen binding energy modulation
abstract: In Zr 2 Co-based alloys, Hf/Fe/Ni co-alloying tunes the interstitial hydrogen binding energy and mitigates the unit cell volume expansion, yielding the Zr 1.5 Hf 0.5 Co 0.5 Fe 0.3 Ni 0.2 alloy with excellent cycling stability.

title: Fabrication of multifunctional eutectogels for wearable sensing applications
abstract: We used a binary solvent strategy to construct a multi-crosslinking network of eutectogel, which exhibits exceptional mechanical behavior, self-healing, and adhesion to multiple substrates due to diverse interactions and accessible functional groups.

title: Strain engineering of ScN thin films and its effect on optical, electrical, and thermoelectric properties
abstract: Insertion of crystal defects (dislocations, point defects and lattice distortion) plays a crucial role in thermoelectric/optical properties and can be controlled in thin films of the narrow-band-gap semiconductor ScN.

title: First-Principles Study of Dual-Site Sr-M (M = Ga, Hf, Ge) Co-Doping in the Perovskite Solid Electrolyte Li 0.5 La 0.5 TiO 3 : Effects on Li⁺ Migration and Electronic Insulation
abstract: The perovskite Li 0.5 La 0.5 TiO 3 (LLTO) is an attractive solid electrolyte for all-solid-state battery applications because of its favorable Li⁺ transport characteristics. Its properties could be further...

title: Anion-competition regulation of PbI 2 frameworks for two-step fabricated perovskite solar cells
abstract: A multifunctional ionic liquid enables anion-competition-regulated Pb 2+ coordination, directing the growth of oriented porous PbI 2 frameworks for scalable, high-efficiency, and stable perovskite solar cells.

title: Laser-induced graphene-based corrugated bimodal sensor for strain and contact monitoring in wearables and intelligent robotics
abstract: Fabricated via a rapid one-step process, the laser-induced graphene-based corrugated bimodal sensor enables strain and contact monitoring for wearables and intelligent robotics.

title: Decoupling anodic passivation from electrodeposition: synergistic crystallographic and electronic modulation for energy-efficient EMD electrowinning
abstract: A novel Ti–Mn anode transforms the anisotropic HCP matrix to a symmetric BCC, establishing a conductive interfacial network to drastically lower cell voltage for energy-efficient EMD electrowinning.

title: Oriented, lightweight and compact graphite-sheet-fin-based solid ammonia carriers enabling risk-free, sustainable onboard ammonia energy supply
abstract: We propose novel graphite-sheet-fin solid ammonia carriers for risk-free onboard ammonia energy supply, and establish an integrated system for low-carbon ammonia-blended combustion and exhaust gas treatment for meeting stringent emission standards.

title: Hierarchical hollow MnTe 2 /CoTe 2 composite nanospheres assembled from porous nanosheets: synergistic structural engineering for high-performance hybrid supercapacitors
abstract: Hollow MnTe 2 /CoTe 2 composite nanospheres assembled from nanosheets are synthesized for hybrid supercapacitors.

title: Deep learning framework for accurate prediction and high-throughput search of the thermoelectric figure of merit in skutterudites
abstract: The integration of artificial intelligence and machine learning is transforming the landscape of materials discovery, facilitating unprecedented acceleration in the exploration of vast chemical spaces and the prediction of material properties.

title: Ultralow-loading Pt single atoms anchored on N-doped carbon-encapsulated Mo 2 C microspheres for efficient hydrogen evolution
abstract: Ultralow-loading Pt single atoms anchored on N-doped carbon-coated Mo 2 C microspheres exhibit exceptional HER performance.

title: A Systematic Theoretical Study on the Structure-Property Relationships of Fluorinated Linear Carbonates Based on Molecular Simulation and Machine Learning
abstract: Electrolyte engineering utilizing fluorinated linear carbonates (DMC, EMC, and DEC) holds immense promise for high-voltage lithium metal batteries. Despite extensive research, a systematic understanding of how fluorination patterns precisely dictate...

title: Dual-active Ca–Li alloy anodes enable the development of ultra-stable and ultra-low-cost liquid metal batteries
abstract: A dual-active Ca–Li anode alters Ca-based LMB discharge, suppressing self-discharge and enhancing stability. With a cost-effective Pb cathode, Ca 65 Li 35 ‖Pb cell achieves lowest electrode cost among reported Ah-level LMBs.

title: Significantly improved high-temperature energy storage performance of PP films by grafting-cross-linking modification
abstract: A modification method suitable for industrial production was proposed. The molecular structural stability was significantly enhanced, resulting in enhanced orientational polarization and reduced relaxation losses.

title: Polystyrene brush-supported dual Ir complex/N-heterocyclic carbene catalysts for efficient complex three-component dual-catalyzed photocatalysis
abstract: A polystyrene brush-based quasi-homogeneous sustainable photocatalytic platform for the green and efficient synthesis of fine chemicals in complex three-component dual-catalyzed photocatalysis.

title: Molecular origin of negative lithium transference in electrolytes with star-shaped multivalent anions
abstract: Large-scale molecular dynamics simulations illustrate that highly correlated cation–anion motion leads to negative t 0+ on the order of 1 in lithium electrolytes with star-shaped multivalent anions.

title: Tailoring TiO 2 nanotubes with an iron-based MOF precursor for enhanced photoelectrochemical degradation of methyl red
abstract: A metal–organic framework (MOF), MIL-88A, was used as a precursor through calcination for hematite (α-Fe 2 O 3 ) grown on titania nanotubes (TiO 2 NTs) to prepare an anode for methyl red photoelectrocatalytic discoloration.

title: Biomass-derived potassium-doped reduced graphene oxide-like material for high-performance supercapacitors: experimental and computational analysis
abstract: Synthesis of potassium-doped rGO from persimmon fruit for supercapacitor applications.

title: Balancing fluorine density and pore architecture in nanoporous polyketaminal networks for exceptional SF 6 /N 2 separation
abstract: Precise fluorine modulation in polyketaminal networks balances chemical affinity and steric effects. PKAN-2 achieves an exceptional SF 6 /N 2 selectivity of 150.1 by optimizing ultramicroporous confinement, surpassing heavily fluorinated counterparts.

title: A fresh perspective on accelerated degradation studies for proton exchange membranes
abstract: Driven by the increasing demand for green hydrogen, proton exchange membrane (PEM) water electrolyzers are positioned as a key technology in the sustainable energy market.

title: Role of microstructure in the lattice thermal conductivity and thermoelectric performance of Cu 2+ y Zn 1− y SnS x Se 4− x alloys
abstract: Literature values of the lattice thermal conductivity for Cu 2+ y Zn 1− y SnS x Se 4− x (CZTSSe) systems showing inverse trends with S and Se contents, attributed to phonon grain-boundary scattering.

title: Interfacial electron transfer-driven activity enhancements of carbide/alloy heterostructured catalysts toward water electrolysis for high-performance anion exchange membrane water electrolysis
abstract: Carbide/alloy heterostructured catalysts, Mo 2 C/NiMo for HER and Mo 2 C/FeNiMo for OER, are developed for activity enhancements driven by interfacial electron transfer to deliver an ultrahigh current density of 2.645 A cm −2 at 2.0 V.

title: High-energy ball-milling induced defect centers in hexagonal boron nitride (h-BN) for supercapacitor applications
abstract: Hexagonal boron nitride (h-BN), a two-dimensional material known for its thermal stability and robust mechanical properties, is considered in fields ranging from nanoelectronics to energy storage.

title: Dual-salt-enabled atomic engineering of Zn–N 4 sites through Na/Cl modulation for high-performance bifunctional oxygen electrocatalysis
abstract: This dual-salt-assisted electrospinning and pyrolysis strategy provides a general design principle for developing next-generation MNC catalysts beyond traditional transition-metal systems.

title: Realizing high energy storage performance in (Na 0.47 Bi 0.47 )Ba 0.06 TiO 3 -based ceramics with a slush-like polar state
abstract: Realizing highenergy storage performance in (Na0.47Bi0.47)Ba0.06TiO3-based ceramics by engineering a slush-like polar state.

title: Using B-site deficiency to trigger in situ formation of a nano composite high entropy double perovskite oxide air electrode for protonic electrochemical cells
authors: 'You-dong Kim', 'Jayoon Yang', 'Peter Rand', 'Praveen Kumar', 'Hyun Sik Kim', 'Neal P. Sullivan', 'Ryan O'Hayre'
abstract: In situ high-entropy perovskite oxide nanocomposite improve fuel cell and electrolysis performance of protonic ceramic electrochemical cells.

title: Selective proton-coupled electron transfer regulated by potential of zero charge for CO 2 electroreduction on tailored cobalt phthalocyanine
abstract: Modulating the PZC of CoPc can facilitate selective proton coupling to regulate catalytic activity, product selectivity, and CoPc stability.

title: Photoinduced metastable-charging of d-orbital electrons of Re centers enhances interfacial charge migration behavior for efficient coupling conversion
abstract: Metastable charging of d-orbital electrons at the interfacial Re centers constructs a gradient charge migration channel, thereby endowing the ReS 2 /ZnIn 2 S 4 heterostructure with high-efficient photochemical coupling activity.

title: Mechanism-driven interpretable modeling of hydrogen solubility in organic compounds via a hierarchical transformer
abstract: This study presents HiRegFormer, a hybrid model that accurately predicts hydrogen solubility by integrating molecular and thermodynamic features. Coupled with SHAP analysis, it offers interpretable insights for hydrogen storage material design.

title: Versatile synthesis of nanosized Ni–CeO 2 catalysts with tunable composition for power-to-gas applications
abstract: This work introduces a sustainable and versatile synthesis platform for nanosized metal–ceria catalysts with tunable composition, directly relevant to renewable energy conversion and CO 2 utilization.

title: Boosting piezo-photocatalytic performance in antibiotic wastewater treatment through graphitic carbon nitride–zinc oxide based heterojunction engineering
abstract: This work constructed a g-C 3 N 4 –ZnO@PVDF film to boost piezo-photocatalysis. PVDF piezofield suppresses electron-hole recombination, while the g-C 3 N 4 –ZnO heterojunction guides directional electron transport, achieving 99.15% tetracycline degradation.

title: Energy filtering and anisotropic structural response in polyaniline:CSA hybrids for flexible thermoelectrics
abstract: The present study highlights the prospects of thermopower tunability in hybrid α-MgAgSb blended polyaniline:CSA flexible films by elucidating and utilizing energy filtering, anisotropic structural response and charge localization effects.

title: The subtle helpers: how spectator ions govern the efficiency of electrolytic water splitting
abstract: This work systematically investigates the role of ‘spectator ions’ in OER, and offers theoretical guidance for optimizing electrocatalytic systems through rational ion selection.

title: Conformational isomerism tunes barocaloric potential of MIL-53(Ga)-fum frameworks
abstract: The existence and significant impact of conformational isomerism in the MIL-53-fum family on material properties was demonstrated. Adsorption studies showed improved longevity and refrigeration performance for the uniquely rigid MIL-53(Ga)-fum( c ).

title: Unveiling the potential of pristine and metal-doped biochar for improved fermentative biohydrogen production from whey wastewater
abstract: Ni-doped cow-dung derived biochar enhanced dark fermentative hydrogen production from whey wastewater by promoting microbial attachment, EPS secretion, and extracellular electron transfer, resulting in improved hydrogen yield and COD removal.

title: Approaching the reactivity of anions in battery electrolytes via conceptual density functional theory
abstract: Conceptual density functional theory provides descriptors (such as chemical hardness, the Fukui function, and the dual descriptor) that enable valuable computational insights into the reactivity of battery electrolyte components.

title: Recent advances in multi-metallic organic frameworks and their derivatives for catalysis, electro-/photocatalysis and sensing applications
abstract: This review offers a critical view of multi-metallic organic frameworks (MMOFs) and their derivatives, focusing on their synthesis, challenges, and the role of each metal present in MMOFs for catalysis, electro-/photocatalysis, sensing applications.

title: Thermoelectric properties of (In, Cr) 2 Ge 2 Te 6 layered compounds
abstract: This study systematically investigates the crystal structures and thermoelectric properties of layered (In, Cr) 2 Ge 2 Te 6 compounds, revealing that InCrGe 2 Te 6 solid solution exhibits ultralow lattice thermal conductivity and a maximum zT of 0.38.

title: MOF-integrated bilayer nanofibrous membranes for radiative-cooling-enhanced atmospheric water harvesting
abstract: A dual-layer PM-PC nanofibrous membrane with MIL-101 cooling and CB photothermal layers enables efficient, continuous atmospheric water harvesting by combining night-time passive cooling for adsorption and daytime solar heating for regeneration.

title: Self-doping enables flexible Ag 2 Se bulk materials for room-temperature thermoelectric generators and coolers
abstract: Achieving both deformability and high efficiency in thermoelectric materials (TEs) remains challenging, as most high-performance TEs are inherently brittle and rely on toxic tellurium.

title: Vanadium-driven oxygen vacancy modulation in MoO 3 nanosheet cathodes for aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: Vanadium doping drives the morphology evolution of MoO 3 from nanorods to nanosheets, creating oxygen-vacancy-rich structures for enhanced Zn 2+ storage.

title: Cathode properties of MgV 2 O 4 spinel for magnesium rechargeable batteries: effect of synthesis route on structure and electrochemical performance
abstract: Solvothermal MgV 2 O 4 spinel forms a more ordered structure and improves Mg 2+ conduction, enabling higher energy density based on a capacity of 175 mAh g −1 at 2 V in Mg rechargeable batteries than the mechanically milled one.

title: Correction: In situ investigation of Li permeation through grain boundaries in garnet-based solid electrolytes
abstract: Correction for ‘ In situ investigation of Li permeation through grain boundaries in garnet-based solid electrolytes’ by Sung Heo et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2026, 14 , 7388–7393, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TA09003B.

title: Recyclable, hybrid filler/PVA nanocomposite films with enhanced hydrophobic, barrier, UV-resistance and flame-retardant properties for packaging applications
abstract: Sustainable, flame-retardant, and barrier-efficient PVA-based active packaging materials.

title: Unconventional pharmaceutical drug-templated in situ electrochemical synthesis of redox-active Fe-MOF/MWCNT hybrids for point-of-care voltammetric pH sensing
abstract: First report of etodolac-drug as a molecular template for in situ electrochemical growth of redox-active Fe-MOFs on Fe-MWCNT electrodes, enabling Nernstian PCET behaviour and rapid one-drop pH sensing on a disposable screen-printed platform.

title: Exploring confined media in hybrid mesoporous silicas via thermoporosimetry
abstract: Geometry dominates over surface chemistry in thermoporosimetry, enabling transferable calibration across hybrid mesoporous silica.

title: A highly efficient electrode material based on waste plastic derived rGO decorated with polypyrrole and zinc oxide nanoparticles for supercapacitor applications
abstract: The rising concern over non-biodegradable plastics, along with the global energy issue, necessitates new and sustainable solutions.

title: Charge transport mechanism in HfO 2 -modified ZnO composite for NO x gas sensing applications
abstract: The development of efficient NO ₓ gas sensors and multifunctional dielectric materials is strongly governed by defect-mediated charge transport in oxide ceramics.

title: A degradation-free platform for intrinsic comparison of metal nanoparticles in methane-fueled SOFC anodes
abstract: Hybrid colloidal deposition and ALD encapsulation yield stable, high-activity PBMO anodes for hydrocarbon SOFCs. Pt, Ru, and Ni nanoparticles enable intrinsic activity comparison under wet CH 4 at 700 °C, while Al 2 O 3 suppresses sintering and coking.

title: High-Density Phosphorus-Doping toward Metal-Free Carbon Sphere to Boost H2O2 Selective Electrosynthesis
abstract: While heteroatom doping is competent to enhance 2e⁻-ORR activity of metal-free carbon catalysts, high-density phosphorus doping remains underexplored due to large atomic radius of P atom and oxidation tendency. Herein,...

title: Coaxial wet-spinning fiber-shaped Zn-ion batteries enabled by Zn powder and cellulose nanofiber reinforced gel electrolyte
abstract: An integrated core-sheath Zn-ion battery with cellulose nanofiber-reinforced gel electrolyte and Zn powder anode is fabricated via coaxial wet-spinning. This unique design enables ultra-stable and dendrite-free cycling for over 1600 h.

title: Unravelling lone pair induced bonding effects on thermal conductivity in metal chalcogenides using machine learning potentials
abstract: We present a fine-tuned MatterSim framework for efficient κ L prediction in pnictogen chalcogenides, capturing bonding effects beyond SCALP via Wigner heat transport formulation, enabling scalable screening beyond conventional κ L descriptors.

title: Multiscale structural design of epoxy vitrimers for stabilizing high-temperature dielectric insulation
abstract: This work elucidates the high-temperature dielectric failure mechanisms of epoxy vitrimers and pioneers a multiscale filler-engineered strategy to architecturally stabilize dynamic networks, enabling superior insulation robustness.

title: Role of Lewis base-type N sites of MOFs in achieving the preferential exposure of Zn(002) and HER-suppressed long-life zinc anodes
abstract: Schematic of the Zn deposition across the various N-containing framework-modified Zn anode based on Lewis acid-base theory.

title: Correlating Li + jump pathways and vibrational properties in lithium aluminum germanium phosphate glasses
abstract: The exponential enhancement of dc conductivity with Li 2 O is governed by the increase in carrier mobility. The facile migration of Li + is attributed to fractal conduction pathways generated through the depolymerization of the phosphate network.

title: Rational synthesis of Pd dual-atom catalysts via molecular precursor engineering for selective hydrogenation
abstract: This study demonstrates the rational synthesis of Pd dual-atom catalysts via molecular precursor (Pd 4 L 4 ) engineering for selective hydrogenation.

title: Unlocking the potential of Ni-rich NCM811 cathodes: chlorine substitution as a pathway to prevent oxygen release and transition-metal dissolution
abstract: Cl anion doping increases barriers for O 2 − formation and O 2 release, stabilizes the surface lattice, and suppresses TM dissolution in NCM811, enabling improved stability and cycle life of Ni-rich cathodes.

title: Graphene oxide-regulated hydrogel membrane for extreme heavy metal recovery: the role of size exclusion and ultralong three-dimensional channel adsorption
abstract: Three-dimensional channel adsorption in graphene oxide-regulated hydrogel membranes with great potential for the sustainable and eco-friendly recycling of heavy metals.

title: Synergistic optimization of thermoelectric performance in polycrystalline and crystalline SnS via Na doping and Se alloying
abstract: The development of high-performance and eco-friendly thermoelectric materials is crucial for sustainable energy conversion.

title: Deciphering the role of LiBr as a redox mediator in Li–O 2 aprotic batteries
abstract: Here we elucidate the interactions between solvents and the LiBr redox mediator in Li–O 2 batteries, demonstrating that singlet oxygen generation is dependent on the solvent's Lewis acidity.

title: Phase interface engineering of defect-rich 1T/2H-WX 2 /RGO (X = S, Se) nanosheets for efficient microwave absorption and supercapacitor applications
abstract: The purpose of this study is to enhance the comprehensive performance of materials in microwave absorption and supercapacitors by investigating the interfacial processes and the phase regulation mechanisms.

title: Efficient and stable perovskite solar cells processed with fluoroethylene carbonates
abstract: Perovskite solar cells based on triple cation metal halide perovskite processed with fluoroethylene carbonate exhibit over 24% power conversion efficiency and suppressed photocurrent hysteresis.

title: Noble-metal-free metal hydroxide Co-catalyst coupled Mn( ii )-doped CdS nanorods with bridged charge transport for enhanced photocatalytic hydrogen generation
abstract: Noble-metal-free metal hydroxide (Ni(OH) 2 , Co(OH) 2 , and Fe(OH) 3 ) co-catalyst modified CdS NRs nanorods employ Mn( ii ) dopants as long-lived charge-carrier bridges for enhanced photocatalytic hydrogen generation from water.

title: Predictive structure–property design rules for quasi-2D Dion–Jacobson Sn-based perovskites
abstract: We establish molecular design rules for Dion–Jacobson tin perovskites, correlating spacer polarity, stericity, and rigidity with thermodynamic stability, moisture resistance, and electronic behavior to identify optimal organic cations.

title: Silver–bismuth perovskite-inspired materials: chemistry, optoelectronic properties, and emerging applications in photovoltaics and beyond
abstract: We review Ag–Bi perovskite-inspired materials, linking structures, defects, processing, and stability to performance and applications like photovoltaics, X-ray detectors, memory devices, and nonlinear optics.

title: Enhanced proton uptake in triple‑conducting Ba y Sr 1‑ y (Fe 0.75 Mo 0.25 ) 1‑2 x Zn x Zr x O 3‑ δ perovskites
abstract: The proton uptake behavior of the BaySr 1‑ y (Fe 0.75 Mo 0.25 ) 1‑2 x Zn x Zr x O 3‑ δ materials intended for application in solid oxide and protonic ceramic fuel and electrolysis cells (SOFCs/SOECs, PCFCs/PCECs) is extensively studied to elucidate the role...

title: Sustainable magnesium phosphate cement from industrial by-products: long-term chloride resistance and non-alkaline corrosion protection mechanism
abstract: To address the dual challenges of solid waste valorization and the need for durable rapid-repair materials in chloride environments, this study develops a sustainable magnesium phosphate cement (sust-MPC) utilizing low-grade MgO by-products and circulating fluidized bed (CFB) fly ash.

title: Designing recyclable inorganic–organic hybrid polymer coatings through integrating silicone-based molecular scission points
abstract: Redefining the role of silicones in the chemistry of inorganic–organic hybrid polymer coatings by introducing them as molecular break points in addition to their function as hydrophobicity enhancers.

title: Activating interfacial polarization via snowflake-sphere dual-vacancy heterostructures for efficient S-scheme photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: Snowflake-like Cu 2− x S/ZnCdS v photocatalysts with dual vacancies create strong interfacial polarization, synergistically enhancing the built-in electric field and achieving outstanding hydrogen evolution under visible light irradiation.

title: In situ growth of mixed-valence 2D α-MnO x nanosheets within interlayer spaces of multilayer Ti 3 C 2 MXene as an efficient air cathode for rechargeable Li–O 2 batteries
abstract: The growth of mixed-valence 2D α-MnO x nanosheets within interlayer spaces of multilayer Ti 3 C 2 MXene creates a high-performance 2D/2D heterostructure designed to overcome the sluggish kinetics and poor stability of traditional battery cathodes.

title: Interfacial C–S bonding stabilizes phase-tailored Ni heterosulfides on carbon nanofibers for bifunctional electrolytic water splitting
abstract: Three-phase Ni 3 S 4 /NiS/Ni heterojunction on 3D carbon nanofibers enables fast charge transport, abundant active sites, and strong interfacial bonding, delivering efficient and durable bifunctional catalysis for alkaline hydrogen and oxygen evolution.

title: Multifunctional polymers in perovskite photovoltaics: bridging efficiency, stability, and manufacturing
abstract: Polymers have emerged as multifunctional enablers in the evolving architecture of perovskite solar cells (PSCs), addressing key challenges in film formation, interface engineering, defect modification, and device longevity.

title: Dual-Stabilization Strategy Enabled High-Entropy Sulfide@Carbon Spheres for High-Performance Sodium Storage
abstract: Despite their promise for grid-scale energy storage, sodium-ion batteries (SIBs) are hindered by the lack of robust anodes capable of tolerating severe volume changes during alloying-based sodiation. Herein, we present...

title: The role of water molecules in piezoelectric-like effects in chitosan-based biodegradable films
abstract: We systematically reveal for the first time how processing, specifically hydration, governs piezoelectric-like responses in chitosan, achieving d 33 values of 11.3 pC N −1 .

title: Stabilisation of various phases of MnO 2 for an affordable supercapacitor
abstract: Phases of MnO 2 were synthesized by a facile hydrothermal method. A phase diagram and phase interconversion mechanism were studied. Their electrochemical performance was compared, and a device was fabricated to evaluate energy storage applications.

title: Stimuli-responsive material-based smart supercapacitors: design, progress, and perspectives
abstract: This review provides a timely summary of the design of stimulus-responsive materials and their applications in the construction of smart supercapacitors, with a focus on the influence of external forces/stimuli on electrochemical performance.

title: Dynamic Confinement of Cu3 Clusters in T-C3N2 Pores: An Adaptive Interface for Selective CO Electroreduction to C3 Products
abstract: The rational design of catalysts with dynamically adaptive active sites is crucial for steering multi-carbon product formation in CO electroreduction. Herein, we propose a confined and flexible triatomic Cu3 cluster...

title: When solid polymers meet Na-ions: unraveling solvation dynamics and molecular design principles for next-generation high-energy sodium metal batteries
abstract: Solvation-centered molecular design tailors salt dissociation, ion transport, and interfaces in solid polymer electrolytes – a roadmap to safe, high-energy sodium metal batteries.

title: Microwave-assisted one-pot sol–gel synthesis of tungsten silicate microspheres with dispersed WO x and their activity in ethanol dehydration
abstract: This study introduces a novel microspherical W–SiO 2 heterogeneous catalyst for alcohol dehydration, prepared via an innovative microwave-assisted condensation synthesis method.

title: A high-performance wearable thermoelectric device with epoxy resin/PA/AlN composite heat sink
abstract: Wearable thermoelectric devices (WTEDs) have garnered significant interest for applications in personal thermal management and energy harvesting from the human body.

title: CO 2 electroreduction into C 2+ products on Cu surfaces: recent advances, challenges, and opportunities
abstract: A comprehensive review highlighting all the recent strategies and perspectives in enhancing C–C coupling selectivity in electrocatalytic CO 2 RR.

title: Phosphorus-doped hard carbon derived from waste PET bottles as a superior anode material for lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Waste PET bottles were upcycled into P-doped hard carbon via one-step H 3 PO 4 pyrolysis. The optimized sample delivered 765 mAh g −1 at 0.2 A g −1 and 531 mAh g −1 at 2 A g −1 , demonstrating PET waste as a promising high-performance anode material.

title: Nitrogen-rich mesoporous carbon nanospheres with atomic Fe–N 4 sites for efficient oxygen reduction
abstract: Fe-doped nitrogen-rich mesoporous carbon nanospheres, featuring abundant accessible atomic Fe–N 4 sites and rapid mass transport, exhibit superior ORR activity and four-electron selectivity.

title: Interlayer polarization induced by Ag–N 2 motifs in spherical covalent organic framework driving efficient CO 2 photoreduction
abstract: Interlayer polarization promotes electron transfer and modulates active sites, enabling efficient, natural-light-driven CO 2 reduction.

title: Rational design of noble-metal-free cocatalysts for balanced H 2 adsorption–desorption and accelerated charge separation toward efficient light-driven hydrogen production
abstract: A hierarchical Ni 2 P nanosheet array–graphene composite cocatalyst prepared via low-temperature phosphorization achieves a high photocatalytic hydrogen evolution rate of 20035 µmol g −1 h −1 with eosin Y as the photosensitizer.

title: Steering ion transport direction for a thin-film electrochromic energy storage device
abstract: Through a coplanar interdigitated electrode, we achieved control over the internal E-field direction and material diffusion processes in the device. Thereby enabling a single-layer electrochromic device to achieve memory effect and energy storage.

title: Unveiling charge-storage mechanisms in transition metal chalcogenide supercapacitors: understanding through in situ / operando spectroscopy and DFT studies
abstract: A schematic representation of charge-storage mechanisms in transition metal chalcogenide supercapacitors by integrating in situ / operando spectroscopy and DFT studies.

title: The Influence of Crystal Facet Exposure of Metal Oxide Semiconductors for Gas Sensing Performance
abstract: The surface atomic arrangement and electronic structure of metal oxide semiconductor (MOS) gas-sensing materials directly determine their gas sensing performance. This article systematically reviews recent research advances and underlying mechanisms...

title: Fe, Co, and ZIF-8 co-doped catalysts with carbon nanotubes and a SiOC composite backbone for high-temperature PEM fuel cell cathodes
abstract: Fe, Co, and N functionalised nanocarbon composite material development for Pt-free oxygen reduction electrocatalysts in high temperature PEM fuel cells.

title: Hybridized piezoelectric–triboelectric effects for self-powered sensing in thermally drawn nanocomposite fibers
abstract: Thermally drawn fiber-based piezoelectric–triboelectric hybrid nanogenerators enable energy harvesting and self-powered wearable sensing by converting biomechanical motion into electrical signals for health-monitoring applications.

title: Deuterium-induced hydrogen bond strengthening: a novel strategy for high stability in hybrid perovskites
abstract: Our work shows that deuterating the MA + cation in MAPbBr 3 strengthens N–D⋯Br hydrogen bonds. This molecular reinforcement boosts thermal stability and significantly improves resistance to moisture/oxygen degradation.

title: Selective CO 2 methanation over Ni catalysts on Nb-doped SrTiO 3 via interfacial electronic engineering
abstract: The metal–support interface and electronic properties govern CO 2 adsorption, hydrogen activation, and hydrogenation pathways, which strongly affect CO 2 activation efficiency and product distribution.

title: Donor–acceptor-type β-ketoenamine-linked covalent organic frameworks for enhanced photocatalytic hydrogen peroxide production
abstract: Two cyano-functionalized β-ketoenamine-linked COFs with donor–acceptor architectures are developed for efficient photocatalytic H 2 O 2 production, benefiting from enhanced charge separation and oxygen adsorption.

title: Regulating silanols in MFI zeolites toward rational design of anti-coking catalysts for methane dehydroaromatization
abstract: The silanol group is an important coke deposition site and has a significant impact on the catalytic performance of methane dehydroaromatization (MDA).

title: Hexabenzocoronene@LAPONITE® ionic cage: a secondary nanoassembly as an efficient elastic ion platform
abstract: A unique supramolecular secondary nanoassembly is first constructed using inorganic disc-like nanoclays (LAPONITE®) and organic discotic conjugated molecules (hexabenzocoronene).

title: Highly efficient cooperative CO 2 electroreduction to CH 4 on a copper cluster/C 60 hetero-structured catalyst
abstract: A copper cluster/C 60 hetero-structured catalyst could realize simultaneous activation of CO 2 and dissociation of H 2 O, further cooperatively promoting the formation of the *CHO intermediate, thereby directing CO 2 -to-CH 4 conversion.

title: Halide solid electrolytes: composition tuning, structural design, and performance optimization for all-solid-state lithium batteries
abstract: A review of halide solid-state electrolytes: from material properties (conductivity, stability, and mechanics) to all-solid-state lithium battery performance.

title: Confining high-entropy alloys within MOF-derived architectures: a dual-site strategy boosting photothermal CO 2 methanation
abstract: High-entropy alloys (HEAs) offer immense potential for catalytic transformations, yet they suffer from severe sintering and limited active site accessibility due to their typically dense architectures.

title: Nanodiamond-based TiO 2 nanocomposite separators for enhancing performance of flexible paper-based supercapacitors
abstract: Material synthesis and supercapacitor device fabrication based on a TiO 2 @DND composite over carbon interdigitated electrodes and the scheme of the subsequent charge storage mechanism.

title: Boosting solid oxide H 2 O and CO 2 co-electrolysis on Sr 2− x Fe 1.5− y Mo 0.5 Ni y O 6± δ by in situ exsolution of FeNi alloy nanoparticles
abstract: Mixed ionic electronic conducting perovskites decorated with catalytically active FeNi-based nanoparticles are promising alternative fuel electrodes for steam and carbon dioxide co-electrolysis in solid oxide cells.

title: A comprehensive review on the chemical processes and catalytic mechanisms involved in the conversion of biomass to carbon materials
abstract: This review summarizes mechanistic insights into biomass-to-carbon conversion strategies, highlighting catalytic pathways, impurity control, heteroatom tolerant catalysis, and AI guided design for scalable, high-performance carbon materials. Figure elements reprinted with permission; see article for…

title: Tris-amido naphthoquinone: toward high-capacity and stable organic cathodes for aqueous zinc batteries
abstract: Tris-amido naphthoquinone (TANQ), featuring a high density of redox centers, is demonstrated as a promising candidate for high-performance aqueous zinc–organic batteries.

title: From plastic waste to value-added chemicals: a trimetallic MOF strategy for electrochemical PET upcycling
abstract: Electrocatalytic upcycling of polyethylene terephthalate (PET) plastics into value-added chemicals provides a sustainable route to simultaneously mitigate plastic pollution and enable energy-efficient chemical synthesis.

title: Interfacial electronic modulation in electrochemically reconstructed CoOOHCuO@CeO 2 for efficient electrooxidation of 5-hydroxymethylfurfural
abstract: In CoOOHCuO@CeO 2 , CeO 2 serves as an electronic modulation center, directing electron transfer from Cu to Co via the formed Co–O–Ce interface, which enhances charge transfer efficiency and promotes the conversion of 5 hydroxymethylfurfural.

title: Superior energy storage performance in Bi 0.5 Na 0.5 TiO 3 based ceramics via synergistic design of high-entropy and superparaelectric-relaxor strategy
abstract: Entropy engineering (1.77R) induces a superparaelectric-relaxor state, achieving an ultra-high recoverable energy storage density of 12.2 J cm −3 with 81% efficiency, demonstrating great potential for pulse power devices.

title: Engineering active sites via strong metal–support interaction for enhanced catalysis on hydroxyapatite-supported transition metals
abstract: Strong metal–support interaction engineered in transition metal/hydroxyapatite catalysts enables efficient peroxymonosulfate activation via radical and non-radical pathways, achieving ultrafast phenol degradation with excellent stability.

title: A highly active NiCe(Fe)O x H y electrocatalyst for the oxygen evolution reaction with ultralow Fe leaching
abstract: CeO x incorporation disperses Fe species in Ni(OH) 2 –CeO 2 , suppressing Fe leaching by >98%. Dispersed Fe modulates interfacial charge at Ni(Fe)(OH) 2 –Ce(Fe)O 2 boundaries, generating dual Ni–O sites that synergistically enhance OER activity.

title: Porous carbon nanosheets supported Co single-atom catalysts via dual-molten-salt synergy for advanced zinc–air batteries
abstract: A single-atom Co catalyst constructed from three-dimensionally stacked carbon nanosheets exhibits high catalytic activity across the full pH range and outstanding stability, enabling high-performance zinc–air batteries.

title: Anion-engineered chalcogenide perovskites Ba 2 HfCh 4 : from light emitters to photovoltaics
abstract: Through anion (Te) alloying, stable Ba 2 HfCh 4 perovskites are tuned from direct-gap emitters with large exciton binding energies to optimal photovoltaic absorbers, achieving predicted efficiencies up to ∼27%.

title: Bipolar conjugated microporous polymer with intrinsically fast reaction kinetics for high-energy-density and high-rate-capacity symmetric all-organic lithium-ion batteries
abstract: A conjugated microporous polymer (PTTA) integrating a donor–acceptor structure with hierarchical pores and bipolar redox-active sites is developed for facilitated kinetics and high capacity. PTTA-based SAOBs show highenergy density and power density.

title: Defect-tailored ZnO nanoflowers enable efficient, metal-free ammonia synthesis through coupled piezoelectric and photocatalytic activation
abstract: ZnO–O V nanoflowers combine photo + piezocatalysis for bias-free N 2 reduction. Light + ultrasound drive charges to O V sites, boosting NH 3 with no hydrazine; 15 N verifies. DFT show stronger N 2 adsorption, lower *N 2 → *NNH barrier.

title: Design of multibranched diketopyrrolopyrrole polyelectrolytes as versatile materials for efficient photocatalytic hydrogen evolution and organic solar cells
abstract: We present multibranched polyelectrolyte polymers that demonstrate improved hydrophilicity, and reduced charge transport resistance, leading to an enhanced HER rate of 55.81 mmol g −1 h −1 and a superior power conversion efficiency of 16.78%.

title: A 3D polymorphic Cu-based ultramicroporous MOF capable of CO 2 Uptake and Conversion
abstract: Cu(trz 2 An) displays high selectivity and separation efficiency for N/CO₂ and CH/CO₂ mixtures. It also shows promising catalytic activity toward CO₂ reduction, particularly favouring the formation of ethylene.

title: Rational embedding of DMAP into covalent triazine frameworks for efficient heterogeneous organocatalysis
abstract: Easy construction of DMAP-incorporated covalent triazine frameworks as robust and green catalysts for the highly efficient synthesis of thiazolidinethione and esterification reactions.

title: A nickel–molybdenum carbide-based composite catalyst enables CO 2 electroreduction to syngas with an accurately adjustable CO/H 2 ratio
abstract: A new concept for efficient CO 2 electroreduction to syngas with an accurately adjustable CO/H 2 ratio is unveiled by developing a Ni–MoC composite catalyst by physically mixing a preferencial CO evolution unit and a preferential H 2 evolution unit.
