title: Micro-supercapacitors for smart transportation and low-altitude economy applications: advances in material innovations, device architectures, and system integration
abstract: This review highlights micro-supercapacitors (MSCs) as critical enablers for smart transportation and low-altitude economy applications, addressing the need for light weight, high power, and safe energy storage.

title: Decoupling electrical conductivity and Seebeck coefficient via isoelectronic alloying in the 9-4-9-type Ca 9− y Eu y Zn 4.7 Sb 9 (0 ≤ y ≤ 5.0) Zintl phase
abstract: The decoupling mechanism of electrical conductivity and Seebeck coefficient in 9-4-9-type Ca 9− y Eu y Zn 4.7 Sb 9 was uncovered.

title: CsCl seed layer engineering for buried-interface modification enabling >20% efficiency in single-source vapor-deposited perovskite solar cells
abstract: The CsCl seed layer enables regulated crystallization and interfacial engineering in single-source vapor-deposited perovskite films, leading to a PCE of 20.26%, which ranks among the highest-performing dry-processed single-junction PSCs.

title: Unveiling the interaction between fragments of ABX 3 halide perovskite and Ti 3 C 2 F 2 MXene monolayer
abstract: We investigate the interaction between the fragments of halide perovskite (A, B, X, AX, BX 2 ) and the Ti 3 C 2 F 2 MXene surface using ab initio density functional theory calculations.

title: Breaking the activity–stability trade-off in acidic oxygen evolution reaction via steering charge-compensation mechanisms in RuO 2
abstract: Alkaline earth metal-doped RuO 2 breaks acidic OER activity–stability tradeoff; Sr-RuO 2 achieves 199 mV at 10 mA cm −2 with 200 h stability at 1 A cm −2 in PEMWE.

title: Halide removal and SiO 2 @TiO 2 composite passivation: enhancing InP QD photooxidation stability for WLEDs
abstract: Residual halide ions on InP QDs accelerate oxidative degradation via moisture adsorption. Halide removal plus SiO 2 @TiO 2 shell construction modulates hydrophobicity and seals mesopores, enhancing stability. This extends WLED T85 lifetime beyond 530 h.

title: A robust core–shell design to stabilize MnO 2 and activate Prussian blue for high-performance zinc-ion batteries
abstract: A robust NiCoPBA@MnO 2 core–shell cathode delivers a high capacity of 270.1 mAh g −1 and excellent stability, enabled by a dual bimetallic redox mechanism.

title: A long-term stable thermochromic smart window of PNIPAM-based hydrogels with excellent water retention and visible light modulation
abstract: A PNIPAM-based composite hydrogel smart window with multiple interpenetrating networks has been designed and prepared, and exhibits excellent phase transition cycling stability and energy-saving performance.

title: Assembling ultrathin Ni(OH) 2 nanosheets on poly(triazine imide) crystal hollow tubes for efficient CO 2 photoreduction
abstract: Poly(triazine imide) (PTI), featuring extended conjugation and a crystallized structure, holds promise for photocatalytic CO 2 reduction, yet its performance remains constrained by inefficient charge separation and slow CO 2 activation kinetics.

title: Leveraging chiral induced spin selectivity to improve the efficiency and selectivity of electrocatalytic reduction
abstract: Chiral-induced spin selectivity (CISS) enables spin-polarized electron transfer, modulating key steps in electrochemical reduction reactions. By stabilizing spin-sensitive intermediates, CISS enhances activity and selectivity, offering a new strategy for catalyst design.

title: Advancement in nanocarbon-based thermoelectric materials: surface modification strategies, efficiency analysis, and applications
abstract: The review article summarizes the recent advances of nanocarbon-based thermoelectric materials.

title: Unexpected phase selectivity in germanosilicate zeolite synthesis: discovery and structure of HPM-18, a novel stable d4r-containing structure
abstract: Discovery of the new HPM-18 reveals an unexpected phase selectivity change in germanosilicate zeolites with double four-ring units.

title: ICP-assisted pre-lithiation of silicon thin-film electrodes for achieving high initial coulombic efficiency for lithium-ion batteries using inductively coupled plasma for lithium impregnation
abstract: Silicon-based anodes offer the promise of significantly enhanced energy density for next-generation lithium-ion batteries but are challenged by initial coulombic efficiency and rapid capacity decay during charge–discharge cycling.

title: Effect of electrochemical etching parameters on the morphology and photocatalytic activity of porous 4H-SiC flakes
abstract: Unlock face-dependent photocatalytic performance in porous 4H-SiC flakes fabricated by electrochemical etching establishing SiC as a next-generation platform for clean-energy and environmental remediation.

title: Interfacial engineering of a polyphenylene sulfide fiber membrane for high-performance alkaline water electrolysis
abstract: In situ growth of polydopamine on a PPS fiber membrane endows the membrane with high ionic conductivity and a superior gas barrier, achieving high-performance alkaline water electrolysis.

title: Robust interpenetrating network type amphoteric ion exchange membranes for high performance vanadium redox flow batteries
abstract: Fluorine-free, robust polyethylene-poly- p -methylstyrene interpenetrated network-based crosslinked amphoteric polyelectrolyte separators have been architected for all vanadium redox flow battery (VRFB) applications.

title: Flexible electromagnetic functional fibers with synergistic dual-loss mechanisms: achieving ultra-broadband absorption and mechanical robustness
abstract: Functional particles modified with CTAB and OP-10 form hydrogen bonds with PVA, enhancing particle dispersion and interfacial polarization.

title: Interface-engineered integration of nickel–iron phosphide with carbon for efficient and stable oxygen evolution in alkaline media
abstract: This NiFe–P@C catalyst, synthesized via Couette–Taylor flow, achieves a low 233 mV overpotential for OER. Phosphorus modulation and flow-assisted deposition enhance interfacial charge transfer and long-term alkaline stability.

title: A 3D-printed ZnO/CNT mesh@Cu composite electrode for dendrite-free and ultra-stable lean-lithium metal batteries
abstract: The lithiophilic ZnO guides homogeneous Li deposition, and the conductive CNT network eliminates localized current. The porous mesh further accommodates volume change, ensuring a dendrite-free and mechanically robust anode.

title: Oxy-sulfide semiconductors for hydrogen production from pure water: materials design, performance, and stability
abstract: Oxy-sulfides are mixed-anion semiconductors capable of bridging the complementary strengths of oxides (structural stability) and sulfides (narrow band gap, enhanced conductivity) for the photocatalytic H 2 production from pure water.

title: Tuning the structure and photocatalytic performance of tri- s -triazine-based polyimides through temperature-controlled imidization
abstract: Temperature-controlled imidization modulates the polymerization degree, radical generation, and photocatalytic efficiency of tri- s -triazine-based polyimides, revealing a narrow optimal thermal window for enhanced visible-light-driven photocatalysis.

title: Preparation of waterborne polyurethane/silsesquioxane/carbon nanotube aerogel with rigid-flexible framework for mechanically tough and wide pressure-range properties for high-performance piezoresistive sensing
abstract: Piezoresistive sensors lack stability over wide pressure ranges. A WPU–HSQ 8 /CNT aerogel offers toughness, stability, fatigue resistance, and steady 0–5 MPa responses. Joint motion tracking validates use. This resolves the stability-sensing range trade-off.

title: A PCM-enhanced thermoelectric generator with enhanced power density and lifespan
abstract: A phase change material-integrated thermoelectric generator (PCM-TEG) sandwiches TEG modules between two PCM layers, converting passive thermal storage into active energy conversion for simultaneous heat management and continuous power generation.

title: Single-ion solid polymer electrolytes by design: chemistries, architectures and functional trade-offs
abstract: This review highlights recent advances in single-ion polymer electrolytes (SIPEs), emphasizing how tailored chemistries, macromolecular architectures, and controlled morphologies govern their structure–property relationships.

title: Photoelectron extraction in BiOI: an atomistic perspective
abstract: Local frontier orbital energies, top of the valence and bottom of the conduction band, as a function of their position relative to the BiOI/ZnO interface. The interfacial conduction band minimum can trap electrons from BiOI, limiting transfer to ZnO.

title: Spectroscopic investigation of the electrochemical growth and stability of am-hydr-IrOx grown from IrTi alloys
abstract: Amorphous hydrous iridium oxide thin films (am-hydr-IrOx) grown through electrochemical treatment of sputtered iridium thin films are an interesting materials class for the oxygen evolution reaction (OER). They boast high...

title: Sequential donor–acceptor dual modulation in conjugated microporous polymers enables efficient photocatalytic synthesis of symmetric ureas, benzothiazoles and functionalized indoles
abstract: Oxime functionalization in CMPs optimizes donor–acceptor interactions, promoting charge separation and oxygen activation for efficient ROS-driven visible-light photocatalysis.

title: Light-driven ammonia synthesis from nitrogen and hydrogen
abstract: This review summarizes recent advances and mechanisms in light-driven ammonia synthesis from nitrogen and hydrogen, emphasizing the unique role of light in overcoming scaling relationships to facilitate NH 3 production under mild conditions.

title: Architecting nanographenes for efficient energy storage by tailoring molecular conformation and packing
abstract: Regioisomeric nonplanar nanographenes that suppress restacking, expand interlayer spacing, and accelerate Li-ion diffusion have been utilised as Li-ion battery anodes delivering high specific capacity and exceptional long-term cycling stability.

title: An amino-functionalized metal–organic framework for efficient separation of xenon/krypton
abstract: An ultramicroporous metal–organic framework with compact pore space and amino functional groups was synthesized for Xe/Kr separation, which showed large Xe adsorption capacity at low pressure and excellent Xe/Kr selectivity.

title: Recent advances in carbon-based materials for supercapacitors: synthesis, electrochemical properties, and impact of machine learning
abstract: Supercapacitor (SC) technology is one of the pioneering energy storage technologies of the current century.

title: Polymer-modified nonwovens: functional surfaces for separation, sensing, biomedical and smart textile applications
abstract: Abstract nonwoven fabrics have rapidly evolved from low-value commodities into next-generation materials platforms underpinning healthcare, environmental remediation, and clean-energy technologies.

title: Surface-engineered Mo 2 TiC 2 T x MXene for moisture-resilient high-performance energy storage
abstract: Surface engineering of two-dimensional Mo 2 TiC 2 T x MXene via argon ion irradiation is demonstrated as an effective strategy to enhance the electrochemical energy storage performance while simultaneously imparting moisture resilience.

title: Hydroxy functional groups modulate S-scheme MIL-125-NH 2 @COF heterojunctions for regulated photocatalytic production of hydrogen peroxide
abstract: This work demonstrates great potential of hydroxyl-functionalized COF/MOF-based S-scheme heterojunction for efficient photocatalytic H 2 O 2 production, providing mechanistic insights into the role of functional groups in regulating charge dynamics.

title: Light-promoted dual coking-elimination effects enable highly efficient photothermocatalytic dry reforming of methane on Ni/CeO 2 –Al 2 O 3
abstract: The outstanding catalytic activity of Ni/CeO 2 –Al 2 O 3 in photothermal catalytic dry reforming of methane stems from its dual anti-coking feature.

title: Optimising an electron-rich 2D Fe,B-Ti 3 C 2 T x /N-doped mixed metal oxide interface for industrial-scale oxygen evolution in seawater
abstract: This research develops a stable, high-performance electrocatalyst with an electron-rich interface between modified MXene and nitrogen-doped nickel molybdenum oxide, enabling efficient high-current operation while suppressing chlorine evolution.

title: Sprayable superhydrophobic photothermal/electrothermal anti-icing coating based on evaporation-induced surfactant flocculation self-assembly
abstract: Solvent evaporation-induced surfactant flocculation enables in situ self-assembly of hierarchical micro–nanostructures, yielding a durable superhydrophobic coating that delays icing and enables rapid photothermal/electrothermal de-icing.

title: Single-cluster polyoxometalate catalysts via modular electrostatic assembly on a cationic covalent organic framework for furfural electroreduction
abstract: Single-cluster polyoxometalate catalysts on cationic COFs via electrostatic assembly enable modular design and high activity for furfural electroreduction.

title: Artificial interphase layers on sodium metal anodes: preparation strategies, design principles, and future outlook
abstract: In this review, the preparation methods, design principles, electrochemical behaviors and future perspectives of artificial interphase layers on sodium metal anodes are systematically summarized.

title: Machine learning-guided scalable manufacturing of high-efficiency perovskite solar modules
abstract: With 23 process features, optimized XGBoost accurately predicts a PCE of 27.57%. SHAP analysis and simulations reveal FA-based perovskite scale-universality based on p-i-n structure and Slot-Die process.

title: Induced chirality in metal porphyrin-based biomimetic catalysts promotes ORR activity by enabling spin-polarization effects
abstract: Inducing spin-polarization by employing chiral camphor sulfonic acid with metalloporphyrin complexes, mimicking the functionality of haemoglobin, for enhancing the ORR activity of the overall system.

title: Honeycomb-structured mixed-valence LiV 3 O 8 /C with cathode–anode bifunctionality for lithium-ion batteries
abstract: A single vanadium-based compound enables dual-window lithium storage for bifunctional electrode operation and simplified lithium-ion batteries.

title: Promoting effect of Cu 2+ ions on electrochemical ammonia oxidation using CuO electrocatalysts
abstract: A [Cu(NH 3 ) 4 ] 2+ complex, generated from dissolved Cu 2+ , not only promotes the electrochemical ammonia oxidation reaction (eAOR) over CuO electrocatalysts, but also forms dynamic cycles of dissolution and deposition via Cu 2+ ediated eAOR.

title: Ionic liquid-armored MXene membranes enabling high-performance moisture-driven power generation
abstract: A moisture-driven power generator utilizing ionic liquids and MXene is constructed to achieve exceptional power performance under high-temperature moisture conditions.

title: Synergistic self-assembly and high-entropy dual engineering of the Ruddlesden–Popper air electrode for high-performance and stable reversible protonic ceramic cells
abstract: Through the synergistic integration of high-entropy engineering and self-assembly technology, we constructed a composite air electrode consisting of a stable high-entropy bulk phase and highly active nanoparticle surfaces.

title: Phase-selective synthesis of cubic WN and hexagonal WC carbide via solid–phase reaction pathways controlling for electrochemical applications
abstract: Investigation of WO 3 evolution under various reaction conditions reveals that temperature is decisive parameter for phase separation between WN and WC, whereas C 2 H 4 N 4 addition governs selective formation of different tungsten-carbide polymorphs.

title: Improving carrier transport aided by Sb doping enables high-efficiency DMF-processed CIGSSe solar cells with sub-thickness
abstract: One effective Sb doping method is proposed to promote grain growth and construct a gradient bandgap in DMF-processed CIGSSe.

title: An electronic transmission bridge via 3D N-doping for steering urea electrosynthesis
abstract: 3D N-doped Cu-N-C single-atom catalysts serve as an “electronic bridge” to facilitate d–p–p hybridization, reducing C–N coupling energy barriers and improving selectivity and stability for efficient electrochemical urea synthesis from CO 2 and NO.

title: A tetraphenyl- p -phenylenediamine- and benzodithiophene-4,8-dione-based conjugated microporous polymer as a robust cathode for durable aqueous Zn batteries
abstract: A permanently porous TPDA-Ph-BDT conjugated microporous polymer cathode enables high capacity and ultralong cycling stability in aqueous Zn batteries, delivering 154 mA h g −1 and 96% retention over 50 000 cycles at 20 A g −1 .

title: Unraveling anisotropic Li-ion transport in Li 3/8 Sr 7/16 Ta 3/4 Hf 1/4 O 3 via machine learning molecular dynamics and first-principles modeling
abstract: An accurate on-the-fly machine learning force field (MLFF) was developed. Molecular dynamics simulations using the MLFF reveal that anisotropic Li-ion migration occurs predominantly along the z -axis, with an activation energy of 0.28 eV.

title: Unexpectedly weak phase dependence of defect-assisted nonradiative recombination in halide perovskites
abstract: The nonradiative capture coefficients of iodine interstitials exhibit an unexpectedly weak phase dependence, irrespective of the different defect transition levels.

title: Unveiling a conversion–intercalation dual mechanism in Cr-doped TaSe 2 electrodes for high-capacity primary–secondary lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Cr doping can effectively tailor the electrochemistry of TaSe 2 , through a conversion–intercalation dual mechanism, which offers a promising route to gain high-capacity electrode materials with hybrid energy-storage strategies.

title: Self-powered intelligent emergency alarm system based on a CNT/cellulose aerogel TENG for firefighter thermal protection
abstract: A self-powered CCHA-TENG-based intelligent fire alarm system with ultralow thermal conductivity was developed.

title: Quantum dot–microbe hybrid systems for solar-to-chemical conversion
abstract: This review shows how QD photophysics, interfacial charge transfer, and microbial metabolism together govern QMH performance. It outlines multiscale design principles for programmable, selective, and scalable semi-artificial photosynthesis.

title: Pressure-free sintering of high-performance crystalline/amorphous Ag 2 S 0.5 Te 0.5 /nylon films for flexible thermoelectric devices
abstract: A Ag 2 S 0.5 Te 0.5 /nylon film was prepared showing excellent flexible and high thermoelectric performance.

title: Improved stability of BiVO 4 photoanode for photoelectrochemical oxidation: fundamentals, strategies and applications
abstract: This review summarizes BiVO 4 photoanode instability mechanisms and recent strategies (protective coatings, cocatalysts, electrolyte engineering) to enhance long-term stability for PEC water splitting and organic oxidation.

title: Improved electrocatalytic oxidation of artificial seawater with an applied magnetic field in a forward osmosis system
abstract: Electrocatalytic oxygen production from seawater is facilitated by applying a magnetic field and using a forward-flow osmosis cell.

title: Correction: Synergistic role of self-induced CdIn 2 S 4 nanopyramid sacrificial layer for the enhanced photocatalytic activity of TiO 2 nanotube-based chalcogenide heterostructures
abstract: Correction for ‘Synergistic role of self-induced CdIn 2 S 4 nanopyramid sacrificial layer for the enhanced photocatalytic activity of TiO 2 nanotube-based chalcogenide heterostructures’ by Nilem Khaliq et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2026, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TA05742F.

title: Initial state of charge controls interfacial kinetics for approaching 100% efficiency in aqueous redox electrolytes
abstract: Tuning the initial state of charge to 50% in static aqueous [Fe(CN) 6 ] 3− /[Fe(CN) 6 ] 4− electrolytes yields nearly 100% coulombic efficiency by confining operation to a robust medium-potential kinetic window.

title: Facile synthesis of porous PtBiTe nanoplates with high C 1 pathway selectivity and robust resistance to CO poisoning for the ethanol oxidation reaction
abstract: Facile synthesis of porous PtBiTe nanoplates by using Bi 2 Te 3 nanoplate templates with excellent activity and stability, high C 1 pathway selectivity, and robust resistance to CO poisoning for the ethanol oxidation reaction.

title: Off-stoichiometry-driven electronic structure modulation leads to high thermoelectric performance in n-type InSb: an experimental study with theoretical insights
abstract: The off-stoichiometry in InSb via Sb-deficiency induces resonant-like defect states around the Fermi level, E F , resulting in enhanced Seebeck coefficient and thereby thermoelectric performance.

title: Structure of iridium oxide catalysts dictates performance differences for proton exchange membrane water electrolyzers
abstract: A systematic catalyst loading study reveals the higher catalytic activity of amorphous iridium oxide arises from bulk participation via suggested electrochemical descriptors, while identifying loading-independent intrinsic electrochemical properties.

title: Regenerable and stable calix[4]pyrrole-based porous organic polymer networks for highly effective iodine capture
abstract: This study designs C4P-based porous organic polymers for efficient iodine capture, an uptake capacity of 3.96 g g −1 with rapid adsorption rate was obtained using C4P-Tab, providing a sustainable macrocyclic adsorbents design for iodine capture.

title: Laser-induced MXene/LIG ternary heterostructure: construction and health monitoring application of a high performance self-powered friction sensor
abstract: A flexible MLL-TENG with a ternary heterostructure of V 2 CT x –LIG–V 2 O 5 was fabricated by an innovative strategy combining laser-induced and chemical bonding, achieving highly efficient electromechanical energy conversion.

title: Synergistic heterojunction engineering and the photothermal 3D confinement effect for enhanced photocatalytic overall water splitting
abstract: A WO 2 @C/Mn 0.5 Cd 0.5 S S-scheme heterojunction, crafted via a multifaceted design, synergizes charge dynamics optimization with the photothermal confinement effect, enabling high-efficiency and full-spectrum solar water splitting.

title: Interface strain engineering of a Ru–RuO x /graphene aerogel composite boosts HER, OER and ORR applications
abstract: DFT calculations predict the relationship between RuO x /Ru interface strain and electrocatalytic reaction activity. Interfacial strain engineering of Ru/RuO x can obtain efficient catalysts for multiple electrocatalytic reactions (HER/OER/ORR).

title: Harnessing slow photons in 3D silica photonic crystals for efficient and catalyst-free removal of chromophoric organic pollutants
abstract: Chemically inert SiO 2 inverse opals act as a catalyst-free purification platform that synergizes adsorption and slow photon effect to enhance pollutants degradation, accelerating reaction kinetics up to 17-fold while maintaining high regenerability.

title: Development of a multifunctional aminated Zr/Ce bimetallic MOF-assisted membrane for suppressing radical-induced deterioration in hydrogen fuel cells
abstract: An amine-functionalized Zr–Ce MOF was designed to immobilize Ce for radical scavenging and Zr for stability. Integrated into sPEEK, it enabled durable, high-performing PEMs, offering a distinct strategy beyond conventional Ce-based additives.

title: Construction of δ-MnO 2 /rGO cathodes via in situ oil–water interface reaction for enhancing electrochemical performance of aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: A unique oil–water interface reaction with subsequent thermal reduction strategy was proposed to construct the δ-MnO 2 /rGO cathode and enhance the electrochemical performance of aqueous zinc-ion batteries through synergistic modulation mechanisms.

title: Hexameric structure-dependent catalysis of oxalate oxidase for mitigating the shuttle effect in high-temperature Li–S batteries
abstract: A natural thermostable enzyme, oxalate oxidase, is adopted to suppress the severe shuttle effect in high-temperature lithium–sulfur batteries.

title: 2D metal–organic framework: harnessing π–d conjugation in bimetallic synergy for electrocatalytic hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: Bimetallic 2D MOF CuCo-THQ demonstrates efficient HER activity in alkaline media with low overpotential, favourable kinetics, and strong durability. Synergistic Cu–Co interactions enhance the catalytic performance beyond that of Cu-THQ and Co-THQ.

title: Non-stoichiometric design for engineering oxygen vacancies and enhanced ionic transport in Cu–Mg-doped bismuth vanadate electrolytes
abstract: A lattice-engineering strategy via non-stoichiometric design is developed for BIVCUMGOX solid electrolytes. Precise modulation of oxygen vacancies and shortened ion-hopping distances significantly enhances the low-temperature ionic conductivity.

title: Interfacial electrochemistry of Zn anodes in aqueous electrolytes with combined organic cation and halide additives
abstract: Trace-level TBAI enables synergistic cation–anion regulation at the Zn interface, suppressing dendrite growth and enhancing electrochemical stability in aqueous Zn batteries.

title: Modulating (001) plane growth in β-Ni(OH) 2 precursors: a pathway to controlling lithiation kinetics and enhancing the structural integrity of LiNiO 2
abstract: Strategically restricting (001) plane growth in β-Ni(OH) 2 precursors lowers lithiation activation energy, yielding structurally robust LiNiO 2 cathodes with superior electrochemical performance and stability.

title: High photovoltaic efficiency in bulk-stacked one-dimensional GeSe 2 van der Waals crystal
abstract: Germanium diselenide (GeSe 2 ) has recently attracted substantial interest as a rare example of a one-dimensional (1D) van der Waals material.

title: Integrating Single-Site Co Catalytic Center into Zr-MOF without Sacrificing Light-Harvesting Units for Enhanced Photocatalysis
abstract: Metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) are promising photocatalysts for solar fuel production, but some conventional methods for introducing catalytic sites often sacrifice the visible-light-harvesting components. Herein, a Zr-MOF with single-site Co centers...

title: Unleashing potential of novel 2D-Bi 2 S 3 /1D-SnO 2 heterostructure thin film anodes for light-fostered asymmetric electrochromic supercapacitors
abstract: A 2D Bi 2 S 3 decorated 1D SnO 2 , n–n type heterostructure integrated with PEDOT:PSS exhibits photo-assisted supercapacitor and electrochromic behaviour, enabling efficient solar energy conversion, storage, and multifunctional device applications.

title: Smart self-healing polymers: innovations in material design and applications for electronic skin and energy devices
abstract: Smart self-healing polymers offer sustainable solutions for energy storage and sensing by autonomously repairing damage while enhancing durability and reliability, and enabling next-generation power devices and flexible electronic skin sensors.

title: Demystifying the dual-functional electrocatalytic activity of the 2D Co-doped NbSSe Janus transition metal dichalcogenide for O 2 reduction and H 2 evolution reactions
abstract: Developing highly efficient dual-functional electrocatalysts is crucial for advancing energy conversion and storage technologies, particularly for the O 2 reduction reaction (ORR) and H 2 evolution reaction (HER).

title: Time-phase-controlled exsolution of FeCoNi ternary alloy nanoparticles on perovskite anode catalyst for enhanced dual-functional catalysis and protection in ammonia-fueled protonic ceramic fuel cells
abstract: We present a novel exsolution strategy to fabricate a Sr 2 Fe 1.5 Mo 0.35 Co 0.1 Ni 0.05 O 6− δ perovskite anode catalytic layer, with in situ formation of FeCoNi alloy nanoparticles with enhanced catalytic activity for ammonia decomposition and durability.

title: Advanced ternary ion-pair redox electrolytes for direct-contact dye-sensitized solar cells under one-sun and ambient lighting
abstract: An advanced TPAA˙ + ⋯Co 3+ ⋯I 3 − redox system in DSSCs enables dye regeneration and redox mediation, promoting stepwise charge transfer and achieving 11.51% under one sun and 34.84% at 7000 lux.

title: Enhanced activity and durability of Ir single-atom catalysts for the electrocatalytic oxygen evolution reactions through synergistic electronic coupling with Co 3 O 4 matrix
abstract: Ir single atoms anchored on Co 3 O 4 surface for efficient oxygen evolution reaction in alkaline medium.

title: Synthesis of triazine-based covalent organic framework nanoparticles as cathode materials for lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: Covalent organic frameworks (COFs) hold promise as ideal sulfur carrier materials for lithium–sulfur battery cathodes due to their well-defined pore structures and abundant heteroatom sites.

title: Temperature-Decoupling Solar Lithium Enrichment System with Closed-loop Energy Cycle via Superhydrophilic transparent coatings
abstract: With the depletion of fossil energy, the demand for Lithium (Li) has globally surged and the extraction of Li must be environmentally and socially responsible for a sustainable future. Solar...

title: Dual-site cationic disorder engineering for ultralow amorphous-like thermal conductivity in thermal barrier coatings
abstract: High-entropy pyrochlore oxides with lattice disorder induced by Y 3+ occupancy at both A and B sites exhibit tailorable thermophysical properties.

title: Nanohybrids of Ti3C2Tx MXene with Ni-based structures: Enabling high performance electrocatalyst for water splitting
abstract: In hydrogen production technologies, the greener way of electrocatalytic water splitting techniques has gained considerable attention in the recent pasts because of utilizing renewable energy as a source and producing...

title: Engineering a flower-like Cu-BTC/BiOI heterostructure for efficient photodegradation of antibiotics in authentic aquatic environments
abstract: A novel Cu-BTC/BiOI heterojunction was developed to enhance photocatalytic efficiency for tetracycline degradation by suppressing electron–hole recombination and promoting superoxide radical generation.

title: Ni single-atom anchored N-doped carbon deposited on BaTiO 3 for efficient piezocatalytic CO 2 reduction
abstract: Deposition of Ni single-atom anchored N-doped carbon on BaTiO 3 siginificantly promotes piezocatalytic CO 2 reduction.

title: Highly robust and efficient dye rejection enabled by rGO/MXene heterogeneous membranes
abstract: The resulting rGO/MXene heterogeneous membranes exhibited a high water permeance of up to 401.2 L m⁻ 2 h⁻¹ bar⁻¹ together with 99.7% rejection of dye molecules.

title: Design of polymer–metal nanocatalyst interfaces for the electrochemical CO 2 reduction reaction
abstract: We summarize the recent advances in the eCO 2 RR using metal nanocatalysts modified with hydrophobic, conductive, ionic and porous polymers.

title: Electro-upcycling of PET plastic coupled with hydrogen production using the NiCe@NiTe electrocatalyst
abstract: Microplastic reformation in conjunction with the electrochemical hydrogen evolution has merits for producing value-added chemicals and mitigating environmental pollution.

title: Recent Advances in Organic Conjugated Materials for Photocatalytic Hydrogen Evolution: Design Strategies and Photocatalytic Performance
abstract: Converting abundant solar energy into valuable and clean hydrogen fuel through photocatalytic water splitting represents a highly promising strategy to address the global energy and environmental crises. In recent years,...

title: Capillary-Marangoni synergism enabled salt-resisting sodium alginate hydrogel foam for efficient solar-driven water harvesting
abstract: A hierarchically porous SA-PPy@MF evaporator is fabricated via a “crosslinking-oxidation” strategy. The synergy of capillary water transport and Marangoni salt backflow enables a high evaporation rate and excellent salt resistance.

title: Defect-surface engineering of La-doped ceria for microwave-assisted hydrogen production
abstract: La-doped CeO 2 enhances microwave absorption and defects, enabling ultralow-temperature redox cycling and high H 2 yields, providing a design framework for scalable, electrified H 2 production.

title: Microwave-assisted deep eutectic solvent synthesis of Sr-doped LaNiO 3 -derived Ni-based oxides for efficient NH 3 decomposition
abstract: Microwave-assisted deep eutectic solvent (DES) synthesis enables Sr-doped LaNiO 3 to exsolve socketed Ni nanoparticles with exceptional NH 3 decomposition performance.

title: Flow-regime-controlled mass transfer intensification for fabricating superior silicon–carbon anode materials
abstract: Under the flow-regime engineering strategy, the gas–solid mass transfer of ultrafine particles is significantly enhanced, which further promotes the coating uniformity and electrochemical performance of silicon–carbon anode materials.

title: Computational and experimental insights into variable temperature propylene (C 3 H 6 ), propane (C 3 H 8 ), and hydrogen sulfide (H 2 S) sorption in ultra-high permselectivity CANAL ladder polymers
abstract: Molecular dynamics and Monte Carlo simulations elucidate the microstructure of CANAL-Me-Me 2 F and analyze multi-temperature sorption and related energetics for the experimentally challenging propylene, propane, and hydrogen sulfide penetrants.

title: Preparation of copolyester elastomers by introducing a lactic acid-based monomer with enhanced mechanical properties via micro-crosslinking
abstract: A polyester elastomer with excellent thermoplasticity, good degradability, and high toughness was successfully prepared by using the lactic acid-based monomer SLA and glycerol for micro-crosslinking.

title: Oriented tubular metal–organic framework superstructures via conjugate acid–base pair-mediated growth regulation
abstract: Oriented tubular MOF superstructures are synthesized via a bottom-up approach driven by a conjugate acid–base pair strategy, which selectively directs anisotropic growth and dissolution–recrystallization.

title: A sulfonated cellulose separator for high-areal-capacity and long-lifespan Zn–Br 2 batteries
abstract: Sulfonated cellulose separator inhibits Br 3 − shuttling and facilitates uniform zinc deposition, enabling the construction of high-performance Zn–Br 2 batteries.

title: A multifunctional upconversion-quantum dot photocatalytic platform for near-infrared-driven environmental remediation and complex organic synthesis
abstract: Near-infrared (NIR) Energy Transfer Upconversion (ETU) overcomes the photon energy limitations of single-photon processes via nonlinear optical effects, demonstrating unique advantages in deep material penetration and multi-electron transfer.

title: Advanced aqueous Zn–halogen batteries with multi-electron transfer chemistry catalyzed by organic compounds: insights into mechanisms and prospects
abstract: This review provides a comprehensive understanding of the halogen reaction mechanism in ZHBs catalyzed by organic compounds, particularly regarding the selection criteria for achieving multi-electron halogen conversion reactions.

title: Photothermal–excitonic trade-off in TiO 2 /BiCuSeO heterojunctions for optimal solar-driven hydrogen production
abstract: PGEC BiCuSeO photothermally complements TiO 2 photocatalysis, enabling full-spectrum solar H 2 production. Balancing TiO 2 excitons and BiCuSeO phonon heating is crucial for peak performance.

title: Surface functionalized vapor grown carbon fiber supported dysprosium stannate composites for the enhanced detection of furaltadone: a synergy of DFT modeling and experiments
abstract: DySnO@VGCF hybrid sensor for ultrasensitive electrochemical detection of furaltadone with DFT insight.

title: Pressure-triggered dynamically tunable TiO 2 /CNFs@Co/C aerogel derived from MXene/PAN@ZIF-67 for broadband microwave absorption
abstract: In increasingly complex electromagnetic environments, developing high-performance and dynamically tunable electromagnetic wave (EMW) absorption materials is essential.

title: Engineering Al-doped ZnO/PDMS flexible triboelectric nanogenerators through SiO 2 interlayer and MoS 2 nanofillers for enhanced performance
abstract: Synergistic integration of Al-doped ZnO, a SiO 2 electron-blocking interlayer, and a MoS 2 –PDMS nanocomposite results in a flexible high-output triboelectric nanogenerator capable of lighting 400 LEDs and maintaining long-term operational stability.

title: Optical sensors for in situ real-time detection of ammonia
abstract: The figure illustrates the calibration of an electrode-based fluorescence sensor, establishing the correlation between fluorescence intensity and ammonia concentration for in situ detection.

title: Interphase-microstructure synergy in Al foil anodes enabled by ball-milled Al–CNT composites
abstract: Powder-metallurgy Al–CNT composite foil anodes combine ultrafine grains with a prelithiation-induced Li 2 O-rich interphase, suppressing reaction localization and enabling long-cycle LIBs.

title: Qualitative trends versus quantitative accuracy: a case study of LiMn 1.5 Ni 0.5 O 4 cathode material
abstract: Performances of several DFT functionals (PBE, PBE+ U , r 2 SCAN, and HSE06) to describe structural, electronic, and electrochemical properties of the ordered spinel LiMn 1.5 Ni 0.5 O 4 are evaluated.

title: Chemical pre-regulation of 2D perovskite nucleation enables phase-homogeneous 2D/3D perovskite interfaces
abstract: A bifunctional chelation-based strategy controls 2D perovskite nucleation in 2D/3D heterojunctions. Methylsulfonic anhydride homogenizes 2D phase formation, suppresses phase segregation, and improves carrier lifetime, efficiency, and stability.

title: Carboxylated CNT–vanadium hexacyanoferrate cathode enabling high-energy-density quasi-solid-state H 2 batteries with enhanced low-temperature performance
abstract: A carboxylated CNT–vanadium hexacyanoferrate cathode enables high-energy-density quasi-solid-state H 2 batteries with fast proton storage, stable cycling, and improved low-temperature performance.

title: The impact of dendrites and related compositional fluctuations on hydrogen absorption thermodynamics in bcc multi-principal element alloys
abstract: Microsegregation has an insignificant effect on hydride formation enthalpy and entropy, despite differences in the equilibrium plateau slope compared to homogenised alloys. Melt-spinning successfully avoids dendrites, but impedes activation.

title: Urea-enhanced solubilization of lithium nitrate for improved lithium metal cycling: experimental and molecular dynamics investigation
abstract: Urea-modified solubilization of LiNO 3 in carbonate-based electrolyte promotes superior Li + ion transport, leading to a significantly enhanced diffusion coefficient compared to traditional electrolytes.

title: Interfacial charge transfer engineering in V 3 O 5 /ZnO p–n heterostructures for enhanced SERS performance
abstract: The MOF-on-MOF-derived V 3 O 5 /ZnO heterostructure detects MB at 10 −8 M with a 5.9 × 10 4 SERS enhancement factor. TA spectroscopy confirms its high SERS performance, resulting from efficient charge transfer between the heterojunction and MB.

title: Boosting the photoelectrochemical performance of ZnIn 2 S 4 photoanodes via antimony-induced defect and surface homojunction engineering
abstract: Sb-induced O-related defect engineering promotes lattice/near-lattice oxygen incorporation and a surface/bulk homojunction in ZnIn 2 S 4 , thereby enhancing charge separation and improving PEC activity under anodic operation.

title: Steering two-electron O 2 electroreduction on a Co–N–C catalyst via native carbon-defect engineering for highly efficient H 2 O 2 production
abstract: An intrinsic carbon-defect-modified Co–N–C catalyst was prepared via a simple thermal etching approach for highly selective hydrogen peroxide electrosynthesis.

title: Flexible and reusable piezo-responsive ternary bionanocomposite membrane for energy generation and catalytic removal of organic pollutants
abstract: A flexible and reusable chitosan-based ternary bionanocomposite membrane integrates self-powered energy harvesting with efficient piezocatalytic remediation of dyes, pharmaceuticals, and bacteria.

title: Synergistic modulation of CoOOH by dual-defects to enhance the catalytic performance of the oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: This study achieves precise electronic modulation of CoOOH by introducing sulfur dopants and cobalt vacancies simultaneously. The dual-defect strategy optimizes the d-band center and intermediate energetics, enabling enhanced catalytic performance.

title: Single-atom Ce synergizes icosahedral Pt 2 Ce nanoparticles to promote performance enhancement for zinc–air batteries
abstract: This study successfully proposed a method to enhance catalytic activity and stability through the synergistic effect between Pt-based nanoparticles and CeNC. The Pt 2 Ce/CeNC-600 exhibited an excellent oxygen reduction reaction (ORR) performance.

title: Synergistic defect engineering and cocatalyst loading in a Bi 2 MoO 6 -OVs/MoS 2 photoanode for efficient power generation in H 2 O 2 photoelectrochemical cells
abstract: By integrating an oxygen vacancy-engineered Bi 2 MoO 6 -OVs/MoS 2 photoanode with a cornstalk-derived porous carbon/iron phthalocyanine cathode, the system enables efficient solar-to-fuel-to-electricity conversion.

title: Increasing polymer molecular weight enables low-donor-content, efficient and scalable semi-transparent organic solar cells
abstract: Increasing the donor’s molecular weight improves transport and enables high-efficiency solar cells with reduced donor content, which in turn improves transparency, leading to boosted semitransparent photovoltaics.

title: Al 2 O 3 -assisted hierarchical Ni–Al surface alloying for scalable fabrication of nano-roughened Ni foam cathodes with superior HER activity and durability in alkaline water electrolysis
abstract: Al 2 O 3 -assisted reconstruction converts Ni foam into a hierarchical nanotextured cathode. The engineered Ni 2+ /Ni 0 interface and superhydrophilic structure enhance catalytic synergy and bubble release, ensuring stable HER in alkaline electrolysis.

title: Plastic power: recycled polymers as precursors for electrodes in energy storage devices
abstract: Escalating plastic pollution and the growing demand for sustainable energy storage call for innovative, integrated solutions.

title: Unravelling the HER activity of functionalized biochar: a pathway to cost-effective electrocatalysis
abstract: This review highlights the improved HER performance achieved by functionalized biochar through progressive structural and electronic refinement.

title: Dual-function tetramethylammonium fluoride additive for high utilization aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: Cation electrostatic shielding and anion interface engineering enable stable Zn symmetric cell cycling and enhanced full-cell performance with a KVO cathode.

title: Atomically dispersed copper in TiO 2 supported NiO nanoparticles drives high CH 4 productivity, selectivity, and stability in CO 2 methanation via reversible oxygen vacancy generation
abstract: In situ XAS and APXPS reveal that incorporation of atomic Cu species induces oxygen vacancies in the NiO lattice that activate CO 2 , while adjacent NiO sites dissociate H 2 , enabling synergistic and durable CO 2 -to-CH 4 conversion.

title: A graphite paper anode with low lattice mismatch enables corrosion inhibited zinc batteries
abstract: Low-cost commercial graphite paper anodes enable long-life aqueous zinc-ion batteries by resisting corrosion and enabling uniform zinc deposition, demonstrated in cells exceeding 10 000 cycles and validated in practical pouch cells.

title: Grotthuss-type proton transport governed by oxygen coordination environment in VO 2 polymorphs
abstract: Proton-insertion coupled electron transfer (PICET) offers fast-charge energy storage; however, predictive links between oxide crystal structures and proton mobility remain limited.

title: Conductive Cu 3 (HHTP) 2 nanorods anchored on biomass-derived carbon wood as a binder-free cathode for high-performance Li–CO 2 batteries
abstract: Conductive Cu 3 (HHTP) 2 nanorods anchored on biomass-derived carbon wood create a binder-free cathode for Li–CO 2 batteries, enabling fast CO 2 conversion, reversible Li 2 CO 3 formation/decomposition, and stable cycling for over 1000 cycles.

title: Building ion selective channels through bulky camphorsulfonic acid side chains in sulfonated polybenzimidazole membranes for vanadium redox flow batteries
abstract: A bulky, non-planar camphorsulfonic acid side chain is grafted onto sulfonated polybenzimidazole (SPBICa) to simultaneously create free volume with proton/vanadium ion-sieving capability and promote the formation of interconnected ionic clusters.

title: A high-performance supercapacitor electrode based on Zn/V co-doped NiMoO 4 : a cation–cation doping strategy
abstract: Rational design of electrode materials with tailored structural and electronic properties is crucial for the development of high-performance supercapacitors.

title: Air stable photo-redox active elusive Cr( iii )-tri-dithiolene-radical complexes as a new class of pseudo-supercapacitors with high capacitance: enhancement of energy storage with heavier chalcogenide
abstract: Two atomically precise photo-redox active Cr( iii )-triradical complexes were isolated, characterised and utilised as anodes for pseudo-supercapacitor studies.

title: Architecting an ‘electron highway’: cobalt-doped tubular carbon nitride for superior photocatalytic PMS activation
abstract: A synergistic “electron highway” in Co-doped carbon nitride drives efficient photocatalytic peroxymonosulfate activation for rapid tetracycline degradation.

title: Correlating charge heterogeneity and first-cycle irreversible capacity loss in lithium-ion batteries
abstract: By quantitatively linking Ni charge heterogeneity to irreversible lithium loss, our study provides a mechanistic foundation for mitigating IRC loss and optimizing Ni-rich cathode performance.

title: Effective and selective ethylene glycol electrooxidation with compositionally controlled Pt–Au bimetallic electrocatalysts
abstract: Compositionally fine-tuned PtAu thin films prepared with magnetron sputtering boost ethylene glycol electrooxidation activity and glycolic acid selectivity, through modifying the electronic and geometrical structure of Pt by surrounding Au atoms.

title: Toward quantum science- and technology-enabled heterogeneous catalysis
abstract: This perspective discusses how advances in quantum science and technology can be utilized to improve, design, and better understand heterogeneous catalysis.

title: Tailoring I-rich argyrodite sulfides via low-energy mechanical alloying for all-solid-state Li-metal batteries
abstract: A fast Li-ion conducting I-rich argyrodites with excellent stability with Li metal was successfully demonstrated by a controlled mechanical alloying method.

title: Lattice vs. surface water: correlation between the proton conductivity and microstructure of faujasite zeolites upon water adsorption
abstract: Reduction of zeolite particles size leads to a shift in the proton transfer mechanism from the vehicular mechanism through zeolite channels to the energetically more favourable Grotthuss mechanism of the surface adsorbed water layers.

title: Boosting triboelectric charge generation using high-performance functional MAX phase nanofillers incorporated in silicone elastomers
abstract: PDMS-Ti 3 AlC 3 MAX composites boost TENGs via dielectric and charge control. The 10 wt% device delivers 230 V, 28.5 µA, and 20.5 W m −2 , showing elite durability and stability for energy harvesting.

title: Axial-sulfur-ligand-induced coordination symmetry breaking in Co–N 4 motifs for enhanced oxygen reduction reaction and durable rechargeable zinc–air batteries
abstract: Axial sulfur atom breaks the planar symmetry of a cobalt–nitrogen active site (S–Co–N 4 ) within a cyclodextrin-derived single-atom catalyst. This asymmetrical geometry drives a profound charge redistribution that accelerates the ORR activity, enabling efficient and durable ZABs.

title: Au-decorated Zn x Mg 1− x O solid solutions with coexisting acidic and basic sites for enhanced photocatalytic methane conversion to ethane
abstract: 3Au–Zn 0.95 Mg 0.05 O with Zn 2+ /Mg–OH Lewis pairs photocatalyzes CH 4 to C 2 H 6 (2700 µmol g −1 h −1 , 85% selectivity) via acid–base activation of CH 4 and Au-stabilized ˙CH 3 to prevent overoxidation.

title: Dual-function Pt/Ir-doped CdS catalysts: defect engineering for visible-light HMF photo-oxidation and hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: We systematically controlled the surface defect density of CdS nanoparticles by doping the surface with Li + , Ir 4+ , and Pt 2+ ions and evaluated the resulting effects on the photocatalytic and electrocatalytic performances.

title: Metallopolymers via thermal dealkylation of unstrained bisphosphanylferrocene precursors
abstract: Metallopolymers with 1D phosphorus chains are accessible from simple unstrained precursors and feature a narrow band gap associated with red-shifted absorption in the UV-vis range.

title: Pulse-mediated refaceting of copper. Influence on 5-hydroxymethylfurfural electrocatalysis
abstract: We show that the terrace-to-defect ratio on copper can be tuned by varying the reduction potential during the application of potential pulses. The influence of different refaceted surfaces on 5-hydroxymethylfurfural electrocatalysis is discussed.

title: Triple-function of hydroxyacetone in suppressing water activity toward long-lived aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: HA achieves triple synergy: modulating Zn 2+ solvation, restructuring H-bond network, and forming a water-poor interfacial layer. This enables >3200 h Zn‖Zn cycling and 99.27% CE in Zn‖Cu cells, highlighting a promising strategy for long-life AZIBs.

title: MXene-anchored TTPP-stabilized Co single atoms for accelerated kinetics and durable Li–Cl 2 batteries
abstract: Co single atoms enhance Cl 2 adsorption and lower the LiCl/Cl 2 conversion barrier, accelerating reaction kinetics and improving reversibility. Therefore, the SACo-TTPP@Mo 2 TiC 2 T x cathode could operate stably for more than 500 h.

title: Supramolecular donor–acceptor engineering enables efficient intermolecular charge separation for enhanced photocatalytic hydrogen production
abstract: Supramolecular D–A architectures were constructed through robust π–π interactions, resulting in significantly enhanced intermolecular charge separation and improved photocatalytic hydrogen production.

title: Transition metal-based single-atom catalysts used for the CO 2 reduction reaction to CO
abstract: This review summarizes the application of Fe, Co, Ni, and Cu SACs in the electrocatalytic reduction of CO 2 to CO, covering synthesis strategies, reaction mechanisms, performance optimization, and future challenges.

title: Na 3 LaP 2 O 8 -enabled microstructural engineering for enhanced mechanical robustness and ionic transport behavior in NaSICON solid electrolytes
abstract: Na 3 LaP 2 O 8 modification optimizes the microstructure of NaSICON-type NZSP with reduced porosity, refined grain structure, and suppressed microcracks, enhancing mechanical robustness and ionic transport.

title: MOF-based and MOF-derived electrocatalysts for anodic reactions coupled with hydrogen evolution
abstract: This review summarizes metal–organic frameworks-based electrocatalysts for coupling the HER with value-added anodic oxidation reactions, quantitatively analyzes the metal elements, and highlights catalyst performance and design strategies.

title: Customizing phase separation in non-fullerene-based organic photovoltaics: the impact of interfacial morphology
abstract: This work designed asymmetric 1OM and symmetric 2OM non-fullerene acceptors based on Y6-BO via methoxyl modification. The lower surface energy yields optimal morphology, thereby achieving an inverted organic photovoltaic device with a PCE of 19.1%.

title: Highly efficient and free-standing WS 2 /C electrocatalyst for solar-cell-driven hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: A highly efficient, free-standing WS 2 /C composite electrocatalyst with abundant exposed WS 2 edge active sites for hydrogen evolution is successfully synthesized.

title: A hydroxyl-assisted ionic bifunctional hyper-crosslinked polymer as an efficient and recyclable heterogeneous catalyst for CO 2 cycloaddition
abstract: Hydroxyl-assisted ionic bifunctional hyper-crosslinked polymer catalyzes CO 2 cycloaddition to cyclic carbonates with high yield and recyclability under mild conditions.

title: Aliovalent gallium dopants remove Ti 3+ defects and improve photocatalytic and photoelectrochemical water oxidation properties of LaTiO 2 N
abstract: Ti 3+ recombination defects in LaTiO 2 N can be suppressed with aliovalent gallium (Ga 3+ ) dopants, as confirmed with electron paramagnetic resonance (EPR), X-ray photoelectron, optical absorption and surface photovoltage spectroscopy.

title: Simultaneously tackling discrete island effects and interfacial resistance in PEM electrolyzers via a scalable bilayer catalyst design
abstract: A metallic interlayer overcomes intralayer and interlayer conductivity limitations and unlocks high performance at ultra-low Ir loadings.

title: Enhanced electrochemical performance of multilayer WO 3 /MnO 2 thin films via sputtering on graphene substrate for high-stability supercapacitors
abstract: A flexible, binder-free multilayer WO 3 /MnO 2 electrode on graphene delivers high-performance supercapacitor with 494 F g −1 specific capacitance and 89% retention after 10000 cycles, offering a scalable route to next-generation flexible energy storage.

title: Simultaneous construction of robust solid-electrolyte interphases by trifluoromethyl- and isocyanate-functionalized additives for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: FMC and TFMC additives are employed to enhance the interfacial stability of NFM/HC-based sodium-ion batteries.

title: Alkali metal intercalation and rapid exfoliation of two-dimensional fullerene frameworks
abstract: K/Na intercalation of Mg 4 C 60 forms a Mg 2+ /K + (or Na + ) dual-ion lattice and stage-I intermediate at 400 °C. PhCN quenches charge, giving mono-/bilayer 2D-C 60 in 10 min. These 2D sheets show high photocurrent and reduced charge-transfer resistance.

title: Quantifying coordination effects for rational design of MXene-based NRR electrocatalysts
abstract: The coordination environment of single-atom catalysts (SACs) on two-dimensional supports critically dictates their performance in the nitrogen reduction reaction (NRR), yet a unifying design principle remains elusive.

title: Superior oxygen reduction on curved Fe–N 4 sites enabled by molecular infiltration within self-assembled fullerene microbelts
abstract: Precision synthesis of Fe–N–C catalysts with synergistic curved Fe–N 4 sites and Fe 3 C via a molecular-level infiltration-confinement strategy from water-soluble C 60 and assembled C 60 microbelts.

title: CoFe 2 O 4 /resorcinol–formaldehyde resin p–n heterojunctions with a built-in electric field for photo-self-Fenton degradation of tetracycline hydrochloride
abstract: The built-in electric field formed in the CoFe 2 O 4 /RF p–n heterojunction enables efficient in situ H 2 O 2 generation and activation, thereby achieving enhanced photo-self-Fenton degradation of tetracycline hydrochloride.

title: Engineering of Li + -selective quasi-solid electrolytes via zwitterion and poly(ethylene glycol) co-grafting on poly(arylene ether sulfone) for high-performance lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: A self-assembled quasi-solid electrolyte with zwitterion and PEG co-grafting enables fast single Li-ion transport through large conducting domains. Its flexible, stable structure ensures high performance and safety in lithium batteries.

title: Experimental investigation and bottom-up life cycle assessment of forward osmosis desalination using thermo-responsive Janus microgels
abstract: A novel Janus microgel was developed. A multiscale model integrated with life cycle assessment (LCA) was established to optimize the architecture and composition of thermoresponsive hydrogels for sustainable desalination.

title: Ultra-low thermal conductivity and promising thermoelectric performance in the structurally complex Zintl phase: Eu 14 GaAs 11
abstract: New Zintl compound Eu 14 GaAs 11 shows ultra-low thermal conductivity and a high Seebeck coefficient due to its complex structure, indicating potential for further optimization to enhance thermoelectric performance.

title: Hybrid poly(ionic liquid)/polyhedral oligomeric silsesquioxane artificial solid electrolyte interphase for lithium metal batteries
abstract: A pre-formed ASEI on lithium metal based on an imidazole-based poly(ionic liquid) (Im-IL) and POSS exhibits a wide electrochemical stability window and remarkable cycling performance.

title: NiFe alloy nanocrystals anchored on nitrogen-doped carbon: interface engineering for enhanced peroxymonosulfate activation via non-radical dominated pathways
abstract: NiFe alloy nanocrystals anchored on nitrogen-doped carbon (NiFe–N–C) efficiently activates PMS by lowering the O–O bond cleavage energy barrier at Ni/Fe dual sites, generating NiFe IV O and 1 O 2 as the primary reactive species.

title: Constructing a continuous gradient structure significantly enhances the high-temperature energy storage performance of cellulose acetate multilayer composite films
abstract: Developing environmentally friendly and renewable biobased dielectric materials is particularly important in the coming AI era in relation to requirements for high energy storage and application as well as high integration and flexibility.

title: Green hydrogen production by alkaline seawater electrolyzers: a transition from lab-scale research to industrial-scale applications
abstract: Seawater electrolysis offers an alternative to freshwater-based electrolysis owing to its abundant resources and sustainability, making it a key driver in addressing global water scarcity while advancing the hydrogen economy.

title: Upgrading of silicon slag into radiative cooling materials by controlled oxidation with oxygen carriers
abstract: Energy saving and waste recycling are two importance for sustainability of the Earth. Radiative cooling is an effective technology to decrease cooling energy consumption. Realization of low-cost preparation of radiative...

title: High-performance tailored Ag 2 Se thermoelectric networks via electrodeposition for body heat recovery
abstract: Single-step electrodeposition for fabricating high-performance 3D Ag 2 Se thermoelectro networks.

title: Enhanced interfacial charge separation via MnIn 2 S 4 /Zn 2 TiO 4 heterojunction for light-induced fuel production
abstract: A 2D/1D MnIn 2 S 4 /Zn 2 TiO 4 S-scheme heterostructure constructed by in situ growth enables efficient charge separation and promotes CO 2 photoreduction to value-added carbon products under UV-visible light.

title: Governing factors in mildly acidic Zn/MnO 2 batteries: interplay of electrochemical protocols, electrolyte composition, and cell configuration
abstract: We discuss how electrochemical cell parameters such as electrolyte composition and amount, electrochemical protocols, and surface area influence the performance of mildly acidic Zn/MnO 2 batteries.

title: Ball-milling-assisted construction of ternary mixed-metal oxide/g-C 3 N 4 heterostructures for synergistic polysulfide adsorption and catalytic conversion in lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: Ball-milled g-C 3 N 4 /NiCoZn–O heterostructures enable synergistic polysulfide adsorption and catalytic conversion via strong interfacial coupling, accelerating redox kinetics and suppressing shuttle effects for high-performance Li–S batteries.

title: Behind the transduction mechanism of a nanostructured functional material for environmental CO 2 monitoring
abstract: Schematic representation of CO 2 sensing mechanisms over Na-doped In 2 O 3 surface under dry and wet conditions.

title: Theoretical investigation on the dendrite suppression and desolvation promotion effect of the coating material on the Zn metal anode for aqueous zinc-ion batteries: case study of oxide coating
abstract: Multiscale simulations uncover how oxide surface govern Zn 2+ desolvation and diffusion in aqueous zinc-ion batteries. While SiO 2 promotes ultrahigh desolvation, TiO 2 , Al 2 O 3 , and CoO balance desolvation efficiency with low diffusion barriers.

title: Flash Joule heating enabled instantaneous purification and strengthening of CNT films for high-performance flexible aluminum-ion batteries
abstract: Flash Joule heating enables instantaneous purification and structural reinforcement of CNT films, achieving a high specific capacity of 281.97 mAh g −1 at 4 A g −1 and ultra-long cycle life exceeding 9200 cycles as Al-ion battery cathodes.

title: Synergistic interfacial engineering of dynamic coordination and hydrogen bonding enables reprocessable ultra-strong rubber nanocomposites
abstract: Interfacial engineering of HXNBR/ZIF-67 yields recyclable elastomers with self-reinforcement, ultrahigh strength, toughness, and promising lithium-ion battery applications for sustainable energy.

title: AI-assisted enhancement of thermoelectric properties in the Yb 14− x Ca x Mn 1− y AlySb 11 Zintl system
abstract: An AI-guided strategy accelerates thermoelectric materials discovery. In the quinary Yb 14− x Ca x Mn 1− y Al y Sb 11 system, machine learning predicted a high ZT composition, validating a streamlined pathway for complex material development.

title: Polyampholyte Graft Copolymers as Unimolecular Matrices for Noble Metal-Free and Visible Light-Driven Hydrogen Evolution by Eosin Y and Molybdenum Phosphide
abstract: Light-driven water splitting is a promising way to produce hydrogen as a renewable energy carrier in a sustainable way. For this, immobilization of different catalytically active building blocks within soft...

title: Influence of Al doping ratio on properties of ZnO:Al passivating contacts for crystalline silicon solar cells
abstract: The influence of Al doping ratio for ALD ZnO:Al films in SiO x /ZnO:Al/Al 2 O 3 electron-selective passivating contact stacks on c-Si is investigated. Passivation and contact performance are correlated with structural, electrical and optical properties.

title: Facile non-fullerene acceptors for efficient photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: B-2TPIC and T-2TPIC are synthesized with decoupled regulation of energy levels by enhancing short-range intramolecular charge transfer. PM6:B-2TPIC nanoparticles show a higher hydrogen evolution rate than PM6:T-2TPIC nanoparticles.

title: Gas evolution in Ruddlesden–Popper-type intercalation cathodes in all-solid-state fluoride-ion-batteries: implications on battery performance and synthesis of highly oxidized oxyfluorides
abstract: Gas evolution of Ruddlesden–Popper oxides upon fluorination is studied: chemical fluorination with AgF and electrochemical fluorination and reveals oxygen release upon chemical fluorination at 300 °C but not in electrochemical fluorination at 163 °C.

title: Tuning local Lewis acidity in transition-metal-doped Nb 2 C MXenes for enhanced urea electrosynthesis
abstract: Cu@Nb 2 C sits at the apex of the volcano relationship between Lewis acidity and urea synthesis activity, achieving a low limiting potential of −0.34 V with a moderate acidity level.

title: Advancements and challenges in metal selenides for electrocatalytic H 2 evolution
abstract: This review summarizes the research progress of MSes-based HER electrocatalysts, covering advantages, synthesis, enhancement strategies, mechanisms and current challenges, which is of great significance for designing efficient MSes-based catalysts.

title: Towards economically feasible recycling of spent LiFePO 4 black mass: a thermodynamic-assisted targeted delithiation strategy
abstract: Optimizing the thermodynamic stability window of LiFePO 4 /FePO 4 suppresses trace Fe dissolution via unstable HFePO 4 and enables economical electrochemical delithiation for high-purity Li recovery from spent black mass.

title: Oxygen-bridged Fe–Ni heterointerfaces confined in hierarchical Mo x C/N-doped carbon enable bifunctional pathway for efficient oxygen evolution
abstract: A catalyst featuring uniformly integrated Fe–Ni interfacial sites on a transition metal carbide is obtained through carbonization and Fe adsorption, enabling a bifunctional pathway during oxygen evolution that markedly improves catalytic activity.

title: Assembly of a micro-carbonized lignin-cellulose mixture and carbon nanotubes via interfacial interactions to prepare a composite film with superior electromagnetic interference shielding and mechanical performance
abstract: The conductive polymer composite (CPC) is a superior material because of its flexibility and lightweight properties, revealing high application potential in the electromagnetic interference (EMI) shielding field.

title: Metal-ion mediated mesopore engineering in hierarchical porous carbons for enhanced high-rate volumetric capacitance
abstract: We present a scalable mesopore-engineering strategy for alginate-derived HPCs via metal-ion crosslinking, generating ≈5 nm inherited mesopores that balance density and ion transport to enhance volumetric performance.

title: Discovery of oxide Li-conducting electrolytes with corner-sharing frameworks via topology-constrained crystal structure prediction
abstract: TOPIC is a topology-constrained crystal structure prediction algorithm for discovering corner-sharing oxide solid electrolytes. Combined with pretrained MLIPs, TOPIC identifies 92 unreported oxides with fast Li-ion conductivity.

title: An advanced LDH derived photocatalyst with enhanced charge separation for sustainable environmental remediation
abstract: Synergistic integration of TiO 2 NPs, ZnAl-LDH and GO inhibits charge recombination and elevates the visible-light photocatalytic degradation of pollutants.

title: Honeycomb-like structure with excellent thermal regulation and flame-retardant functions boosts Li-ion battery safety
abstract: The honeycomb-like structure possesses excellent thermal regulation and flame-retardant properties, which serve as two strong defense lines for substantially boosting battery safety.

title: First-principles study of the Pt@(Hf 2− x Zr x )B 2 O 2 bifunctional ORR/OER catalysts
abstract: The integer n (ranging from 0 to 32) in the hexagonal catalysts Pt@(Hf 2− x Zr x )B 2 O 2 ( x = n /16) denotes the Hf/Zr solid solution ratio.

title: Oligo(ethylene glycol)-modified benzotriazole-based ultra-narrow bandgap acceptors for efficient transparent organic photovoltaics
abstract: An oligo(ethylene glycol) modified pentacyclic fused-ring ultra-narrow bandgap acceptor BZ-OEG was developed. Adding PA-2Br as the third component, ternary TOPV achieved a PCE of 4.60% and an AVT of 70.0% with a LUE of 3.22%.

title: Investigation on the deactivation periods of ZSM-5/SAPO-34 catalysts in MTA reactions: an impact of product distribution
abstract: This research study reports the effect of carbon deposition on product distribution during different periods in MTA of ZSM-5/SAPO-34 composite molecular sieve.

title: Revealing the role of functional binder PEDOT:PSSTFSI in cathode–electrolyte interphase formation on LiFe 0.4 Mn 0.6 PO 4 electrodes of Li-ion batteries
abstract: The multifunctional PEDOT:PSSTFSI binder boosts Li-ion battery performance and cyclability, while replacing carbon black and PVDF. It enables higher cut-off voltages by forming a thinner and more stable CEI on LiFe 0.4 Mn 0.6 PO 4 -based cathode composite.

title: Boosting the syngas production of a SrTiO 3 photocathode via plasmonic hot electron injection from an Ag underlayer
abstract: Under back illumination conditions, under-layered Ag-SrTiO 3 heterostructure systems demonstrate a high faradaic efficiency of 80% and a notable CO production rate of 12 µmol h 1mg-1 at −0.8 V vs. RHE.

title: Cultivating a self-reinforcing solid-electrolyte interphase for negative-fading enhancement in CH 3 NH 3 PbBr 3 perovskite anodes
abstract: This study stabilizes MAPbBr 3 perovskite anodes via mechanical prelithiation and a tailored electrolyte, creating a self-reinforcing interface that enables an 84.6% ICE and 96% capacity retention after 1000 cycles for lithium ion batteries.

title: Effect of cycling conditions on hydride stability of the Ti 11 Nb 61 Cr 28 alloy
abstract: Hydride destabilization in the Ti 11 Nb 61 Cr 28 alloy is induced by cycling with high-temperature desorption, causing spontaneous hydrogen desorption. This phenomenon is not observed when cycling is performed entirely at room temperature.

title: A comprehensive review of mechanism-based catalyst design and applications for electrochemical ozone production
abstract: This review explores recent advancements and challenges in the development of electrocatalysts for the electrochemical ozone production including mechanistic understanding, electrode preparation, and applications in practical wastewater treatment.

title: Beyond the 2/3 approximation: a multiscale evaluation of the FRET orientation factor in nonfullerene acceptors
abstract: By overcoming ‘Kappaphobia’, we show statistical approximations underestimate κ 2 in high-efficiency organic photovoltaic materials.

title: Dynamic coupling mechanism in NbSe 2 /MoSe 2 heterojunctions for enhanced temperature adaptability of lithium-ion batteries
abstract: The dynamic coupling between NbSe 2 /MoSe 2 heterojunctions and a compatible electrolyte in situ forms a LiF-rich interphase, which synergistically optimizes interfacial ion kinetics, affording an anode with superior temperature adaptability.

title: Designing high-performance asymmetric microsupercapacitors for powering miniaturized electronic devices
abstract: This review emphasizes the latest advancements in asymmetric microsupercapacitors, concentrating on microfabrication techniques, electrode materials, and their incorporation into self-powered microelectronics for future flexible and wearable devices.

title: Self-assembled PrBaCo 2 O 5+ δ –Ba 0.9 CoO 3− δ composite as a highly active and durable oxygen electrode for reversible solid oxide cells
abstract: A B9C phase with mixed electronic and oxygen ionic conductivity was composited with PBC, forming a dual-phase oxygen electrode, PBC–B9C, with a misfit dislocation interface structure, which achieved enhanced catalytic activity and chemical stability.

title: A hydrogel-based passive cooling Janus material with both high solar reflectance and remarkable self-cleaning capability
abstract: A monolayer hydrogel-based passive cooling material with Janus wettability and high solar reflectance is proposed. The hydrophobic upper surface (133°) enables self-cleaning, while the high solar reflectance (80.06%) reduces solar heat gain.

title: The rational design of an ion-conducting and interface-optimized acrylate-based network binder employed in a silicon/graphite anode for Li-ion batteries
abstract: Silicon and graphite composites (Si/C), which feature higher capacities than graphite and lower expansion effects than silicon, have become promising alternatives to commercial graphite anodes, where advanced binder design plays a key role.

title: Dual-functional photocatalysis: bridging hydrogen production and environmental remediation – a review
abstract: Solar-driven photocatalysis enables coupled H 2 production and degradation of organic pollutants in wastewater. This waste-to-energy approach uses contaminants as sacrificial donors, enabling efficient purification and supporting a circular economy.

title: C as a bridge and Bi as a photothermal converter to trigger visible-light catalytic CO 2 reduction over BiOBr by in situ solid-state reduction
abstract: BiOBr composite with triple active sites of carbon acts as an electronic export medium, Bi NPs act as photothermal converters, and O v act as gas adsorption activators, displaying excellent visible-light photothermal CO 2 reduction activity.

title: In situ construction of a Cs 3 Sb 2 Br 9 /CeO 2 heterojunction for efficient photooxidation of 5-hydroxymethylfurfural
abstract: The Cs 3 Sb 2 Br 9 /CeO 2 heterojunction constructed by O V engineering delivers benchmark photocatalytic HMF oxidation, providing a simple but effective strategy for photocatalyst design.

title: Dual-carbon batteries using fluorination and defluorination reactions
abstract: Dual-carbon batteries based on reversible fluorination/defluorination with LiF@carbon cathodes and graphite anode are demonstrated. A concentrated sulfolane electrolyte enhances LiF utilization, delivering 356 mAh g −1 with notable cycling stability.

title: Oxygen-vacancy-engineered urchin-like CoMoO 4 epitaxially grown on partially oxidized Ti 3 C 2 MXene anchored on nickel nanocones for an efficient oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: A defect-rich Ti 3 C 2 /TiO 2 –CoMoO 4 nanocomposite on Ni nanocones enables highly active and stable OER performance through enhanced conductivity and abundant redox-active sites.

title: A smart photodynamic ionic liquid nanogel fiber-based actuator with efficient photothermal conversion and self-cleaning achieved by supramolecular assembly
abstract: A micro-/nanosystem actuator was fabricated by ionic liquid supramolecular assembly, and has applications as an advanced intelligent actuator and in self-cleaning evaporation systems with solar harvesting, energy storage, and pathogen eradication.

title: Modulating exciton dissociation and charge transfer by extending π-conjugation in thiazole-based COFs to boost photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: Extending π-conjugation in thiazole-based COFs enhances exciton dissociation and charge transfer, significantly boosting their photocatalytic hydrogen evolution activity.

title: Boosting lithium-ion battery performance: the role of a novel carbonate-based ionic liquid electrolyte additive
abstract: Imidazole–carbonate inner-salt ionic liquids enhance ion transport via strong Li + coordination. Their frontier orbital energies stabilize SEI/CEI and suppress decomposition, enabling ME-10 to achieve high rate capability, stability, and capacity.

title: Crosslinked, highly conductive binder unlocks low-temperature silicon anodes for lithium-ion batteries
abstract: A novel multifunctional binder with enhanced adhesion strength and mixed ionic–electronic conductivity is designed by integrating PEDOT:PSS with guar gum (GG). It has great potential for use in harsh environments, such as cold regions.

title: Thin-film composite membranes for efficient CO 2 capture: evaluation of different polymer substrate selection strategies
abstract: Various polymer substrate membranes with excellent performance for CO 2 capture.

title: Natural mineral engineering of zinc anodes: attapulgite-based interfacial modification for dendrite-free aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: An investigation into the mechanisms of DTM, NaMMT, and ATP coatings on the electrochemical performance identified ATP as the most effective modifier.

title: Tunable three-component metal–organic frameworks consisting of spanning bis(tridentate) linkers with six N/O-donor atoms: design, synthesis, properties and applications
abstract: Flexible dual-linker-based three-component self-assembly provides a new paradigm for constructing functional 2D and 3D MOFs and understanding their structure–property-relationships in heterogeneous catalysis and environmental remediation.

title: Impact of vapor phase Ge incorporation and reaction pathways on the performance and stability of Cu 2 Zn(Ge,Sn)Se 4 solar cells
abstract: Composition shifts driven by the Cu/Sn precursor ratio and SnSe 2− x and/or GeSe 2− x vapor affect defects despite similar end compositions. Post-deposition treatment delivers 12.5% efficiency at Ge/(Ge + Sn) = 9%, showing a promising physical route.

title: Manipulating electronic and geometric effects of Pt nanoparticles exerted from Co single atoms in the selective hydrogenation of epichlorohydrin
abstract: The electronic and geometric effects of Pt nanoparticles exerted from Co single atoms can play important roles in the hydrogenation of epichlorohydrin.

title: Sustainable photocatalytic CO 2 conversion using microalgae as a carbon-negative scavenger
abstract: Photocatalytic CO 2 conversion using microalgae as a sacrificial agent offers an environmentally friendly pathway for efficient CO 2 capture (through photosynthesis by microalgae) and CO 2 conversion (through photocatalysis).

title: Mechanical properties of cathode–electrolyte interphase layers in high-voltage lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Quantitative mechanical mapping via scanning force microscopy reveals that the cathode–electrolyte interphase on high-voltage LiNi 0.5 Mn 1.5 O 4 (LNMO) electrodes mechanically homogenized during extended cycling, forming an organic-dominated network.

title: Metal–organic framework on fullerene (MOFOF) derived Co-anchored hierarchical carbon nanocomposite for catalytic nitroarene reduction
abstract: A novel method for synthesizing a Co-anchored hierarchical carbon nanocomposite from a metal organic framework (MOF) on fullerene (MOFOF) for catalytic nitroarene reduction.

title: Precursor degree of polymerization engineering to understand the sodium storage behavior of closed pores in hard carbon
abstract: Tuning the degree of polymerization of precursor disrupts compact lignified structures and releases oxygen radicals, resulting in hard carbon with higher closed-pore volume. This strategy is universal for straw and nutshell biomass.

title: Reconstruction-driven YO 6 –Sn interactions in Y-doped SrSnO 3 for stable acidic CO 2 -to-HCOOH electrocatalysis at industrial current densities
abstract: Y-doped SrSnO 3 constructs YO 6 –Sn interactions, achieving selective CO 2 -to-HCOOH reduction in acids with >90% FE at 200 mA cm −2 . The interface stabilizes in situ metallic Sn, suppressing dissolution to enable excellent 180 h stability.

title: Inhibiting defect formation and degradation at the buried interface of tin perovskite solar cells by modulating PEDOT:PSS with sodium alginate
abstract: Bilateral modulation of the buried interface using natural sodium alginate serves to reduce PSS and optimize the perovskite film quality, thereby delivering enhanced device performance of tin-based perovskite solar cells.

title: Heptazine-based conjugated microporous polymers for enhanced light absorption and charge separation in photocatalytic hydrogen peroxide production
abstract: A heptazine-based CMP achieves enhanced photocatalysis for H 2 O 2 production via improved light absorption and charge separation.

title: Synergistic regulation of bulk and interfacial properties in PEO electrolyte using CaF 2 for stable lithium metal batteries
abstract: The CaF 2 additive enhances Li + transport and stabilizes the electrolyte/Li interface via a LiF and Ca–Li alloy interphase.
