title: Atomic layer deposited conductive tungsten coating enabling ultrahigh-rate capability for commercial LiFePO 4 cathodes
abstract: A conformal tungsten (W) coating was applied to commercial LiFePO 4 cathode particles, which improved the electrical conductivity by 224% and delivered a rate capability of 113.5 mA h g −1 at 30C.

title: Colloidal phase control of Ni–P nanocrystals reveals a P-site hydrogen evolution reaction mechanism distinct from Ni-rich analogues
abstract: Phase-pure Ni x P y enables detailed investigations of Ni–P interactions across different phases, highlighting the P-site driven hydrogen evolution reaction mechanism in P-rich Ni x P y .

title: Crosslinking-free preparation of thermoplastic triblock liquid crystal elastomer actuators
abstract: A class of thermoplastic SBS-based LCEs without additional covalent or non-covalent crosslinkers, which could be fabricated into monodomain actuators by the microphase-separated PS nanodomains as self-locking physical crosslinks.

title: An ultrathin carbon-shelled indium oxide catalyst for electroreduction of CO 2 to formate at ampere-level current density
abstract: An ultrathin carbon-shelled indium oxide catalyst exhibits high selectivity and stability in the electrochemical CO 2 reduction to formate.

title: High-entropy engineering for achieving linear-like behavior and superior energy storage in Bi 0.5 Na 0.5 TiO 3 -based lead-free relaxor ferroelectrics
abstract: The enhancement of energy storage performance via high-entropy regulation of near-linear behavior.

title: Precursor-engineered prelithiation strategy CaMoO 4 enhancing ion transport in composite solid electrolytes for flexible interdigitated micro-supercapacitors
abstract: Solid polymer electrolytes (SPEs) are often limited by low ionic conductivity and poor interfacial ion transport, which restrict their application in flexible solid-state supercapacitors.

title: Combined experimental and theoretical elucidation of PCET pathways in electrocatalytic alcohol oxidation and its application in PET upcycling
abstract: Mechanistic study of alcohol oxidation over a Co-doped Ni-based catalyst (aCo BDC/NF) revealed an HAT pathway for C–H bond breaking as the rate-limiting step. The catalyst is used for electrochemical PET upcycling.

title: Self-healing hydrogels in flexible energy storage devices: mechanisms, applications, and prospects
abstract: Schematic diagram of SHHEs and practical applications in flexible energy storage technologies.

title: CuZn nanoalloy encapsulated within tannic acid-functionalized carbon nanotubes for efficient electrocatalytic urea synthesis
abstract: A CNT-confined CuZn nanoalloy modified with tannic acid enables efficient electrocatalytic C–N coupling of CO 2 and NO 3 − by enhancing CO 2 adsorption, suppressing nitrate over-reduction, and promoting CO–NH 2 coupling toward high-yield urea synthesis.

title: Strategies to enhance the supercapacitance performance of 2D MXenes through defect engineering, doping, hybridization, and magnetic field assistance: recent progress and challenges
abstract: Strategies to enhance the supercapacitance performance of rising 2D MXenes through defect engineering, doping, hybridization, and magnetic field assistance.

title: High thermoelectric performance in rhombohedral GeSe ingots achieved by Pb alloying
abstract: A brilliant ZT of 1.35 at 723 K and a conversion efficiency of 5.5% are achieved in Pb alloyed GeSe ingots.

title: Thermal Transport in SrSnO 3 Revealed by First-principles Theory, Raman Thermometry and Machine Learning
abstract: The thermal conductivity (κ) of SrSnO 3 is systematically investigated using a comprehensive approach integrating Raman thermometry, laser flash analysis (LFA), first-principles calculations based on density functional theory (DFT) combined with...

title: Achieving directional zinc ion transport in a belt-like orderly arranged vanadium dioxide cathode material
abstract: We propose a strategy of utilizing directional ion transport to shorten the diffusion path of Zn 2+ by using orderly arranged belt-like VO 2 .

title: Graphene-Like Carbon Fibres Derived from Cotton Waste for High-Performance Supercapacitors: Computational and Experimental Investigation
abstract: Graphene-like carbon fibres (GLCFs) demonstrated their excellence for supercapacitors (SCs) due to their physical and structural properties. However, the complex synthesis routes for GLCFs hinder their widespread use in SCs....

title: Coupling biomass carbon engineering with phosphorus chemistry for high-performance flexible zinc-ion hybrid supercapacitors
abstract: Phosphorus doping boosts Zn-ion storage in fennel-seed derived activated carbon. As a ZIHSC cathode, it performs well in liquid and gel electrolytes, where framework polarization strengthens Zn–O interactions, improving performance significantly.

title: Transient behavior of self-healable ultra-stretchable carboxylic acid-doped polyaniline films for sustainable and re-processable polymer electronics
abstract: This work investigates carboxylic acid dopants in PANI/PAAMPSA systems, showing how dopant structure governs mechanical, electrical, and self-healing properties. The films rapidly degrade in water and soil and can be recast into new sensors for sustainable electronics.

title: Unraveling interfacial electron transfer dynamics in noble metal cocatalyst modified ferroelectric photocatalysts via nonadiabatic molecular dynamics simulations
abstract: The average density of states ( D ) descriptor is established to predict interfacial electron transfer efficiency in composite photocatalysts, guiding the rational design of high-performance composite photocatalytic systems.

title: Angular Strain-Induced Polarization in a Ferroelectric Ammonium Cyclic-Phosphate and its Wind Speed Sensing Application
abstract: Amino-phosphate-based ferroelectrics exhibit strong ionic conductivity, biocompatibility, high dielectric constants, and pronounced ferroelectric properties. Considering these attributes, we propose a strategy to enhance ferroelectric performance through angle strain engineering in...

title: Engineering a buried interface using a conductive polymer to mediate carrier behavior for efficient Solar-driven water splitting on a Si-based photocathode
abstract: A suitable conductive polymer on Si creates an ideal buried interface that facilitates enhanced interfacial charge extraction, leading to efficient solar-driven H 2 production.

title: Trade-off between O 2 activation and active-site regeneration on biaxially strained Co-doped MoS 2 monolayers: a density functional theory study
abstract: Biaxial strain promotes O 2 activation and dissociation at Co@V S in MoS 2 , but excessive strain deepens oxide-like product wells and hinders oxygen removal, revealing an activation–regeneration trade-off.

title: Dual confinement strategy for Pt–Co intermetallic electrocatalysts with superior durability in proton exchange membrane fuel cells
abstract: A novel dual confinement strategy produces highly ordered L1 0 -PtCo electrocatalysts. This robust structure effectively inhibits metal dissolution, achieving near-zero performance loss after 30 000 cycles in PEM fuel cells.

title: Advanced 3D ceramic thin-film capacitors: research progress and dielectric energy storage potential
abstract: Integrating ceramic films onto high-surface-area 3D substrates boosts specific surface area, expands charge storage interfaces, and thus markedly enhances energy storage density versus conventional 2D configurations.

title: Engineering high-energy, high-rate NASICON cathodes via Cu doping and microwave-induced grain refinement for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: Synergistic Cu doping and microwave-induced grain refinement unlock high-energy, high-rate NASICON cathodes for sodium-ion batteries.

title: Theoretical insights into encapsulation-induced CO 2 reduction selectivity reversal on Ru supported on TiO x catalysts
abstract: SMSI-driven TiO x encapsulation tunes catalyst electronics and shifts CO 2 reduction selectivity: exposed Ru/interface sites favor CH 4 , while fully covered TiO x promotes CO formation via redox C–O cleavage.

title: Polymer additives enhance charge transport balance in Y6 single-component organic solar cells
abstract: Polymer additives enhance the photovoltaic performance of Y6-based single-component organic solar cells by improving hole transport and suppressing recombination without forming a donor-rich BHJ network.

title: Industrial-oriented large-area perovskite solar cells: fabrication technology integration, defect passivation, and stability optimization
abstract: This review addresses the scientific challenges of scaling perovskite solar cells from laboratory to industry, as outlined in the graphical abstract. It highlights key bottlenecks like film heterogeneity, transport losses, and stability.

title: Harnessing structural disorder: amorphous–crystalline heterostructures as a platform for next-generation photocatalysis
abstract: This review summarizes recent advances in amorphous–crystalline heterostructure photocatalysts, and their advantages, synthetic strategies and applications are detailed and discussed to outline critical directions for future development.

title: Intercalated montmorillonite for fabricating a highly conductive and overall-stable solid composite electrolyte for advanced lithium metal batteries
abstract: Pre-intercalated MMT offers continuous Li + transport channels and strong interaction with residual solvent in the SCE for lithium metal batteries.

title: The role of precursor decomposition in low-temperature solution-based synthesis and crystallization of Li-garnet solid electrolyte films
abstract: This work investigates the impact of precursor decomposition on the crystallization of solution-processed Li-garnet solid electrolyte films. We track crystallization and identify precursor contributions to decomposition reactions and formed phases.

title: Surface Reconstruction Tunes Band Edges and Lattice-Oxygen Reactivity on BiVO 4 (010) Photoanodes
abstract: We unveil how reconstruction-driven surface chemistry controls interfacial electronic levels and reactivity at aqueous BiVO 4 (010) interfaces. Dielectric-dependent hybrid functional molecular dynamics is applied to six electrochemically stable surface terminations of...

title: The “two birds with one stone” strategy of flower-like multi-shell hollow microsphere/boron nitride composites achieves dual functionalities of efficient microwave absorption and excellent thermal management
abstract: Electronic devices not only face electromagnetic interference issues but also must address challenges such as performance degradation and reduced reliability caused by heat accumulation.

title: Core-ligand modulation alters core–shell coordination to produce stable supercooled phase-change materials for long-term heat storage and release
abstract: Mn-acetamidomethanol@erythritol core–shell coordinated phase-change materials achieve both stable energy storage and controllable release for long-term thermal energy management.

title: Catalysis in motion: unlocking mechanistic insights with dynamic transmission electron microscopy
abstract: We highlight how DTEM reveals catalytic dynamics and outline key advances, challenges, and future opportunities.

title: Mobility of interfaces in silicon-based anodes
abstract: Silicon (Si) is a promising anode material for next-generation lithium-ion batteries (LIBs), but its practical use is limited due to severe degradation during electrochemical cycling.

title: Complex influence of stack pressure on BiF 3 cathode materials in all-solid-state fluoride-ion batteries
abstract: This study probes stack-pressure effects in ASSFIBs with BiF 3 |BaSnF 4 |Sn cells. A high 180 MPa boosts cycling by improving F − transport, mitigating oxygen-related interfacial degradation and stabilizing phase evolution of BiF 3 cathodes.

title: Asymmetrically channeled two-dimensional membrane for stable, selective lithium-ion transport
abstract: Design of two-dimensional asymmetric channels effectively regulates ion transport selectivity while suppressing membrane swelling to enhance membrane stability, enabling efficient lithium extraction.

title: Revealing the pseudocapacitance charge storage mechanism of sulfur-doped carbon supercapacitors in non-aqueous electrolyte through in situ EPR
abstract: The charge storage mechanism of sulfur-doped carbon in non-aqueous electrolytes remains poorly understood. Using in situ EPR spectroscopy we propose that its pseudocapacitive behaviour is governed by a reversible polaron-to-bipolaron transition.

title: Explainable ensemble learning reveals site-driven d z 2 orbital occupancy tuning for enhanced hydrogen evolution on metal-doped Ni-loaded BN catalysts
abstract: This work integrates first-principles calculations with ensemble learning to accelerate the discovery of bimetallic Ni–TM2@BN single-atom catalysts for the hydrogen evolution reaction. Theoretical models and descriptors are employed to predict and optimise the material's HER activity.

title: Rethinking catalyst design for the hydrogen evolution reaction: single-phase or multi-phase?
abstract: This review rethinks the traditional dichotomy between homogeneous single-phase and heterogeneous multi-phase HER electrocatalysts. An extended Sabatier principle is proposed to optimize the entire reaction pathway across catalytic domains.

title: Creating percolating Li diffusion pathways via Ge substitution enables the fabrication of thick-electrode oxide all-solid-state batteries
abstract: A high energy density all-solid-state battery via Li percolation network formation.

title: Voltage-induced phase transformation: key to enhanced cycling stability in NiS pseudo-supercapacitors
abstract: By optimizing the operating voltage window of the device, NiS was prevented from entering the harmful potential region, thereby fundamentally suppressing the phase transition of NiS to β-Ni(OH) 2 , significantly enhancing cycle stability.

title: Data-efficient training of machine learning interatomic potentials for MAX-phase synthesizability prediction
abstract: A fined-tuned MLIP for prediction of MAX-phase synthesizability.

title: Tough, self-healing and sensitive elastomer composites with efficient electromagnetic interference shielding for skin temperature detection
abstract: A novel elastomer composite featuring a segregated CNT network and dynamic imine bonds, with remarkable toughness, self-healing capability, outstanding thermal sensitivity and excellent EMI shielding for skin temperature detection.

title: Doping design on reconstructed NbAs(001) surfaces for enhanced hydrogen evolution: theoretical calculations and experimental validation
abstract: The surface reconstruction of NbAs(001) combined with Ta doping synergistically optimized the hydrogen adsorption process, significantly improving the HER performance of Weyl semimetal and highlighting its potential for electrocatalytic applications.

title: Manipulating the d-band center of Cu( i )-MOFs for the catalytic ignition of [BMIM]N(CN) 2 IL-H 2 O 2 propellants
abstract: Ligand electronegativity modulates Cu-SH′ through Cu-S 3 coordination to shift the d-band center of Cu( i ) closer to the Fermi level. This boosts ˙OH and 1 O 2 from H 2 O 2 , making [BMIM]N(CN) 2 -H 2 O 2 hypergolic with ultrafast ignition ( t id = 18.4 ms).

title: Fluorinated polymer electrolyte via dual-salt coupling for solid-state lithium metal batteries
abstract: A novel polymer solid-state electrolyte featuring a highly electronegative fluorinated polymer backbone and a dual-salt system was designed by a material coupling strategy. This achieves functional decoupling, enabling superior battery performance.

title: Carboxylate-functionalized polymers of intrinsic microporosity for high-performance osmotic power conversion
abstract: Harvesting sustainable osmotic power from salinity gradients using reverse electrodialysis (RED) holds significant importance for addressing the global energy crisis.

title: Continuous heat harvesting by an ionogel mixed with PEDOT:PSS under both fluctuated and steady temperature gradients
abstract: An ionogel with PEDOT:PSS is a mixed ion-electron conductor that can continuously supply electricity under a steady temperature gradient, with thermopower 2–3 orders higher than conventional electronic thermoelectric materials.

title: Boosting initial coulombic efficiency in cobalt-free Li-rich layered oxides via facile gas–solid interface modification
abstract: Simultaneous Nb 5+ doping and spinel phase construction via gas–solid interface modification drastically enhance the ICE and Li + diffusion kinetics of Co-free Li-rich layered oxides.

title: Over 50 wt% loaded nanoparticulate Ru on hollow carbon spheres as efficient catalysts for the conversion of levulinic acid to γ-valerolactone under ambient conditions
abstract: Nanoparticulate Ru on hollow carbon spheres exhibited excellent catalytic performance for the conversion of LA to GVL at 100 °C under 5 bar H 2 .

title: Hot electron-driven nitrate-to-ammonia conversion on nanocatalysts: insights from quantum dynamics simulations
abstract: Ultrafast hot-electron transfer enables selective NO 3 − → NH 3 reduction on CdSe quantum dots.

title: Defect engineering and triangular copper as key drivers for ultralow thermal conductivity in Cu 2 ZrS 3 polytypes
abstract: Copper-rich sulfides are promising materials for energy-conversion applications due to their environmental compatibility, cost-effectiveness, and earth abundance.

title: Mixed-SAM interface enables efficient and stable semi-transparent perovskite solar cells for day–night ambient-light energy harvesting
abstract: Co-assembled Me-4PACz/TMLA mixed SAMs stabilise the buried interface to withstand DMF/NMP inks, enabling dense ultrathin perovskite films with suppressed nanoporosity and ∼15% efficient semitransparent ST-PSCs for day–night ambient-light harvesting.

title: Synergistic activation of grain boundaries with dual salts enables fast lithium percolation in LATP-based solid state electrolytes
abstract: Non-equilibrium grain boundary structures of LATP-based solid-state electrolyte were fabricated by the dual-salt strategy for fast Li ion conduction. And precise mechanisms of charge carrier transport were investigated at grain scale.

title: High-temperature performance investigation of adiponitrile-based electrolytes for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: ADN participates in the formation of a compact and stable CEI film, and suppresses the side reactions occurring at elevated temperatures.

title: Electrodeposited dual-phase nickel selenide heterostructure for hydrazine-assisted water splitting
abstract: An electrodeposition method is used to grow a NiSe 2 /Ni 0.85 Se heterostructure on Ni foam at room temperature. NiSe 2 /Ni 0.85 Se exhibits bifunctional activity for water splitting in a hydrazine medium, achieving 10 mA cm −2 at 0.11 V vs. RHE.

title: A pyromellitic diimide-based negolyte for neutral aqueous organic redox flow batteries with high power density
abstract: A design to lower the redox potential of aromatic diimide through conjugated structure manipulation for constructing high-power flow battery is reported.

title: What role does LiF play In anode-free lithium metal batteries?
abstract: LiF-dominated interphase is uniquely critical due to the high shear modulus for physical dendrite suppression, ultra-low electronic conductivity to prevent electrolyte depletion, and high interfacial energy for uniform planar lithium plating.

title: Decahedral platinum–copper nanoframes enriched with defects for highly efficient electrocatalytic hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: The Pt–Cu nanoframe (Pt–Cu NFs) architecture with strain effects, atomic steps, and twin defects collectively optimizes the electrocatalytic HER performance.

title: From synthesis to machine learning validation for the thermoelectric Zintl phase Ba 1− x Eu x Zn 2− y Al y Sb 2 system
abstract: New Zintl phases in the Ba 1− x Eu x Zn 2− y Al y Sb 2 system were synthesized via a machine-learning validated approach. The Eu/Al co-substitution induces atomic disordering, suppressing κ tot to 0.24 W m −1 K −1 and yielding a ZT = 0.50 at 755 K.

title: Harnessing an mpg-C 3 N 4 photocatalyst for the selective oxidative coupling of amines to yield azoaromatic compounds
abstract: A metal-free mesoporous mpg-C 3 N 4 photocatalyst drives the selective oxidative coupling of arylamines to yield azoaromatics under mild conditions, offering high efficiency, broad substrate scope, recyclability, and mechanistic insight.

title: High-throughput screening of piezoelectric hybrid organic–inorganic perovskites via density-functional theory and machine learning
abstract: We establish a “distribution-aware” density functional theory-machine learning workflow for scalable discovery of high-performance and flexible piezoelectric hybrid organic–inorganic perovskites.

title: Designing high-performance P3-type Na 2/3 [Ni 1/3 Mn 2/3 ]O 2 cathodes for Na-ion batteries
abstract: New synthesis route using spinel precursors yields high-performance P3-type Na 2/3 Ni 1/3 Mn 2/3 O 2 , overcoming limitations of conventional P3-structured Na-ion battery cathodes.

title: Improved buried interface contact in inverted perovskite solar cells with dual-site-binding molecules
abstract: We introduce ethyl (methylthio)acetate (EMA) as a novel dual-site modifier for inverted perovskite solar cells. Its carbonyl and methylthio groups synergistically passivate defects and improve perovskite crystallization, enabling a PCE of 25.07%.

title: Store or catalyze? The M–O bond decides
abstract: How M–O bonding governs the transition between charge storage and catalytic activity.

title: Sodium-ion battery development since 2020 with future perspectives
abstract: Schematic representation of a sodium-ion battery, illustrating Na + transport between the cathode and anode during charge and discharge, along with representative crystal structures of typical electrode materials.

title: From capture to circularity: carbon-based adsorbents bridging adsorption, regeneration, and destruction pathways for sustainable PFAS remediation
abstract: Per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFASs) persist in water systems due to their extreme chemical stability and weak degradability, demanding treatment approaches that extend beyond simple capture.

title: Noble metal catalysts for methanol steam reforming: advances and future directions for hydrogen production
abstract: This review summarizes recent advances in noble-metal catalysts for MSR, covering mechanisms, synthesis, and performance. It highlights key challenges and future directions to spur research, applications, and the hydrogen economy.

title: Engineering Hierarchical Porosity in MOFs for Host-Guest Chemistry with Large Organometallic Complexes
abstract: The microporous nature of metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) often limits their capacity to incorporate large molecular guests, such as organometallic catalysts. In this work, we demonstrate a defect-engineering strategy for the...

title: Radiation hardness analysis of 2D layered perovskite photovoltaics in space environments
abstract: We report fully 2D-based space photovoltaics using 2D Ruddlesden–Popper perovskite as a light absorber and MoS 2 and graphene as charge transport layers.

title: Rational design of organic layer/3D silver foam electrodes for electrochemical CO 2 reduction reaction at diluted concentration
abstract: An organic-Ag foam interfacial design enhances local CO 2 availability and stabilizes key intermediates, enabling highly selective CO production even under diluted CO 2 conditions.

title: Screen-printed supercapacitors based on vitamin B 2 functionalized carbon electrodes and deep eutectic solvent electrolytes
abstract: A flexible supercapacitor was fabricated via screen-printing with cellulose diacetate, graphite ink, riboflavin-functionalized activated carbon, and a deep eutectic solvent electrolyte, offering a non-toxic, sustainable solution for low-power wireless sensor applications.

title: Quaternary morpholinium-mediated defect control in high-performance perovskite solar cells
abstract: A multifunctional quaternary ammonium salt, EMMorBr, is designed for post-treatment of perovskite surfaces. The resulting inverted perovskite solar cells deliver a champion efficiency of 26.14% and demonstrate remarkable operational stability.

title: Regulation strategies and design prospects of high-performance catalysts for DMFCs: from monometallic Pt catalysts to high-entropy alloys
abstract: This work summarizes advances, regulation strategies and structure–activity relationship of HEAs as performance catalysts for MOR, revealing the evolution from monometallic Pt to high entropy, highlighting key gaps and design challenges.

title: Trinuclear copper supramolecular frameworks for boosting the electrocatalytic reduction of CO 2 to C 3 products
abstract: The HCOO group on trinuclear copper optimized the electronic structure in supramolecular frameworks and promoted C–C coupling, while K + in the electrolyte suppressed the HER, ultimately enabling the generation of C 3 products during CO 2 reduction.

title: Fluorine-doped β-Ni(OH) 2 –Ti 3 C 2 MXene composite: a bifunctional electrode
abstract: F-Ni(OH) 2 –Ti 3 C 2 exhibits superior electrocatalytic activity and delivers an excellent specific capacity of ∼242 mAh g −1 at 1 A g −1 in an alkaline electrolyte. The hybrid capacitor delivered a high specific energy of 62 Wh kg −1 in gel electrolyte.

title: p–n junction-induced bipolar charge micro-regions driving enhanced redox kinetics for efficient overall water splitting
abstract: A p–n junction catalyst incorporating a BIEF was constructed by growing NiFe LDH nanosheets on SCN nanotubes. This heterostructure uses dual charges to locally enrich OH − and H + , optimizing the catalytic microenvironment for HER and OER.

title: Pressure-engineered migration bottlenecks and non-monotonic ionic conduction in NASICON solid electrolytes
abstract: Under compression, NASICON-type solid electrolytes exhibit two competing effects that collectively determine their ionic transport.

title: Spatial confinement-stabilized copper single-atoms in amphiphilic zeolite for enhanced Fenton-like catalysis
abstract: The stable Cu + single-atom catalyst (Cu 2 @amZ-200) synthesized by utilizing the spatial confinement effect of zeolite simultaneously achieves high activity and stability in the Fenton-like reaction.

title: Microenvironment engineering to modulate the tolerance of Pt/Pd-based electrocatalysts for methanol crossover in oxygen reduction reaction
abstract: Aiming at the methanol crossover phenomenon, a comprehensive review of anti-poisoned Pt/Pd-based ORR electrocatalysts attempts to elucidate their anti-poisoning mechanism and provide a research foundation for their development and application.

title: Cr doping regulates FeNi alloy nanoparticle exsolution on Sr 2 Fe 1.1 Ni 0.2 Cr 0.2 Mo 0.5 O 6− δ cathode to facilitate CO 2 electrolysis
abstract: Cr element doping was studied to regulate the density of exsolved FeNi nanoparticles in Sr 2 Fe 1.1 Ni 0.2 Cr 0.2 Mo 0.5 O 6− δ perovskite and improve its CO 2 electrolysis activity.

title: Atomic insights into a family of single transition metal atom-doped MXenes for catalytic conversion of lithium polysulfides in lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: Single-transition-metal-atom-doped MXenes can significantly suppress the polysulfide shuttle effect in lithium–sulfur batteries.

title: Predicting the HER activity of SACs on MXenes with simple features and interpretable machine learning models
abstract: An interpretable machine learning model was developed using DFT data to predict hydrogen adsorption energies, enabling accurate and transparent screening of HER catalysts with performance comparable to the intrinsic accuracy of DFT methods.

title: Triisopropylsilylethynyl-substituted solid additives for morphology optimization affording high-efficiency organic solar cells
abstract: Two triisopropylsilylethynyl (TIPS)-based solid additives, 2T-Si and TT-Si, were designed and enabled high-performance organic solar cells with efficiencies of 19.54%.

title: On the transport properties of K 2 ZnV 2 O 7
abstract: Variable temperature PXRD of K 2 ZnV 2 O 7 showing impurity KVO 3 melt and recrystallise. The molten KVO 3 causes the high conductivity.

title: Atomic-scale mechanism of alloy anodes mitigating polysulfide-induced degradation in lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: Atomic-scale mechanisms of polysulfide-induced degradation mitigation in lithium–sulfur batteries using alloy anodes: chemically integrated SEI in Li–Mg and physically segregated barrier formation in Li–Al and Li–Zn systems.

title: Chromium and molybdenum dual-metal nanoclusters: synergistic catalysis of sulfur redox for high performance Li–S batteries
abstract: The Cr/Mo@N-C catalyst utilizes nitrogen-doped graphene to anchor lithium polysulfides (LiPSs), while the Cr and Mo nanoclusters synergistically accelerate redox kinetics and LiPSs conversion.

title: Pore-space-partitioned and hetero-atom-enriched dual-redox scalable metal–organic framework synergistically boosts overall water splitting
abstract: The synergistic metal–ligand redox interplay in a pore-partitioned, heteroatom-enriched Co( ii ) MOF enables high-efficiency electrochemical OER and HER activity in alkaline media, and further delivers significant total water splitting performance.

title: Lignin for sustainable electronics: the interplay of structure, morphology and chemistry in modelling dielectric properties
authors: 'Marianna Ambrico', 'Ofelia Durante', 'Sebastiano De Stefano', 'Rosarita D'Orsi', 'Domenico Aceto', 'Paolo Francesco Ambrico', 'Nadia Martucciello', 'Filippo Giubileo', 'Sandra Rivas', 'Alessandra Operamolla', 'Antonio Di Bartolomeo'
abstract: We show that lignins, when extracted using technical pulping or organosolv processes, exhibit chemical features that highligh its versatility in energy storage devices like microsupercapacitors paving the way for its integration into green electronics.

title: Computational screening of bioinspired mixed ionic-electronic conductors
abstract: A computational funnel approach is compared to a genetic algorithm for the design of bioinspired mixed conductors.

title: Cross-talk and internal resistance in high-voltage graphite‖LiNi 0.5 Mn 1.5 O 4 cells: sulfolane vs. carbonate-based electrolytes
abstract: High-voltage cathode active materials such as LiNi 0.5 Mn 1.5 O 4 offer promises of high-energy density lithium-ion batteries, but their implementation is hindered by electrolyte instability and cross-talk phenomena that compromise cycling performance.

title: Acoustic cavitation engineering of LLZTO-coated separators for moisture-resistant lithium metal batteries
abstract: Acoustic cavitation reconstructs LLZTO into a highly dispersible coating for polypropylene separators, enabling uniform Li-ion flux, suppressed dendrite growth, improved interfacial stability, and durable high-performance lithium metal batteries.

title: Geometry-engineered copper redox interfaces drive highly selective CO 2 reduction to C 2 products
abstract: Copper complexes synchronized with heterogeneous metal oxide surfaces enable highly selective CO 2 -to-ethanol conversion through interfacial synergy.

title: Coulombic efficiency-driven optimization of health-aware charging protocols: an experimental investigation on LCO/graphite lithium-ion cells
abstract: A coulombic efficiency-driven fast charging framework enables adaptive C-rate control across SOC and SOH. Threshold- and surrogate-based optimization use coulombic efficiency as a degradation proxy to balance speed and lifetime.

title: Supramolecular oscillation-assisted ion transport in solid electrolytes
abstract: A solid polymer electrolyte that breaks classic performance trade-offs via “supramolecular oscillation-assisted ion transport” is presented, enabling electrochromic devices with rapid response and significantly enhanced stability.

title: Convergent modulation of CHA zeolites using substituted phenols and ammonium salts
abstract: Unified etching-assisted zeolite growth regardless of organic/inorganic etchants: optimal nucleophile amount inversely correlates with K a while maximizing CHA crystallinity and porosity.

title: Phonon RIXS signatures reveal polaron formation in oxygen redox Li- and Na-ion battery cathodes
abstract: Vibrationally resolved O K-edge RIXS reveals phonon signatures of strong electron–phonon coupling in oxygen-redox Li- and Na-ion cathodes. Despite different lattice dynamics, both form stabilized oxygen-hole polarons at full charge.

title: Unmasking active sites in linker coordinated nickel–cobalt hydroxide for the electrocatalytic iodide oxidation reaction
abstract: In this study, we identified the active sites for the OER and IOR using the BZ-NiCo-H catalyst. The Ni 3+ in β-Ni(O)OH acts as active sites for the IOR, whereas Ni 4+ in γ-Ni(O)OH acts as active sites for the OER.

title: Synergistic geometric and electronic modulation in Ni–Ce layered double hydroxides for enhanced oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: Hexagonal Ni–Ce LDH nanosheets grown on stainless steel exhibit significantly enhanced OER activity arising from the synergistic effects of Ce incorporation. The optimized Ni 0.75 Ce 0.25 –LDH delivers low overpotential and outstanding durability.

title: Vapor-phase pillarization of MXenes for engineering hierarchical interlayer porosity
abstract: A facile, low-waste vapor-phase pillaring strategy enables the synthesis of silica pillared MXene with significantly enhanced surface area and hierarchical porosity.

title: Achieving self-supported nanoporous Cu catalysts with good conductivity for hydrogen evolution via dealloying amorphous ribbons
abstract: This work reports the fabrication of nanoporous Cu–Au self-supporting catalysts via dealloying, achieving balanced HER performance/cost and providing insights into amorphous catalyst design for hydrogen production.

title: Hierarchically structured embossed current collectors with multi-primer carbon layers for high-performance catholyte-based solid-state lithium-ion batteries
abstract: A catholyte all-solid-state lithium-ion battery with a plasma-treated CNT/graphene bilayer on aluminum improves interfacial adhesion and charge transport, enabling safer polymer cells.

title: A low-cost, scalable aerogel evaporator via machine learning-assisted steel needle templating for solar desalination
abstract: This study presents a low-cost, scalable needle-templated method to fabricate a D1.4N8-GSA evaporator with vertical channels, achieving high evaporation efficiency and rate, superior to evaporators fabricated by unidirectional freezing.

title: Bulk Ca-doping-induced surface modification enabling high-performance O3-type layered cathodes for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: Optimized synthesis protocol can realize bulk Ca-doping and surface Ca-protecting layer for NFM layered cathode, which synergistically improves both electrochemical cyclability and air storage stability.

title: Nickel-based catalysts for plastic pyrolysis: advances in product selectivity, yield optimization, and emerging pathways for thermochemical recycling
abstract: Recent advances in nickel-based catalysts for plastic pyrolysis, emphasizing reaction mechanisms, catalyst design, and emerging innovations that enhance product selectivity, yield efficiency, and sustainable polymer recycling.

title: The spin thermoelectric effect in nonmagnetic chiral materials
abstract: p–n junctions are commonly applied in thermoelectric cooling devices based on the Peltier effect (top). Here in addition to the Peltier effect chiral layer is added that enhance the thermoelectric effect by ordering the electronics spin (bottom).

title: Carrier dynamics tailored by electron/hole trapping domains in asymmetric graphitic carbon nitride for superior photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: DFT-guided supramolecular self-assembly produced curled g-C 3 N 4 nanobelts with electron/hole trapping domains. Their synergy in promoting Frenkel exciton dissociation kinetics and mechanisms for enhanced photocatalytic H 2 production are revealed.

title: Design and synthesis of a heteroatom-rich Ni-MOF with a rare bis(µ-aqua) core for polarity-mediated heterogeneous catalysis
abstract: In this work, a new robust Ni-based metal-organic framework (MOF), Ni-MOF, has been synthesized using an oxygen-bearing tetrapodal pyridyl ligand, tetrakis(4-pyridyloxymethylene)methane (4-TPOM), and a polar angular dicarboxylate linker, dibenzothiophene-5,5′-dioxide-3,7-dicarboxylate (3,7-DBTDC...

title: Recent advances in the application of transition metal-based catalysts in electrocatalytic ammonia synthesis and C–N bond formation
abstract: This review outlines the progress and challenges of transition metal electrocatalysts in C–N coupling from N-sources like N 2 and NO x − , emphasizing the need for mechanistic studies and improved stability.

title: Flexible redox active porous organic polymer gels via noncovalent assembly for high energy supercapacitors
abstract: A phytic acid-mediated flexible POP hydrogel transforms insoluble porous organic polymers into processable and electroactive electrodes for high-performance flexible supercapacitors.

title: Durable alkaline seawater oxidation electrocatalysis over NiFe layered double hydroxide nanosheet arrays enabled by 2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic acid functionalization
abstract: 2,5-Dihydroxyterephthalic acid (DHTA) modifies NiFe layered double hydroxide nanosheets on Ni foam. NiFe LDH@DHTA/NF achieves an industrial-scale j of 1000 mA cm −2 with an overpotential of only 370 mV and maintains stable electrolysis for 900 h.

title: Efficient ammonia synthesis via electrocatalytic nitrate reduction over a [8 + 2]-connected three-dimensional metal-bipyridine covalent organic framework
abstract: TU-82, a 3D [8 + 2]-connected bcu COF, was post-synthetically metalated to yield TU-82-M ( M = Fe, Cu). TU-82-Fe shows outstanding activity and selectivity for electrocatalytic nitrate (NO 3 − ) reduction to ammonia via atomically dispersed Fe sites.

title: Pd composition and dispersion control selectivity in photocatalytic methane oxidation
abstract: Pd-modified brookite TiO 2 nanorods enable selective methane-to-methanol conversion via tunable Pd oxidation states and charge carrier dynamics.

title: Dual-amide strategy for quaternary aqueous electrolytes
abstract: A dual-amide electrolyte system overcomes the trade-off between interfacial stability and ionic mobility in aqueous batteries. Synergistic solvation control via acetamide and caprolactam enables long-term cycling with minimal capacity fade.

title: A Synergistic Approach Combining Cu Doping and CuO/C Intertwined Network for the Na4Fe3(PO4)2P2O7 Cathode: Enhancing High-Rate Performance and Structural Robustness
abstract: Na4Fe3(PO4)2P2O7 (NFPP) has emerged as a highly promising cathode material for sodium-ion batteries owing to its high theoretical capacity and stable three-dimensional framework structure. However, its poor intrinsic electronic conductivity,...

title: Insight into a robust graphdiyne@carbon nanotube electrode for rapid lithium-ion storage
abstract: Rigid graphdiyne@carbon nanotube (GDY@CNT) electrode is developed via a mild and sustainable approach, and demonstrates high reversible capacity, excellent rate capability, and long-term cyclability.

title: Taming *OH intermediates via defect engineering to suppress oxygen evolution for efficient glycerol electrooxidation-coupled hydrogen production
abstract: A Dy/S co-doping strategy for the bimetallic oxide, NiMoO 4 , is proposed, where the 4f-3p-3d gradient orbital coupling is further enhanced by the introduction of lattice S vacancies via Ar plasma etching.

title: Insights into the selective hydrogenation of cinnamaldehyde on low-loading Pd based catalysts
abstract: This study explores how catalysts containing a low palladium loading (0.5 wt%) contribute to the selective hydrogenation of cinnamaldehyde.

title: Asymmetric Coupling Induces Spin-Flipping in NiFe-MOF Cocatalyst for Boosting BiVO 4 Photoanode Water Oxidation
abstract: Water splitting via photoelectrochemical (PEC) system is a promising approach for sustainable hydrogen production. Yet, photogenerated charge accumulation and recombination impede it by water oxidation kinetics due to not only...

title: Gradient-wettability oxide-Pt/C electrocatalysts for stable seawater hydrogen evolution via superaerophobicity and surface acidity
abstract: We develop gradient-wettability oxide–Pt/C electrocatalysts (Pt 5 /C/oxide-50) that integrate hydrophilic oxides with hydrophobic Pt/C, enabling stable seawater hydrogen evolution via superaerophobicity and surface acidity.

title: Synergistic upper critical solution temperature–lower critical solution temperature microgel–hydrogel composites for bidirectional thermoresponsive smart windows with superior optical modulation and dimensional stability
abstract: This purely polymeric, dimensionally stable microgel–hydrogel synergizes UCST–LCST for tristate switching without shrinkage. It delivers exceptional transparency and solar modulation for buildings and greenhouses.

title: Shape-change programming of zwitterionic hydrogels via chemical gradients directed by surface energy
abstract: We present a method to induce anisotropic swelling in pH-responsive hydrogels through polarity differences between zwitterionic and non-zwitterionic monomers. This results in chemical gradients that enable controlled deformations upon pH change.

title: Unveiling the distinct effect mechanisms of H 2 O: aggravating and mitigating SO 2 poisoning of Fe 2 O 3 and α-MnO 2 catalysts in low-temperature NH 3 -SCR
abstract: Elucidating the mechanisms of coexisting SO 2 and H 2 O, especially H 2 O's dual function in aggravating or mitigating SO 2 poisoning pathways, is pivotal for the rational design of high-performance, poison-resistant low-temperature SCR catalysts.

title: Proton exchange membranes with perfluorobenzenesulfonic acid groups for vanadium redox flow battery applications
abstract: Poly(arylene perfluorophenylsulfonic acid)s deliver outstanding proton conductivity, selectivity, and long-term electrochemical stability. The findings offer clear design guidelines for durable fluorine-lean high-performance flow battery membranes.

title: Decoupling the capacity fade contributions in polymer electrolyte-based high-voltage solid-state batteries
abstract: IR growth-driven cumulative capacity fade is decoupled from kinetics-driven capacity fade of PE-based-SSBs to reveal a dominant role of the latter.

title: High thermoelectric performance in Bi 2 Se 2 S compounds via multi-element doping using a double-halide perovskite
abstract: Cs 3 Bi 2 I 6 Cl 3 doping forms a solid solution in Bi 2 Se 2 S, boosting carrier concentration. Meanwhile, multi-scale structural defects intensify full-spectrum phonon scattering, lowering thermal conductivity and yielding a peak ZT of 0.9.

title: Carboxylated MWCNT-induced mesoporous Fe 3 O 4 constructs hierarchical heterointerface aerogels for ultra-high-efficiency microwave–terahertz broadband absorption
abstract: Inadequate interfacial polarization relaxation and insufficient synergy between magnetic and dielectric loss mechanisms constitute a major bottleneck impeding the advancement of wave-absorbing materials.

title: Phosphate buffer-induced depletion of nickel redox sites limits oxygen evolution on stainless steel anodes
abstract: Phosphate buffers selectively dissolve nickel to a depth of approximately 4–6 monolayers on 304 stainless steel anodes, depleting nickel redox-active sites and irreversibly suppressing OER, unlike KOH, which forms a nickel-rich active layer.

title: Leveraging battery performance through mechanically interlocked polymers
abstract: Mechanically interlocked polymers possess significant potential as advanced battery materials for enhanced battery performance, including as electrolytes, and as electrode binders and electrode coatings. We discuss the interface of research between batteries and mechanically interlocked polymers.

title: Sustainable lignin-based ionic hydrogel for high-performance moisture-induced electricity generation and self-powered wearable sensing
abstract: Sustainable lignin-ionic hydrogel rapidly synthesized (10 min) enables dual functionality: high-sensitivity strain sensing (GF 15.2) and high-output moisture-electric generation (1.71 V, 2.5 mA) for self-powered wearable applications.

title: Built-in electric field driven S-scheme Bi 2 O 2 CO 3 /Bi 5 O 7 Br heterojunction for superior photocatalytic mineralization of polystyrene microplastics
abstract: The Bi 2 O 2 CO 3 /Bi 5 O 7 Br S-scheme heterojunction was prepared, which improved the mobility of photogenerated charges and effectively degraded polystyrene microplastics.

title: Solvent effect on the electrocatalytic nitrogen reduction reaction: a deep potential molecular dynamics simulation with enhanced sampling for the case of the ruthenium single atom catalyst
abstract: Deep potential molecular dynamics simulations with enhanced sampling were employed to investigate the solvent effect on the electrocatalytic NRR and obtained the energy barrier of the rate-determining step consistent with experiments.

title: Mechanistic insights into superionic conduction via homo-interface design for next generation semiconductor ionic fuel cells
abstract: A homo-interface engineered by Co 2+ surface loading on CeO 2 creates a strong built-in electric field and interconnected oxygen-vacancy channels, enabling superionic conduction with low activation energy in low-temperature SIMFCs.

title: Tailoring molecular orientation to enhance photocurrent and fill factor in green-solvent processed organic solar cells
abstract: This study provides a framework showing that in thin films, “green” solvents affect molecular orientation, crystallization, and thus device properties. This effect increases with thickness, while additives can restore the preferred orientation.

title: Impurity band engineering and hierarchical defect scattering enable high zT in Co-doped AgSbTe 2
abstract: Co doping suppresses Ag 2 Te formation in AgSbTe 2 and creates an impurity band above the valence edge. Besides, multiscale defects enhance phonon scattering, reduce thermal conductivity, and deliver a peak figure of merit of 1.6 at 648 K.

title: Role of multi-scale interfacial modulation in CO 2 electroreduction to C 2+ products over copper-based catalysts
abstract: A systematic summary of multi-scale interface engineering strategies, from the spatial microenvironment to catalyst surface, for enhancing C 2+ selectivity in electrochemical CO 2 reduction over Cu-based catalysts.

title: Synergistic interface and entropy engineering in ferrite/nitride heterogeneous high-entropy composites for broadband microwave absorption
abstract: Synergistic entropy and interface engineering are applied to design ferrite/nitride high-entropy composites, achieving remarkable absorption (−62.38 dB RLmin, 6.72 GHz EAB) and an ultra-wide 13.81 GHz bandwidth in a gradient metamaterial.

title: Stable silica-modified zirconia with tunable acidity for enhanced catalytic transfer hydrogenation of levulinic acid and esters to γ-valerolactone
abstract: Controlled silica loading reduces zirconia particle size and tunes the Brønsted-to-Lewis acid balance, giving an optimum at intermediate SiO 2 content for efficient, hydrogen-free γ-valerolactone production via transfer hydrogenation.

title: Efficient photocatalytic synthesis of quinazolinones using nitrogen-modulated benzothiadiazole-based covalent organic frameworks
abstract: Three benzothiadiazole-based D–A COFs were synthesized by modulating the position of the nitrogen atoms, in which HIAM-0201 exhibits the best photocatalytic performance due to its strong D–A interaction and low exciton binding energy.

title: Boosting water oxidation on Cu 2 V 2 O 7 using atomic-scale frustrated Lewis pairs
abstract: Atomic-scale frustrated Lewis pairs were constructed on Cu 2 V 2 O 7 to synergistically promote O–H bond cleavage, while simultaneously optimizing the proton adsorption/desorption energy barriers at VO 4 tetrahedral active sites.

title: Post-polymerization modification towards polymer-supported metalloporphyrins for heterogeneous electrocatalysis
abstract: A one-pot synthesis of polymer-supported metalloporphyrin catalysts is demonstrated. The random-coil polymer chain allows coordination of axial ligands and distal groups to the active centre, thereby enhancing electrochemical catalysis performance.

title: Separating critical elements from NdFeB magnets with aminophosphonic acid functionalised 3D printed filters and their detailed structural characterisation
abstract: The combination of 3D-printed filter technology and methanesulfonic acid enables efficient recovery of rare earths from NdFeB magnets, yielding a 99.4 ± 0.4% pure rare-earth fraction and four fractions of remaining elements.

title: Water-based inks for inkjet-printed Cu(In, Ga)(S, Se) 2 thin film solar cells with a power conversion efficiency of 15%
abstract: As a highly promising on-demand manufacturing technology, inkjet printing offers a viable pathway for the cost-effective and large-scale production of thin-film solar cells.

title: Broadening the horizons of Fe-metal in static batteries with anion exchange membranes
abstract: An anion exchange membrane enables an efficient operation of an iron-based batteries.

title: Interface-engineered silicon nano-quill electrodes for durable extreme temperature lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Thermally robust silicon nano-quills (SiNQs) enable safe, high-current battery operation at 100 °C over prolonged cycling.

title: Lotus-root-fiber inspired stretchable and self-healing ionic thermoelectric gels with carbon nanotubes for enhanced high-temperature performance
abstract: MWCNTs are incorporated into the i-TE gel G-FeCN 4−/3− to simultaneously enhance stretchability, self-healing capacity, and i-TE performance with a P max /(Δ T ) 2 of 1.7 mW m −2 K −2 and S i of 11 mV K − 1 at 313 K.

title: Cascade transformed MOF-derived CoFeS@NiV-LDH flower-on-leaf heterostructures for binder-free hybrid supercapacitors
abstract: Metal–organic frameworks (MOFs) are promising materials for electrochemical energy storage due to their high surface areas and tunable porosity, but poor conductivity and inaccessible pores often limit their overall performance.

title: Self-supported ruthenium-doped cobalt oxide for high current density anion exchange membrane water electrolysis
abstract: Despite achievements in catalyst developments for lab-scale anion exchange membrane water electrolysis (AEMWE), industrial demands necessitate robust, high-activity electrocatalysts.

title: Bottom-up redox state engineering of Ni/GDC thin-film anodes for carbon tolerant syngas-fueled SOFCs
abstract: Ceria species migrated onto Ni surfaces by strong metal–support interactions exhibit different Ce redox states through redox-state engineering, which modulate the reverse Boudouard reaction at the interface and thereby tune carbon coking tolerance.

title: Exploring the Electrocatalytic Oxygen Evolution/Urea Oxidation Activity of Solution-Combusted CuO/MnO2: Machine Learning Insights from OER Performance
abstract: Driven by strategic and environmental imperatives, replacing fossil fuels with hydrogen from water splitting as a suitable and promising substitute energy carrier is essential. The electrochemical oxygen evolution reaction (OER)...

title: Electrocatalytic activity of 1D/3D TiO 2 tubular layers/Ni-modified MXene microsphere heterojunction electrodes
abstract: Controlling the morphology and size of MXene-based materials through top-down, electrochemical-assisted synthesis methods is crucial for improving catalytic performance and environmental safety issues compared with traditional etching techniques.

title: Tailored hybridization of MIL-101(Cr) with graphene oxide enables enhanced hydrogen storage and delivery
abstract: The first systematic comparison of different hybridization strategies for MIL-101(Cr)/GO composites that simultaneously evaluates both gravimetric and volumetric H 2 storage and delivery performance.

title: Predictive phase formation in high-entropy diborides with first-principles calculations
abstract: Potential single-phase HEBs were identified through the evaluation of entropy forming ability (EFA), computed via high-throughput density functional theory calculations.

title: A porphyrin-based covalent organic framework with an aminoxyl radical for deep-red light photocatalysis
abstract: Under deep-red light irradiation, Por-sp 2 c-COF, a porphyrin-based covalent organic framework, significantly enhances the selective oxidation of organic sulfides by coupling with a catalytic amount of ABNO.

title: Tuning the distribution of Al atoms in the framework of beta zeolites and its impact on the alkylation of phenol
abstract: Tuning the aluminum distribution in beta zeolites to increase their performance in the alkylation between phenol and tert-butanol.

title: Novel heterocyclic solvent for high-performance lithium–metal and lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: 2-Methylfuran modulates solvation and interfacial chemistry, suppressing the polysulfide shuttle and enhancing Li–S battery performance.

title: Early intervention of thermal runaway by phloroglucinol-releasing microcapsules enables safe high-energy-density lithium-ion batteries
abstract: This study develops thermoresponsive microcapsules that release phloroglucinol during early-stage thermal runaway (TR), effectively modifying reaction pathways in high-energy Li-ion batteries. The microcapsules reduce peak temperature by over 200 °C.

title: Defect-rich Ni-TDC by Fe( iii ) ions substitution at Ni2 sites for high-performance asymmetric supercapacitors
abstract: Defect-rich Ni-TDC nanoflowers by Fe( iii ) ions substitution at Ni2 sites have great potential for assembling high-performance energy storage devices.

title: Cationic defect-engineered CuMn 2 O 4 photothermal membranes to leverage interfacial solar steam generation
abstract: Defect-rich CMO NF membrane deliver broadband light absorption, enhanced hydrophilicity, and efficient photothermal conversion. It maintains structural stability and effectively purifies saline and dye-contaminated water.

title: Thermal treatment of high-density polyethylene films to increase crystallinity for the fabrication of superior radiation-grafted anion-exchange membranes for fuel cells
abstract: Heat treatment (115 °C, 24 h) of HDPE film (T) led to higher crystallinities ( cf. untreated, UT). HDPE(T) films yield radiation-grafted AEMs with more consistent ion-exchange capacities, and higher bulk water contents and fuel cell performances.

title: Trans-interfacial distribution of Se vacancies in Bi 2 Se 3 core and carbon nanofiber matrix enables long-lifespan and ultra-stable sodium storage
abstract: Se vacancies penetrated Bi 2 Se 3 nanospheres embedded in N-doped carbon nanofibers as anodes, which effectively enhanced electronic conductivity and mitigated volume strain, thereby enabling cycling stability and a long lifespan.

title: Molecular insight into visible light driven photocatalytic hydrogen peroxide synthesis using a heptazine-imide structure
abstract: The photocatalytic activity of melamine, melem, and melem-phthalimide condensed molecules for hydrogen peroxide generation under visible light in the presence of ethanol as a sacrificial material was investigated.

title: Vacancy-mediated dual-step phosphorization-sulfurization of MnMoO 4 for efficient acidic hydrogen evolution
abstract: This TOC illustrates that the synergistic incorporation of P and S modulates the electronic structure and defect landscape of hydrothermally deposited MnMoO 4 , generating a higher density of catalytically active sites.

title: Synergistic Effects of Phosphorus Doping and Radial Pores in WSe2/C Microspheres for Enhanced Room-Temperature NO2 Sensing
abstract: Room-temperature gas sensor based on two-dimensional (2D) transition metal dichalcogenides (TMDs) have been limited by insufficient gas adsorption capacity and restricted gas diffusion. Herein, a facile and general synthesis strategy...

title: Hydrogen-bonded organic supramolecular layered structure for efficient Grotthuss proton conduction in aqueous proton batteries
abstract: Carbonyl- and imine-functionalized organic molecule with a supramolecular hydrogen-bonded layered network is reported to have one of the highest proton storage capabilities to date.

title: Multifunctional Sb 2 O 3 –Bi 2 O 3 nano-leaves for record-high supercapacitor energy density and efficient HER and OER catalysis
abstract: Hierarchically porous Sb 2 O 3 –Bi 2 O 3 nano-leaves on nickel foam enable efficient energy storage and water splitting. Synergistic effects and oxygen vacancies deliver 1738 F g −1 capacitance and low overpotentials of 112 mV (HER) and 248 mV (OER).

title: Interfacial electronic modulation in Au–tungstic acid for enhanced PEC performance
abstract: Harnessing cooperative effects at semiconductor–metal interfaces offers new opportunities for efficient solar-to-chemical conversion.

title: Switching water splitting photoredox preference by geometric modulation of charge consumption sites in Bi 4 TaO 8 Cl single crystals
abstract: By modulating the ratio of oxidative facets to reductive facets of Bi 4 TaO 8 Cl single-crystal nanoplates, the relative abundance of surface sites acts as the key governor of water splitting preference: from proton reduction to water oxidation.

title: 4- tert -Butylpyridine coordination to alkylammoniums in perovskites
abstract: The mechanisms regarding 4- tert -butylpridine ( t BP) coordination to alkylammoniums via hydrogen bonding are revealed by both theoretical and experimental investigations, plugging a key knowledge gap in the research field of perovskite solar cells.

title: Synergistic spin-state modulation in Co/N co-doped TiO 2 for enhanced photoelectrocatalytic oxygen evolution under external magnetic fields
abstract: The oxygen evolution reaction (OER) remains a major kinetic bottleneck in alkaline water splitting, necessitating innovative approaches to enhance efficiency.

title: Synthesis of nonstoichiometric pseudobrookite (Fe 2− x Ti 1+ x O 5 ) from ilmenite for use in flexible microsupercapacitors
abstract: Pseudobrookite derived from ilmenite is processed into a flexible microsupercapacitor, illustrating a sustainable pathway that transforms earth-abundant minerals into wearable energy-storage technologies.

title: Synergistic 2D structure design and high-entropy engineering in MnFeCoNiCu nanoalloy/carbon nanosheet composite to optimize impedance matching for high-attenuation microwave absorption
abstract: This work synthesizes MnFeCoNiCu high-entropy nanoalloy/carbon sheet composites via self-foaming and carbothermal shock for microwave absorption. The composite exhibits excellent impedance matching and a minimum reflection loss of −54.4 dB.

title: Ultrafast plasmonic hot-hole transfer mediated efficient charge separation in a Cu 2− x S/CdZnS heterostructure
abstract: Ultrafast plasmon-generated hot-hole transfer from Cu 2− x S to CdZnS drives efficient charge separation and hot-carrier utilization, enhancing photoconductivity for next-generation optoelectronic devices.

title: Solid-state synthesis of efficient supported iridium catalysts for the acidic oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: This work presents a solid-state synthesis method for highly active and durable supported Ir catalysts for the acidic OER, with particular focus on the synthesis mechanism and structural evolution during stability tests.

title: Enhanced photocatalytic hydrogen peroxide production over g-C 3 N 4 /MoS 2 composites through phase-interface engineering: electron transfer and synergy mechanism
abstract: Synergistic phase and interface engineering in g-C 3 N 4 /MoS 2 heterostructures enables high efficiency H 2 O 2 synthesis by optimizing thermodynamic driving forces and charge transfer kinetics.

title: Acetonitrile-driven structural change of Li 3 InCl 6 enables amorphization of PEO-LiTFSI for enhanced room-temperature performance
abstract: Acetonitrile-activated Li 3 InCl 6 preferentially anchors TFSI – anions, disrupting PEO crystallization and forming an amorphous Li + -transport network for fast room-temperature ion conduction in solid-state lithium batteries.

title: Bimetallic (Co/Ni, Ce) MOF decorated V 2 CT x MXene/CNT for high energy flexible zinc-ion capacitor
abstract: This study reports the design and fabrication of a flexible all-solid-state zinc-ion capacitor based on a-ternary composite, comprising V 2 CT x MXene, functionalized carbon nanotubes, and cobalt/nickel–cerium bimetallic metal–organic frameworks.

title: Water-modulated electrolyte enables the development of advanced low-temperature Zn-ion batteries
abstract: 1,3-Dioxolane/water (95 : 5, vol) is screened for 1 m Zn(BF 4 ) 2 electrolyte, which delivers robust low-temperature performance. Zn‖MgVO full cells operate at −30 °C with >90% capacity after 300 cycles at 1C and sustain >2000 cycles at 10C.

title: Lithium hydride synergy with Fe/N-co-doped porous carbon for efficient ammonia synthesis
abstract: The LiH/Fe–N–C catalyst exhibits efficient ammonia synthesis under mild conditions, highlighting the crucial role of the synergistic interaction between nitrogen vacancies and Fe–H species in promoting dinitrogen activation and hydrogenation.

title: Unlocking high-performance zinc batteries via haloacetamide-regulated nucleation and interface chemistry
abstract: Haloacetamide additives stabilize Zn anodes by regulating nucleation and suppressing zinc hydroxy sulfate formation. Hydroxide scavenging and halide-assisted reconstruction enable compact, reversible Zn deposition, improving cycling stability.

title: Slow-light enhanced photocatalytic water splitting over Cu-incorporated 3D ordered macroporous ZIF-8
abstract: Cu-doped 3DOM ZIF-8 prepared via PS/dual-solvent boosts photocatalytic H 2 ; Cu 3D-ZIF-325 is 29× pristine ZIF-8, offering a new MOF strategy.

title: Multiscale binder engineering enables high-kinetics Prussian blue analogue cathodes for aqueous Na-ion batteries
abstract: Binder effects in aqueous Na-ion batteries are probed by comparing PVDF with PTFE for NiHCF cathodes. PVDF's better adhesion and wettability optimize the mesostructure, significantly enhancing electrode kinetics versus PTFE-based counterparts.

title: Accelerating solid-state battery design: predicting ionic conductivity with machine learning potentials
abstract: Achieving high ionic conductivity in solid-state electrolytes (SSEs) is critical for next-generation batteries, yet exploring the vast chemical space is hindered by the computational expense of first-principles methods.

title: Simultaneous enhancement of power factor and suppression of thermal conductivity in bulk TlFe 1.6 Se 2 via embedded atomically thin FeSe layers
abstract: Layered TlFe 1.6 Se 2 , composed of FeSe monolayers confined within a bulk crystal, exhibits higher thermoelectric performance than bulk FeSe. Fe-vacancy ordering enhances the Seebeck coefficient, while low lattice thermal conductivity further improves ZT .

title: Precursor chemistry governing morphology and cation disorder in AgBiS 2 solar absorber for photoelectrochemical water splitting
abstract: This work demonstrates that precursor coordination asymmetry in AgBiS 2 suppresses cation disorder and establishes morphology engineering for enhancing photoelectrochemical performance via optimized one-dimensional nanostructures.

title: Concentration-tailored interphase engineering in solid-state polymer electrolytes for high-voltage lithium metal batteries
abstract: Achieving long-term stability remains a challenge for solid-state lithium metal batteries, primarily due to low ionic conductivity, narrow electrochemical stability windows and poor interfacial contact inherent in many solid polymeric systems.

title: Regulating Ni oxidation states through ruthenium incorporation in Ni based catalysts
abstract: Small variations in Ru loading on Ni nanowires control OER activity through Ru morphology. Dispersed sub-nanometric Ru clusters stabilise high-valent Ni and boost performance, whereas Ru nanoparticles leach and yield Ni-like activity.

title: Essential role of temperature-dependent band gaps in (p × n)-type transverse thermoelectrics: partial gap analysis of Re 4 Si 7
abstract: New analysis allows temperature-dependent energy gap of transverse thermoelectrics to be measured from experimental resistivity and Seebeck data.

title: Construction of a self-sacrificing selenium interfacial coating and its performance in all-solid-state lithium-ion batteries
abstract: A method of in situ selenium-induced thermal reduction is designed to address interfacial incompatibility in all-solid-state batteries by constructing an interfacial layer, which enhances cycling performance and establishes practical viability.

title: Engineering oxygen vacancies with atomically dispersed WO x : a strategy for superior CO 2 hydrogenation performance and stability on Pd/CeO 2
abstract: This study presents a catalyst design principle based on vacancy engineering that promotes CO 2 activation by precisely controlling oxygen vacancies with atomically dispersed WO x .

title: Proton coupled electron transfer mechanism in low-energy electrochemical CO 2 desorption from Tiron-mediated amine solutions
abstract: CO 2 captured by amines as bicarbonate is released via PCET. Screening two amines and three modified graphite felts shows MDEA with P-doped felt gives the best interface, speeding CO 2 release and reducing energy demand.

title: Tailored quantum dots as a multifunctional electrolyte additive for highly reversible zinc anodes in alkaline batteries
abstract: Cadmium selenide quantum dots are introduced as a multifunctional electrolyte additive to simultaneously stabilize the zinc anode and enhance reaction kinetics in alkaline zinc batteries.

title: Computational–experimental assessment of transition-metal doping of Co 3 O 4 for acidic oxygen evolution reaction with balanced activity and stability
abstract: A generalizable computational approach for identifying effective dopants of Co 3 O 4 for acidic oxygen evolution reaction (OER), with experimental validation of Cr doping with balanced activity and stability.

title: Illumination-boosted capacitance with NiFe 2 O 4 : design and performance of symmetric and asymmetric photo-powered supercapacitors with self-generated voltage
abstract: Nickel ferrite photoelectrodes facilitate efficient light-driven charge separation and storage, markedly enhancing supercapacitor performance under illumination. The optimized device exhibits increased capacitance and self-induced voltage.

title: Atomic-scale mechanisms of interphase formation at lithium–glassy sulfide electrolyte interfaces
abstract: Illustration of SEI formation and composition of sulfide glassy solid electrolytes.

title: Sustainable conductive ink for printed “greener” supercapacitors
abstract: The increase in electronic waste highlights the need to develop sustainable, bio-based, and recyclable electronic systems.

title: Rare-earth co-doping promotes high-capacity and long-cycle Ni-rich layered oxide cathodes via lattice stabilization and interface engineering
abstract: The Yb/Y rare-earth co-doping strategy stabilizes the bulk crystal structure and electrode–electrolyte interface for high-capacity and long-cycle Ni-rich layered oxide cathodes.

title: Upcycled spent hand warmers as sustainable multifunctional hosts for high-performance lithium–sulfur cells
abstract: Spent hand warmers and desulfurized sulfur powder are upcycled into multifunctional composite cathodes that integrate catalytic, chemisorptive, and conductive components, transforming waste into advanced lithium–sulfur cells.

title: A transition-metal-and-solvent-free dual-graphite battery
abstract: Molten salt dual-graphite batteries eliminate the dependence on cathodic transition metal ions and volatile and flammmable organic solvents to enable sustainable and safer energy storage.

title: Fe doping-regulated electron transfer and Co d-band center: boosting ORR/OER catalysis of Co@NCNTs for stable zinc–air batteries
abstract: Fe–Co@NCNTs-5 self-supporting electrodes for liquid and flexible Zn–air batteries.

title: Bis-α,ω-bisacylphosphane Oxides: Simple Access to Crosslinked Polymers with Tunable Properties
abstract: We present an efficient one-pot synthesis of bis-α,ω-bisacylphosphine oxides (Bis-BAPOs) obtained by coupling α,ωdibromoalkanes with sodium bis(mesitoyl)-phosphide. The resulting tetrafunctional photoinitiators display absorption characteristics similar to the parent BAPO, yet...

title: Investigating Ti–Nb alloys as supports for iridium oxide water oxidation electrocatalysts
abstract: This study elucidates the role of support electronic conductivity in Ti–Nb alloys in governing the oxygen evolution reaction activity of IrO x within a thin-film model system.

title: Green flower-like Bi 2 WO 6 supported on chitosan beads: a powerful and sustainable visible-light-driven water treatment photocatalyst
abstract: Water pollution from organic dyes, antibiotics, and other chemicals poses a significant threat to water quality and availability.

title: Flexible energy: energy harvesting and storage with liquid metals
abstract: This review charts how liquid metals are revolutionizing flexible energy technology, enabling conformal power sources for wearable electronics, soft robotics, and autonomous systems.

title: A benzothiadiazole-vinyl-linked POSS porous hybrid polymer enables photocatalytic oxidative coupling of amines in air
abstract: Constructing a photoactive benzothiadiazole-vinyl-linked POSS-based porous hybrid polymer for boosting visible-light-driven solvent-free photocatalytic oxidative coupling of amines in air.

title: “Rock-solid” CoB-based sponge electrodes for overall seawater splitting at industrial-level current density
abstract: The “rock-solid” CoB-based sponge electrodes were achieved via a one-step mild electroless plating method. Combing with the AEMWE, it operates for more than 600 h at an current density of 500 mA cm −2 .

title: B-site Ni-doping and MgO/TiO2 modified CaMnO3-δ for thermal energy storage
abstract: Thermochemical energy storage (TCES) is recognized as one of the most promising energy storage technologies to date, capable of mitigating the spatiotemporal mismatch in the utilization of solar energy. Calcium-based...

title: Photothermal-Stable Flexible Perovskite Solar Cells Enabled by PbI2 Suppression with Amino-Functionalized Graphene Quantum Dots
abstract: The upper interface of inverted flexible perovskite solar cells (FPSCs) is prone to degradation under photothermal stress, leading to the formation of lead iodide (PbI2) that severely compromises both device...

title: S-scheme Zn 2 In 2 S 5 /CdS nanosheet-on-nanosheet heterostructures with tight interfacial contacts promote efficient H 2 O 2 photoproduction from pure water
abstract: Photocatalytic hydrogen peroxide (H 2 O 2 ) production from pure water emerges as an innovative green strategy, utilizing sunlight and water to produce a valuable chemical in an environmentally benign manner.

title: Inducing antiferromagnetism in a vinylene-linked 2D covalent organic framework via metal complex anchoring
abstract: An Ir( iii ) complex was post-synthetically anchored onto a vinylene-linked covalent organic framework, which exhibits antiferromagnetic properties, offering a new route toward magnetic induction in COFs.

title: Probing Ag Filamentary Networks in a Neuromorphic Device using Surface-Enhanced Raman Scattering
abstract: For progress in artificial intelligence (AI), the way ahead involves the design of neuromorphic devices that combine low energy consumption with cost-effectiveness and sustainability. Among emerging neuromorphic systems, silver (Ag)-based...

title: Revealing the direct role of cobalt in oxygen evolution reaction initiation over high-performance iridium-cobalt oxide catalysts
abstract: Cobalt ions and oxygen vacancies in iridium oxide induce charge redistribution, enabling cobalt to promote water dissociation and facilitating O–O intermediate coupling, thus stabilizing the oxidation pathway mechanism in acidic media.

title: Site-Specific Fluorination on Donor–Acceptor Polymers Enhances Intramolecular Charge Transfer for Photocatalytic Hydrogen Evolution
abstract: The development of polymer photocatalysts remains a central challenge in achieving efficient solar-to-fuel conversion. A key limitation lies in the use of weak donor units, which hampers the optimization of...

title: Scandium-stabilized zirconia electrolyte for intermediate-temperature solid oxide fuel cells: phase stability regulation, bilayer/composite structure design and machine learning-guided optimization strategies
abstract: Scandium-stabilized zirconia offers significantly higher ionic conductivity than conventional yttria-stabilized zirconia in the 600–800 °C range, making it an excellent electrolyte candidate for intermediate-temperature solid oxide fuel cells.

title: In situ / operando characterization of MXene electrodes for energy storage applications
abstract: Importance of in situ/operando characterization techniques.

title: Vacancy-rich Ti 3 C 2 O x (MXene)-anchored Co nanocluster catalyst enhances the selective hydrogenation of cinnamaldehyde
abstract: A vacancy-rich MXene modification-induced electronic effect improves the catalytic properties of Co nanoclusters, reaching a superior activity at low metal loading with 23.6 h −1 TOF and 65.5% selectivity to CO in cinnamaldehyde hydrogenation.

title: Amplified emission from halide perovskite quantum dots by exciton–plasmon-coupled energy transfer in the neutral and trion states on gold nanoparticles
abstract: Exciton–plasmon coupled enhanced absorption and emission rates, and photoluminescence intensity from halide perovskite quantum dots for high-performance solar cells, light-emitting diodes, lasers, and photocatalysts.

title: Curvature as the missing descriptor of sodium storage in hard carbon
abstract: Through operando / ex situ methods and theoretical calculations, this work reinterprets the data, proves intercalation along the whole voltage profile, and identifies curvature as a very important descriptor for sodium storage in hard carbon.

title: A comprehensive and critical review on the application of conductive polymers as coatings for aqueous zinc-ion battery positive electrode materials
abstract: This review provides a detailed assessment of the incorporation of conductive polymers in common A-ZIB positive electrode materials, drawing attention to potential misinterpretations and offering strategies for good experimental practices.

title: Engineering multiwalled carbon nanotube modified titanium carbide MXene nanocomposites for flexible symmetric supercapacitors in printed electronics
abstract: MXene-based micro-supercapacitors (MSCs) are promising power sources for wearable electronics and distributed IoT systems, yet their scalability is limited by MXene restacking and complex fabrication protocols.

title: Enhancement of the thermoelectric performance via defect formation and device fabrication for Cu 26 Ti 2 (Sb,Ge) 6 S 32 colusite
abstract: Sulphur deficiency in colusite results in the formation of interstitial defects within the sphalerite-like framework, thereby leading to enhanced thermoelectric performance.

title: Atomically dispersed Pt catalyst on ceria-carbon for suppressing C–C cleavage in glycerol electrooxidation
abstract: Atomically dispersed Pt on defect-rich ceria-carbon enables highly selective glycerol electrooxidation toward C3 products, particularly glycerate. It favors single-carbon adsorption, suppressing C–C cleavage.

title: Application of multi-task learning in analysing the methane working capacity of metal–organic frameworks
abstract: The interpretable multi-task learning framework predicts methane adsorption and working capacity under multiple pressure conditions, revealing pressure-dependent structure–performance mechanisms in metal–organic frameworks.

title: Molecular scissoring strategy to modify band gap and molecular motion for high-performance solar desalination and water purification
abstract: A “molecular scissoring” strategy prepares IEIC-4F with enhanced photothermal performance. Integrated with a hierarchical microporous sponge, it forms a self-floating evaporator for seawater desalination and photocatalytic water purification.

title: Digital and experimental design of CO 2 -responsive polymers based on acrylamide monomers for carbon capture
abstract: his study provides a comprehensive evaluation of the CO 2 capture performance of the new developed CO 2 -responsive polymers with a particular emphasis on their integration into membrane adsorbers for direct air capture (DAC) applications.

title: Plasmon-induced charge separation and accumulation in Ag 2 S/Cu 2− x S S-scheme junction for broad-spectrum photothermal-assisted photocatalysis
abstract: A plasmon-mediated S-scheme junction is demonstrated in Ag 2 S/Cu 2− x S nanotubes, which show efficient photocatalytic hydrogen generation driven by enhanced light absorption, notable photothermal conversion, and ultrafast charge separation.

title: Synergistic oxidation-induced exsolution–reconstruction enables heterostructured Cu/Zn hollow fibers for high current density syngas electrosynthesis
abstract: A heterostructured Cu/Zn hollow fiber gas-penetrable electrode is developed through a synergistic oxidation-induced exsolution–reconstruction strategy, exhibiting a high current density and stability for electrocatalytic CO 2 reduction to syngas.

title: Molecular linker engineering CdS/Cu 2 ZnSn(S,Se) 4 interface for efficient photovoltaics
abstract: A universal molecular linker strategy enables the formation of high-quality CZTSSe/CdS heterojunctions by simultaneously controlling CdS nucleation and passivating CZTSSe surface defects, thereby enhancing photovoltaic efficiency.

title: Asymmetric Fe single atom on BN for boosted nitrate electroreduction to ammonia
abstract: Asymmetric FeN 2 O 2 coordination on boron nitride precisely regulating adsorption energies of NO x reduction intermediates to boost nitrate-to ammonia electrocatalysis.

title: Tetrafluoroethylene telomers as electrolyte additives for high-performance, radiation-tolerant lithium and potassium batteries
abstract: γ-Radiation-synthesized tetrafluoroethylene telomers serve as a multifunctional electrolyte additive, enhancing electrochemical performance across multiple battery cathodes and enhancing their radiation resilience.

title: Fabrication and enhanced photocatalytic properties of dual S-scheme heterojunction TiO 2 /MoS 2 /C 3 N 4 nanotube arrays
abstract: A dual S-scheme heterojunction TiO2/MoS2/C3N4 photocatalyst for organic contaminant degradation applications.

title: Solid-state NMR reveals mixed side-chain organization across pores in amphiphilic covalent organic frameworks
