abstract: 2D 1 H– 1 H spin diffusion experiments in solid state NMR spectroscopy revealed direct insights into side chain arrangement across pores.

title: Macroscopical graphyne-based membranes and thin films: design, preparation and application
abstract: This review emphasizes the controlled design, interfacial interactions, and co-assembly fabrication of macroscopic graphyne-based membranes and thin films. It also discusses their applications and outlines future challenges in this field.

title: Integrated multiscale structural engineering of Fe–N–C electrocatalysts and device components for high-performance PEMFCs
abstract: High-performance PEMFCs with Fe–N–C catalysts are achieved by multiscale structural engineering and component design. Concave curvature enhances Fe–N 4 activity, while mesopores and a hydrophobic metal foam flow field improve mass transport.

title: Size-engineered cMOF/o-Cu 2 O heterostructures activate the *CHO selectivity switch for the C2 versus C1 pathway during CO 2 electroreduction
abstract: An innovative “advanced *CHO selectivity switch control” mechanism was achieved by engineering the size of the cMOF/o-Cu 2 O heterostructures. This strategy enables precise modulation of the CO 2 RR pathway.

title: In situ FTIR probe via CO electrooxidation: Pt-skin vs. Pt-skeleton surfaces
abstract: Alloys of Pt with Ni and Co form ordered and catalytically active ‘Pt-skin’ surfaces with uniquely tuned electronic character when subject to thermal annealing.

title: Multiscale structural regulation enables ultra-rapid and stable high-temperature sensing in fergusonite ceramics
abstract: Ta doping in CaCe 2 Nb 2− x Ta x MoO 12 ceramics induces CeO 2 formation and structural changes (strain, distortion), resulting in enhanced temperature sensing performance (linearity, sensitivity, stability, response time) across a wide range.

title: Room-temperature electron-selective passivating contact with titanium–peroxo complexes for high-efficiency silicon solar cells
abstract: An annealing-free electron-selective passivating contact for n-type c-Si solar cells enabled by titanium–peroxo complexes with a champion PCE of 21.9%.

title: 2D and 3D organic/inorganic hybrid perovskites for electrochemical energy storage applications
abstract: Organic/inorganic hybrid perovskites show great promise for Li-ion batteries. Cl-doped 3D hybrids achieved superior cycling stability, while 2D hybrids exhibited capacities over 1000 mA h g −1 , the highest among perovskite-based anodes.

title: Correction: High-entropy engineering tuning electrochemical activity and stability of a layered double perovskite oxygen electrode for high-performance protonic ceramic cells
abstract: Correction for ‘High-entropy engineering tuning electrochemical activity and stability of a layered double perovskite oxygen electrode for high-performance protonic ceramic cellsʼ by Zhipeng Sun et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2026, 14 , 4124–4135, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TA06570D.

title: Elastic phase-change fibers with thermal energy storage based on a tunable TPU aerogel network constructed via a universal ambient pressure drying strategy
abstract: A universal solvent replacement-drying strategy enables scalable production of highly porous and elastic TPU aerogel fibers, serving as skeletons for loading phase-change materials to produce phase-change fibers with thermal energy storage.

title: Iridium nanospheres with high active site density as efficient OER electrocatalysts for proton exchange membrane water electrolyzers
abstract: Porous Ir NS provides abundant Ir sites and exposed IrO x active centers, which lower the catalytic energy barrier and promote oxygen desorption, thus improving catalytic activity and stability.

title: Synergistic V/S co-doping induces dual-site activation and superhydrophilicity in BiOI for enhanced photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: V/S co-doping endows BiOI with dual-site activation and superhydrophilicity, enabling efficient visible-light-driven H 2 evolution of 1260.8 µmol h −1 .

title: Redox-mediated origin of Fe–Ni synergy in oxygen evolution catalysis
abstract: Solution-phase Fe–triethanolamine complexes facilitate Ni oxidation toward catalytically active NiOOH phases, enhancing oxygen evolution through redox-mediated activation pathways.

title: Enhancing hydrogen spillover and oxygen vacancies in CdZrO x solid solution catalysts via the synergistic effect of Cu and Ga for efficient CO 2 hydrogenation to methanol
abstract: In this study, a solid solution catalyst with dual H 2 activation sites of Cu and Ga was constructed, and the enhanced hydrogen spillover effect and oxygen vacancy concentration significantly improved the methanol synthesis performance.

title: Bridging structure and dynamics: mechanistic insights into CMK-3 incorporated amide–hydride composite systems for solid-state hydrogen storage
abstract: Quasielastic neutron scattering (QENS) shows that mesoporous carbon reduces BH 4 − reorientational energy barriers, enhancing local dynamics and improving hydrogen absorption/desorption kinetics.

title: Sustainable catalytic route to limonene from renewable 1,8-cineole: Fe–Al 2 O 3 driven conversion and process optimisation
abstract: Fe–Al 2 O 3 enables the first highly selective vapour-phase conversion of renewable 1,8-cineole to limonene through tunable acid–redox surface sites.

title: Structural origins of photocatalytic properties in Ruddlesden–Popper Sr n +1 Ti n O 3 n +1 ( n = 1, 2) and their topochemically fluorinated phases Sr n +1 Ti n O (3 n +1)− x F 2 x ( x ≈ n )
abstract: Topochemical fluorination and RP layering tune local Ti coordination, symmetry, and local polarity in Sr-based titanates, establishing structure–photocatalysis relationships.

title: Amine-silane-induced Ag–Cu dendrites on Al/ZnO: an integrated SERS monitoring and photodegradation platform for triphenylmethane dyes
abstract: Using APTES as inducer and coupling agent, a recyclable Al/ZnOA/Ag-Cu dendritic architecture is constructed. It shows ultra-sensitive/selective SERS and excellent photocatalytic performance for triphenylmethane dyes.

title: In situ ligand stripping of CeO 2 nanocrystals in anion exchange membranes for enhanced water electrolysis
abstract: In situ ligand stripping of uniformly dispersed CeO 2 nanocrystals within QSEBS creates highly conductive and durable AEMs, enabling 2.52 A cm −2 at 2.0 V in AEMWE operation.

title: Molecularly cooperative bifunctional polymers enable speciation resilient uranium removal in continuous flow wastewater systems
abstract: BA-PPSD, a bifunctional resin integrating amidoxime and pyridinium moieties, enables rapid and selective uranium sequestration across diverse speciation, achieving high capacities and reduction to potable limits with sustained regenerability.

title: Asymmetric coordination of metal–organic frameworks with enhanced metal–oxygen hybridization for Li–O 2 batteries
abstract: Ni-DABDT are synthesized as cathodes for Li–O 2 batteries to promote O 2 chemisorption and accelerate oxygen redox. The Li–O 2 batteries exhibit low overvoltage, superior rate performance and long-term stability.

title: A harsh-environment tolerant, ultra-strong yet tough bio-based adhesive via general branched-topology engineering: break adhesion-toughness conflicts
abstract: A general topological engineering strategy is proposed: resveratrol and aliphatic diglycidyl ethers form a rigid–flexible network; precise tuning of soft chain length confers strong adhesion, high toughness, and environmental adaptability.

title: Bifunctional and durable MoSe 2 –RuSe 2 heterojunction catalyst for alkaline seawater splitting
abstract: We report an in situ grown MoSe 2 –RuSe 2 heterojunction network array on nickel foam as an efficient, stable seawater electrolysis electrocatalyst.

title: Ni-catalyzed degradation of polyimide separators in quasi-solid-state Li-ion batteries
abstract: In the quasi-solid-state lithium-ion battery system, Ni dissolution and migration from the NCM811 cathode leads to the catalytic decomposition of the PI separator.

title: Mitigating volume expansion in alloy-type anodes for potassium-ion batteries: mechanistic insights and material engineering strategies
abstract: Alloy-type anodes are promising for K-ion batteries: multi-electron reactions, high capacity, suitable potentials and abundance. This review summarizes advances and synthesis, outlining a roadmap, mechanism and engineering guided future directions.

title: Impact of graphene quantum dot edge shapes on high-performance energy storage devices
abstract: Zigzag (ZZ) and armchair (AC) nitrogen-doped graphene quantum dots were synthesized hydrothermally and electrochemically. ZZ N-GQDs exhibited higher specific, pseudocapacitive, double-layer and quantum capacitances compared to their AC counterparts.

title: Engineering the linkage of mesoporous covalent organic frameworks for enhancing selective photocatalytic oxidation of organic sulfides
abstract: The cyano-vinylene-linked covalent organic framework, TFPT-sp 2 c-TCN, outshines its imine-linked analogue, TFPT–TDA, in the selective photocatalytic oxidation of organic sulfides with oxygen.

title: Facile embedding of aromatic carbon rings into the g-C 3 N 4 framework for boosting piezocatalytic water splitting
abstract: A facile aromatic carbon ring embedding strategy significantly enhances the piezocatalytic H 2 evolution of g-C 3 N 4 by strengthening dipole moment, π-conjugation and charge separation.

title: Seed crystal phase governs interfacial coherence and strain in Janus Cu 2− x S/CdS heteronanorods
abstract: Cu 2− x S seed phase serves as a structural switch in Janus Cu 2− x S/CdS heteronanorods, governing the formation of strained defective junctions versus coherent low-strain contacts for efficient charge transport and photocatalytic H 2 evolution.

title: Single-atom dispersed high-entropy alloys for nitrogen conversion: from N 2 activation to NO x reduction
abstract: This review highlights how integrating single atom catalysts with high entropy alloy matrices enables atomic level control of electronic structure, coordination and reaction energetics, thereby advancing sustainable N 2 fixation under mild conditions.

title: Retarding effect of carboxyl-containing dissolved organic matter derived from the thermochemical conversion of spent coffee grounds on formaldehyde-free water-based coatings
abstract: Retarding the effects of SCG-derived carboxylated DOM on water-based formaldehyde-free coatings.

title: Rolling-enabled high-performance free-standing Ag 2 Se/PVDF composite films for flexible thermoelectric devices
abstract: A hot-rolling process was employed to fabricate high-performance, free-standing Ag 2 Se/PVDF composite films, synergistically enhancing both thermoelectric performance and mechanical flexibility.

title: Environmentally friendly and energy-saving anode for nonferrous metal electrowinning operating through liquid–liquid phase separation
abstract: The developed Pb–Ag–Al alloy anode in this work exhibits excellent performances in synergistic reduction of harmful pollutants and energy consumption during the zinc electrowinning process.

title: Decorating Zn-MOF-74 on ZIF-8 to increase gas transport and plasticization resistance in mixed-matrix membranes
abstract: ZIF-8 nanoparticles were functionalized with Zn-MOF-74 via facile ligand exchange strategy, introducing open metal sites and adjusting pores that enhance polymer-MOF compatibility and molecular transport, improving CO 2 separation performance.

title: Zwitterion-engineered covalent organic frameworks achieving record proton conductivity for high-performance proton batteries
abstract: Zwitterion-engineered COF electrolyte achieves record proton conductivity with low acid loading, enabling high-performance proton batteries.

title: A bismuth ferrite-based lead-free piezoelectric ceramic surpassing PZT-8 in thermal stability
abstract: This work develops a defect-engineered bismuth ferrite-based lead-free ceramic, achieving industry-leading temperature stability surpassing PZT-8 for high-temperature electronics.

title: Metal–(organic cocrystal) framework with a photothermal effect boosting the photocatalytic degradation of pollutants
abstract: Ac@[Ca-NDI] MOCF combines the advantages of OCs and MOF, showing synergistic photothermal and photocatalytic effects. This MOCF demonstrates excellent phenol degradation performance and offers a new strategy for OC preparation.

title: Ce–La ratio-controlled structural transitions in Ni/Ce–La oxide catalysts for direct decomposition of methane
abstract: The Ce/La ratio controls the structure of Ni/Ce–La catalysts, determining Ni dispersion and methane activation. An optimal Ce/La ratio promotes surface Ni 0 formation and enhances methane conversion and carbon crystallinity.

title: Borate-deficient decavanadoborate additives boost Nafion membrane performance: enhanced proton conductivity and power density in humidified PEMFCs
abstract: The integration of novel fillers with defect engineering serves as an effective strategy for enhancing the performance of Nafion-based proton exchange membranes (PEMs).

title: Quantum confinement vs. mesoporosity in SnO 2 : oxygen vacancies dictate dominant formate selectivity in hybrid CO 2 electrolysis with unprecedented 480 mV energy savings
abstract: Hybrid co-electrolysis integrates CO 2 reduction with biomass oxidation for efficient formic acid production. Surface-engineered SnO 2 nanoflowers enhance charge transfer, selectivity, and sustainable carbon valorization using earth-abundant catalyst.

title: Indolo[3,2- b ]carbazole-based MOF for oxidative coupling of amines powered by blue light
abstract: Indolo[3,2- b ]carbazole is used for the first time as a photosensitizing ligand to construct a Zn-MOF, which serves as a heterogeneous catalyst for the photocatalytic coupling reaction of benzylamine under blue light with high efficiency.

title: Entropy-driven durability enhancement of PtM 3 (M = transition metal) type alloy catalysts for the oxygen reduction reaction
abstract: High-entropy design provides a new solution for balancing cost reduction and catalytic performance for low-platinum (low-Pt) alloys.

title: Data–knowledge dual-driven design of a lattice-strain-controlled L1 0 -PtNiCoFeMnCrGa/CNT multifunctional catalyst
abstract: Low-cost, high-activity multifunctional Pt electrocatalysts remain elusive. Under the guidance of a data–knowledge dual-driven approach, Ni, Co, Fe (X), Mn, Cr (Y), and Ga (Z) are quickly determined in a PtXYZ high-entropy alloy system.

title: Dynamic Cu valence state cycling regulates urea–OH − adsorption competition: electrocatalytic mechanism and application of Cu x S/FeS/NF
abstract: The 20-Cu x S/FeS/NF heterojunction catalyst with dynamic Cu valence state cycling enables low-cost and high-performance urea oxidation-assisted overall water splitting for hydrogen production and wastewater remediation.

title: WO 3 anchored Fe single-atom composite for efficient photo-Fenton degradation of organic pollutants
abstract: Fe/WO 3 single-atom catalyst shows the synergistic effect in the photo-Fenton system for tetracycline degradation.

title: The application of low-temperature processed metal oxide electron transport layers in flexible perovskite solar cells
abstract: Flexible perovskite devices have garnered significant attention due to their promising applications in wearable electronics and portable energy systems.

title: Internal stress and dislocation-mediated phase structure and conduction mechanism in flash-sintered Na 1/2 Bi 1/2 TiO 3 -based ceramics
abstract: Na 1/2 Bi 1/2 TiO 3 (NBT) is widely regarded as a multifunctional material, exhibiting dual capabilities as both an insulating piezoelectric material and an oxygen ion conductor.

title: Accelerating discovery through integration: a DFT validated machine learning framework for screening MOF photocatalysts
abstract: An ML-DFT workflow rapidly screens MOF photocatalysts for CO 2 reduction: MGT predictions pre-filter candidates, cutting compute time from weeks to hours while retaining accuracy.

title: Greener and scalable MXene fabrication enabled by supercritical CO 2 : a mini-review
abstract: Supercritical CO 2 -enabled MXene synthesis is examined via a process-knob-to-metric framework linking green metrics and scalability, guiding the design of safer, sustainable, and industrially relevant processes.

title: Intergranular to transgranular lithium filamentation in a garnet solid-state electrolyte
abstract: Conversion of inadvertent Li 2 CO 3 to LiF at grain boundaries in garnet LLZO changes the fracture mode from intergranular to predominantly transgranular while also increasing critical current densities for lithium growth-driven cell failure.

title: Unveiling the role of a trinuclear copper( ii ) cluster in bifunctional OER and HER electrocatalysis: insights from experiments and theory
abstract: A combined study of experimental and theoretical investigations of electrochemical water splitting using a novel trinuclear copper( ii ) cluster [Cu 3 (L)(OAc)(Cl) 2 ]·3H 2 O as an efficient bifunctional electrocatalyst for HER and OER has been described.

title: Structure-dependent electronic modulation of Pt on perovskite surfaces: bifunctional oxygen catalysts for rechargeable Zn–air batteries
abstract: The electronic modulation of surface platinum is facilitated by the hexagonal structure of the perovskite. This structural feature enables especially remarkable cycle stability in Zn–air batteries through a distinctive oxygen spillover pathway.

title: Compressive strain-mediated bond length tailoring in CuO reveals the bridge to efficient electrocatalytic CO 2 -to-ethylene conversion
abstract: Lattice strain from Cd doping modulates the electronic structure of Cu, enhancing selectivity toward C 2 H 4 .

title: Sb-doped BiTeI/graphene composite as an efficient electrocatalyst for the hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: Sb-doped BiTeI/graphene composites were synthesized by chemical vapor transport and mechanical stirring, which exhibited excellent HER performance due to the modulated electronic structure by Sb-doping and enhanced conductivity from graphene.

title: On-site decoking of the Ca-looping process enables 1000 cycles of stable thermochemical energy storage
abstract: O 2 is introduced for on-site regeneration of Al/Mn/Ce-co-doped CaO-looping materials . Mechanistic studies reveal that the oxygen vacancies from Ce doping in Ca-based materials facilitate the catalytic splitting CO 2 into carbon deposits.

title: Alloying-type anode materials for dual-ion batteries: an inceptive review
abstract: This article reviews the key challenges and strategies for dual-ion batteries with alloying-type anodes.

title: Novel redox-active vanadium( v )-dithiolene complexes for efficient supercapacitive energy storage: role of selenium functionalization on pseudocapacitive properties
abstract: Two redox-active vanadium( v )-dithiolene complexes were synthesized, which showed strong pseudocapacitive behavior, high charge storage and excellent cycling stability, highlighting their promise for next-generation energy storage applications.

title: Polymer chain length governs ion transport and interfacial dynamics in aqueous zinc-metal batteries
abstract: Polymer chain length governs Zn 2+ migration and interfacial adsorption. PNVP -M creates continuous Zn 2+ transport channels and forms a uniform, ion-conductive organic–inorganic SEI on Zn anode, ensuring efficient Zn 2+ migration.

title: Zinc-induced ordering in L1 0 -type platinum-based nanoalloys for the electrocatalytic oxygen reduction reaction
abstract: Introducing low-melting-point Zn to L1 0 -PtM (M = Co, Ni) boosts thermal stability by raising phase transition ( T PT ) and lowering diffusion temperatures ( T AD ). This enables highly ordered alloy nanocatalysts to form via low-temperature annealing.

title: Cation Exchange-Induced Nickel Active Sites on FeOOH Cocatalyst for Enhanced Photoelectrochemical Water Oxidation of BiVO4 Photoanode
abstract: Developing highly efficient and stable BiVO4 photoanodes facilitates high-efficiency Photoelectrochemical (PEC) water splitting, offering a promising pathway for converting solar energy into hydrogen energy. Our study focuses on the rational...

title: Unified effects of lanthanoid elements and magnetic fields on the electrocatalytic CO 2 reduction reaction
abstract: Charge transfer as the unifying principle: from lanthanoid doping to electromagnetic fields in CO 2 electroreduction.

title: Printed N'S-MXene@C/Cu micro-supercapacitors with cryo-tolerant hydrogel for wireless self-powered motion monitoring
abstract: A cryo-tolerant, self-powered sensing system integrating printed N,S-MXene@C/Cu micro-supercapacitors and PVA/LiCl–EG multifunctional hydrogel enables wireless human motion monitoring via Bluetooth.

title: Electrolyte effects on the stability and CO binding of copper chloride complexes for electrochemical separation of CO/N 2
abstract: Carbon monoxide separation from industrial waste gases could contribute largely to carbon circularity.

title: Surface engineering strategies to mitigate phosphoric acid poisoning in oxygen reduction catalysts for HT-PEMFCs
abstract: The sustainable production and efficient utilization of hydrogen are central to achieving global carbon neutrality.

title: Isomorphic transformation in undoped ZrCo hydrogen isotope storage alloy achieves high-temperature stable cycling
abstract: The cycling stability of ZrCo hydrogen isotopes storage alloy in fusion reactor is crucial for their engineering applications. In this work, we completely studied the relationship between hydrogen-induced disproportionation reaction...

title: Soft-Chemical Scalable One-pot Aqueous-medium Synthesis of Na₃(VO)₂(PO₄)₂F-Based Cathodes: Compositional Tuning and Electrochemical Performance in Na- and Li-Ion Batteries
abstract: One of the most important cathode materials for Na-ion batteries, Na3(VO)2(PO4)2F, and its compositional variants have been synthesized employing four different and facile one-step soft chemical routes. The as-synthesized compounds,...

title: Dual-Function Electrolyte Design Enabling Sulfur Redox-Mediated Charging and Anode Stabilization in Zn-Air Batteries
abstract: Rechargeable Zn-air batteries are limited by sluggish oxygen evolution reaction (OER) kinetics and Zn anode instability. Here, we report a thiourea-based electrolyte that enables sulfur redox-mediated charging while simultaneously stabilizing...

title: Aluminium siting in zeolite RTH from a combined machine learning – NMR approach
abstract: Combining NMR with machine learning opens new opportunities to address the still‑challenging elucidation of aluminum siting in zeolite frameworks.

title: Adsorption kinetics of small molecules on FePt metallic electrodes by classical molecular dynamics simulation
abstract: Adsorption-time analysis in the methanol oxidation reaction reveals the adsorption dynamics of molecular reactants.

title: Mn-Doped Cobalt Phosphide Non-Noble Catalyst: Boosting HER for Stable and High-performance Zn-CO2 system
abstract: Carbon capture, utilization, and storage (CCUS) is recognized as a critical pathway for decarbonization, but current approaches are limited by energy-intensive operation and suboptimal conversion efficiency. In this context, the...

title: Photogenerated carrier-assisted electrocatalysis: mechanistic perspectives and catalytic advances
abstract: A carrier-centered framework is proposed for photogenerated carrier-assisted electrocatalysis, linking carrier dynamics, transfer pathways and action modes to catalyst design and applications in water splitting, fuel cells and metal–air batteries.

title: Photocatalytic dehydrogenation of light alkanes to olefins: mechanistic principles and catalyst engineering
abstract: This review summarizes the fundamental mechanisms of photocatalytic C–H bond activation and recent advances in ethane-to-ethylene and propane-to-propylene conversion, with emphasis on the catalyst engineering strategies involved.

title: Supercapacitor-based CO 2 capture enhanced by electrolyte pH control
abstract: CO 2 adsorption capacity in supercapacitive swing adsorption (SSA) can be improved by using basic electrolytes.

title: A cobalt-yttria heterointerface on nitrogen- and sulphur-doped carbon as a durable electrocatalyst for metal–air batteries and overall water electrolysis
abstract: The development of an efficient, cost-effective trifunctional electrocatalyst is critical for advancing next-generation energy conversion and storage technologies.

title: Highly efficient photoreforming of plastic waste for hydrogen production: recent advances and prospects
abstract: Auxiliary methods including thermal, electric, magnetic, ultrasound, and Fenton-like assistance enhance plastic photoreforming for efficient solar-driven hydrogen and value-added chemical production.

title: Design principles for fluoroethylene carbonate additive–electrode compatibility in nanoporous sugarcane bagasse-based hard carbon sodium ion anodes
abstract: Effect of fluoroethylene carbonate (FEC) additive on sodium storage behavior in nanoporous hard carbon anodes.

title: Synergistic regulation of dual monomers for constructing high bandgap and high-polarity polyimide with high-temperature capacitive energy storage performance
abstract: Employing a dual-monomer synergistic strategy, a wide-bandgap, high- polarity polyimide is constructed. The derived film achieves a discharge energy density of 2.68 J cm −3 with >90% efficiency at 200 °C, showing superior thermal-dielectric stability.

title: Synergy of rare earth single atoms and Pt nanoclusters@N-doped carbon for improved alkaline hydrogen evolution
abstract: Purposely constructed Pt NCs -Pr SAs @NDC favors the adsorption of H ads and OH ads on Pt and Pr sites, respectively, allows 25-fold mass activity promotion at only 10% Pt loading of commercial Pt/C and stable operation at 500 mA cm −2 for 500 h in AEMWE.

title: CO 2 adsorption-induced anisotropic mechanical response in an 8-fold interpenetrated diamondoid metal–organic framework
abstract: Hybrid Monte Carlo/molecular dynamics simulations are applied to explore CO 2 -induced structural transition of a flexible MOF.

title: Role of Mo and N doping ratios in regulating the zinc storage performance of δ-MnO 2 : a systematic study from single to co-doping
abstract: Mo/N co-doping in δ-MnO 2 suppresses manganese dissolution and structural degradation, enhances H + /Zn 2+ intercalation, and improves electrochemical performance.

title: Superspreading-photoinitiated in situ construction of a hydrogel electrolyte enabling high-performance and long-cycling zinc-ion batteries
abstract: This work develops an in situ hydrogel electrolyte via superspreading-photoinitiated creating a robust Zn anode interface, suppressing dendrites and side reactions, boosting cycling stability for safe high-performance Zn-ion batteries.

title: Interface-driven construction of three-dimensional silver–carbon aerogels via microwave radiation for thermal regulation
abstract: Construction of three-dimensional nanoscale silver–carbon aerogels based on interface-driven microwave assembly for efficient thermal regulation.

title: Activating SnO x -coated Cu 2 O photocathodes for efficient photoelectrochemical CO 2 reduction and unassisted tandem device integration
abstract: The Cu 2 O photocathode, with uniformly distributed SnO x nanoparticles, enabled efficient CO 2 to formate conversion. When integrated with semitransparent BiVO 4 photoanode, the tandem device achieved concurrent formate production at both electrodes.

title: Pentavalent Ta-substitution enhances ionic conductivity and critical current density in NASICON for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: NASICON-type materials are promising electrolytes for all-solid-state sodium-ion batteries (ASSSBs); however, the role of pentavalent dopants (V 5+ , Nb 5+ and Ta 5+ ) in Na 3 Zr 2 Si 2 PO 12 (NZSP) remains insufficiently understood.

title: Four-phonon scattering and multi-valley characteristics induce high thermoelectric performance in TlAgSe: a first-principles investigation
abstract: Four-phonon scattering and multi-valley characteristics induce promising high thermoelectric performance in TlAgSe for both p-type and n-type doping.

title: Fundamental understanding of electrocatalytic ammonia oxidation to nitrogen and the design of non-noble metal-based electrocatalysts and devices
abstract: A fundamental understanding of mechanism studies, synthetic methods, and prospective applications of low-cost non-noble metal based electrocatalysts for ammonia oxidation to nitrogen.

title: The design of BaTiO 3 lead-free ceramics enabling an ultrahigh electrocaloric effect and a wide working-temperature range
abstract: We disclose a novel lead-free ceramic with a high-performance ECE. Phase coexistence, and an optimized ferroelectric domain configuration and ultra-fine grains were constructed by the {Nb 5+ , In 3+ } co-doping of a Ba 0.9 Ca 0.1 TiO 3 ceramics.

title: Ultra-strong, recyclable, self-healable and transparent polythiourethane-urea elastomer via physical–chemical dual-reversible networks synergy
abstract: A thermoset elastomer with high strength and toughness is constructed via the synergy of multiple reversible physical crosslinks and dynamic chemical crosslinks, enabling chemical recycling into re-crosslinkable materials.

title: Amplified excited-state diverse effects arising from ligand regioisomerism in Cu( i ) complexes for advanced applications
abstract: Designing earth-abundant photoactive coordination complexes that combine molecular tunability with device-relevant performance is essential to advance next-generation optoelectronic and solar-energy technologies.

title: Biomimetic inspired liquid crystal elastomers with dual-mode actuation featuring vapor self-oscillation and photothermal bending
abstract: Biomimetic liquid crystal elastomers integrating azobenzene enable vapor-driven self-oscillation and photothermal bending under orthogonal stimuli, producing blooming, crawling, and octopus-like motions for adaptive soft robotics.

title: Optimizing the piezocatalytic hydrogen production activity of metal–organic frameworks through precise defect engineering
abstract: The introduction of defects in UiO-66 MOFs improves the charge separation and polarization, leading to a stronger piezoelectric response and thus a higher piezocatalytic hydrogen generation efficiency compared with their pristine counterparts.

title: A hydroxychloride-triggered spinel composite electrode for energy-efficient and long-lasting hydrogen production from brine electrolysis
abstract: Highly oxidation-prone Co 2 (OH) 3 Cl was employed as a trigger, and combined with a stable CoFe 2 O 4 catalyst to engineer a Co 2 (OH) 3 Cl@CoFe 2 O 4 composite electrode, which efficiently drove the oxygen evolution reaction in brine electrolytes.

title: Bridging classical and quantum interpretation of chemical state analysis by XPS/HAXPES to resolve short-range order in amorphous alumina films
abstract: Combined XPS/HAXPES and atomistic simulations revealed that thermally driven bonding rearrangements are linked to the emergence of nanoscale gas transport pathways in alumina.

title: Simultaneous optimization of thermoelectric and mechanical properties in p-type NbFeSb via entropy engineering
abstract: Entropy engineering enables the synergistic enhancement of both thermoelectric and mechanical performance in NbFeSb-based half-Heusler alloys.

title: Enhancing the rate capability of Na 4 Fe 3 (PO 4 ) 2 (P 2 O 7 ) by partial co-substitution of V 3+ and Al 3+ for Fe 2+
abstract: Partial co-substitution of V 3+ and Al 3+ for Fe 2+ in NFPP lowers the band gap and Na + diffusion energy barrier of NFPP, thus effectively improving its rate capability.

title: Thermal transport in Ag 8 T S 6 ( T = Si, Ge, Sn) argyrodites: an integrated experimental, quantum-chemical, and computational modelling study
abstract: Argyrodite-type Ag-based sulfides combine exceptionally low lattice thermal and high ionic conductivity, making them promising candidates for thermoelectric and solid-state energy applications.

title: OSC-Net: a multi-fidelity machine learning model for organic solar cells
abstract: OSC-Net, a novel multi-fidelity machine learning framework, enables accurate prediction of organic solar cell (OSC) performance and accelerates OSC materials discovery.

title: A novel phosphorus–nitrogen-containing hardener toward anti-flammable, smoke-suppressed and low-dielectric epoxy thermosets
abstract: Epoxy resins suffer from poor dielectric properties and flammability. Adding the multifunctional P–N curing agent TFDP improves char formation, flame retardancy, and lowers the dielectric constant and loss.

title: Goldene monolayer as a highly effective catalyst for polysulfide anchoring and conversion
abstract: DFT calculations were performed to investigate how lithium sulfide and polysulfide clusters (Li 2 S, Li 2 S 2 , Li 2 S 4 , Li 2 S 6 , Li 2 S 8 , and S 8 ) bind to goldene.

title: Decoding physical mechanisms governing elastic moduli in inorganic materials through interpretable machine learning
abstract: Interpretable ML with 34 key features predicts shear ( G ) and bulk ( K ) moduli ( R 2 = 0.892/0.949), reveals the physical origins, and uncovers synergistic and compensatory feature interactions for rational elastic property design of inorganic materials.

title: Recent advances and design strategies on positive electrode materials for aqueous rechargeable magnesium batteries
abstract: This review summarizes positive electrode materials and design strategies for aqueous magnesium batteries, including Mn/V-based, PBAs, polyanionic, LDHs, and organic materials, for high voltage and long cycle life.

title: Rigid and electrode-compatible multicomponent organic crystals for piezoelectric energy harvesting
abstract: Piezoelectric l -argininium amidosulfonate hemihydrate, shapeable into polycrystalline discs electroded with flexible Cu and Al tapes, exhibited an output voltage of 15 V in response to mechanical stimuli in the form of finger tapping.

title: Dynamic coordination flexibility unlocks efficient electrocatalytic nitrate reduction to ammonia over molecular cobalt catalysts
abstract: Dynamic coordination endows Co–homopiperazine catalysts with adaptive structure. The non-conjugated skeleton stabilizes intermediates, boosts *NO hydrogenation, and achieves high NH 3 yield, ideal faradaic efficiency and good durability.

title: Understanding the catalytic site structure in metal poly(heptazine imide)–crystalline carbon nitride
abstract: General and efficient experimental–computational strategy to understand the catalytic site structure in metal poly(heptazine imide), such as nickel poly(heptazine imide) (Ni-PHI), for surface and single-atom catalysis (SAC).

title: Dual functions of Lewis acid–base synergy and ZnSe-CoSe 2 heterojunctions toward stable solid-state lithium-sulfur batteries
abstract: This research proposes a nitrogen-doped carbon-coated transition metal selenide heterostructure (ZnSe-CoSe 2 @NC) as an efficient catalyst to enhance bidirectional sulfur conversion in PVDF-based solid-state lithium-sulfur batteries.

title: Developing a series of calcium-doped pyrochlore iridates for the oxygen evolution reaction in PEM water electrolysis
abstract: Calcium doping of Pr 2 Ir 2 O 7 pyrochlores enhances OER mass activity while significantly suppressing iridium dissolution, leading to up to a 100% increase in the stability number under acidic OER conditions.

title: A fluorine-enriched gel electrolyte with universal compatibility for advanced lithium metal batteries
abstract: A compatible CF-GPE strategy is developed for dual battery systems. It suppresses redox mediator shuttling in Li–O 2 batteries and inhibits lithium dendrites in Li-ion batteries, enabling stable cycling and enhanced electrochemical performance.

title: Controlling nitric oxide reduction to ammonia on defective α-In 2 Se 3 via ferroelectric polarization switching
abstract: The stability, activity, and selectivity of catalysts depend on the ferroelectric polarization switching. V Se –In 2 Se 3 ↓ exhibits superior NORR catalytic performance compared to V Se –In 2 Se 3 ↑.

title: Comparison of the Arrhenius parameters between conventional hydrothermal and microwave-assisted synthesis methods for tin oxide nanoparticles
abstract: A framework involving in situ PDF analysis with Avrami and Arrhenius modeling suggests that microwave-enhanced crystallization of SnO 2 nanoparticles is better explained by an elevated apparent pre-exponential factor, not a reduced activation energy.

title: Efficient synthesis of a protic organic ionic plastic crystal as an ionic conductive matrix for energy storage
abstract: A novel and scalable synthesis route for DBUHFSI protic organic ionic plastic crystal , validated as a conductive matrix for all-solid-state batteries.

title: Unleashing and harnessing capacity performance by a diffusion dominant process of CoNi-ZIF for high energy density asymmetric supercapacitors
abstract: The figure illustrates the diffusion-dominant process in CoNi-ZIF along with capacitive/diffusion percentages during the electrochemical studies.

title: Hexaazaphenalene-based pore-space-partitioned metal–organic frameworks for enhanced CO 2 capture and photocatalytic fixation
abstract: Hexaazaphenalene-based pore-space-partitioned metal–organic frameworks show remarkable CO 2 uptake and efficient photocatalytic epoxide cycloaddition, realizing the high-performance synthesis of cyclic carbonates under ambient conditions.

title: Synergistic CuO/MgO nucleation for low-supercooling Na 2 SO 4 ·10H 2 O in battery thermal management
abstract: Flower-like CuO and porous MgO act as dual nucleation agents that suppress severe Na 2 SO 4 ·10H 2 O supercooling from 15.1 to 1.8 °C while preserving latent heat and enabling efficient 30–50 °C thermal buffering for battery thermal management.

title: Zn-anode interphase regulation by electrolyte additives in aqueous two-electron Zn–I 2 batteries
abstract: The tetramethylurea additive represents an effective strategy for tailoring a protective Zn/electrolyte interphase that suppresses Zn corrosion, thereby providing practical design principles for durable aqueous Zn–I 2 batteries.

title: Influence of sequential perovskite deposition at the self-assembled monolayer interfaces in inverted perovskite solar cells
abstract: Interfacial investigation on self-assembled monolayers in inverted perovskite solar cells depending on perovskite deposition method: one-step and two-step deposition methods, performing interfacial characterization.

title: Dual-functional perylene diimide derivative for efficient organic solar cells and interfacial solar steam generation
abstract: A dual functional perylene diimide derivatives exhibiting a remarkable efficiency of 20.25% for organic solar cells and an excellent evaporation rate 3.7 kg m −2 ·h −1 for interfacial solar steam generation.

title: Autonomous aqueous H 2 O 2 production with a carboxylate-functionalized polythiophene
abstract: A functionalized polythiophene autonomously produced ∼1 × 10 −4 mol H 2 O 2 per g polymer (18% doping level) in aqueous O 2 solutions via ORR. This work opens new applications driven by this process and elucidates degradation mechanisms in OE devices.

title: Accelerating the discovery of high-efficiency donor–acceptor pairs in organic photovoltaics via SolarPCE-Net guided screening
abstract: An asymmetric graph neural network framework (SolarPCE-Net) is introduced for organic photovoltaics, achieving improved power conversion efficiency prediction and offering chemically interpretable design insights.

title: Regulating spin polarization by diatomic doping of a NiFe layered double hydroxide to achieve efficient electrocatalytic water splitting
abstract: Synergistic spin-regulation in NiFe-LDH is achieved via spatially isolated Ce and Mn doping. Ce creates high-valent Fe 4+ , while Mn induces high-spin Fe via strain, resulting in a highly active spin-selective channel for superior water splitting.

title: Optimizing interfacial hydroxyl adsorption on an ordered Ru-Sn intermetallic compound for efficient hydrogen evolution
abstract: An ordered Ru 3 Sn 7 intermetallic compound that enables the uniform distribution of H/OH adsorption sites and prevents the coverage of Ru sites.

title: Origin of thermal induced variation in performance and negative reactance of inorganic CsPbI 2 Br perovskite solar cells
abstract: High-temperature thermal cycling reveals that negative reactance and inverted hysteresis are linked to anisotropic lattice expansion and halogen migration.

title: High-entropy engineering tuning electrochemical activity and stability of a layered double perovskite oxygen electrode for high-performance protonic ceramic cells
abstract: A high-entropy perovskite Pr 0.2 La 0.2 Sm 0.2 Nd 0.2 Gd 0.2 BaCo 2 O 5+ δ is developed as a high-performance oxygen electrode material for PCCs with enhanced proton uptake capacity, higher concentration of oxygen vacancies, and improved thermal stability.

title: Regulation of interfacial stability and lithium-ion transport in LiMn 0.6 Fe 0.4 PO 4 cathodes via a boroxine-based electrolyte additive
abstract: A boroxine-based electrolyte additive constructs B–O and Li–F functionalized interphases on high-voltage LMFP cathodes, regulating interfacial reactions and facilitating Li + transportation for prolonged cycling.

title: Intermetallic synergy between CeO x –Co accelerates the selective production of p -xylene from PET plastic waste over core–shell type cobalt phyllosilicate catalysts
abstract: CeO x modification of Co-PS improves Co 0 exsolution and establishes strong CeO x –Co interfacial sites, enhancing surface acidity and hydrogen spillover, thereby promoting efficient PET hydrogenolysis with high selectivity toward p -xylene.

title: Novel Ti( iii )/Ti( iv ) mixed-valence doping for enhancing the structural stability and specific capacity of a P2-layered Na 0.67 Ni 0.2 Mn 0.8 O 2 cathode for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: Incorporating the Ti( iii )/Ti( iv ) couple into the P2-type layered cathode generates a new redox peak at 1.85 V, enhancing the specific capacity and cycling stability.

title: Boosting C 2 H 4 electrosynthesis from CO 2 via Cu-nanoparticle-anchored carbon aerogels as tubular gas-flow-through electrodes
abstract: A novel coaxial gas-flow-through reactor overcomes mass transfer limits and reveals Cu-size-dependent selectivity for high-rate CO 2 RR towards C 2 H 4 electrosynthesis.

title: Dual engine drive: the synergy of Mn and Ti 4 O 7 achieves high-performance acidic water oxidation
abstract: The development of electrocatalysts that simultaneously exhibit high activity and robust stability for the oxygen evolution reaction (OER) is critical to advancing proton exchange membrane water electrolysis (PEMWE).

title: High-entropy Co–Zn–Cd–Cu–Mn sulfide ceramic nanoflowers as efficient microwave absorbers with photothermal performance
abstract: Tunable crystallinity in nanoflower-structured high-entropy CoZnCdCuMn sulfides enables regulation of dielectric loss and impedance matching. Moreover, the sample shows rapid photothermal response for electromagnetic protection in cold environments.

title: Direct regeneration of cathodes from spent LIB black mass through an integrated roasting-flotation method with impurity-tailored self-decontamination
abstract: In this work, supported by the reactions of residual metal impurities and F-elements inherent in black mass, the regenerated cathodes were successfully obtained through subsequent flotation separation and a tailored solid-phase sintering process.

title: Nb doping strategy for active site modification in Co 3 O 4 to enable concurrent hydrogen production and glycerol valorization for efficient formate production
abstract: Glycerol oxidation replaces energy-intensive OER, enabling simultaneous hydrogen production and selective conversion of biomass-derived glycerol into value-added formate.

title: A high-performance iron-tetrahydroxyanthraquinone metal-quinone network for solid-state organic batteries
abstract: A well-designed iron-tetrahydroxyanthraquinone MQN is used as the cathode material for solid-state organic batteries to achieve high rate capability and long cycle life.

title: NaBH 4 -mediated syntheses of colloidal gold nanocatalysts in water: are additives really needed?
abstract: Only water, HAuCl 4 and NaBH 4 are needed to obtain size-controlled, stable and catalytically active nanoparticles.

title: Influence of current collector materials on the electrochemical performance of aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: Graphene and carbon paper current collectors achieve high capacity and stability in aqueous Zn-ion batteries, emphasizing their superiority over metallic counterparts.

title: Cobalt-free compositionally complex nanocomposites with superior performance and stability for application as oxygen electrodes in solid oxide cells
abstract: Compositionally complex cobalt-free perovskite–fluorite nanocomposites exhibiting superior electrochemical performance and long-term stability as oxygen electrodes for solid oxide cells.

title: Structural effects of composition tuning in A-site disordered perovskite La 0.5 Li 0.5− x M x TiO 3 (M = Na, K) nanorods for fast interfacial transport for solid composite electrolyte design
abstract: Using DFT and STEM, we show the impact of La 0.5 M 0.5 TiO 3 (M = Li, Na, K) A-site composition on bulk and surface structure, connecting structure to interfacial Li-ion transport for composite electrolyte design for next-generation energy storage.

title: Improved cycle life and Li-ion transport parameters at low temperature in doped Ni-rich NMC cathodes
abstract: B and Sn–B co-doping of NMC955 enhances structural stability and Li-ion transport across −5 °C to 45 °C. Sn–B co-doping reduces charge-transfer resistance 8-fold at −5 °C and improves rate capacity by 25%, outperforming single-dopant strategies.

title: Lattice oxygen activation enabled by Fe doping in double perovskite oxides for efficient zinc–air batteries
abstract: Fe doping activates lattice oxygen (LOM) in double perovskite oxides, tuning the electronic structure to boost oxygen evolution reaction (OER) activity for durable zinc–air batteries (ZABs).

title: Constructing ternary double bulk heterojunction organic solar cells via layer-by-layer deposition method
abstract: The ternary double-BHJ OSCs based on PM1:BTP-eC9/PM1:L8-BO prepared by LbL deposition method achieve a PCE of 19.62% due to excellent molecular arrangement orderliness, crystallinity, and D/A bicontinuous interpenetrating networks.

title: Direct recycling of lithium-ion battery materials: separation and regeneration
abstract: The rapid development of lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) has led to an urgent need for efficient recycling.

title: 3D additive manufactured thermoelectrics: breaking efficiency barriers in waste heat recovery
abstract: This timely review discusses the advantages and limitations of various 3D printing approaches for thermoelectric materials, highlighting their impact on the properties of the materials and device integration.

title: Engineering metal–support interactions of ceria-supported cobalt catalysts for durable hydrogenation of levulinic acid to γ-valerolactone
abstract: Engineered Co/CeO₂ interactions achieve 100% γ-valerolactone yield and >900 h stability. Optimal 1% Co loading maximizes Co²⁺-oxygen vacancy sites, enabling durable biomass conversion.

title: Utilization of electron-donating linkers in M-series dimerized acceptors to achieve high-voltage and efficient organic solar cells
abstract: An electron-donating 3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene linker is used to construct an efficient dimerized small-molecule acceptor, which exhibits a power conversion efficiency of 17.31%, an open-circuit voltage of 0.95 V, and enhanced operational stability.

title: Interfacial confinement of ultra-low Pt single atoms via Co–Pt–C/N electronic bridging for efficient hydrogen evolution
abstract: Pt single atoms (1.35 wt%) anchored at Co interfaces via Co–Pt–C/N bridging achieve 8 mV (acidic electrolyte) and 18 mV (alkaline electrolyte) overpotentials for HER.

title: The critical impact of electrolyte concentration on Al 3+ redox and stability of CuHCF in aqueous aluminum-ion batteries
authors: 'Vishnu Priya H. Radhakantha', 'Julius Tiongson', 'Udupa Sujit Manjunatha', 'Luke O'Dell', 'Aninda J. Bhattacharyya'
abstract: Al(NO 3 ) 3 concentration governs Cu/Fe redox activity and degradation in aqueous AIBs.

title: Hydrogels as next-generation anti-corrosive coatings: design, mechanisms, and prospects
abstract: This review focuses on the fabrication of hydrogels via physical and chemical cross-linking and the anticorrosion mechanisms of hydrogel coatings on metal surfaces, with a particular emphasis on physical barrier effects and active protection.

title: Investigation of heat transfer in a semicrystalline polymer by combining molecular simulation and machine learning
abstract: Heat transfer in a semicrystalline polymer is investigated by combining molecular simulation and machine learning and the molecular mechanism is discussed.

title: Multiscale design of a 3D GDE breaks mass transport barriers for efficient H 2 O 2 electrosynthesis
abstract: Mass transport critically governs gas-involving electrochemical systems by controlling reactant accessibility and interfacial dynamics.

title: Understanding the oxygen reduction/evolution reactions (ORR/OER) on bimetallic Pt–Ir electrocatalysts using in situ Raman spectroscopy and DFT
abstract: Raman spectroscopy reveals low oxygen coverage on Pt sites, correlating with enhanced ORR activity. In line with DFT calculations, a clear weakening by ∼38 cm −1 ≈0.48 kJ mol −1 of Ir–O–Pt compared to the Ir–O–Ir bond results in lower OER activity.

title: Spacer cation electronic structure controls excited-state pathways in two-dimensional hybrid perovskites
abstract: Tetrazine spacer cation electronic structure controls charge- and energy-transfer pathways in two-dimensional (2D) organic–inorganic hybrid perovskites (OIHPs).

title: A dielectric-gradient composite gel polymer electrolyte synergistically enhances ion-transport and interfaces for high-voltage lithium-metal batteries
abstract: A dielectric-gradient composite electrolyte constructed by spatially grading high-ε NaNbO 3 and low-ε Al 2 O 3 in PVDF-HFP matrix synergistically stabilizes electrochemical interfaces, enabling high-performance high-voltage Li-metal batteries.

title: Cerium-doping and nitridation effects on nickel-based metal organic frameworks for alkaline water oxidation
abstract: Synthesis routine for making the cerium-doped-nickel-nitride composite.

title: Facet-dependent oxygen evolution on IrO 2 from machine-learned potential-driven atomistic models
abstract: Machine learned Interatomic potential with explicit solvation model identifies facet dependent oxygen evolution activity on IrO 2 .

title: CVD-grown phase-pure V 2 C nanosheets with pseudocapacitive behavior for fast and stable lithium-ion storage
abstract: We report a facile and scalable chemical vapor deposition (CVD) method for synthesizing high-quality, phase-pure V 2 C nanosheets using VCl 3 and vanadium foil as dual vanadium sources and CH 4 as the carbon source.

title: Efficient and selective seawater oxidation by manganese ferrite electrocatalysts obtained via a vapor phase strategy
abstract: Efficient and selective seawater splitting can be successfully promoted by green manganese ferrite electrocatalysts fabricated and tailored by an original vapor phase route.

title: Collaborative phase separation induced by double noncovalent dynamic crosslinkers in polyurethane for supertoughness and triboelectric generator electrodes
abstract: A strategy involving two noncovalent dynamic crosslinkers aims to address the trade-off in PU properties. Collaborative phase separation topology is confirmed. Both excellent mechanical and condutive performances make it a good electrode material.

title: Mechanochemically engineered defect-rich Zn/Co-ZIF-8 solid solutions for enhanced electrochemical water splitting
abstract: Mechanochemical route tunes disorder in Zn/Co-ZIF-8 solid solutions via precursor control, yielding a defect-rich amorphous phase with high active sites, electron density and charge transport, enabling superior bifunctional OER/HER electrocatalysis.

title: Advances and challenges for Mg 3 (Sb,Bi) 2 -based thermoelectric materials and devices
abstract: This review summarizes the progress in Mg 3 (Sb,Bi) 2 thermoelectric materials and analyzes key challenges in practical applications, aiming to achieve low- and medium-temperature waste heat recovery and solid-state cooling.

title: The role of AI in the advancement of 2D materials for biosensing and nanoelectronics
abstract: Significant advancements in biosensing and nanoelectronics have been driven by 2D materials like graphene, MoS 2 , and hBN, integrated with artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML).

title: Concentration dependent salt-polymer-dynamic bond interactions dictate non-monotonic conductivity and viscoelasticity in vitrimer electrolytes
abstract: Tuning salt in vitrimer electrolytes yields non-monotonic trends with three distinct regimes in modulus, relaxation times and ionic conductivity. An optimal salt concentration maximizes both conductivity and bond exchange while preserving modulus.

title: Stack-Pressure-Dependent Interfacial Capacitance Governs Electrochemical Contact in All-Solid-State Batteries
abstract: All-solid-state batteries (ASSB) are regarded as next-generation lithium-ion batteries capable of simultaneously delivering high energy density and enhanced safety. For the practical realization of ASSB, a quantitative understanding of solid–solid...

title: A strong, healable, recyclable bio-based adhesive via a bone-inspired triple network
abstract: Inspired by the hierarchical structure of natural bone, a fully bio-based adhesive featuring a triple-network design integrates excellent environmental stability, efficient self-healing, multiple re-bonding capability, and recyclability.

title: Revealing insight into defect-mediated charge dynamics and reaction intermediates stabilization for CO 2 photoreduction on S-scheme catalyst
abstract: An S-scheme CTS/rGO/CW heterojunction with sulfur and oxygen vacancies exhibits enhanced CO 2 photoreduction, with directional charge flow and stabilized intermediates facilitating the production of CO and CH 4 .

title: High-throughput screening of Janus t-phase TMXY semiconducting materials for thermoelectric applications aided by machine learning
abstract: Integrating high-throughput DFT and ML, we screen 2D Janus t-TMXY monolayers, identifying dynamically stable candidates: ligand asymmetry tunes transport properties. These show superior thermoelectric performance, with energy conversion potential.

title: Orchestrated spatial confinement and phase engineering with SDS for air-stable and high-rate alluaudite sodium-ion cathodes
abstract: A multifunctional surfactant-mediated strategy simultaneously enhances the environmental stability and accelerates reaction kinetics of alluaudite-type sodium iron sulfate (NFS).

title: Electrolyte-affinitive porous separators enabled by nitrile integration and polymerization-induced phase separation for stable lithium metal batteries
abstract: A one-step PIPS strategy employing ternary monomers produces a porous separator with high electrolyte affinity and thermal robustness, enabling dendrite-suppressed lithium metal batteries with long-term cycling stability.

title: Controlled Selenization within a N-Doped Carbon Shell Enables Stable MnO-MnSe Heterointerfaces for Long-Life Li-S Batteries
abstract: In this study, a MnO-MnSe heterostructure encapsulated in a nitrogen doped carbon (NC) layer (MnO-MnSe@NC (1:2.5) ) was fabricated via controlled selenization and employed as a multifunctional coating for lithium-sulfur...

title: Low-cost and sustainable hard carbon derived from the peony shell for the stable sodium-ion anode
abstract: The peony shell exhibits high feasibility as a sustainable raw material for the hard carbon anode of the sodium-ion battery.

title: Heterostructure LDH/oxides as p-n type Electrocatalyst for the alkaline Water Splitting: An Experimental Assessment via Temperature-dependent Study
abstract: Understanding reaction kinetics and catalytic mechanism plays a pivotal role in developing low-cost materials for water splitting reaction. Herein, a hierarchically structured MnFe-LDH/Mn2O3 heterostructure was fabricated on Ni foam using...

title: Gellan gum-based ionic conductive hydrogel with self-healing capability, piezoionicity and ionic thermoelectricity as flexible sensors
abstract: Schematic of the synthesis process and the mechanism of ionic conductivity, piezoionicity and ionic thermoelectricity of the PGM hydrogel.

title: Synthetic Strategies, Structural Engineering and Mechanistic Insights into Carbon Dots for Sustainable Photo- and Electro-Catalysis
abstract: Carbon dots (CDs) have emerged as a versatile class of metal-free nanomaterials for sustainable catalytic applications owing to their low cost, tunable surface and structure functionality, excellent optoelectronic properties, and...

title: New insights into ultra-deep oxidative desulfurization in the presence of competing molecules over alumina-supported vanadium-promoted tungsten oxide catalysts
abstract: γ-Al 2 O 3 -supported V–W catalysts were prepared by ultrasonication-assisted IWI. The optimized 3V17W/Al catalyst achieved complete DBT conversion in 2 min (TOF 25 s −1 ). 3V17W/Al outperformed 20W/Al due to higher dispersion of active sites and acidity.

title: Few-layer MoS 2 dispersion induced by sulfur atom sharing to promote CO 2 hydrogenation to methanol
abstract: With the ongoing interest in developing more stable and versatile catalysts for CO 2 hydrogenation to methanol, molybdenum sulfide (MoS 2 ) has been recently proposed as an alternative material.

title: Revealing the impact of Na–Ag single-site Co-doping in CZTSSe
abstract: A single-site co-doping strategy is proposed by using Na and Ag ions, which both target the same copper lattice site in CZTSSe. The synergistic effects of Na and Ag can help to achieve a V oc of 559 mV and a FF of 67.3% and an overall PCE of 13.2%.

title: MXenes as emerging 2D materials for hydrogen generation: advances and future prospects
abstract: MXenes are promising two-dimensional materials with high electrical conductivity, large surface area, and tuneable surface terminations, enabling efficient electrocatalytic, photocatalytic and photoelectrocatalytic hydrogen evolution performance.

title: Interface-engineered graphene oxide membranes for high-performance fluorine-free fuel cells
abstract: An interface-engineered graphene oxide membrane is developed as a fluorine-free electrolyte for fuel cells. Protonic interfacial modification, defect-rich GO, and ultrathin membrane design enable high power density, surpassing PFSA membranes.

title: Anchoring electron-delocalized CeO 2 on porous carbon for expediting polysulfide kinetics toward high-loading Li–S batteries
abstract: The incorpration of electron-delocalized CeO 2 into pollen-derived carbon facilitates the coversion of sulfur species.

title: Interfacial electron transfer of a MOF-derived Co–B–P catalyst for the hydrogen evolution reaction with improved activity and stability
abstract: CoO NSs are transformed to CoB/Co 2 P HNA via ZIF-67 templating, boronization, and phosphorization. The resulting catalyst shows superior HER activity and serves as a model for understanding Co–B–P interfacial mechanisms.

title: Decoupling oxygen redox from O 2 release in Li- and Mn-rich layered cathodes: mechanisms, metrics, and design rules
abstract: This review outlines how decoupling reversible oxygen redox from O 2 release stabilizes Li- and Mn-rich layered cathodes, mitigating transition-metal migration, structural reconstruction, and voltage fade.

title: The effect of Nafion on electrochemical nitrate reduction over CoRu alloy catalyst
abstract: Nafion on Co 9 Ru 1 promotes H 2 O dissociation, enhances NO 3 − adsorption and N–O bond activation, facilitates subsequent protonation, selectively promoting eNO 3 − RR without enhancing the competing HER.

title: Harnessing water evaporation: flexible generators for next-generation self-powered systems
abstract: This review outlines advances in material and flexible generator strategies enabling sustainable power from water evaporation for next-generation self-powered flexible electronics.

title: Beyond inertness: a critical perspective on design strategies for stable thermoelectric interfaces
abstract: This perspective defines quantitative ‘target design windows’ for contact resistivity and shear strength, mapping four dominant strategies to guide the transition from empirical metallization to engineered reliability.

title: Creating multi-electron reactions in a NASICON-type Na 7 V 4 (P 2 O 7 ) 4 (PO 4 ) cathode for sodium-ion batteries by activating reversible V 4+ /V 5+ redox reactions
abstract: Capacity enhancement is driven by activating multi-electron reactions via V 4+ /V 5+ redox couples.

title: Rationally designed multifunctional hyperbranched polymer as a trace additive for stable zinc anodes
abstract: A hyperbranched polymer (N-HPG) with multifunctional groups is rationally designed as a trace electrolyte additive for aqueous zinc-ion batteries.

title: Mixed-valence engineered bipolar polyimide covalent organic framework film for multicolor displays and dual-band electrochromism
abstract: Dual-band electrochromic materials can selectively modulate the absorption or transmission of light, enabling effective photothermal regulation of solar radiation.

title: Si-doped vanadium pentoxide/graphene xerogel nanocomposite cathodes with excellent cycle life for Li-ion batteries
abstract: Si-doped V 2 O 5 nanoribbons sitting on a graphene sheet significantly improve cycle stability compared to that of pristine V 2 O 5 on graphene.

title: Dielectric shells as resonant mode tuners: unlocking superior visible-spectrum photon capture in plasmonic perovskite solar cells
abstract: The dielectric shell is not a passive shield but an active resonant tuner. It enables higher-order plasmonic modes, unlocking a superior light management strategy focused on the visible spectrum and boosting perovskite solar cell efficiency by 43%.

title: Turning off 2D reconstruction with a sulfone-modified passivator for efficient and stable inverted perovskite solar cells
abstract: Turning off 3D to 2D reconstruction with sulfone-modified passivation delivers 25.21% efficiency and durable inverted perovskite solar cells.

title: Correction: Efficient piezo-photocatalysis via a g-C 3 N 4 /Ti 3 C 2 T x heterojunction: synergy of piezoelectric and built-in fields
abstract: Correction for ‘Efficient piezo-photocatalysis via a g-C 3 N 4 /Ti 3 C 2 T x heterojunction: synergy of piezoelectric and built-in fields’ by Sihai Sun et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2026, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TA07251D.

title: Tuning the crystal facets of MoP 2 in a phosphide–nitride heterostructure via interface engineering for energy-efficient ethanol oxidation-coupled H 2 production
abstract: Efforts to enhance the electrocatalytic activities of transition-metal-based nanocatalyst systems are important to improve their viability and commercial exploitation.

title: Quasi-1D selenohalides: first-principles insights into thermoelectric and photovoltaic applications
abstract: Energy harvesting potential of quasi-1D selenohalides is explored, with BiSeBr ( zT ≈ 0.81) capable of thermoelectric power generation and SbSeBr (SLME ≈ 31%) promising for high-efficiency photovoltaic devices.

title: Durability and degradation of Anion Exchange Membranes in water electrolyzers
abstract: Anion Exchange Membrane degradation can occur through thermal, chemical and mechanical pathways, but there are several strategies that can help mitigate these phenomena and increase AEM durability.

title: Machine-learning prediction of metal sulfide photocatalysts for sacrificial hydrogen evolution under visible light irradiation
abstract: Ag 2 CdGeS 4 and Cu 2 CdMS 4 (M = Ge and Sn) predicted by machine learning were experimentally developed for sacrificial hydrogen evolution under visible light.

title: Enhancing valence-band charge localization via zinc doping into MnWO 4 to promote selective ammonia electrooxidation
abstract: An atomically precise quantity of zinc( ii ) doping into the MnWO 4 lattice localizes the valence-band charge at the manganese sites to enhance the anodic capability and selectivity of the ammonia oxidation reaction (AOR) towards nitrate formation.

title: Novel activated carbon-supported multi-metal oxides for peroxydisulfate activation and efficient organic pollutant degradation: insights into operations, transformation mechanism, and DFT study
abstract: A novel and magnetic recoverable catalyst ACMO was prepared by decorating CoFe 2 O 4 and Fe 3 O 4 nanoparticles onto AC surface for peroxydisulfate (PS) activation to efficiently degrade BPA through radical and non-radical-mediated mechanisms.

title: Bonding-regulated construction of covalent organic framework-supported frustrated Lewis pairs for 1,4-hydrosilylation of α,β-unsaturated ketones
abstract: Covalent incorporation of HBCF effectively avoids the loss of active sites, which makes NBDE-FLP and NBDE-QA-FLP show good catalytic activity. Mechanistic studies reveal the different catalytic pathways of bonding-regulated COF-FLPs.

title: NiSe/ZnSe heterojunctions derived from truncated rhombic dodecahedra for stable and high-power sodium-ion batteries
abstract: In this study, a spherical NiSe/ZnSe heterojunction composite is designed through selenization of a precursor with a unique truncated rhombic dodecahedral configuration.

title: Correction: Controlled aminolysis of multilayer polyurethane composites for sustainable upcycling into waterborne polyurethane emulsions
abstract: Correction for ‘Controlled aminolysis of multilayer polyurethane composites for sustainable upcycling into waterborne polyurethane emulsions’ by Meenakshisundaram Vaishali et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2025, 13 , 38194–38208, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TA05214A.

title: Engineering CoO/Co 3 O 4 heterostructures coupled with adjacent Fe single atoms to boost tandem electrocatalytic nitrate reduction for high-efficiency ammonia production
abstract: The efficient tandem FeSA@CoO/Co 3 O 4 NC electrocatalyst couples dual active sites: FeSA sites reduce nitrate to nitrite, while CoO/Co 3 O 4 sites drives nitrite conversion, accelerating the eNO 3 RR kinetics and boosting performance.

title: Decoding mixed-ion effects in halide electrolytes: entropy as a unified measure of a soft and disordered lattice
abstract: High vibrational entropy and two-body entropy induced by ion mixing correlate with the low activation energy for Li-ion diffusion, which serve as a unified measure for lattice softening and disordering.

title: From low- to high-entropy layered transition metal oxide cathodes: recent progress on spray-drying technologies in materials design for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: This review highlights spray-drying as a scalable route for entropy-engineered Na x TMO 2 cathodes, improving stability and performance in SIBs, while clarifying entropy misclassifications and correlating composition and electrochemical behavior.

title: Photocatalytic semi-hydrogenation of acetylene to ethylene in water powered by a copper-functionalized hydrogen-bonded organic framework
abstract: A Cu-loaded hydrogen-bonded organic framework enables visible-light-driven semi-hydrogenation of acetylene to ethylene in water, combining photosensitizer and catalyst in a single material to achieve ≥99.9% selectivity under mild conditions.

title: The effect of intrinsic defects in grain boundaries on non-radiative recombination in CsPbI 3 and CsPbBr 3
abstract: Grain boundaries in CsPbBr 3 and CsPbI 3 host excess Pb-related defects compared to bulk, increasing non-radiative recombination and lowering photoluminescence response.

title: Magnetic-field-assisted cascade electrocatalysis: simultaneous H 2 O 2 generation and ˙OH radical production for coupled hydrogen evolution and water disinfection
abstract: Magnetic-field-assisted cascade electrocatalysis for enhanced ·OH radical generation and efficient water disinfection, achieving simultaneous high-performance green hydrogen production.

title: Construction of an In-BDC MOF artificial interface layer on a zinc metal anode for ultra-long stability zinc-ion capacitors
abstract: The regulation mechanism of the In-BDC MOF modification layer on zinc deposition behavior.

title: Artificial intelligence-driven unraveling of the critical factor of heteroatom-doped carbon-based ORR catalysts
abstract: This work leverages artificial intelligence (AI) to explore the crucial factor in optimizing heteroatom-doped carbon-based ORR catalysts.

title: Synergistic prediction of phase and hardness in high-entropy alloys via the integration of machine learning and active learning strategies
abstract: Due to the extensive compositional space and limited sample size, achieving comprehensive and accurate predictions of the microstructure and properties of high-entropy alloys (HEAs) using machine learning models remains a challenging task.

title: Fe-assisted nitridation-induced reconstruction of Mo–Fe–Ni molybdates enables durable alkaline seawater oxygen evolution and Zn–air batteries
abstract: Fe-directed nitridation converts Mo–Fe–Ni molybdates into a conductive nitride–oxide heterostructure. The Fe activates ammonia, forming MoO 2 , Mo 2 N, FeN and Ni 4 N interfaces that enhances alkaline seawater OER and enable durable Zn–air batteries.

title: Benzothiadiazoles incorporated in a metal–organic framework for improved photocatalysis in azacycle synthesis
abstract: We use UiO-66 as the host and 2,1,3-benzothiadiazoles-based dicarboxylic acids as linker for systematically tuning the catalytic behavior of photo-responsive MOFs. The resulting MOFs exhibit enhanced one-step photocatalytic synthesis of azacycles.

title: Remediation of perfluorooctane sulfonic acid (PFOS) using supported lipid bilayers on mesoporous-SiO 2
abstract: Polar headgoups and hydrophobic tails of lipids perfectly accommodate the ionic headgroups and fluorinated tails of perfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS). Remediation occurs when supported lipid bilayers on nanoporous SiO 2 particles incorporate the PFAS.

title: Proton source selective semi-hydrogenation of alkynes: a water-powered selective photocatalyst based on nickel single-atoms on poly(heptazine imide)
abstract: Ni 2+ single atoms on PHI photocatalyze the selective semi-hydrogenation of alkynes, using water as the proton source.

title: Designing Organic Cathodes for Aqueous Zinc Batteries: Materials Chemistry of Carrier Selectivity, Mechanistic Validation, and Practical Benchmarking
abstract: Organic cathodes for aqueous zinc batteries are attractive because both their donor environments and structural organization can be tuned at the molecular and framework levels. The central materials challenge, however,...

title: Face-sharing structure perovskite air electrodes with boosted oxygen diffusion and durability for reversible protonic ceramic cells
abstract: BaCo 0.6 Fe 0.4 O 3− δ perovskite with a face-sharing motif boosts ORR/OER activity and durability in reversible protonic ceramic cells by lowering the oxygen vacancy formation energy and proton migration barrier.

title: Regulating ordered structure and MOR catalytic performance of L1 0 -FePt nanoparticles through Mn doping and magnetic-field assistance
abstract: The structural optimization process driven by Mn doping and magnetic field application.

title: Oxygen vacancy-rich Fe 3 O 4 -enriched P-Fe 2 TiO 5 sites generated by Na incorporation and NiP deposition for accelerated electrocatalytic OER
abstract: Na-incorporated Fe 2 TiO 5 –Fe 3 O 4 (Na-Fe-FT) catalyst developed using a modified sol–gel method, which was loaded onto an NiP matrix to create a dual-functional electrode for electrocatalytic OER and photocatalytic HER.

title: Anion exchange membranes composed of poly( m -terphenylene)s and pendant ammonium head groups for anion exchange membrane water electrolyzers
abstract: Anion exchange membranes featuring meta -kinked terphenylene in the backbone and hexyl spacer-tethered ammonium head groups were designed and synthesized to enhance both ion conductivity and chemical stability for water electrolyzers.

title: Beyond lithium paradigms: distinct electrochemo-mechanical behaviors of sodium-ion batteries
abstract: Internal mechanical compensation between electrodes results in SOC-independent macroscopic stiffness, while a thermodynamic stress–potential divergence drives a distinct voltage bifurcation during the compression of sodium-ion batteries.

title: Rapid and High-Precision Photoinduced 3D Printing Enabled by Thioacetal Mediated Cationic Degenerate Chain Transfer Polymerization
abstract: Cationic photopolymerization offers inherent advantages for additive manufacturing, including oxygen tolerance and darkcuring capability, yet its practical implementation in high-resolution 3D printing remains limited by the lack of molecularlevel control....

title: Ca 2+ substitution induced phase coexistence and synergistic regulation of Ti 3+ defects to improve piezoelectric–photocatalytic water splitting performance of BaTiO 3
abstract: BaTiO 3 -Ca 0.2 photoanode with a coexisting tetragonal-orthorhombic phase involving Ti 3+ defects boosts piezo-photocatalytic water splitting performance via enhanced built-in piezoelectric field and shallow energy levels from orbital modulation.

title: Reversible luminescence detection of dihalogenomethanes in a 3D CuI coordination polymer assembled by a thioether-functionalized POSS ligand
abstract: Reversible adsorption of dihalomethane solvents in hybrid POSS-CuI cubane-based 3D coordination polymers restricts local molecular motion and suppresses non-radiative decay, resulting in luminescence turn-on/off response.

title: Synergistic DFT and Machine Learning Screening of Z-Scheme g-SiC/TMD Heterostructures for Efficient Overall Water Splitting
abstract: To mitigate the heavy reliance on non-renewable resources such as fossil fuels, photocatalytic water splitting has been recognized as a promising method for generating clean and renewable energy. However, achieving...

title: Upcycled Ni–Co–Mn oxide bifunctional electrocatalyst from spent LIBs for electrochemical water splitting
abstract: Upcycling spent lithium-ion battery cathodes via DES recycling yields a Ni–Co–Mn oxide electrocatalyst with bifunctional activity for alkaline–saline water splitting, highlighting a sustainable waste-to-green hydrogen strategy.

title: Synergistic interactions and structural engineering of bimetallic catalysts for electrocatalytic energy conversion
abstract: This work explores the synergistic effects of bimetallic electrocatalysts, including their applications in CO 2 reduction, water splitting, and ammonia production, highlighting key advancements in catalyst design and performance.

title: Ionically engineered Zn@Fe 3 O 4 nanocomposite hydrogels with stretchable mechanics and high-performance electrochemical storage for wearable supercapacitors and strain sensors
abstract: Herein, we report Zn@Fe 3 O 4 nano-composite hydrogels (n-CHs), in which uniformly dispersed nanoparticles (NPs) within a p(AAm-co-LMA)/IGEPAL®CA-630 network reinforce the polymer structure and promote rapid ion transport.

title: In-Cell Electropolymerization of 10-(N-Pyrrolyl)phenoxazine into a Phenoxazine-Grafted Polypyrrole Cathode for High-Voltage and Ultrastable Li-Ion Batteries
abstract: Organic electrode materials face critical challenges of dissolution in electrolytes and limited operating voltage, which hinder their practical application. To overcome these barriers, we designed a novel electropolymerizable molecule, phenoxazine-functionalized...

title: Carbon-encapsulated electrocatalysts for oxygen electrocatalysis
abstract: Carbon-encapsulated electrocatalysts for oxygen electrocatalysis were reviewed, pointing out both the advancement and challenges. Further investigations should emphasize the fundamental theories, synthetic methods, and in situ characterizations.

title: In situ preparation of pseudo-graphite domains in hard carbon via molecular engineering towards high-performance Na-ion batteries
abstract: Hard carbon has gained significant attention as a potential anode material for sodium-ion batteries owing to its affordability and high specific capacity.

title: Mechanistic Elucidation on the Detoxification of Hexavalent Chromium Using a Magnetic "Anion Sponge" Fe₃O₄-pC₃N₄/PANI Composite with Redox Capability: Synergistic Design and Computational Study
abstract: Water remediation of toxic hexavalent chromium (Cr(VI)) commonly involves singlefunction materials based on physical adsorption. In this research, Fe 3 O 4 -protonated C 3 N 4 /polyaniline (Fe 3...

title: Thermal alkyne–azide cycloaddition enables the fabrication of fluorescent COF thin films for sensitive chemical warfare agent sensing
abstract: A fluorescent covalent organic framework (COF) thin film was developed as a fast-responding, low-detection-limit sensor, enabling the first application of COF thin films for vapor-phase chemical warfare agent detection.

title: Engineering highly active surfaces in molybdenum-integrated cobalt telluride electrodes for enhanced battery-type charge-storage in supercapacitors
abstract: Mo-interconnected CoTe 2 nano-dots provide abundant active sites and fast charge transfer. The MCT-3 electrode delivers 691 C g −1 with strong stability, while the MCT-3//AC device achieves 122 Wh kg −1 , showing promise for hybrid supercapacitors.

title: Degradation mechanisms and mitigation strategies for high-voltage layered oxide cathodes for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: This review analyzes degradation of layered oxide cathodes in sodium-ion batteries on bulk and interface failure. The strategies of elemental doping, structural engineering, and surface modification are discussed for practical sodium-ion batteries.

title: Tailoring high concentration electrolytes for supercapacitors: the impact of anion structure on ion transport and charge storage
abstract: A family of fluorinated anions is investigated to determine the optimal anion structure and concentration to maximise supercapacitor performance.

title: Optimizing external quantum efficiency in photocatalysts via first-principles optics and carrier transport modeling
abstract: This study demonstrates that for materials with shallow absorption edges across a broad spectral range, maximizing light absorption is just as important as minimizing losses from charge recombination.

title: Integration of SnO 2 -MXene photo-supercapacitor with perovskite solar cell in two-electrode configuration
