abstract: A multifunctional SnO 2 -MXene nanocomposite enables a compact two-electrode photo-supercapacitor that integrates perovskite solar cell and supercapacitor functionalities for efficient solar energy harvesting and storage.

title: Polydimethylsiloxane-assisted low-energy anodization: a fluoride-free tunable strategy for engineering porous TiO 2 films
abstract: PDMS-assisted anodization suppresses oxygen evolution to enable energy-efficient, fluoride-free formation of uniform and strongly adherent porous TiO 2 films, while silicate ions provide additional energy reduction and enhanced corrosion resistance.

title: Selective nitrogen doping on porous hard carbons using polymer dopants for high-performance fuel cells
abstract: Nitrogen-doped carbons are emerging as promising electrocatalysts for the oxygen reduction reaction in proton exchange membrane fuel cells, yet their practical use remains limited by high synthesis cost and insufficient electrochemical stability.

title: Ni–Cr–Fe–Mo heterojunction enables efficient and ultra-stable hybrid seawater electrolysis at industrial current densities
abstract: A multi-active-site Ni–Cr–Fe–Mo heterojunction was fabricated by powder metallurgy. The stable HER reaction under an industrial current density of 2 A cm −2 was achieved through a reaction pathway relay mechanism of the active sites.

title: DFT investigation of active sites in metal-doped ferrierite zeolites for selective methane oxidation to methanol
abstract: DFT shows metal-dependent C–H activation in FER zeolites. Partial d-orbitals metals (Fe, Co, Ni) favor CPET while d 10 Cu + prefers HAT. Oxyl-radical [CuO] + and [Cu(µ-O)Ni] 2+ exhibit superior activity. This guides methane-to-methanol catalyst design.

title: Bonding synergy in In 2 TiO 5 enables high-capacity fast lithium storage
abstract: In 2 TiO 5 is designed as a representative conversion–alloying–insertion anode, leveraging the high theoretical capacity of In 2 O 3 and excellent rate capability of TiO 2 through structure unit rearrangement.

title: Surface decoration of Ba-enriched Fe-based air electrodes with Co-based catalysts for robust protonic ceramic electrochemical cells
abstract: A Co/Fe separation strategy via surface decoration simultaneously enhances electrocatalytic activity and thermomechanical compatibility in protonic ceramic electrochemical cells.

title: Solid additive engineering for high-efficiency organic solar cells: categories, mechanisms, and perspectives
abstract: This review summarizes recent advances in solid additives (SAs) for high-efficiency organic solar cells (OSCs), focusing on their categories, working mechanisms, and future research directions for SA engineering to accelerate OSCs' commercialization.

title: A dual-site Mo–Mg 2 Ni catalyst enhances de/hydrogenation kinetics of magnesium with high phase stability
abstract: Mg 2 Ni suffers from relatively weak catalyst–hydrogen interactions. We report that the catalyst–hydrogen interaction strength of Mg 2 Ni can be systematically controlled by Mo doping.

title: A-site high-entropy engineering enables a cobalt-free perovskite air electrode with exceptional activity and durability for reversible solid oxide cells
abstract: This study proposes an A-site high-entropy strategy by introducing small-radius ions La 3+ , Pr 3+ , and Ca 2+ into BSF air electrode. It achieves synergistic optimization of oxygen transport capability and surface catalytic reactivity for RSOCs.

title: Incorporating unique B–N motifs into graphene for efficient photocatalytic CO 2 reduction
abstract: B–N motif engineering of graphene achieves efficient photocatalytic CO 2 reduction by optimizing band structure and light absorption.

title: Lithium superionic behavior and defect robustness in LiNbOCl 4 : a first-principles molecular dynamics study
abstract: LiNbOCl 4 exhibits superionic Li transport with thermal and mechanical robustness, but LiCl Schottky defects disrupt dynamic bottleneck breathing and suppress ionic conductivity.

title: Comparative N₂ Fixation Performance of Diverse Micro- and Nanosized 2D Materials Measured by Gas Chromatography
abstract: The photocatalytic conversion of N2 to NH3 using 2D materials has been extensively studied owing to its high conversion efficiency. N2 fixation is the first and rate-determining step, which depends...

title: Precise synthesis of sub-3 nm platinum-based intermetallic alloys with a freeze-microwave confinement strategy for the hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: By integrating interface regulation with physical confinement, we propose a “freeze-microwave confinement” strategy, which provides an extensible and effective route for the controlled synthesis of sub-3 nm Pt-based IMCs.

title: Discovery of inaccessible supersaturated metastable Li 3 VO 4 –Li 2 MoO 4 solid solutions via spray drying for high-performance anodes
abstract: Millisecond spray drying enables overcoming the immiscibility gap in solid-solution, forming a supersaturated metastable phase of Li 3 VO 4 –Li 2 MoO 4 for high-power Li-ion storage.

title: Central aromatic ring substitution on non-fullerene acceptors boosts the performance of organic photovoltaics
abstract: Applying different aromatic substituents to the central core of non-fullerene acceptors led to distinct molecular planarity and crystallinity. The ternary devices based on CH-Th delivered an outstanding power conversion efficiency of 19.94%.

title: Atomic-level growth engineering of Si via acetylene–silane co-deposition for enhanced cycle stability and high Li + dynamics in Si/C anodes
abstract: Late-stage SiH 4 –C 2 H 2 co-deposition regulates Si nucleation in carbon pores, producing uniform nanosized Si, which promotes a stable inorganic-rich SEI, faster kinetics, and improved cycling stability.

title: Construction of carbon-based frustrated Lewis pairs for sustainable hydrogen evolution through heteroatom bond formation
abstract: This work designs Frustrated Lewis Pairs (FLPs) on biochar using DFT-guided B/N co-doping, achieving outstanding HER performance with low overpotentials in both acid and alkaline media.

title: Self-supported layered heterostructure derived from recycling waste silk for high performance low-temperature sodium storage
abstract: The fabricated textile-based composite electrodes (TCEs) exhibit excellent rate performance (0.26 mAh cm −2 at a current density of 5 mA cm −2 ) and robust cycling stability, retaining capacity over 1000 cycles at 2 mA cm −2 .

title: A general strategy for dramatic enhancement of aerogels via an aerogel-functionalization-aerogel approach
abstract: An aerogel-functionalization-aerogel strategy is proposed by introducing superhydrophobic silica aerogel powders into five types of aerogels based on chitosan, gelatin, polyvinyl alcohol, methyl cellulose, and polyacrylamide are synthesized.

title: First-principles thermodynamic assessment of Sr-containing secondary phase formation in strontium-substituted lanthanum manganites for solid oxide cell applications
abstract: Sr-secondary phase formation is a potentially significant degradation mode with direct impact upon solid-oxide cell (SOC) commercial viability. A first-principles based thermodynamic study was performed for La 1-x Sr x MnO 3±δ (LSM) perovskites to...

title: Fe-doped CuCo 2 S 4 thiospinel as a high-performance oxygen electrocatalyst for rechargeable all-solid-state zinc–air batteries
abstract: Fe incorporation into the CuCo 2 S 4 lattice markedly enhances bifunctional oxygen electrocatalysis and is effective for next-generation Zn–air batteries.

title: S-doped defective Pd{111} facets endow Au@AuPd nanowire networks with superior electrocatalytic performance for alkaline ORR
abstract: The presence of defective Pd{111} facets endows S-doped Au@AuPd nanowire networks with superior performance for the alkaline ORR.

title: Activating BaWO 4 ceramics through atomic-level Ni doping for enhanced hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: An atomic-level Ni-doped BaWO 4 ceramic was developed as a self-supported electrocatalyst for enhanced hydrogen evolution reaction. This catalyst exhibits outstanding hydrogen evolution reaction (HER) performance and extremely high stability.

title: Plasma-photocatalytic gCeO 2 for selective Fe( ii ) and Mn( ii ) oxidation: charge-carrier-driven reactive oxygen species pathways
abstract: The Fe( ii )/Mn( ii ) removal mechanisms using gCeO 2 by PPC system.

title: Electrically conductive MOF@carbon foam composites for atmospheric water harvesting through internal Joule heating and light irradiation
abstract: Electrically conductive MOF–carbon foam composites for atmospheric water harvesting that can be regenerated by Joule heating or sunlight.

title: Nafion vs. Fumion: impact of ionomers on the alkaline oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: In alkaline water electrolysis, selecting adequate ionomers for improving stability and activity is crucial to electrocatalyst function.

title: Conquering self-discharge in supercapacitors: synergy of mechanisms and cross-scale mitigation strategies
abstract: An overview of self-discharge mechanisms in supercapacitors, including physicochemical insights, mitigation strategies, and emerging concepts aimed at enhancing energy retention and device longevity.

title: Emergence of GeSe-Based Thermoelectric Materials Empowered by Structural Modulations and Metavalent Bonding
abstract: The IV-VI semiconductor GeSe has recently attracted significant attention in thermoelectric research due to its structural similarity to SnSe-based thermoelectric materials. However, despite favorable theoretical predictions, the experimental realization of...

title: Tailoring Re-loaded core–shell Ni structures embedded in mesoporous silica for the selective transformation of levulinic acid into γ-valerolactone
abstract: A Re-doped Ni@mSiO 2 core–shell catalyst with uniform metal dispersion enables the efficient and sustainable hydrogenation of levulinic acid to γ-valerolactone, showing high activity, selectivity, and reusability for sustainable biomass valorization.

title: Nanocrystallization of triazolate-based metal–organic frameworks boosting photocatalytic hydrogen peroxide production
abstract: Downsizing bulk Cd-BTBQ crystals into nanocrystals alters the charge-transport properties and reaction pathways, thereby enabling highly efficient and durable visible-light-driven H 2 O 2 production through a dual-channel photocatalytic mechanism.

title: Asymmetric electronic coupling in Fe–Cu dual-atom sites enables accelerated oxygen electrocatalysis for high-performance Zn–air batteries
abstract: A coordination-environment engineering strategy constructs FeCu dual-atom sites in porous N-doped carbon nanotubes. Strong Fe–Cu coupling tunes the Fe d-band and oxygen adsorption, boosting ORR/OER kinetics and Zn–air battery performance.

title: Tuning aerogel morphology using water–ionic liquid binary mixtures: a mechanistic journey from synthesis to carbon properties
abstract: Ionic liquid (IL)–water mixtures, as porogen media, can tune the pore size and hydration properties of the forming polymer and carbon aerogels by the fact that IL ions establish strong interaction with monomers and oligomers during polymerization.

title: Coupled defect chemistry and redox dynamics in a bismuth gahnite system for self-adaptive tribological interfaces
abstract: The synergistic effect of a chemically-sustaining lubrication mechanism caused by structural doping, redox reactivity and a tribo-oxidative feedback loop.

title: Emergent Disorder Screening in Ag-Zn Co-doped Magnetic MnTe Thermoelectrics: Carrier Concentration-Stabilized Mobility for Enhanced Electronic Transport
abstract: MnTe is intrinsically attractive as a magnetic thermoelectric material due to its large Seebeck coefficient and inherent magnetic ordering; however, its practical thermoelectric performance is limited by poor electrical conductivity...

title: Design principles of biphenylene-supported dual-atom catalysts for efficient and selective nitrate reduction to ammonia
abstract: This work establishes biphenylene as a robust support for stabilizing dual-atom catalysts and provides transferable mechanistic and descriptor insights for guiding the design of next-generation catalysts for selective nitrate-to-ammonia conversion.

title: Towards high-energy-density aqueous Zn-ion batteries: from fundamental challenges to integrated design strategies
abstract: Aqueous Zn-ion batteries (AZIBs) offer safe, low-cost energy storage, but their energy density is limited by cathode instability, anode irreversibility, and side reactions. This review provides strategic directions toward high-energy-density AZIBs.

title: Atomically dispersed Fe–TM pairs on g-C 6 N 6 : a comparative theoretical insight into heteronuclear dual-atom catalysts for N 2 reduction to form ammonia and urea
abstract: HDACs pair two distinct metal atoms in intricately engineered fashion, providing a unique atomic precision, synergistic reactivity, and adjustable electronic properties for unlocking reaction pathways inaccessible to their single-atom counterparts.

title: Enhancing carrier lifetime in SrTiO 3 via metallic doping: insights from first-principles calculations and non-adiabatic molecular dynamics
abstract: Al–La co-doping effectively eliminates oxygen-vacancy trap states in SrTiO 3 , extends carrier lifetime to 327.87 ns, and hinders carrier recombination through accelerated quantum decoherence.

title: Degradation of oxynitride based photoanodes
abstract: Investigating the current evolution of oxynitride photoanodes over time revealed two exponential decay processes. In-depth analysis of the showcase LaTiO 2 N identified surface oxidation and cocatalyst dissolution as causes of the current decrease.

title: Porous sulfur-rich polymers from spirobifluorene-derivatives via inverse vulcanization for efficient Hg 2+ capture
abstract: Direct single-step synthesis of microporous sulfur-rich polymers with high surface area and mercury capture properties.

title: Pressure driven interplay of Rashba, Rashba–Dresselhaus and persistent spin texture in lead-free quasi-2D hybrid perovskites
abstract: Hydrostatic pressure induces octahedral distortions leading to interplay of Rashba, Rashba–Dresselhaus, and persistent spin texture.

title: Decoupling mechanical and interfacial failure in silicon anodes via a rigid-flexible binder design for high-energy-density lithium-ion batteries
abstract: The rigid-flexible CMC/SBR binder builds a robust 3D network for Si anodes, synergistically improving structural integrity and interfacial stability, delivering high ICE, durable cycling, and outstanding full-cell stability (98.2% retention).

title: Polyamines with reactive CO 2 diffusion for carbon capture: the obvious and the unexpected
abstract: Polyamines have emerged as a leading materials platform for sorbents in direct air capture (DAC) of CO 2 and membranes for post-combustion capture (PCC) due to their reactions with CO 2 .

title: Scavenger-free and high-efficiency photoreduction of concentrated Cr( vi ) via MOF@COF heterojunctions: the role of interfacial charge transfer
abstract: This work demonstrates the superior photocatalytic performance of UiO-66-NH 2 @HDU-105 composites for hazardous Cr( vi ) detoxification, providing a robust strategy for heavy metal remediation via MOF-based heterostructures.

title: Nano-palladium dispersed double-shelled carbon hollow nanostructures: a novel approach to store hydrogen
abstract: The work explores a novel strategy for hydrogen storage under near-ambient conditions by synergistically combining nanoscale engineering techniques and the spillover mechanism.

title: Elucidating the carbochromism of K 3 [V(O 2 ) 4 ] with ab initio modeling
abstract: Direct Air Capture (DAC) reactions are increasingly being studied for their potential role in climate mitigation strategy.

title: MXenes as multifunctional mediators in lithium–sulfur batteries: a data-driven review
abstract: A meta-analysis of 200+ studies maps MXene strategies for Li–S batteries, linking composition and synthesis to stability and performance trends to guide next-gen battery design.

title: A single-phase high-entropy metal phosphide for efficient hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: A novel single-phase high-entropy metal phosphide (Mo 0.6 Co 0.5 Ni 0.4 Cu 0.3 Mn 0.2 P) is synthesized and it exhibits excellent HER performances for freshwater and seawater splitting.

title: Highly selective one-step CO 2 conversion to liquid alcohols through plasma synergistic MOF catalysis
abstract: Low-temperature plasma (LTP) assisted catalysis enables not only the conversion of CO 2 into liquid products under mild conditions, but also significant enhancement in catalytic efficiency through plasma–catalyst synergy.

title: Selective photocatalytic conversion of lignin via metal-doped anodic TiO 2 nanotubes
abstract: Lignin, an underutilized lignocellulosic biomass often considered waste, is a rich source of fuel and aromatic chemicals.

title: Machine learning discovery of medium-entropy thermoelectric materials with ultralow lattice thermal conductivity
abstract: Machine learning combined with DFT calculations enables rapid discovery of the medium entropy thermoelectric material In 2 Bi 4 Pb 4 Se 13 with ultralow lattice thermal conductivity.

title: Engineering pore-confined ultrafine Pt nanoparticles in hollow mesoporous carbon spheres for synergistically enhanced bifunctional electrocatalysis in sustainable hydrogen evolution and ethylene glycol oxidation
abstract: Pore-confinement influence of hollow mesoporous carbon spheres on the size and electronic structure of ultrafine Pt for enhanced electrocatalytic hydrogen evolution and ethylene glycol oxidation.

title: Femtosecond laser micro–nano technology for the construction of superhydrophilic/aerophobic electrodes for efficient full pH hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: CoP/fs-Mo electrodes with superhydrophilic/aerophobic properties show excellent HER activity over a wide pH range.

title: Reconstructing hydrogen bond networks to enable highly reversible iron metal anodes
abstract: Ace adsorption inhibits side reactions in Fe-based systems, enabling uniform Fe deposition without dendrite formation—overcoming key challenges of irregular deposition and ensuring enhanced stability for related applications.

title: Thermally self-crosslinkable polyetherimide dielectrics with superior high-temperature energy storage efficiency
abstract: The tailored crosslinking architecture functions as a trapping site to suppress charge migration and conduction loss under a high-temperature electric field. The decreased interchain spacing contributes to effectively restricting dipole rotation.

title: Jahn–Teller distortion in the oxygen evolution reaction: from fundamental insights to catalyst design
abstract: Electrocatalysts with Jahn–Teller distortion have been reviewed for the electrochemical oxygen evolution reaction involving basic fundamentals, characterization techniques, and their application.

title: Toward controllable CVD synthesis of graphenes on copper substrates: roles of oxygen species
abstract: The roles of oxygen-containing species in graphene CVD growth on copper substrate are reviewed to detail their catalysing and templating impact on morphology, layer number, domain configuration, stacking mode, and defect engineering of graphene.

title: Realizing ultralow lattice thermal conductivity in CuInTe 2 by controlled cation disorder
abstract: Cation-sublattice disorder engineered by CdTe alloying governs phonon transport in CuInTe 2 through the synergistic effects of defect scattering and lattice softening.

title: Dynamic origins of cation-modulated stability in tin-based perovskites revealed through combined fine-tuned machine learning interatomic potentials and experiments
abstract: Fine-tuned MLIP-MD reveals cation-dependent degradation of Sn-based perovskites, showing Cs suppresses water penetration, pristine FAMASnI 3 undergoes physical dissolution, and Rb triggers I − loss and Sn–O oxidation leading to severe structural failure.

title: X-ray imaging with AI-driven super-resolution deep learning for investigating battery electrode microstructural properties over cycling
abstract: A deep learning framework converts X-ray computed tomography (XCT) images with low-resolution and large field of view (FoV) into XCT images with high-resolution and large FoV, enabling visualization of fine microstructure over representative areas.

title: PVDF nanofiber separator with sustained LiNO 3 additive release for dendrite-suppressed anode-free lithium metal batteries
abstract: Sustained additive release from the PP/PVDF/LiNO 3 separator overcomes LiNO 3 insolubility, enabling dendrite-suppressed anode-free batteries.

title: High temperature grain boundary resistance in Yb 14 (Mg,Mn)Sb 11 thermoelectrics
abstract: Grain boundary resistance was found to limit thermoelectric performance in Yb 14 MgSb 11 from room temperature to 1200 K. No similar resistance was observed in Yb 14 MnSb 11 , demonstrating the importance of composition in engineering boundary behavior.

title: A sustainable bioinspired nano-assembly of live marine bacteria for mineralization of phenothiazine dye
abstract: A bioinspired nano-assembly of live marine bacteria enables efficient mineralization of phenothiazine dye, highlighting a sustainable approach for microbial–nanomaterial-assisted wastewater remediation.

title: Sustainable dual usage of spray-pyrolyzed Sb:SnO 2 thin films for pollutant degradation and power generation
abstract: Minimal material loaded Sb-doped SnO 2 thin film degraded large volume of organic dye and also served as a MFC cathode, delivering high bioelectricity and significant COD reduction in wastewater treatment.

title: Three-dimensional ordered macroporous Mn–Ce composite oxide catalysts with excellent low-temperature toluene oxidation performance: synergistic effect and reaction mechanism
abstract: A set of Mn–Ce composite catalysts with three-dimensionally ordered macroporous (3DOM) structures were synthesized via the PMMA colloidal crystal templating approach.

title: Electrochemical germane (GeH 4 ) synthesis via dual-descriptor screening and integrated theoretical-experimental validation
abstract: Integrated DFT-based dual-descriptor screening (*Ge and *GeH) and experiments identify Cu as an optimal catalyst for selective electrochemical GeH 4 synthesis from GeO 2 , linking thermodynamics and kinetics to design guidance.

title: Enhancing high-temperature capacitive energy storage performance of BOPP films via constructing a dual-barrier structure
abstract: Experiments reveal the high-temperature failure mechanism of BOPP film. A double barrier structure was constructed to impede carrier injection into the dielectric. The energy storage density of the composite film is 365% that of BOPP film.

title: Bifunctional catalyst directed closed-pore engineering in hard carbon for enhanced sodium storage
abstract: A bifunctional “catalysis-self-templating” strategy is proposed to engineer closed-pore rich hard carbon. The resulting anode exhibits a remarkable reversible capacity of 432.3 mA h g −1 with a high plateau capacity.

title: Selection of a single atom for surface charge modulation to promote CO 2 activation and stabilize the *COOH intermediate for solar fuels
abstract: Valence electron count and electronegativity are used as descriptors to rationally anchor single atoms on WO 3 . This optimizes surface charge localization and β-CO 2 formation, significantly boosting photocatalytic CO 2 reduction efficiency.

title: Atomic layer deposition in proton exchange membrane fuel cells: precision control from catalysts to membrane electrode assemblies
abstract: ALD serves as a transformative tool for PEMFCs engineering, enabling atomic-scale design of core-shell catalysts, functional membranes, and GDLs interfaces, bridging lab-scale innovations to high-performance, durable MEAs.

title: Interfacial functionalization of porous Li 0.33 La 0.557 TiO 3 enabling fast lithium-ion transport in solid polymer electrolytes
abstract: Incorporating inorganic electrolyte fillers within solid polymer electrolytes (SPEs) is generally considered an efficient strategy for constructing rapid Li + transport channels, thereby markedly enhancing ionic conductivity.

title: Interfacial stress limits PbTe module reliability: porous Fe foam mitigates thermal-mismatch stress
abstract: A compliant porous Fe-foam interlayer mitigates interfacial thermal stress, enabling stable and efficient operation of PbTe thermoelectric modules under repeated thermal cycling.

title: Enhancing bifunctional electrocatalytic performance of electrodeposited medium entropy alloy films by configurational entropy engineering
abstract: Potentiostatic electrodeposition of MEA films and their application as electrocatalysts for overall water splitting, delivering efficient performance and sustained stability.

title: Mechanistic insights into defect-governed ion migration and phase instability in mixed-halide perovskites
abstract: We reveal defect-governed ion migration in FAPb(I 1− x Br x ) 3 cells: dark transport is dominated by halide interstitials, whereas illumination activates FA-cation migration correlated with deep-level defect density.

title: Nanocomposites with tunable dielectric properties: BaTiO 3 loaded cellulose nanocrystal-liquid crystalline polymers
abstract: Self-assembled nanocomposites: quasi spherical BaTiO 3 within a cellulose-liquid crystalline polymer.

title: Enhanced photocatalytic hydrogen evolution via the optimization of interfacial energy and mass flows
abstract: A biphasic system converts liquid water to water vapor via the photothermal effect, boosting photocatalytic H 2 evolution.

title: Cu-decorated Sc 2 CFN monolayer for efficient CO 2 capture and activation
abstract: The efficient reduction of CO 2 is essential for mitigating carbon emissions and advancing sustainable energy technologies.

title: Engineering ordered small-molecule assemblies for photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: This review presents a multi-scale design framework for ordered small-molecule assemblies, highlighting how molecular features, supramolecular arrangement, and exciton dynamics jointly govern charge separation and photocatalytic hydrogen evolution.

title: TiO 2 /Cu 2 O heterojunction embedded PDMS composite with superhydrophobic surface for efficient raindrop energy harvesting
abstract: Boosting droplet TENGs via TiO 2 /Cu 2 O p–n heterojunctions in PDMS (high dielectric constant, built‑in field) plus fluorination for trapping yields 3.4 mW, lighting 400 LEDs and sensor.

title: pH-activated dynamic ionomer-acetal networks for self-healing and recyclable electrophoretic acrylic coatings
abstract: Ionomer–acetal networks forming uniform pH-activated self-healable coatings via EPD. Silane-modified MWCNT-induced roughness enhances anti-corrosion, delays icing, and enables rapid deicing with recyclable, re-coatable behavior.

title: From a toxic pollutant to a catalyst: one-pot synthesis of Fe 3 O 4 -Ni-DBP/NF for efficient overall water splitting
abstract: Schematic synthesis route of the one-pot preparation of Fe 3 O 4 -Ni-DBP on an NF substrate.

title: The cross-boundary catalytic effect of anions and cations enhances the hydrogen storage performance of magnesium-based hydrogen storage materials
abstract: MgH 2 offer several advantages, including a high hydrogen storage density of up to 7.6 wt%, making them highly attractive for solid-state hydrogen storage applications. Equipment images reproduced with permission, see paper for details.

title: All-polymer heterojunction nanoparticles enable reproducible and enhanced hydrogen evolution photocatalysis
abstract: Replacing small-molecule acceptors (Y6) with polymerized analogues (PJ1-R) in organic nanoparticles yields a 2.5× increase in photocatalytic hydrogen evolution with much enhanced reproducibility due to reduced crystallinity and better intermixing.

title: Oxygen release/incorporation behavior in hexagonal perovskite BaFeO 3 explored by 18 O/ 16 O isotope exchange reactions
abstract: The 18 O-isotope exchange was successful for the 12R-type hexagonal perovskite, BaFeO 3 . This reaction occurred at temperatures as low as 400 °C, which suggests oxygen-ion mobility at such low temperatures.

title: The redox chemistry of La 0.5 Sr 0.5 Cr 0.2 Mn 0.8 O 3− δ and its application in high capacity anodes of oxygen ion batteries
abstract: Solid-state oxygen ion batteries (OIBs) are a novel technology for electrochemical energy storage, based on the exchange of oxygen between two mixed-conducting oxide electrodes via an oxide ion-conducting electrolyte.

title: Mixed-anion electrolytes: from bulk speciation to interfacial dynamics in divalent metal electrodeposition
abstract: Mixing anions is a promising strategy for multivalent electrolyte design, tunning the bulk cation solvation structure to improve the electrochemical processes in applications such as metal depositions for batteries and catalysis.

title: Floatable ionic photothermal aerogels for water and energy harvesting under solar light
abstract: Sunlight-driven steam and electricity generation using calcined loofah sponge and sulphonated graphene oxide in phosphate-PVA–CMC matrix; evaporation rate of 2.8 kg m −2 h −1 , output voltage of 0.64 V.

title: Imparting dispersibility and electrochemical activity to chlorine-terminated MXenes via oxygen enrichment
abstract: MXenes with mixed chlorine–oxygen terminations show enhanced dispersibility, oxidation resistance, and anodic stability, enabling robust solution processing.

title: Design of binder-free slurry electrodes for lithium-ion batteries via identification of electronic and ionic transport networks
abstract: Design principles for binder-free electrodes enabled by quantitative evaluation of electronic and ionic transport networks via impedance and DRT analysis.

title: Efficient electron channels at the TiO 2 /BaTiO 3 nanodot interface for enhanced CO 2 photoreduction
abstract: Unraveling electron transport channels across S-scheme heterostructures constitutes a pivotal research focus in advancing photocatalysis.

title: Atomistic insights into the phase stability of high-Na-content P2 layered cathodes
abstract: Transition-metal-modulated Na site preference energy enables the design of high-Na-content P2 layered oxide cathodes.

title: Simple additive strategy to boost initial coulombic efficiency by mitigating PTFE decomposition in dry battery electrodes
abstract: Dry-processed battery electrodes with additives suppress PTFE decomposition, enhancing initial coulombic efficiency and cycling stability through hydrogen-bond-assisted LUMO elevation and a preserved fibrillation network.

title: Carbon-based catalysts for the hydrazine oxidation reaction: a promising low-energy route for hydrogen generation beyond conventional water splitting
abstract: The applicability of carbon-based electrocatalysts for the hydrazine oxidation reaction.

title: Composite solid electrolytes based on nickel-doped lithium aluminum silicate ceramics enable lithium metal batteries with enhanced interfacial stability and high-rate capability
abstract: Ni-doped Li‑aluminosilicate ceramics were developed to fabricate PMPV-LANSO-LiTFSI composite solid electrolyte, which enables stable Li symmetric cycling (400 h) and high-performance full batteries, showing low-cost promise for Li metal batteries.

title: In situ interfacial assembly of P-MXene@COF hybrids for flame-retardant and smoke-suppressive epoxy resin
abstract: In situ interfacial assembly of P-MXene@COF enhances the flame retardancy and smoke suppression of epoxy resin via gas-phase radical quenching and condensed-phase shielding, while maintaining mechanical properties.

title: Extending the electrochemical stability window of Ti 3 C 2 T z MXenes via Ta–Hf co-substitution for durable proton exchange membrane fuel cell catalyst supports
abstract: MXenes are an emerging class of two-dimensional transition metal carbides with surface chemistry tunability, and high surface area, making them promising candidates for electrocatalyst support materials in proton exchange membrane fuel cells.

title: New MXene-derived anthracene-based metal–organic framework with controllable morphology for high-performance sensing
abstract: This work emphasizes the advantages of MXene-derived MOFs and the impact of framework nanoscale morphology on enhancing MOF-based sensor performance.

title: Highly flexible, strong and dynamic recovery solid-solid phase change materials based on polythiourethane–polyurethane for thermal management
abstract: Dynamic TCB-crosslinked SSPCMs achieve zero leakage, tunable phase change (15.2–51.5 °C), high latent heat (77.0 J g −1 ), robust mechanics (28.3 MPa strength, 976% ductility), and >90% recyclability for sustainable thermal management.

title: Carrier scattering considerations and thermoelectric power factors of half-Heuslers
abstract: The thermoelectric (TE) PF transport properties of 13 n-type and p-type half-Heusler alloys are computationally examined using Boltzmann transport and ab initio scattering rate considerations.

title: Can recycled e-waste polymers power the future of sensors?
abstract: Recycled polymers from e-waste are good candidate materials for sensors from the point of view of circularity and the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

title: Medium-entropy iron-series metal sulfide for energy-saving hydrogen production and highly-efficient direct hydrazine borane fuel cells
abstract: The medium-entropy CoNiFeS catalyst exhibits splendid catalytic performance for hydrazine borane oxidation reaction, which is crucial for highly-efficient direct hydrazine borane fuel cells and energy-saving HBOR‖HER electrolyzers for H 2 production.

title: Piezoelectric and triboelectric synergies in MXene/BaTiO 3 /PAN composite nanofiber membranes for self-powered sensors
abstract: This study develops an MXene/BaTiO 3 /PAN composite nanofiber membrane prepared using electrospinning technology for self-powered sensor applications.

title: Interfacial regulation with 2D MoN nanosheets enables the development of dendrite-free lithium metal anodes
abstract: A lamellar Li/MoN composite anode fabricated by a facile folding-rolling method enables uniform Li deposition and accommodates volume change. The inorganic-rich SEI and fast Li + diffusion kinetics yield exceptional cyclability in 1 Ah pouch cells.

title: Controlled reconstruction of F-doped NiFe-LDH into a hierarchical electrocatalyst for saline water oxidation: elucidating the dual role of fluorine in surface stability and active phase formation
abstract: This work demonstrates the controlled reconstruction of F-doped NiFe LDH on Ni foam (F-LDH/NF) into a hierarchical F-doped NiFeOOH–NiFe LDH/NF architecture (R-F-LDH/NF), achieving exceptional OER performance in saline media.

title: Electronic Frontiers of borophene: a computational mini-review on properties and emerging applications
abstract: Computational insights from DFT and MD reveal how borophene polymorphism governs performance in batteries and electrocatalysis, offering guidance for structure-driven design while addressing stability and scalability challenges.

title: A glucose-mediated independent pyrolysis strategy optimises the pore structure of MOF-derived carbon catalysts to promote the conversion of HMF to FDCA
abstract: A glucose-mediated independent pyrolysis strategy is utilized to modulate the pore structure of Cu-based MOF-derived carbon catalysts, enabling the efficient conversion of HMF to FDCA.

title: In situ alloying reaction for constructing rich magnesiophilic sites toward highly stable and high-rate rechargeable magnesium batteries
abstract: A vacuum thermal evaporation technology constructs an ultra-flat Bi nanoparticle interface on the Mg (Bi@Mg), which forms an in situ Mg 3 Bi 2 alloy layer with magnesiophilic sites to accelerate Mg 2+ transport and induce uniform Mg deposition.

title: CO 2 reduction on doped-ceria electrodes: controlling CO selectivity and coking with applied bias
abstract: On doped ceria, operando studies decouple electrochemical CO from rWGS. Ce 3+ sites promote CO formation. Bias controls CO yield in the overall syngas composition via creation of Ce 3+ sites. But excessive Ce 3+ promotes CO-mediated coking in PDC/GDC.

title: Advances in layered cathodes for nonaqueous aluminum-ion batteries: structure engineering and future prospects
abstract: Rechargeable aluminum-ion batteries (AIBs) have emerged as promising candidates for next-generation energy storage systems owing to their high theoretical capacity, inherent safety, and cost-effectiveness.

title: Inhibiting Jahn–Teller distortion of δ-MnO 2 cathode using 3d and 4d block elements doping to improve the aqueous zinc ion battery performance
abstract: This table of contents highlights the effect of 3d and 4d block ion doping (Fe 3+ , Mo 6+ , Rh 3+ ) into a δ MnO 2 cathode material, demonstrating suppression of Mn 3+ -induced Jahn–Teller distortion effect.

title: All-solid-state lithium-oxygen batteries with high areal capacity and fast rate capability enabled by amorphous lithium peroxide
abstract: This work reports all-solid-state Li–O 2 batteries, using a composite polymer electrolyte, achieving high areal capacity, fast kinetics, high efficiency, and long cycle life. Film-like amorphous Li 2 O 2 is observed, guiding next-generation designs.

title: Resolving the true sodium storage sites in hard carbon: the essential role of hydrogen termination
abstract: This study establishes realistic defect structure models that incorporate H-termination. Utilizing these models through first-principles calculations, we determine the atomic configurations of Na storage sites under realistic conditions.

title: Combining chemical, geometric, and novel topological features to develop generalizable machine learning models for predicting mechanically stable MOFs
abstract: We present a generalizable, interpretable ML model to predict MOF bulk modulus using novel topological and chemical features. Trained on diverse data, it enables high-throughput screening of ∼435k MOFs for mechanical stability.

title: Sustainable poly(butylene furanoate)/carbon nanotube composite fibers for wearable thermoelectric generators
abstract: Herein, a sustainable composite fiber is developed by bio-based polymer poly(butylene furanoate) (PBF) and single-walled carbon nanotubes (SWCNTs) via wet spinning, enabling a wearable thermoelectric generator with a voltage of 1.5 mV on human wrist.

title: Two-dimensional mercury( ii )–acetylide framework with built-in dipole field for efficient photocatalytic CO 2 -to-CO conversion
abstract: A 2D Hg( ii )-acetylide framework with coherently aligned dipole moments creates a built-in electric field. It achieves 338.57 μmol g −1 h −1 CO evolution with 65% selectivity via synergistic piezoelectric-photocatalytic charge separation.

title: Tuning conjugated chain ends in small molecular acceptors for enhancing molecular packing in binary blend acceptors and inducing high efficiency in planar junction organic photovoltaics
abstract: PM6/L8-BO:Y5-IC-TT devices achieve 19.3% PCE (17.4% for PM6/L8-BO). Y5-IC-TT's small dihedral angles induce large blend binding energies in parallel/antiparallel orientations, resulting in a larger packing coherence length.

title: Synergistic band engineering and ferroelectric polarization in Sn/S co-doped BaTiO 3 for integrated photovoltaic energy storage
abstract: This study innovatively proposes an approach to directly store the energy generated by the photovoltaic effect within the material itself by leveraging its inherent energy storage properties.

title: Framework-to-carbon conversion strategy for nitrogen–silicon co-doped microporous carbons with superior energy storage performance
abstract: Silicon-containing covalent triazine frameworks are carbonized into N/Si co-doped microporous carbons with hierarchical porosity, high conductivity, and excellent supercapacitor performance.

title: Effect of preconditioning on cycling performance of aqueous Zn-ion batteries
abstract: In this study, we provide valuable insights into the fast rate preconditioning effects on optimization of a zinc ion (Zn–MnO 2 ) battery performance for long duration.

title: Hybrid solid electrolytes by design: architectural pathways toward next-generation solid-state batteries
abstract: Conventional solid-state electrolytes often suffer from limited ionic conductivity and poor interfacial compatibility, motivating the development of hybrid solid electrolytes (HSEs) that combine the flexibility of polymers with the stability...

title: Controlling {001} facet exposure in TiO nanoflakes for enhanced polysulfide trapping in lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: TiO nanoflakes with {001} exposed facets have been successfully synthesized through a magnesiothermic reduction reaction. Used for separator modification, they exhibit excellent blocking and catalytic effects on the shuttle and conversion of LiPSs.

title: Quinone redox centers enable charge localization and oxygen activation for high-efficiency H 2 O 2 photosynthesis in covalent organic frameworks
abstract: Quinone-engineered π-conjugated COFs promote charge separation and O 2 activation for selective two-electron photosynthesis of H 2 O 2 .

title: Benchmarking operando neutron diffraction for high-power Li-ion batteries
abstract: Neutron powder diffraction probes structural changes and lithium distribution in battery electrodes, but operando measurements are challenging due to the need for both reliable electrochemistry and sufficient material for good signal-to-noise.

title: Noncompetitive sequential Co–Fe coordination for high-density Fe/Co dual-atom catalysts toward efficient acidic oxygen reduction
abstract: Sequential Co–Fe coordination enables high-density dual-atom synergy, favoring a dissociative ORR pathway.

title: Flexible interfacial modification layers towards enhanced performance in LATP-based all-solid-state batteries
abstract: A flexible PEO/LiTFSI/LZP interlayer on LATP via drop-solution improved interfacial kinetics and stability of the LATP pellet. This raised the CCD to 1.58 mA cm −2 and enabled stable Li/LFP all-solid-state battery cycling for 250 cycles at 0.5C.

title: Understanding the degradation process in zinc–iodine hybrid flow batteries
abstract: Zinc–iodine hybrid flow batteries (ZIHFBs) represent promising stationary energy storage with a theoretically high volumetric capacity (>250 Ah L −1 ).

title: Understanding the spin effects of single-atom alloys on the electrocatalytic nitrogen reduction reaction
abstract: DFT-calculated adsorption energies and features of single-atom alloys are used for machine learning. Spin-dependent features identified by machine learning are rationalized by fundamental chemical principles and used to understand spin effects.

title: Selective amphiphilic polyelectrolyte complex bilayers with sub-nanometer effective pore sizes and high permeance
abstract: We introduce a family of membranes formed by coating two layers of oppositely-charged amphiphilic polyelectrolytes on a porous support, creating a complex. These membranes offer high organics rejection, high permeance, and tunable selectivity.

title: Molecular-geometry-guided lithium salt initiator strategy for high-performance poly(1,3-dioxolane) electrolytes in lithium metal batteries
abstract: A molecular-geometry-guided strategy identifies LiPO 2 F 2 as an optimal initiator for a modified in situ polymerized PDOL electrolyte with the near-planar PO 2 F intermediate.

title: Pressure–temperature driven phase transition and optoelectronic improvement in lead-free hybrid perovskite MASnI 3
abstract: Pressure- and temperature-driven phase transitions in MASnI₃ were revealed by in situ synchrotron XRD analysis, showing a tetragonal-to-orthorhombic II transformation accompanied by local structural distortion and short-range order evolution.

title: Lewis-acid-engineered polymer electrolytes for all-solid-state lithium batteries
abstract: Poly(ethylene oxide) (PEO)-based solid-state polymer electrolytes are promising for all-solid-state lithium metal batteries owing to their intrinsic flexibility, cost-effectiveness, and facile processability. However, their practical application is hindered by low...

title: Morphology-Controlled Monolithic SrTiO 3 /Ti Nanocatalysts with Dispersed Rh Single Atoms and Clusters for Highly Selective Photocatalytic CO 2 Reduction to CH 4
abstract: In this work, morphology-controlled SrTiO 3 /Ti film catalysts were constructed on Ti foil via a two-step hydrothermal strategy, followed by the introduction of highly dispersed Rh species to regulate the photocatalytic...

title: Computational Discovery of Tetragonal MBene Phase: Diamond-Shaped Boron Lattices, Viable MAB Precursors, and Selective CO 2 Reduction
abstract: MBenes are a class of two-dimensional transition-metal borides derived from layered MAB phases. Here we use first-principles calculations to predict a new tetragonal MB (1:1) MBene phase featuring a diamond-shaped...

title: Radical-trapping-enabled ultra-durable erythritol for high-performance mid-temperature thermal energy storage
abstract: Erythritol, with a high melting enthalpy of ~340 J/g, is promising for mid-temperature thermal energy storage, but suffers from poor thermal stability due to oxidative chain reactions on its hydroxyl...

title: High-rate laminar Zn plating in aqueous zinc batteries: from S-PANI grafted bacterial cellulose to a stable metal anode
abstract: A self-assembled BC scaffold, derived from natural sources, regulates Zn 2+ electrodeposition in rechargeable aqueous zinc-ion batteries (RAZIBs).

title: 10.6% Efficient solution-processed Cu 2 ZnSnS 4 solar cells via cation substitutions and Li doping
abstract: Copper–zinc–tin–sulfide (Cu 2 ZnSnS 4 , CZTS) kesterites a promising earth-abundant and non-toxic absorber for next-generation thin-film photovoltaics.

title: Micropore engineering of biomass-derived carbon for durable, high-loading aqueous all-organic pouch batteries
abstract: Molecule-specific micropore engineering of biomass-derived activated carbons enables high quinone utilization and exceptional durability in aqueous all-organic batteries, achieving ∼17 Wh kg −1 and 99.75% capacity retention over 3000 cycles.

title: Construction of Janus particles from single-domain ferroelectric PbTiO 3 nanoplates for synergistic photocatalytic H 2 evolution and value-added organic transformation
abstract: Janus particles are widely considered ideal photocatalysts for solar-to-chemical conversions since charge carriers can be directionally separated to opposite sites and back reactions can be suppressed. Nevertheless, there are very...

title: Bubble evolution dynamics and interfacial management strategies for high-flux water electrolysis: a review
abstract: The review bridges fundamental bubble lifecycle dynamics (left) with active and passive field-induced management strategies (right) to enable high-efficiency water electrolysis.

title: Atomic-scale mapping of dissolution/deposition pathways in MnO 2 polymorphs for aqueous Zn–MnO 2 batteries
abstract: Atomic-scale mapping of dissolution/deposition pathways in MnO 2 polymorphs for aqueous Zn–MnO 2 batteries.

title: Electrochemo-mechanical coupled behaviors in sodium-ion batteries upon stack pressure
abstract: Owing to their low cost, environmental friendliness, and high safety, sodium-ion batteries (SIBs) are considered promising alternatives to lithium-ion batteries (LIBs).

title: CO 2 electroreduction to ratio-tunable syngas with Cu–In–Ag electrodes: from electronic structure regulation to mechanistic understanding
abstract: Developing ternary metal catalysts for tunable syngas from CO 2 electroreduction enables efficient conversion of waste CO 2 to fuel, supporting a sustainable future.

title: Voltage-driven surface reconstruction of ternary liquid metals for enhanced electrocatalytic CO 2 conversion
abstract: A ternary GaInBi liquid-alloy catalyst was engineered, demonstrating superior reactivity for CO 2 electrocatalytic conversion to formate.

title: Reactor-based approach for achieving high-silica zeolites: a fed-batch strategy
abstract: Distinct features of the Fed-batch reactor facilitate the synthesis of high silica zeolites, which are key elements in sustainable catalysis.

title: Green and Controllable Synthesis of Large-Scale High-Quality Two-Dimensional Boron Nitride by Ambient-Pressure Chemical Vapor Deposition
abstract: Although two-dimensional (2D) hexagonal boron nitride (h-BN) has been synthesized on metal foil by chemical vapor deposition (CVD) for several years, toxic precursors and low pressure are still used during...

title: Pr 6 O 11 -driven electron modulation via d–f orbital hybridization for alkaline seawater electrolysis
abstract: Pr 6 O 11 -induced d–f orbital hybridization endows CoFeOOH with high activity, robust corrosion resistance, and excellent durability, thus promoting efficient and stable alkaline seawater electrolysis.

title: Research progress and future challenges of CO oxidation catalysts: preparation, catalytic performance, reaction mechanism and anti-poisoning strategies
abstract: This review summarizes the progress and challenges in CO catalytic oxidation, outlines the properties and design strategies of metal-based catalysts, and discusses the causes of catalyst poisoning and solution strategies.

title: Electronic-structure regulation of graphdiyne-supported dual-atom catalysts drives efficient urea synthesis from CO 2 and N 2
abstract: The electronic-structure regulation of graphdiyne-supported dual-atom catalysts enables efficient and highly selective electrochemical urea synthesis from CO 2 and N 2 via a descriptor-guided design strategy.

title: Additive-mediated morphology regulation: a critical strategy toward efficient and stable organic solar cells
abstract: This review summarizes the research progress on additives for NFA OSCs, covering their classification based on halogen content, working mechanisms, and design principles, to guide the development of eco-friendly and simple-structure additives.

title: Fe3C/Fe Nanoparticles Embedded in N-Doped Carbon as Catalysts for Electrocatalytic Nitrogen Reduction to Ammonia
abstract: The electrocatalytic nitrogen reduction reaction (NRR) offers a sustainable route for ammonia synthesis but is severely hindered by the inertness of N₂, its low solubility, and the competing hydrogen evolution...

title: Unveiling the role of electrode architecture and illumination direction in heterojunction photo-supercapacitors performance and charge storage mechanism
abstract: Five engineered photo-supercapacitors demonstrate how electrode symmetry, heterojunction design, and illumination geometry control light-driven charge storage, enabling highly efficient solar-charging devices.

title: Redox-active metal–organic framework as an anode-active material for rechargeable aqueous manganese dioxide batteries with very high-rate capabilities
abstract: The current work demonstrates rechargeable aqueous metal–organic framework manganese dioxide batteries exhibiting high-rate capabilities.

title: High-quality Cu–Al nanocrystals for enhanced CO 2 electroreduction
abstract: Cu–Al nanocrystals with dominant (111) facets were prepared using the CVD method. Specifically, Cu 96 Al 4 nanocrystals exhibit superior CO 2 RR performance due to synergistic effects. Meanwhile, a clear structure–activity relationship was established.

title: Synergistic W and Ti dual doping for P2/tunnel intergrowth formation: a pathway to high stability sodium ion battery cathodes
abstract: Ti and W dual doping combined with a two-step calcination approach promoted the formation of P2/tunnel intergrowth. This strategy produces cathodes with a capacity retention over 80% after 435 cycles at 5C for sodium ion batteries.

title: Evaluating aqueous zinc electrodeposition and stripping at pyrolytic carbon electrodes with tunable surface functionality
abstract: Heteroatom doping of conformal nanometric coatings of pyrolytic carbon (pyC) deposited on two- and three-dimensional substrates produces electrodes that deliver highly reversible Zn metal electroplating/stripping in mild aqueous electrolyte.

title: Synergistic regulation of ester side chains and thermal annealing to enhance electrical conductivity in diketopyrrolopyrrole-based conjugated polymers for thermoelectric generation
abstract: Incorporation of a 10% fraction of polar ester side chains into the all-alkyl polymer PDPPB-0 yields a remarkable >3-fold enhancement in the power factor of PDPPC6B-10 and PDPPO4B-10.

title: Amphiphilic silane-MXene hybrids for high-performance flexible symmetric supercapacitors via additive-free processing
abstract: Flexible and wearable microsupercapacitors demand MXene electrodes with strong substrate adhesion, high conductivity, and long-term mechanical durability—yet conventional MXene inks, relying on polymeric binders, suffer from conductivity loss and delamination due to weak interfacial interactions.

title: Superior-performance InSe/CdS van der Waals heterojunction photodetector for self-powered imaging
abstract: The InSe/CdS van der Waals heterojunction photodetector not only achieves outstanding photoconductive detection performance but also demonstrates exceptional self-powered operation characteristics and excellent low-power imaging capabilities.

title: Tensile strain engineering on Cu nano-dots for high efficiency HMFOR at low potential
abstract: HMFOR dynamics can be regulated by the strengthened *OCHO adsorption on Cu catalysts with controlled lattice tensile.

title: Highly loaded fine PtCo intermetallic compounds on 3D N-doped porous graphene for enhanced and sustained efficiency in PEMFCs
abstract: The L1 0 -PtCo/HGNG catalyst surpasses the 2025 durability benchmarks set by the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) for both the catalyst and its support.

title: Bioinspired poly(acrylic acid)-regulated crosslinked self-healing, quasi-solid polymer electrolytes for flexible supercapacitor applications
abstract: The synergistic interaction driven by the PAA-based synaptic architecture establishes coPUAA as a promising QSPE for flexible supercapacitors.

title: Growth behavior and interface engineering for photovoltaic applications of co-evaporated Sb 2 Se 3 thin films on Mo foil
abstract: NaOH-treated Mo foil induces a MoSe 2 interlayer that controls Sb 2 Se 3 microstructure, transforming a compact absorber into a pseudo-3D architecture with enhanced grain growth, reduces defects, and improved performance in flexible solar cells.

title: Donor–acceptor engineering in conjugated polymer photocatalysts: thieno[3, 2- b ]thiophene-dibenzothiophene sulfone copolymers for noble-metal-free visible-light hydrogen evolution
abstract: Extending the oligothiophene linkers (T → DT → TT) in the D–A polymer TPA-BTDO enhances H 2 evolution by improving coplanarity and reducing charge recombination, with TPA-TT-BTDO achieving HER rates of 101.2 (visible) and 125 mmol h −1 g −1 (sunlight).

title: Integration of chemical component control and a pillared parallel structure for efficient water electrolysis
abstract: Hierarchical bi-metal phosphide electrocatalysts were fabricated with interlayer pillared parallel nanosheet arrays, which can suppress structural deformation and promote rapid gas release to achieve high activity and stability for water splitting.

title: Regulating hydrogen spillover in Pt/M-ZrO 2 catalysed reductive N -methylation of aniline under mild conditions
abstract: Hydrogen spillover, regulated by a high density of surface acidic and basic sites, enhances the low-temperature catalytic activity of Pt/M-ZrO 2 , achieving a near-quantitative yield of NNDMA and outperforming previous studies in turnover frequency.

title: Interface-engineered Z-scheme CoIn 2 S 4 /MoSe 2 heterostructure for enhanced charge separation toward bifunctional photocatalytic H 2 production and O 2 reduction
abstract: An interface-engineered CoIn 2 S 4 /MoSe 2 heterostructure is developed. The constructed Z-scheme junction promotes enhanced charge separation/transfer by easing mass-transfer limits for efficient bifunctional photocatalytic activity towards ORR and HER.

title: Dual-axis interfacial engineering of carbon paper hosts for stable anode-less lithium metal batteries
abstract: A 3D lithiophilic carbon paper host (MgO-preSEI-CP) for anode-less batteries is fabricated via a one-step annealing process, enabling stable bottom-up Li plating.

title: Upcycling discarded photovoltaic backsheets into a polyvinylidene fluoride protective layer for high-performance lithium-ion batteries
abstract: This work achieves the efficient fabrication of a CuO@PVDF nanocomposite by coating CuO nanoparticles with PVDF recovered from discarded photovoltaic backsheets, which transforms injurant into high-value energy storage materials.

title: Intercalation mechanism of C 2 N/blue phosphorene for Li-ion batteries: ultrahigh capacity, fast kinetics and improved compatibility with FSI/FEC electrolytes
abstract: The C 2 N/Blue phosphorene nanocomposite synergistically integrates the advantages of BlueP and C 2 N, overcoming their intrinsic limitations to achieve enhanced interfacial coupling, structural stability, and improved lithium-ion storage performance.

title: Machine learning-assisted screening of copper–cerium bimetallic catalysts for tetracycline degradation
abstract: Machine learning-guided design of CeCu bimetal catalysts tunes the d-band center, enhancing OH adsorption and boosting catalytic activity in advanced oxidation systems.

title: Self-powered cathodic corrosion prevention using triboelectric nanogenerators
abstract: This review summarizes recent advances in TENGenabled cathodic protection, covering self-powered and direct TENG systems, DC-TENG architectures, self-healing materials, dualmode outputs, and multilayer strategies for sustainable corrosion mitigation.

title: Bisphosphate group molecules contribute to the performance of wide-bandgap perovskite solar cells
abstract: The bis-phosphonic acid groups of 1,4-phenylenebis(phosphonic acid) passivate defects at the buried interface, enabling 1.66 eV WBG PSCs with a high V oc of 1.195 V, 21.79% efficiency, and enhanced stability.

title: Synergistic modification of BN and TiO 2 on polyethersulfone multilayered dielectrics for enhanced elevated-temperature energy storage performance
abstract: Rational design of PESU/filler interfaces effectively stagnates electrical tree growth, achieving superior high-temperature breakdown strength and energy storage.

title: Bridging the thermal expansion gap in solid oxide fuel cells: towards robust and efficient energy conversion
abstract: Thermal expansion mismatch critically impacts SOFC durability. This review examines the fundamental mechanisms of thermal expansion in SOFC materials and discusses strategies for tailoring thermal expansion to enhance mechanical compatibility.

title: Surface defect engineering toward efficient photocatalytic NO removal
abstract: Surface defect engineering, including the creation of surface vacancies, heteroatom doping and other defect strategies, is leveraged to enable the efficient photocatalytic conversion of NO into N 2 , NH 3 , and NO 3 − .

title: Constructing an S-scheme heterojunction between defect modified MnCdS-V S /Se and H-GDY for enhanced photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: In this study, S-vacancies were engineered in Se-doped MCS, and H-GDY was introduced to construct an S-scheme heterojunction, thus enhancing photocatalytic hydrogen evolution. The mechanism was clarified using combined techniques.

title: Atomic-level interfacial engineering of a Zr-In 2 S 3 /g-C 3 N 4 Z-scheme heterojunction for enhanced photocatalytic CO 2 reduction
abstract: In situ growth of Zr-In 2 S 3 on g-C 3 N 4 nanosheets forms an atomic-level Zr–N coordinated heterojunction, enabling efficient electron transfer for photocatalytic CO 2 reduction to CO/CH 4 .

title: Photosensitization by [Mo 3 S 13 ] 2− nanocluster cocatalysts enabling visible-light photocatalytic H 2 evolution
abstract: The photosensitization effect of[Mo 3 S 13 ] 2− nanocluster cocatalysts was unveiled by rationally incorporating them into a wide-bandgap ZnS host, leading to active and sustainable visible-light photocatalytic H 2 evolution.

title: Polymer dielectrics enabled by molecular engineering design and charge trap regulation for high-temperature energy storage
abstract: The FPI dielectric film with 0.10 wt% MgO achieves a discharge energy density ( U d ) of 7.15 J cm −3 at room temperature and 4.97 J cm −3 at 150 °C, demonstrating great promise for advanced energy storage capacitors.

title: Pt single atoms/g-C 3 N 4 photocatalysts enabling simultaneous H 2 production and CO 2 absorption through formic acid photoreforming
abstract: A Pt single-atom catalyst anchored on g-C 3 N 4 enables efficient formic acid oxidation.

title: Structural regulation of ionic covalent organic frameworks for enhanced CO 2 capture and catalytic conversion
abstract: Neutral COFs were engineered into high-performance ionic COFs via quaternization. They feature tunable active sites, high surface areas, exceptional stability, and outstanding CO 2 capture capacity and catalytic efficiency in CO 2 cycloaddition.

title: Revealing the effects of NbH x ( x = 1, 2)/MgH 2 interfaces on the dehydrogenation of MgH 2 from first-principles
abstract: First-principles calculations are used to study the effects of NbH x phase on hydrogen release from MgH 2 based on the interface model.

title: Ga alloying-induced electronic modulation of Pt synergized with COFs for boosting photocatalytic CO 2 reduction
abstract: Ga alloying modifies the electronic structure of Pt nanoparticles. The resulting Ga–Pt nanoparticles thereby act as an efficient cocatalyst when integrated within a COF, leading to enhanced syngas production via photocatalytic CO 2 reduction.

title: Fluorinated garnet: benchmarking Li-ion conductivity through structure–transport correlations and trade-offs
abstract: Fluorine doping in LLZO induces local ZrO 6− x F x polyhedral flexibility, breaks Meyer–Neldel compensation, and enhances Li-ion conductivity beyond cation-doped garnet electrolytes.

title: A sulfoxide-functionalized graphitic carbon nitride–cobalt dialkyl phosphate bipyridine heterojunction for photocatalytic hydrogen production
abstract: A 2D–2D heterojunction of sulfoxide-g-C 3 N 4 and a cobalt dialkyl phosphate bipyridine grid complex enables enhanced visible light HER.

title: Electrochemically induced V 5 S 8 /V 2 O 5 heterostructures regulate redox kinetics for aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: Taking advantage of the water/air-sensitive properties of sulfides to construct a V 5 S 8 /V 2 O 5 heterostructure is beneficial for manipulating the redox kinetics and Zn 2+ ion storage performance.

title: Unlocking liquid chemisorption in solid matrices: immobilized deep eutectic solvent-ZIF-8 composites for next-generation CO 2 capture
abstract: Immobilizing a CO 2 -reactive deep eutectic solvent within ZIF-8 enables chemisorption of ultra-dilute CO 2 (∼400 ppm) via carbamate, carbamic acid, and bicarbonate formation, offering a solid-state strategy for next-generation direct air capture.

title: Solid electrolytes for solid-state-supercapacitors and their interface chemistry: gel polymers, ionic liquids, ceramics, and beyond
abstract: Conventional supercapacitors are limited by their use of liquid electrolytes, which pose safety risks, leakage, stability, and flexibility issues. Herein, solid-state electrolytes with interface coatings improve contact, and ionic conductivity.

title: FeCoNiRhPt high-entropy alloy catalyst synthesized via atmospheric plasma-ionic liquid reduction for efficient oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: Plasma-synthesized FeCoNiRhPt HEA nanoparticles form a uniform FCC alloy with a favorable electronic structure and show superior alkaline OER activity and durability to RuO 2 , promising efficient hydrogen production.

title: A 4,4-connected 2D POM-based complex with synergistic mixed-valence Cu I /Cu II for mild and efficient catalytic oxidation of phenols
abstract: A 4,4-connected 2D POM-based complex incorporating mixed-valence copper centers was successfully synthesized, exhibiting high-performance oxidation of 2,3,6-TMP to TMBQ with 98% conversion and >99% selectivity in 13 minutes at 50 °C.

title: Tuning product selectivity in direct electroreduction of NO via phase engineering of MoS 2 nanosheets in a water-fed PEM electrolyzer
abstract: MoS 2 enables selective electrochemical NO reduction in a PEM electrolyzer defined by its crystal phase – 2H-MoS 2 phase achieves a high NH 3 yield with 86% FE at 2.1 V while the 1T′-MoS 2 phase shifts product selectivity more towards N 2 (50% at 1.7 V).

title: Directional electron and hole transfer driven by distinct Pd sites in CdS photocatalysts revealed by nonadiabatic dynamics simulations
abstract: The synergistic interplay between Pd–S 2 and Pd–S 3 sites enables ultrafast and directional photoinduced charge separation.

title: Inter-site coupling and nonlinear density–activity relationship in M–N–C single-atom catalysts
abstract: This work explicitly establishes the relationship between metal loading density, structural stability, and catalytic performance, revealing how loading density governs both catalyst stability and activity.

title: Dual-ion pre-intercalated vanadium oxides derived from V 4 AlC 3 MAX via HF-free synthesis for aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: A novel Ca 2+ /Al 3+ dual-ion pre-intercalated vanadium oxide is derived from V 4 AlC 3 MAX via an HF-free strategy, constructing a stable pillar structure to boost Zn 2+ diffusion and elevate zinc storage performance.

title: Advances in perovskite/C 60 interface engineering for efficiency and stability in perovskite/silicon tandem solar cells
abstract: This review focuses on the progress of perovskite/C 60 interface engineering in high-efficiency and stable perovskite/silicon tandem solar cells, including their functionalities and future application directions.

title: Precision fiber engineering via microfluidic spinning for next-generation smart wearable systems: a review
abstract: Microfluidic spinning enables precise control of fiber architectures and multifunctional integration. This review summarizes principles, structures, and wearable applications of microfluidic-spun fibers.

title: Moisture electric generators with Ti 3 C 2 T x –gelatin nanocomposites driven by the hydrovoltaic effect for self-powered flexible humidity sensors
abstract: Moisture electric generators (MEGs) self-powered by the hydrovoltaic effect were developed from gelatin-incorporated Ti 3 C 2 T x nanosheets as flexible humidity sensors.

title: Activated carbon microtube electrodes with cement and fly ash for enhanced supercapacitor performance
abstract: Fabrication of solid-state supercapacitor NF/ACMT2/FA1/CE1 electrode for energy application.

title: Development of a high latent Al–Si macrocapsule for thermal energy storage in both the core and shell
abstract: Aluminum–silicon (Al–Si) alloys, due to their excellent heat storage density and thermal conductivity, are ideal candidates for high-temperature phase change materials (PCMs).

title: Low-temperature and fast-charge lithium metal batteries enabled by a robust interphase via a dual-additive synergistic strategy
abstract: Combining lithium iron phosphate (LFP) with lithium anodes is crucial to achieve high-safety batteries.

title: Self-adaptive interfaces via electrochemical reconstruction enabling efficient PV-PEC water splitting
abstract: Combining electrochemical reconstruction with a PV-PEC system improved STH efficiency from 0.38% to 0.95% for solar water splitting.

title: Loosely packed catalyst layers promote gas transport and flooding resistance in gas diffusion electrodes for electrochemical CO 2 reduction
abstract: With a loosely packed catalyst layer, Pham and coworkers observed a longer GDE lifetime, attributed to the larger void volume that accommodated liquid seepage while maintaining sufficient space for CO 2 diffusion across the catalyst layer thickness.

title: Rational design of B-site single-atom doped LaMnO 3 for CO chemical looping combustion: a DFT study
abstract: Systematic DFT screening of B-site single-atom doped LaMnO 3 identifies promising catalysts with enhanced CO oxidation activity and reveals key structure–activity–stability relationships.

title: Electrocatalytic hydrogen generation from seawater: advances, opportunities, challenges and future roadmap
abstract: Electrochemical hydrogen production from seawater offers a promising pathway toward large-scale green hydrogen generation while alleviating dependence on freshwater resources.

title: π frameworks: emerging porous supramolecular framework materials for photocatalysis
abstract: This review summarizes the current research progress as well as challenges and future opportunities of newly emerging π frameworks in photocatalysis.

title: In situ polymerization assisted strain release to enable efficient and mechanically robust flexible perovskite solar cells and minimodules
abstract: A poly-ECA interlayer is inserted between SnO 2 and perovskite to strengthen buried adhesion, passivate defects and optimize energy alignment. This strategy delivers 25.7% PCE for small-area flexible PSC devices and 21.9% for large-area modules.

title: A review of transition metal dichalcogenides for supercapacitor applications: materials, performance, and challenges
abstract: Illustration of TMDs based supercapacitor and their engineered structures for advanced supercapacitor performance.

title: Elucidation of superionic conduction in K 2 NiF 4 -type Ba–Li oxyhydrides from a first-principles molecular dynamics study
abstract: The microscopic hydride-ion (H − ) dynamics and superionic conduction characteristics of K 2 NiF 4 -type oxyhydrides, Ba 1.75 LiH 2.7 O 0.9 , were investigated by first-principles molecular dynamics simulations.

title: Revealing critical roles of active layer morphology for suppressing interface-related degradation of organic solar cells under illumination
abstract: We reveal critical roles of active layer morphology for suppressing interface-related degradation of organic solar cells under illumination and provide guidelines for morphology engineering towards both stable and highly efficient devices.

title: Molecular hybridization of multiple resonance cores and responsive heterocycles for versatile optoelectronic and sensing applications
abstract: Hybridizing DABNA with HBT/HBO yields multi-functional narrow-band emitters featuring multi-dimensional responsiveness, RTP, and OLED applications.

title: Multi-scale regulation of MnO 2 dissolution/deposition chemistry in rechargeable aqueous zinc ion batteries
abstract: This review systematically summarizes multi-scale collaborative strategies, encompassing electrolyte engineering and electrode material design, to achieve highly reversible MnO 2 /Mn 2+ dissolution/deposition chemistry in aqueous zinc-ion batteries.

title: Fully conjugated D–A COFs with thiadiazole and thiazolothiazole linkers: tailoring electronic structures for efficient metal-free and Pt-assisted hydrogen evolution
abstract: Fully π-conjugated and sp 2 -carbon-conjugated donor–acceptor COFs Py-CN-BZTD and Py-CN-TZTZ deliver HER among the highest for COFs: 12 200 µmolg −1 h −1 (metal free) and 61 577 µmol g −1 h −1 (Pt-loaded).

title: Freestanding laser-induced graphene/carbon fiber frameworks functionalized with NiO for high-performance non-enzymatic glucose sensing
abstract: A non-enzymatic glucose sensor was fabricated based on a NiO nanoparticle functionalized LW-CFF-B framework. The sensor demonstrated outstanding performance, including fast response, ultrahigh sensitivity and low detection limit.

title: Enhancing the charge transfer and photoelectrochemical performance of the BiVO 4 /WO 3 heterojunction via gradient surface/interface Co–Mo doping
abstract: We prepared a CoBiVO 4 Mo/WO 3 photoanode by selectively introducing Mo at the interface and doping Co on the surface of the heterojunction. This enabled precise control over the spatially graded distribution of the two dopants, boosting charge transfer and photoelectrochemical performance.

title: Designing type-II p-SiCC 4 /XO (X = Sn, Pb) van der Waals heterojunctions with a built-in electric field as promising photocatalysts for highly efficient overall water splitting
abstract: Auxetic type-II p-SiCC 4 /XO (X = Sn, Pb) heterojunctions exhibit a strong built-in electric field that enables long radiation lifetime of interlayer excitons and almost spontaneous efficient solar-driven water splitting.

title: Magnetic enhancements of defect-mediated spin polarized photocatalytic CO 2 reduction of Sn 2 P 2 S 6
abstract: Magnetic-field-enhanced defect-engineered Sn 2 P 2 S 6 enables efficient spin-polarized CO 2 -to-CH 4 photocatalysis.

title: Synergistic interfacial evaporation and electricity generation enabled by Fe–Co PBA derived natural sponges
abstract: This study developed a material that synergistically utilizes both interfacial evaporation and moisture-driven electricity generation by loading Fe–Co PBA onto waste natural sponges.

title: Phase diagrams and chemical expansion upon hydration of proton conductors BaZr x Ce 0.8− x Y 0.1 Yb 0.1 O 2.9 (H 2 O) n (0 ≤ x ≤ 0.8; 0 ≤ n ≤ 0.1)
abstract: This study examines BaZr x Ce 0.8− x Y 0.1 Yb 0.1 O 2.9 proton conductors using X-ray and neutron diffraction with thermogravimetric analyses, revealing how hydration, composition, and temperature affect symmetry, phase transitions, and structural stability.

title: Boron doping modulates the electrostatic interaction of Na-O bond for inducing P2/O3 biphasic structure in layered oxide cathode
abstract: Sodium-ion batteries (SIBs) are promising for large-scale energy storage, yet developing cathode materials with both high capacity and long-term stability remains challenging. Herein, the strategy of modulating the electrostatic interaction...

title: A uniform lithium ion flux and robust interphase enabled by an anion anchoring additive for high energy density Si-based anodes
abstract: An anion-anchoring additive (ISAA) regulates Li + flux and promotes the formation of a robust LiF-rich interphase, effectively mitigating electrode swelling and ensuring superior cycling stability for silicon/graphite anodes.

title: Synergistic structural and electronic engineering for boosted oxygen evolution reaction on nickel–iron hydroxide nanocatalysts
abstract: Facile hydrolysis enables the fabrication of Co-substituted NiFe-LDH hierarchical hollow spheres. Co incorporation markedly promotes electron transfer and surface reconstruction, achieving 1 A cm −2 at 1.79 V with robust 120 h durability in an AEMWE.

title: Synergistic Cu/F co-doped P2-Na 0.67 Ni 0.33 Mn 0.67 O 2 microsphere cathodes for enhanced sodium storage via dual doping and structural design
abstract: Cu/F co-doped microspheres boost P2-type Na 0.67 Ni 0.33 Mn 0.67 O 2 cathodes for Na-ion batteries. This strategy mitigates interfacial side reactions, reinforces structure, and suppresses irreversible oxygen redox & phase transitions upon cycling.

title: Synergistic promotion of oxygen reduction electrocatalysis by chlorine incorporation in biomass-derived S/N co-doped carbon
abstract: Biomass-derived Cl–S/N co-doped carbon enables charge redistribution, stronger O 2 adsorption, and efficient ORR electrocatalytic activity. The catalyst achieves E 1/2 = 0.89 V and powers high-performance aqueous Zn–air batteries.

title: Multilayer (Cr/Au) n /PAA nanoporous membrane for ultrafast, selective CO 2 detection at room temperature
abstract: A robust room-temperature CO 2 sensor based on multilayered (Cr/Au) n /PAA nanostructures exhibits high sensitivity (160.69% at 4440 ppm), rapid dynamics, and long-term stability through engineered Cr–Au heterointerfaces.

title: From conductive filament design to 3D-printed devices: a critical review and development guide for advanced electrochemical applications
abstract: Tutorial guide and critical review on conductive 3D-printing filaments for advanced electrochemical devices.

title: Tuning surface redox chemistry through trace Ni doping in cobalt pyrophosphate (Co 2 P 2 O 7 ) for high-performance supercapacitors: experimental and theoretical insights
abstract: The combination of nickel-doped cobalt phosphate and reduced graphene oxide (rGO) as an electrode material has led to significantly improved charge storage performance in an asymmetric supercapacitor.

title: Emerging hole transport layers for PbS quantum dot solar cells and photodetectors
abstract: Hole transport layers (HTLs) are crucial for QD-based photovoltaics. This study compares p-type QDs, organic HTLs, and hybrids, showing that alternative ligands beyond EDT offer promise for scalable, stable, and efficient QD devices.

title: Proton migration pathways and hydrogen evolution mechanism in g-C 3 N 4 /TiO 2 -B and Li–F co-doped heterostructures: a theoretical study
abstract: The decomposition of water as well as the migration and diffusion of protons enable the Li–F co-doped g-C 3 N 4 /TiO 2 -B(001) heterojunction to exhibit excellent hydrogen evolution performance.

title: Sulfide electrolyte additive enables multi-ionic transfer pathways in alkaline iron redox
abstract: Sulfide-containing alkaline electrolytes fundamentally alter the Fe 2+ /Fe 3+ redox system by enabling multiple ion-transfer pathways, involving hydrosulfide, protons, and hydroxyl ions.

title: Fluid-powered rotation-contact separation hybrid triboelectric nanogenerator for heavy metal ion removal and corrosion protection
abstract: Herein, we have proposed a rotation-contact separation hybrid mode triboelectric nanogenerator (RCS-TENG) for harvesting energy from fluid, which has achieved heavy metal ion removal and corrosion protection.

title: Defect formation and microstructure tuning via proton irradiation to control electrochemical and phase reversibility in layered battery materials
abstract: Proton-irradiation-driven microstructural tuning provides a powerful route to control phase stability and electrochemical reversibility in layered oxide battery materials.

title: Titanium nitride prepared by the urea-glass synthesis gives an active electrocatalyst for the oxygen reduction reaction
abstract: The urea-glass method gives an O-atom rich titanium nitride surface that is a highly active electrocatalyst for the oxygen reduction reaction (ORR).

title: Investigating the shunt resistance of semitransparent organic solar cells via simulating their practical photovoltaic performances
abstract: The shunt resistance ( R SH ) is a key electrical factor limiting the light utilization efficiency (LUE) of semitransparent organic solar cells. A LUE of 8.51% is predicted with R SH = 600 Ω cm 2 and AVT = 49.62%.

title: Synergetic passivation of PbS colloidal quantum dots by bulk heterojunction-type interlayers enables >14% solar cell efficiency
abstract: Y6/Y11-derived NFAs enable synergistic defect passivation and enhanced V OC and FF in high-efficiency PbS CQD solar cells, leading to a champion PCE of 14.02%.
