title: Engineering atomically dispersed Fe sites into TiO 2 for largely enhanced photocatalytic CO 2 reduction to CH 4
abstract: Atomically dispersed Fe atoms incorporated in a TiO 2 photocatalyst could boost the production of CH 4 from photocatalytic CO 2 reduction.

title: Residual oxygen-driven p–n conversion and thermoelectric properties in CrN films
abstract: Oxygen-controlled defect engineering enables p–n conversion in CrN thin films for homojunction and flexible thermoelectric applications.

title: Transformation of clinical waste into heteroatom-doped porous carbon materials towards a highly efficient energy storage device
abstract: Synthesis of heteroatom-doped porous carbon materials derived from plastic biowaste (disposable mask) for a solid-state symmetric device.

title: Design Principles of structured lithium metal anodes for high-energy batteries
abstract: Constructing high-energy batteries through three structural design strategies of LMAs using pure Li, alloys and composite structures.

title: Heteroatom-doped graphene nanoribbons as high-performance electrodes for lithium-ion hybrid supercapacitors
abstract: Electrochemical unzipping of MWCNTs into graphene nanoribbons enables the synthesis of engineered electrode materials for high-performance supercapacitor energy storage.

title: Synergistic Ru-mediated activation of CoP/Co heterostructures on carbon nanofibers toward efficient hydrogen evolution
abstract: Self-supported electrospun carbon nanofiber electrodes provide structural support, protection, and a conductive network, while Ru synergistically modulates the CoP/Co heterostructure to accelerate HER with enhanced activity and durability.

title: Polyaromatic hydrocarbon-based 3D coordination polymeric materials: synergistic effect of nickel( ii ) nitrate and tetraalkynyl anthracene towards electrocatalytic hydrogen production
abstract: A 3D coordination polymer material synthesized by the ultrasonic treatment of Ni(NO 3 ) 2 ·6H 2 O with tetraalkynylated anthracene afforded an exceptional overpotential of 94 ± 14 mV at −100 mA cm −2 for the HER at 90 °C.

title: Carbon monoxide ligation at single metal atom sites in a 2D manganese-cobalt coordination network: equilibrium at room temperature
abstract: Direct insight into ligand dynamics at single metal atom sites suggests tunable reactivity properties of a Mn–Co 2D biomimetic bi-metallic functional material. The CO saturation curve unveils network-mediated adsorption mechanisms.

title: Computational screening of piezoelectric constants in metal–organic frameworks: design principles and ferroelectric-like bond modulation
abstract: This study computes the piezoeletric tensor e of metal–organic frameworks (MOFs) using DFPT, analyzes structure–property relationships for high- e MOFs, and evaluates their mechanical properties and piezoelectric constants d .

title: Molecularly Crowded Water-in-Salt Electrolyte Enabled All Climate Operational High Voltage MnO2 Pseudocapacitor
abstract: Herein we demonstrate the fabrication of laser induced graphene (LIG) supported MnO2 symmetric pseudocapacitor with unprecedented electrochemical performance enabled by 17 m NaClO4 water-in-salt hybrid electrolyte with ethylene glycol (EG)...

title: Ruddlesden–Popper perovskite oxides as emerging air electrodes for protonic ceramic cells
abstract: This article reviews important advances in designing Ruddlesden–Popper perovskite oxides as emerging air electrodes for protonic ceramic cells, aiming to present critical insights for the widespread applications of this technology.

title: In situ and real-time monitoring of the internal temperature, pressure, and current of lithium-ion batteries for long cycle life safety using a flexible multi-parameter sensor array
abstract: The frequent incidents of thermal runaway in lithium batteries have accelerated the development of sensing technologies to ensure service safety by monitoring specific parameters.

title: Trace high-entropy doping enabling high-stability and high-energy-density sodium-ion batteries
abstract: Trace high-entropy doped MZZ–NM exhibits superior structural stability and reversible oxygen redox. Full cells demonstrate 321.6 W h kg –1 energy density with excellent cycling stability.

title: Compartmentalized structure reconstruction in metal–organic frameworks (MOFs) for efficient oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: A strategy of compartmentalization of Ni 2+ –O–Fe 3+ units and conductive Ni@C species in metal–organic frameworks (MOFs) is reported to effectively promote the structural evolution of active NiOOH for efficient oxygen evolution reaction.

title: Highly sensitive piezo-gated bilayer thin-film transistors with all operation mode switching via the synergistic effect of ZnO and porous PVDF-TrFE
abstract: Flexible and wearable electronics are attracting growing interest for next-generation electronic devices, with the piezoelectric and ferroelectric copolymer PVDF-TrFE widely studied for its high piezoelectricity and mechanical flexibility.

title: Crosslinked poly(ionic liquid)s enabled with dication ionic liquid moieties: adaptable lubricant performance under severe and electric field conditions
abstract: Three dual-cation polymeric ionic liquids were prepared and exhibited unique viscosity–temperature performance, friction and wear reduction properties when utilized as multifunctional lubricant additives for 500SN.

title: Modification of sodium niobate energy storage ceramics via doping with a high-entropy phase
abstract: A novel, lead-free, stabilized antiferroelectric energy-storage ceramic has been designed and realized through entropy engineering. The temperature coefficient of capacitance (TCC) fully complies with the X7R standard.

title: Defect engineering in carbon skeletons toward precise edge-nitrogen doping for efficient electrochemical energy storage
abstract: Defect engineering directs selective edge-N incorporation in carbon skeletons, establishing a defect-driven pathway for precise doping and enhanced activity.

title: Construction of a robust and high ion-conductivity SEI using a multifunctional additive to achieve high-rate and large-capacity lithium metal anodes
abstract: TFPA additive enables the formation of LiF/Li 3 PO 4 -rich SEI with high mechanical strength and fast Li⁺ transport on Li metal, which can keep stable Li plating at 5 mA cm −2 as well as superior electrochemical performances in high-voltage full batteries.

title: Boosting piezocatalytic hydrogen production via bismuth vacancy-engineered BiOCl–Bi 2 S 3 heterojunctions fabricated by a light-induced deposition strategy
abstract: A BiOCl–Bi 2 S 3 nanocomposite with Bi vacancies has been synthesized by a photo-deposition method for efficient piezocatalytic H 2 production.

title: Low-Temperature Silicon Anodes from Biosilica via AlCl3-Assisted Magnesiothermic Reduction
abstract: Silicon is a high-capacity anode material, yet its scalable production from sustainable precursors re- quires low-temperature and controllable synthesis routes. Diatom-derived SiO2 provides an abundant biogenic feedstock, but its conversion...

title: Monometallic nickel as a tunable electrocatalyst for alkaline hydrogen evolution: a critical review
abstract: This review highlights recent advances in the synthesis, surface/interface modification, and performance optimization of monometallic Ni HER electrodes, emphasizing how rational engineering can elevate their reactivity toward state-of-the-art levels.

title: Interfacial engineering of Mo-doped Ni 3 S 2 /FeNi 2 S 4 heterostructures for durable industrial level-current-density AEM water electrolysis
abstract: Mo-doped Ni 3 S 2 /FeNi 2 S 4 nanosheet catalyst delivers exceptional OER activity, reaching 3 and 5 A cm −2 at 1.98 and 2.18 V in AEMWE with 200 h stability. Mo-induced charge modulation enhances active sites, enabling efficient hydrogen production.

title: Unraveling the sodium storage mechanism in a redox-active covalent organic framework cathode for Na-metal batteries
abstract: A redox-active covalent organic framework combines pseudocapacitive and faradaic Na storage for fast, stable, high-capacity performance, revealing that calendering-induced electrode densification hinders Na-ion transport by restricting pore access.

title: Nano-graphdiyne derivative and natural amino acid molecule as bilateral charge transport layer dopants for efficient perovskite solar cells
abstract: A novel dual-doping strategy is developed by incorporating serine into the SnO 2 ETL and o-TB-GDY into the Spiro-OMeTAD HTL, respectively.

title: Room-temperature curing sustainable hybrid cross-linked coating enables efficient dynamic icephobicity of unmanned aerial vehicles
abstract: A sustainable dynamic icephobic coating (VTEC) for use in low temperatures, high humidity, and dynamic environments is reported. The VTEC demonstrates outstanding resistance to icing and frost under high humidity conditions.

title: Multiscale design of two-dimensional MoS 3 materials and their electrocatalytic performance for CO 2 reduction
abstract: Leveraging the CBD-GM platform developed by our group, this study presents the first theoretical prediction of a novel two-dimensional layered MoS 3 structure.

title: Effect of severe plastic deformation on thermoelectric properties of BiCuSeO
abstract: High-pressure torsion is used to attain grain refinement and introduce dense dislocations in BiCuSeO. The effect of HPT reduces the bandgap, increases carrier concentration and reduces κ to improve thermoelectric performance of BiCuSeO.

title: Electrodeposited zeolitic imidazolate framework-8 modified zinc anode supported over porous copper structure for rechargeable aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: ZIF-8 protective layer electrodeposited on Zn–Cu structure minimises Zn dendrites and improves aqueous zinc-ion battery performance. The ZIF-8/Zn–Cu//α-MnO 2 cell delivers 204.02 mAh g −1 at 0.4C and retains 92.62% capacity after 1200 cycles.

title: Particle size-dependent bulk and grain-boundary contributions in Li 2 O–B 2 O 3 –Al 2 O 3 solid electrolytes
abstract: Schematic of Li + conduction pathways across bulk and grain boundaries in an oxide solid electrolyte.

title: Research progress on efficient and selective transition metal oxides for photoelectrochemical seawater splitting
abstract: Metal–oxygen coordination environments dictate band structure, charge transport, and surface stability in transition metal oxide (TMO) photoanodes, providing design principles for robust photoelectrochemical seawater splitting.

title: Chromium leaching-induced electronic structure modulation in CoOOH for improved alcohol oxidation reactions
abstract: Metal leaching-induced electronic structure modulation in CoOOH increases the amount of Co 3+ species in the catalyst structure, facilitating benzyl alcohol oxidation.

title: A low temperature solution-chemical process for fluoride salt (NaF) waste immobilization into metal halide perovskite structured Na 2 SnF 6
abstract: Complex salt waste streams generated from chemical reprocessing of spent nuclear fuels and advanced molten salt reactor technologies require innovative materials and processes for effective immobilization and management.

title: Ultrathin defective Fe 2 O 3− x skins on exsolved nanoparticles from a perovskite cathode enable highly active and sulfur-tolerant CO 2 electroreduction
abstract: In situ exsolved FeNi 3 /Fe 2 O 3− x core–shell nanoparticles formed by steam-assisted wet reduction enhance CO 2 electrolysis and sulfur tolerance via surface sulfur redox, delivering high and stable performance in sulfur-containing industrial gas.

title: Weakening OH adsorption on Ru sites by Ni alloying for accelerating alkaline hydrogen evolution of an intermetallic Ru 3 Ni electrocatalyst
abstract: Under alkaline conditions, weakening the OH adsorption at the Ru site avoids the excessive adsorption of OH on Ru sites, prevents the deactivation of Ru, and improves the performance of the alkaline hydrogen evolution reaction.

title: Exploiting nickel–organic-frameworks in the temperature-controlled synthesis of carbonized nickel nanoparticles for magnetic purification of acidic water
abstract: Controlled carbonization of hybrid NiOF powders produces graphite-encapsulated Ni nanoparticles with excellent performance for acidic water remediation. The powders can be easily recovered by magnetic separation after the adsorption process.

title: Unveiling the potential of lignin-derived hard carbon as an anode material for Li- and post-Li-ion batteries: a computational investigation
abstract: Hard carbon is one of the most promising anode materials for post-Li-ion batteries; however, the relationship between the precursor material and performance is still hardly understood.

title: A multifunctional flexible SnTe/MWCNT crystalline hybrid for energy storage and humidity sensing with 3D-printed device integration
abstract: The design of multifunctional materials that unify energy storage and environmental sensing is vital for next-generation flexible electronics and IoT devices.

title: A synergistic catalyst of C 60 -confined Ag 3 Fe 2 and dual-metal sites for breaking the activity and stability trade-off for oxygen reduction
abstract: The C 60 @Ag 3 Fe 2 structure modulates the electronic environment of the Fe–N 6 and Ag-N 5 sites, effectively balancing the adsorption/desorption of intermediates and offering a viable strategy to achieve both high activity and stability in the ORR.

title: A uniform nitrogen-rich interphase optimizes interfacial kinetics for stable graphite anodes
abstract: Rapid phase-inversion precipitation enables the formation of a uniform nitrogen-rich interphase for stable and high-performance graphite anodes.

title: Proton conductivity of mesoporous aluminum organophosphonate enhanced by the affinity of an integral organic linker to water molecules
abstract: Aluminum organophosphonate (AOP)-type mesoporous materials with a superprotonic conductivity of 1.31 × 10 −2 S cm −1 under 95% RH at 90 °C were prepared using Pluronic P123 through the rational design to place abundant –OH groups over organic linker.

title: Fragments of polymers with a Z-scheme-like electron transfer mechanism driven by multiple built-in electric fields for photocatalytic H 2 O 2 production
abstract: In photocatalytic H 2 O 2 production systems, although the built-in electric fields (BIEFs) can promote charge separation, systematic studies on the charge migration mechanisms between different molecular segments induced by BIEFs remain lacking.

title: Engineering the Ti 3 C 2 electronic structure via surface termination synergy for high-efficiency electromagnetic wave absorption
abstract: The Lewis acidic molten salt etching temperature tailors the total number of surface terminations and the Cl/O ratio of Ti 3 C 2 MXenes, enabling customized EMW absorption with either strong attenuation or broadband response.

title: Unlocking the catalytic potential of single-atom Zn on N-doped MXene through intensive metal–support interaction for high-efficiency Fenton-like catalysis
abstract: N-doped MXene tunes the orbital bonding states and electron density of Zn center via strong metal–support interaction, unlocking its potential towards peroxymonosulfate (PMS) activation for clean and efficient pollutant degradation.

title: Theoretical insights into the catalytic mechanisms of LiNi 1/3 Co 1/3 Mn 1/3 O 2 cathode surfaces in diverse ethylene carbonate decomposition reactions
abstract: This work systematically reveals the catalytic mechanisms of LiNi 1/3 Co 1/3 Mn 1/3 O 2 cathode surfaces in EC decomposition reactions through comprehensive first-principles calculations.

title: A bifunctional cerium-containing modification strategy for high-performance high-voltage LiCoO 2 cathodes
abstract: Synergistic Ce-doping and Li 2 CeO 3 coating stabilizes high-voltage LiCoO 2 against lattice oxygen escape and interfacial degradation.

title: Precision synthesis of supported metal nanoparticles: a solid-state chemical approach and the mechanistic understanding
abstract: Our findings establish a unified mechanistic picture of the precision synthesis of supported metal nanoparticle from single-atom moieties.

title: Ammonolysis with N 2 -diluted NH 3 suppresses Ta( iv ) defects in BaTaO 2 N and enhances photocatalytic water oxidation
abstract: X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy, electron paramagnetic spectroscopy, and vibrating kelvin probe surface photovoltage spectroscopy observe defect concentrations, locations and their energetics.

title: Insights into the mechanism of enhanced methane low-temperature coupling conversion over zeolite-encaged Ni–Pt catalysts
abstract: A novel bifunctional zeolite-encaged Ni–Pt catalyst enables efficient low-temperature methane activation and aromatization via Pt-induced electronic modification of Ni, achieving high methane conversion and aromatics selectivity.

title: A dual-site approach in high-entropy oxide aerogels creates active and selective reverse water–gas shift catalysts
abstract: High-entropy oxide aerogels incorporating Ni and Fe into separate sites in the fluorite lattice are highly effective catalysts for the reverse water–gas shift reaction.

title: Tuning the N-intermediate adsorption of Cu catalysts for efficient electroreduction of nitrate to ammonia
abstract: Cu nanoparticles supported on B and N co-doped hollow carbon fibers, called Cu/BNHCFs, were designed and synthesized. The synergistic combination of stable carbon materials with transition metals improves durability and catalytic efficiency.

title: Multi-shell nanostructures minimize diffusion pathways and dual active sites decouple activation for efficient ammonia borane hydrolysis
abstract: Multi-shell structures minimize diffusion pathways, while dual reaction sites decouple reactant activation. Theoretical modeling identifies optimal shell thicknesses below 0.98 µm and a dopant d-band center between −1.4 and −1.8 eV.

title: Stabilising gallium-based liquid metal alloy nanoparticles by carbon encapsulation
abstract: Gallium-based liquid metal (LM) nanoparticles hold an exceptional promise for catalysis, energy storage, and printed electronics due to their high conductivity, fluidity, and dynamic catalytic surfaces.

title: Quasi-solid electrolyte based on cost-effective sodium salt for high-performance sodium-ion supercapacitors
abstract: Schematic demonstration of the prepared QSE synthesised via UV polymerisation, achieving widened ESW and enhanced energy density for flexible energy storage devices.

title: Pressure-tuned 2D hybrid perovskites: emerging insights and future opportunities
abstract: Two‑dimensional (2D) hybrid organic–inorganic perovskites (HOIPs) exhibit pressure‑tunable structures and properties that enable optimized performance in energy and optoelectronic devices.

title: Interfacial pathways in Na 2 B 12 H 12 /SiO 2 nanocomposites boost the ionic conductivity and enable solid-state sodium batteries at room temperature
abstract: In Na 2 B 12 H 12 /SiO 2 nanocomposite solid electrolytes, interface effects boost the ionic conductivity of Na 2 B 12 H 12 by more than 3 orders of magnitude, enabling room-temperature all-solid-state Na metal batteries with impressive capacity retention.

title: Crafting double perovskites/hollow conjugated microporous polymer heterostructure for dual-channel H 2 O 2 production
abstract: Cs 2 AgBiBr 6 perovskites were embedded in a hollow scaffold to form a heterojunction, which enhances charge separation and light absorption, enabling dual-channel H 2 O 2 production via ORR and WOR. It achieves a rate of 3500 µmol g −1 h −1 and 9.66% AQE.

title: Enhanced thermoelectric performance in Fe 2 V 0.8 W 0.2 Al thin films: synergistic effects of chemical ordering and tungsten substitution
abstract: We systematically investigate the combined effect of chemical ordering and W substitution on the thermoelectric properties of Fe 2 V 0.8 W 0.2 Al thin films.

title: Etching-induced electronic modulation in Prussian blue analogue-derived metal sulfides for advanced hybrid supercapacitors
abstract: Using Ni-FePBA as a precursor, a metal sulfide (E9-Ni-FePBA-S) was obtained. The E9-Ni-FePBA-S//AC hybrid supercapacitor delivers 31.4 Wh kg −1 at 1.6 kW kg −1 , demonstrating its promise for high-performance energy storage.

title: Towards mitigating ring opening in TEMPO catholytes for aqueous redox flow batteries
abstract: The feasibility of using TEMPO catholyte in long-cycling aqueous redox flow batteries depends on the effective mitigation of the ring-opening side reactions.

title: Operando benchtop NMR study of ion transport through fluorine-free polymer membranes in a symmetric redox flow cell
abstract: Operando 7 Li-NMR reveals and quantifies charge-balancing ion transport through a fluorine-free biphenyl-isatin-based membrane in a symmetric iron-based redox flow cell.

title: Dual-MOF-derived Ni@Fe-based core–shell heterostructures as trifunctional catalysts for methanol valorization-coupled H 2 production via hybrid water electrolysis
abstract: Ni- and Fe-based dual MOF-derived trifunctional core–shell Ni 2 P@Fe 2 P/NF is developed enabling the fabrication of a methanol-assisted hybrid electrolyzer for low-cost H 2 generation at cathode and subsequent methanol-to-formate valorization at anode.

title: Hydronium-catching interface unlocks strongly acidic Zn metal batteries with high voltage and long-term stability
abstract: A defect-engineered MOF interphase screens water and regulates hydronium at pH 0, enabling partial Zn 2+ desolvation, compact Zn(002) deposition, and stable acidic Zn–PbO 2 , Zn–MnO 2 , and hybrid capacitor devices.

title: Yb/Ho co-doping and rGO bridging enhance visible-to-NIR-light-driven photocatalysis in an S-scheme heterojunction
abstract: Rare earth co-doping and rGO bridging in an S-scheme heterojunction led to improved visible and NIR responsive photocatalysis. Yb/Ho up-conversion broadened light absorption, and rGO functioned as an electron bridge to enhance charge separation.

title: Interface-engineered melt-spun BiSbTe for multiscale phonon scattering and enhanced thermoelectric performance
abstract: BiSbTe thermoelectric materials with a ZT value of 1.56, featuring multiscale hierarchical microstructures, were synthesized by melt-spinning synthesis of nanostructured BiSbTe particles followed by solution-phase coating with polyoxometalates.

title: Synergistic effects at the interface of core–shell Cu 3 P@CoO heterojunctions for enhanced nitrate reduction to ammonia
abstract: Core–shell Cu 3 P@CoO heterojunction interfaces are proposed as a superb NO 3 RR electrocatalyst for ambient NH 3 synthesis, attaining a large NH 3 yield of 9201 µg h −1 cm −2 and a high FE of 92.4% at −0.4 V in an alkaline electrolyte.

title: Multiple synergistic effects induced by Ge doping enhance the thermoelectric performance of n-type PbTe
abstract: Cu/Ge co-doping in n-type PbTe enlarges interstitial voids, enhances Cu solubility, flattens bands, and softens the off-centered lattice, achieving peak zT ∼ 1.4, zT avg ∼ 1.0 (300–773 K), and ∼13% theoretical efficiency.

title: Decoupling the solubility of redox active species and the energy density of redox flow batteries using supersaturated, phase-change electrolytes
abstract: Our supersaturated, phase-change electrolyte paradigm overcomes the energy density limits of redox flow batteries (RFBs) imposed by the solubility of the active species, demonstrating a new strategy for high-energy-density RFBs.

title: Rational design of air-stable hydrogen storage composites: from discrete encapsulation to synergistic hybrid architectures
abstract: This review elucidates the structure–property relationships between encapsulation layers and the performance of air‐stable hydrogen storage composites, offering guidance for the design of next‐generation hydrogen storage materials.

title: Carbon recycling through catalysis: direct CO 2 -to-CH 4 conversion for sustainable fuels
abstract: Integrated thermo-, photo-, and electrocatalysis, along with photothermal, electrothermal, and photo-electrochemical routes, enable efficient CO 2 -to-CH 4 conversion. Advanced catalyst design drives sustainable carbon recycling and energy storage.

title: A bromine-rich artificial interphase to regulate interfacial kinetics in boron-centered electrolytes for magnesium metal batteries
abstract: Sluggish interfacial kinetics hinder the practical use of boron‑centered Mg electrolytes. A bromine‑rich artificial interphase stabilizes the Mg interface, promotes uniform deposition, enhances coulombic efficiency and extend cycling durability.

title: Remote Plasma Polymers of Iron (II) Phthalocyanine in Polyacrylonitrile-Derived Carbon Electrospun Fibers as Electrode for Supercapacitors
abstract: supercapacitor, plasma processing, PAN fibers, electrospinning, plasma polymer, iron (II) phthalocyanine, remote plasma synthesis

title: Multifunctional Soft Anti-Swelling Hydrogels-Based Sensor Reinforced with Carboxylated-MWCNTs and CMC for Human Motion Detection and Air-Underwater Communication
abstract: Wearable hydrogel-based strain sensors that can operate reliably in both air and water are difficult to develop because conventional hydrogels tend to swell, weaken, and lose signal stability in aqueous...

title: Multiporous structure formation in three-way catalyst particles for enhanced catalytic performance
abstract: Multiporous structures provide effective mass transfer to enhance catalytic performance.

title: Heating-rate controlled defect engineering in BiVO 4 thin films for enhanced photoelectrochemical water splitting
abstract: Rapid nucleation-induced oxygen vacancy formation in BiVO 4 : bandgap narrowing and suppressed recombination via shallow donor states.

title: A dual-function gold–zinc ferrite nanocomposite sensor for colorimetric detection of phosphates in environmental water
abstract: The design of effective nanocomposites offers a solution by enhancing the selectivity and differentiation of phosphates.

title: Magnetic nanoparticle constructs for in situ repair of critical cementitious infrastructure
abstract: Cement barriers in critical infrastructures, such as nuclear waste containment, can degrade and crack over time, enabling radionuclide release and further failure mechanisms.

title: Impact of Area Scaling on the Performance and Stability of Spin-Coated Organic Solar Cells
abstract: Organic solar cells (OSCs) suffer from severe upscaling losses that are predominantly governed by resistive limitations, which hinder efficient lateral charge transport and extraction when reaching the centimeter scale with...

title: NiCoO x @NiCo core-shell nanoparticles supported on Ti 3 C 2 T x as cathode electrocatalysts for microbial fuel cells
abstract: The intrinsically sluggish oxygen reduction reaction (ORR) at platinum-group-metal-free cathodes remains a key bottleneck for the practical deployment of microbial fuel cells (MFCs). Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene is a promising conductive scaffold,...

title: Vibrational dynamics and the interplay of Li-ion mobility and thermal transport in LiAlSiO 4
abstract: The Li-diffusion pathway aligns with a representative low-energy phonon displacement vector.

title: Recent advances in non-noble metal-based metal–organic frameworks as electrocatalysts for water splitting: challenges and future prospects
abstract: Water electrolysis represents a pivotal pathway for green hydrogen production, offering exceptional product purity, operational simplicity, and zero pollutant emissions.

title: Closed-loop engineering of chemically anchored lead–carbon composites via thermodynamic-kinetic synergy for ultra-long-life lead–carbon batteries
abstract: A green closed-loop process produces rice husk carbon-based lead-carbon composite. As an anode additive, it enhances capacitance and suppresses H 2 evolution, extending battery cycle life. With 35.23% gross margin, it is industrially promising.

title: Donor-site-acceptor dual-modulation covalent organic frameworks for synergistic U( vi ) photoreduction and immobilization
abstract: By regulating the number ( x = 0, 1, 2, 3) and position ( para -, meta -, ortho -) of N atoms via N-heterocyclic modification, a series of donor-site-acceptor (D-site-A) β-ketoenamine COFs (TpPa-N x ) were constructed for U( vi ) photoreduction.

title: Core-Linked Dimer Acceptors with Flexible Linker for High-efficiency and Robust Organic Solar Cells
abstract: Oligomerized small molecular acceptors (OSMAs) have been applied to tackle the stability challenge of organic solar cells (OSCs) owing to their elevated glass transition temperatures, which can suppress the molecular...

title: Methyl propionate-dominant electrolyte for enhanced kinetics and low-temperature performance of Prussian blue analogue-based rechargeable sodium-ion batteries
abstract: The design of the electrolyte is an essential factor in enhancing fast ion-transport kinetics and overall stability in sodium-ion batteries (SIBs).

title: Rational core-shell design of novel Ni-Co bimetallic MOF@PBA and Al x V 2 O 5 @C as positive and negative electrode materials for high-performance aqueous Zn-ion hybrid supercapacitors
abstract: Aqueous Zn-ion hybrid supercapacitors (ZIHSCs) represent a viable energy-storage technology for power-oriented applications, combining long cycle life with intrinsic safety, low cost, and environmental compatibility. However, their practical development is...

title: Synergistic engineering of a dendritic Ni–Cu–Co–P electrocatalyst via a dynamic hydrogen bubble template for efficient and durable bifunctional water splitting
abstract: Active and ultrastable phosphide-based 3-D electrode was develpoed for enhanced electrochemical hydrogen production.

title: Synergistic modulation of desolvation kinetics and Zn 2+ flux in a dual-biopolymer interface for stable zinc anodes
abstract: Attributed to the abundant polar functional groups and 3D crosslinked network, the CS/Gel protective layer can effectively accelerate the Zn 2+ desolvation behavior and homogenize the zinc ion flux, thus realizing stable zinc anodes.

title: Proton conductivity at the controlled hydrophilic and hydrophobic surfaces of mesoporous aluminum organophosphonates
abstract: Aluminum organophosphonate (AOP)-type mesoporous materials were prepared using Pluronic P123, clearly demonstrating temperature-dependent control of the proton/water transport rate and the mechanism by adjusting the kind of organic linker.

title: Tuning the electronic environment of the atoms coordinating CuCl x species to regulate vinyl chloride production
abstract: The activity of Cu-based catalysts in acetylene hydrochlorination are linearly regulated by phenylphosphine-based ligand engineering.

title: Water exotic states are responsible for the hydrovoltaic effect and controllable evaporation over carbon fibers – spectroscopic and thermodynamic studies
abstract: Control of the carbon material surface allows for control of hydrovoltaic energy and photothermal conversion in Janus evaporators.

title: Electrophoretic fabrication of alcohol-stable CsPbBr 3 nanocrystalline photoelectrodes for formaldehyde production
abstract: Alcohol-stable CsPbBr 3 photoelectrodes were developed using electrophoretic deposition and DDAB ligands. These electrodes efficiently convert methanol to formaldehyde (60% FE) with a high photocurrent (1.45 mA cm −2 ) in polar solvents.

title: Multifunctional self-healing superhydrophobic coating with rapid sunlight-induced recovery and photothermal anti-icing capability
abstract: Superhydrophobic coatings are often constrained by poor resistance to chemical corrosion and mechanical wear, limiting their long-term reliability.

title: Switching peroxomonosulfate activation to a non-radical dominant pathway by a photo-driven Bi 0 mediator in an S-scheme heterojunction
abstract: The construction of an S-type heterojunction for in situ generation of metallic Bi 0 transforms the PMS activation pathway into a non-radical-dominated route where 1 O 2 serves as the primary active species.

title: Tailoring optical and ferroelectric properties in Sb 1− x Bi x SI van der Waals chalcohalides towards solar absorber applications
abstract: The efficiency of solar cells depends on absorber materials with tunable optical and electronic properties. Here we explore the effect of Sb by Bi substitution on the optical and ferroelectric properties of Sb 1− x Bi x SI chalcohalides.

title: Performance and diffusion optimization mechanism in Al-assisted grain boundary diffused Nd–Fe–B magnets
abstract: The grain boundary diffusion (GBD) method employing heavy rare-earth (HRE) elements effectively enhances the coercivity of sintered Nd–Fe–B magnets, yet often at the expense of the squareness factor.

title: Enhancing the operational stability and radiation hardness of p–i–n perovskite solar cells via a hybrid polymer-based self-assembled hole-transport layer
abstract: Instability at the buried interface in p–i–n perovskite photovoltaics, caused by diffusion of self-assembled hole‑transport material (SAM HTM) molecules, can be overcome through the rational design of polymer-based HTMs.

title: Vacancy-defective spinel NiCo 2 O 4 enables high-valent Ni/Co species and adsorbate binding for the electrocatalytic upcycling of polybutylene succinate plastics
abstract: A vacancy-defective NiCo 2 O 4 catalyst with high-valent Ni/Co species and strong adsorbate binding enables the enhanced electrooxidation of 1,4-butanediol, which realizes the upcycling of PBS plastics to value-added C 4 platform chemicals.

title: Engineering electron- and ion-transporting active pore channels in ultrathin covalent organic framework nanosheets for enhanced photocatalysis
abstract: The construction of porous photocatalysts with high-density active sites accessible to both electrons and ions is crucial for enhancing photocatalytic efficiency and improving the economic feasibility.

title: From cluster halides to catalysts: nanostructured molybdenum carbides for efficient hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: Original preparation of powdered nanostructured Mo 2 C HER catalyst from nanometric molybdenum-based cluster and sucrose.

title: Aqueous PVP-to-HOC conversion enables the fabrication of binder/current-collector free flexible LMO cathodes with high energy
abstract: A low-temperature, aqueous PVP → HOC route forms conformal interlayers in CNT/LMO cathodes, enabling the fabrication of flexible, free-standing full cells with high energy density.

title: Demulsification-enabled entanglement anchoring prepared superlubrication hydrogel for antifouling of vessel surfaces
abstract: Hydrogels, known for their high water content and biocompatibility, have garnered attention for applications necessitating lubrication and antifouling properties.

title: Orbital hybridization-induced direct Z -scheme in CuCoO 2 /MoX 2 heterostructures for overcoming kinetic imbalance in overall water splitting
abstract: Delafossite CuCoO 2 is a promising p-type OER photocatalyst, though its application in overall water splitting is hindered by a pronounced kinetic imbalance between the HER and OER pathways, alongside limited operational stability.

title: From ancient wisdom to modern building materials: high-strength, low-carbon bio-mineral composites inspired by traditional architecture
abstract: We report a high-performance, green building bio-mineral composite fabricated under mild conditions by integrating a carbonate mineral binder with wood sawdust.

title: Engineered 3D-printed Bi 4 O 5 I 2 @hematite scaffolds for visible light photocatalytic degradation of cresols
abstract: Here, Bi 4 O 5 I 2 is decorated onto 3D-printed hematite scaffold, results a photocatalysis upto 99.78% degradation of 20 mg L −1 p -cresol within 240 min and retained 84.28% efficiency after 10 reuse cycles with negligible leaching.

title: Enhanced overall water splitting under visible light irradiation over Ir- and La-codoped NaTaO 3 by post KCl flux treatment
abstract: KCl flux treatment of Ir- and La-codoped NaTaO 3 forms K + ion-enriched surfaces by substitution, and the formed K + ion-enriched surfaces function as reduction sites of water to produce H 2 , resulting in improved water-splitting performance.

title: Coordination-layer engineering of Pt single atoms on h-BN for propane dehydrogenation via a p-band descriptor validated by multiscale modeling and knowledge-informed machine learning
abstract: A p-band-center descriptor unifies bipolar electronic regulation of Pt single atoms on h-BN, where carbon doping in the coordination shell oppositely tunes Pt cationic and anionic sites for propane dehydrogenation.

title: Transformative binder-free strategy breaks cyclability limits for high-energy density oxide anode-based lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Fabrication strategy for high-capacity, long-life Li-ion batteries with ultra-stable multi-metal oxide anodes.

title: Static promise versus dynamic reality in a Z -scheme photocatalyst: nonadiabatic dynamics reveal a charge-separation bottleneck in MoSi 2 P 4 /WTe 2
abstract: The practical efficiency of Z -scheme photocatalytic heterostructures is often limited by poorly understood interfacial carrier dynamics.

title: Unveiling reaction dynamics and degradation pathways in microwave-synthesized antimony anodes for Na-ion batteries
abstract: A kinetically driven progressive shift from the conventional reaction pathway to a low-potential alternative pathway is experimentally revealed during sodiation in a nanosized Sb/C composite anode, coinciding with long-term capacity fade.

title: Carbon tetrafluoride – sulphur hexafluoride adsorption and separation with zirconium metal–organic frameworks decorated with fluorinated thiazolium salts
abstract: Fluorinated thiazolium salts embedded in a microporous zirconium MOF enhance its adsorption capacity towards SF 6 and CF 4 . The F-MOFs show a preferential adsorption for SF 6 , achieving an efficient SF 6 /CF 4 separation from binary equimolar mixtures.

title: Engineering a dual-site CuO/Cu 2 O/CeO 2 heterostructure: synergistic Cu + /Cu 2+ and CeO 2 modulation for tandem ammonia electrosynthesis from nitrate
abstract: The in situ formed CuO/Cu 2 O/CeO 2 heterostructure enables NO 3 − -to-NH 3 conversion via [2 + 6]e − tandem pathway, achieving 99.85% FE, 43.37 mg h −1 cm −2 yield, 120 h stability, and 7.10 mW cm −2 in Zn–NO 3 − batteries.

title: Impact of the polymer donor side-chain length on the formation and processing of waterborne nanoparticles for organic solar cells
abstract: This study reveals how side-chain extension modifies the film morphology upon thermal annealing of water-processed organic solar cells. Power conversion efficiencies over 10% was achieved for longer alkyl side chain, with only mild thermal treatment.

title: A failure-cascade view of cathode degradation and intervention strategies in aqueous Zn-ion batteries
abstract: A failure-cascade framework organizes degradation in aqueous Zn cathodes (Mn oxides, V hosts, and PBAs) by linking chemistry-specific triggers to sequential damage, guiding high-leverage intervention strategies.

title: Fluorinated surface-to-bulk engineering of sodium trititanate for developing sodium-ion batteries with high rate capability and long cycling life
abstract: Fluorinated surface-to-bulk modification of Na 2 Ti 3 O 7 lowers the bandgap energy and strengthens the Ti–F bonds, thus effectively improving its rate capability and cycling stability.

title: Synergistic buried interfacial optimization and phase-composition regulation enable highly efficient Dion–Jacobson perovskite solar cells
abstract: Introducing glycine (Gly) into the SnO 2 ETL simultaneously passivates buried-interface defects and optimizes the phase composition in Q-2D DJ perovskite films, enabling an excellent PCE of 18.20% for Q-2D PSCs.

title: Phosphorus–nitrogen dual-doping engineering in hard carbon microspheres for enhanced sodium-ion storage
abstract: Phosphorus and nitrogen co-doped hard carbon microspheres are prepared via a modified Stöber method. Dual heteroatom doping enhances active sites and structural stability, enabling high reversible capacity and long-term cycling for SIB.

title: Correction: Interfacial electrochemistry of Zn anodes in aqueous electrolytes with combined organic cation and halide additives
abstract: Correction for ‘Interfacial electrochemistry of Zn anodes in aqueous electrolytes with combined organic cation and halide additives’ by Sang Hyuk Gong et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2026, https://doi.org/10.1039/D6TA00823B.

title: Trifunctional noble-metal-free multi-site electrocatalysts based on NiMn-LDH/CuCo 2 S 4 /rGO for energy-saving hydrogen generation via the UOR/HER/OER
abstract: A NiMn-LDH/CuCo 2 S 4 /rGO bifunctional electrocatalyst with remarkable active sites and charge transfer improvement resulting from the strong electrostatic coupling between rGO and NiMn-LDH/CuCo 2 S 4 shows remarkable performance for the OER and UOR.

title: Spatial decoupling-engineered MOF-based nanofibrous membranes: in situ construction of a self-alkaline microenvironment for real-time nerve agent simulant degradation
abstract: Chemical warfare agents pose a severe threat to human life and global security due to their extremely high toxicity, rapid lethality, and potential for mass destruction.

title: Cationic Polarity Facilitation with Tailored Aminopyridine Ligands for Bulkier Defect-Free and Air-Stable Perovskite Solar Cells
abstract: Defective crystal framework in the bulk of formamidinium (FA)-dominant perovskite, combined with their inferior phase stability, mainly compromises efficiency reliability and environmental robustness in perovskite solar cells (PSCs). Here, 2,6bis(aminomethyl)pyridine...

title: Designing a fluorinated interphase for rechargeable aqueous Al batteries
abstract: A fluorinated interphase is fabricated on an Al anode through a facile trifluoroacetic acid treatment, which enables the operation of an aqueous aluminum battery with high anode kinetics, suppressed HER and long-term cycling stability.

title: Rapid lithium extraction via solar-thermal interfacial evaporation with zero liquid discharge
abstract: Self-sustaining solar thermal interfacial evaporator with simultaneous extraction of lithium (STEEL) for cogeneration of water and rechargeable battery material.

title: Tantalum oxynitride (TaON): synthesis routes, structural diversity, and solar water splitting activity
abstract: This review summarizes experimentally confirmed polymorphs (β-, γ-, δ-, bixbyite-, and related metastable phases) and computationally-predicted structures, synthesis routes, optoelectronic properties, and solar water splitting performance of TaON.

title: A 3D alloy with (101) oriented planes toward a deeply dischargeable zinc metal anode
abstract: A 3D nickel foam-supported Ni@Zn–Bi anode with a Zn (101) oriented plane attains 1100 hours of cycling with 99.76% coulombic efficiency at 40% depth of discharge.

title: Rational design of a RuO 2 /0.5CeO 2 heterostructure as an efficient and stable electrocatalyst for acidic water splitting
abstract: Interfacial engineering of RuO 2 /0.5CeO 2 heterostructures: inducing an AEM-dominated pathway for enhanced acidic OER performance and durability.

title: Regulating solvation structure and interfacial chemistry via multifunctional acetylurea for highly reversible neutral zinc–manganese flow batteries
abstract: Acetylurea synchronizes Mn 2+ solvation and interfacial chemistry, enabling highly reversible neutral zinc-manganese flow batteries with greatly prolonged cycling.

title: A multifunctional polymer brush network binder for high-nickel cathodes: architecture-driven elastic recovery, adhesion, and Li + transport
abstract: A multifunctional architecture-driven polymer brush network binder enables high-loading cathodes by enhancing mechanical resilience, interfacial adhesion, and ion transport properties.

title: The hidden cost of solvent exchange: how mechanical damage compromises Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene nanosheet properties
abstract: This study provides understanding of how mechanical degradation and residual solvents after solution processing collectively impact the electrical conductivity of MXene nanosheets.

title: Designing narrow bandgap small-molecule acceptors with unilateral vinylene π-bridge for high-performance optoelectronic devices
abstract: A series of NBG SMAs were synthesized via a unilateral vinylene π-bridge and terminal halogenation strategy. The NBG OSCs achieved PCE of 14.2%, while the NIR OPDs exhibited ultralow J d , high R , and superior D *.

title: A multifunctional bismuth-based metal–organic framework with record-high porosity, rare topology, and efficient visible light photocatalysis
abstract: UU-206 is a new bismuth MOF with record porosity, rare topology, strong gas sorption, and efficient visible-light photocatalysis.

title: Advancing aqueous zinc-ion batteries through polymer interface engineering: from mechanisms to applications
abstract: This review introduces a mechanism-oriented framework for polymer protective layers in Zn batteries. It covers two functions, six mechanisms, a structure–property database, failure coupling, and trade-offs for rational material design.

title: Nacre-mimetic Calcium Silicate Hydrate (C-S-H) Composite with High Strength and Toughness
abstract: Low toughness remains an enduring constraint to cementitious materials. Manipulating calcium silicate hydrate (C-S-H), the “genetic component” of cement, which determines mechanical properties of cementitious materials, presents an attractive strategy...

title: Hierarchical Ni-doped Fe 7 S 8 /MoS 2 heterostructures: enhanced oxygen evolution reaction via electronic redistribution and structural reconstruction
abstract: We have developed Ni-Fe 7 S 8 /MoS 2 hierarchical heterostructures on iron foam via one-step in situ sulfidation. The catalyst exhibits excellent OER performance due to its unique morphology, modulated electronic structure, and partial reconstruction.

title: Additive free Co-lean ternary NiCoAl/Ni foam electrode for high-efficiency energy storage and low-grade heat harvesting
abstract: Additive-free, Co-lean NiCoAl LDH microflowers on Ni foam deliver high electrochemical performance and a remarkable Seebeck coefficient, enabling a bifunctional electrode for energy storage and conversion applications.

title: Solar-driven electrochemical nitrate reduction to ammonia catalyzed by a nickel cobalt oxide-palladium heterojunction
abstract: The catalyst of nickel cobalt oxide-palladium heterojunction was effectively constructed and applied to nitrate electroreduction to ammonia, which is solar-driven.

title: Self-Assembly-Driven Nanodot Confinement and Interfacial Engineering of Cobalt Metaphosphates for Electrocatalytic Oxygen Evolution
abstract: Metal phosphates possess attractive structural and electronic characteristics for the oxygen evolution reaction (OER) in alkaline media, yet their application is often limited by poor electrical conductivity and insufficient control...

title: Rare earth-mediated synergistic lattice stabilization and interface protection for suppressed phase transition and superior electrochemical performance in Ni-rich cathodes
abstract: Spray-drying/high-temperature solid-state strategy simultaneously forms ≈3 nm LiSmO2 coating & Sm-dopes Ni-rich cathodes, stabilizing lattice/interface, suppressing side reactions, delivering 94.5% retention (150 cycles, 1.0C) & 153.49 mA h g−1 at 10C.

title: External chirality-driven interfacial spin filtering in magnetically aligned NiFe-LDH facilitates the enhancement of the oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: Here, we uniquely combine magnetic catalysis, chirality, and spin-selective transport, departing from traditional chiral catalytic systems, to establish a new pathway for designing spin-dependent catalytic systems beyond conventional approaches.

title: Boosting CO 2 electrolysis via a metal-free F/Gd co-doped CeO 2 cathode for solid oxide electrolysis cells
abstract: As an efficient device for energy conversion, solid oxide electrolysis cells (SOECs) can utilize renewable energy to efficiently convert CO 2 into CO, realizing both CO 2 resource utilization and chemical storage of renewable energy.

title: Recycling with precision: engineering Ni-rich NMC cathodes through impurity management
abstract: NMC811 resynthesised from industrially recovered precursors achieved battery-grade purity and competitive electrochemical performance. Impurity–structure–morphology relationships are elucidated to inform recycling-enabled cathode engineering.

title: Dehydroxylation driven reactivity in kaolinite: unraveling strain structure interplay for enhanced electrocatalytic water splitting
abstract: Thermal modification of kaolinite: structural rearrangement and coordination evolution.

title: A Universal Magnetic Alignment Strategy for Composite Solid-state electrolytes with Vertically Aligned High-Ionic-Conductivity Pathways
abstract: The low ionic conductivity of polymer-based solid electrolytes limits their application. Conventional enhancements by adding inorganic fillers typically requires material-specific modifications or complex processing. Here, we report a universal magnetic...

title: Understanding dissolution mechanism of oxide perovskites and activity tuning for O 2 evolution by surface doping
abstract: We report a design principle for tuning the oxygen evolution reaction catalytic efficiency and stability of SrMnO 3 , SrFeO 3 , SrCoO 3 , and SrNiO 3 .

title: Synergistic high-entropy engineering in biphasic layered oxides enables high-rate sodium-ion cathodes
abstract: The high-entropy biphasic oxide cathode, P2/O3-MNFCTLM, suppressed unstable O n − formation at high voltage relative to P2-MNF.

title: Highly durable superhydrophobic activated biochar catalyst for biodiesel synthesis: process optimization and economic feasibility analysis
abstract: A superhydrophobic, ether-linked alkyl chain functionalized biochar catalyst enabled efficient biodiesel synthesis with minimal activity loss over multiple cycles.

title: Atomic-scale surface design for tailored nucleation in stable multivalent metal anodes
abstract: Atomically dispersed Zn on porous carbon scaffolds acts as strong magnesiophilic sites for uniform Mg nucleation. Such architecture enables reversible Mg plating/stripping, suppresses dendrite growth, and enhances cycling stability.

title: Higher-order phonon scattering and lattice thermal conductivity prediction via machine learning
abstract: Higher-order phonon scattering processes, such as three-phonon (3ph) and four-phonon (4ph) interactions, critically influence lattice thermal conductivity ( κ L ).

title: Zn 2+ -coordinated hierarchical dynamic crosslinking in phenolized lignin-based polyurethane elastomers: mechanical self-reinforcement and dynamic adaptability enhancement
abstract: Hierarchical dynamic crosslinking integrating phenol-carbamate bonds, Zn 2+ coordination bonds, and hydrogen bonds enables mechanically self-reinforcing and reprocessable lignin-based polyurethane elastomers.

title: Cyclic olefin copolymer-based reinforced anion exchange membranes for water electrolyzers
abstract: Anion exchange membranes (AEMs) have emerged as a promising technology for water electrolysis in hydrogen production since they offer significant cost reduction in choices of electrocatalysts and bipolar plates.

title: Bimetal co-doped nickel sulphides for enhanced seawater-mediated chlorine evolution reaction: an avenue for in situ pollutant mitigation
abstract: Au-Ag dual-doped nickel sulfide electrocatalyst for selective chlorine evolution reaction and in situ NH 4 + -N pollutant mitigation.

title: Correction: Tracking phase transformations in LiMn 1.5+ x Ni 0.5− x O 4 by high resolution X-ray diffraction
abstract: Correction for ‘Tracking phase transformations in LiMn 1.5+ x Ni 0.5− x O 4 by high resolution X-ray diffraction’ by Halvor Høen Hval et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2025, 13 , 41122–41143, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TA05130D.

title: Characteristic time integration to characterize gas diffusion and surface reaction steps of the oxygen reduction reaction via porous electrocatalysts for solid oxide fuel cells
abstract: The theoretical principle of the characteristic time integration (CTI) method is illustrated. The characteristic time of surface reactions caused by gas diffusion is discrete.

title: Bidirectional interfacial interactions and Cu x S bond formation enabled by a thiourea-crosslinked binder for high-performance micro-SiO x anodes
abstract: The thiourea-crosslinked poly(acrylic acid- co -acrylamide) binder interacts strongly with Si, enhances interfacial adhesion with the Cu current collector, and alleviates Si's volume expansion to form a stable SEI layer.

title: Functional off-stoichiometry in Cu(In,Ga)Se 2 . Part II: electronic properties in a wide range of compositions
abstract: We demonstrate the monotonic band gap widening and impeded hole transport in Cu(In,Ga)Se 2 solar absorbers in the Cu-poor regime.

title: Rational design of aqueous zinc-ion battery electrolytes based on intermolecular interactions between co-solvents and anions
abstract: The rational design of THF-based electrolytes via kosmotropic–chaotropic interactions affords tailored AZIB electrolytes, overcoming interfacial instability and extending electrochemical stability.

title: Effect of gluconate molecules on the reaction kinetics of C-(N)-A-S-H and N-A-S-H gel formation
abstract: This study isolated the role of sodium gluconate in early reaction kinetics across a Ca-rich geopolymer that forms a C-(N)-A-S-H gel and Ca-poor geopolymer that forms a N-A-S-H gel.

title: Efficiently predicting pressure-composition-temperature diagrams to discover low-stability metal hydrides
abstract: A first principles sampling-based approach to PCT prediction in compositionally complex alloys is employed to improve out-of-distribution prediction accuracy compared to data-driven models.

title: Shifting the surface oxidation cycle to a high-valence subsurface boosts water oxidation
abstract: Shifting the oxidation cycle to subsurface W5+ in CoWO4− δ disentangles redox and adsorption sites, significantly boosting water oxidation activity and stability.

title: Electrochemical lithiation of the layered superionic conductors AgCrSe 2 and CuCrSe 2
abstract: Lithiation of AgCrSe 2 proceeds through intercalation, conversion, and Ag–Li alloying, forming LiCrSe 2 , Li 2 Se, and metallic Ag and Cr, with this multi-step pathway leading to its high-capacity reduction behavior.

title: Single-atom Bi embedded in the surface lattice of Pd nanoparticles: enhancing catalytic activity and stability for formic acid dehydrogenation
abstract: Single-atom Bi embedded in the Pd surface lattice synergistically tunes the electronic structure of Pd and introduces Lewis acid sites, enabling Bi 1 @Pd/CMK-3 to achieve high activity and excellent long-term stability in formic acid dehydrogenation.

title: Benzotriazole loading in confined ZIF-8@mSiO 2 nanospaces for waterborne anticorrosion coatings
abstract: A hierarchical BTA-ZIF-8@mSiO 2 nanocarrier integrates coordination-based benzotriazole immobilization and mesoporous silica shell engineering to improve coating integrity and enable durable anticorrosion protection.

title: The influence of GaPt droplet purification on the catalytic performance of supraparticles as supported catalytically active liquid metal solutions (SCALMS) in propane dehydrogenation
abstract: GaPt nanocomposites are colloidally synthesized and purified using three washing procedures that directly affect the catalytic stability in propane dehydrogenation of supported catalytically active liquid metal solutions (SCALMS) fabricated thereof.

title: Interfacial tautomerization-modulated S-scheme MOF/COF heterojunctions for enhanced photocatalytic H 2 O 2 production
abstract: The construction of efficient photocatalyst systems for photocatalytic H 2 O 2 production has attracted extensive research interest.

title: Machine learning-guided screening of phase-stable high-entropy Na cathodes to enable EVs and low-cost charging storage systems
abstract: We propose a high-throughput computational screening framework that integrates high-entropy doping strategies with ML to enhance phase stability in O3-type Na layered oxide cathodes.

title: Electrochemical and computational insights into lithium nucleation at Ni(111) and Cu(111) surfaces for anode-free Li-metal batteries
abstract: The high energy density of anode-free Li-metal batteries (AFLMBs) stems from eliminating the graphite anode, allowing lithium (Li) to be directly electrodeposited onto the current collector during charging.

title: Large language model-enabled machine learning for high performance Nd–Fe–B permanent magnet design
abstract: An LLM-based multimodal framework integrates textual and compositional knowledge for heavy rare-earth-free Nd–Fe–B magnet design.

title: Micro-macro synergistic regulation of the Co–N–C bond and dielectric-magnetic-dielectric sandwich structure for promoting broadband microwave absorption
abstract: Tuning the dielectric loss and impedance matching of carbon fibers (CFs) through heteroatom doping and microstructural design is an effective strategy for enhancing their electromagnetic wave absorption (EWA) performance.

title: Nanoscale thermal transport at PbTe grain boundaries: a neural-network-potential molecular dynamics study
abstract: Neural-network-potential molecular dynamics simulations on 24 PbTe grain boundaries (GBs) show that lattice thermal conductivity depends strongly on GB structure, with distinct correlations for heat flow normal versus parallel to the GB plane.

title: Ligand-substituted inorganic–organic hybrid materials for efficient urea-assisted electrocatalytic water splitting
abstract: Pyrimidine/pyrazine ligand tuning yields a NiClOH hybrid that reconstructs efficiently and enables low-voltage, high-activity urea oxidation.

title: Scalable fabrication of all-ceramic composite cathodes via controlled lithium compensation for Li-garnet batteries
abstract: Two solid-phase Li-compensation routes stabilize LiCoO 2 –LLZO composites, suppress LaCoO 3 -type interphases, and enable dense tape-cast membranes delivering mAh-class areal capacities at practical thickness without non-scalable tools.

title: Highly conductive ester-based solid electrolyte exhibiting remarkable stability for safe, sustainable, and high-performance lithium metal batteries
abstract: This work presents the development of the first solid polymer electrolyte, exhibiting electrochemical stability exceeding 4.5 V, enhanced stability against lithium metal, and higher cationic conductivity compared to PEO + LiTFSI

title: Host–guest directed construction of phenothiazine supramolecular frameworks with enhanced oxygen reactivity for visible-light photocatalysis
abstract: A host–guest constructed two-dimensional phenothiazine supramolecular framework reprograms oxygen reactivity, enabling synergistic singlet oxygen and superoxide generation for efficient visible-light-driven green synthesis of benzothiazoles.

title: Recent advances in polymeric materials for direct carbon dioxide capture from ambient air
abstract: This review highlights recent advances in polymer-based DAC systems for CO 2 capture, with a focus on synthesis strategies and structure–performance relationships in functional polymers, polymer composites, MOFs, and COFs.

title: Impact of dispersed crystalline domains on lithium-ion conductivity in amorphous Li 2.99 Ba 0.005 OCl electrolytes
abstract: Dispersed crystalline domains and dense interfaces bottleneck Li⁺ transport in amorphous Li 2.99 Ba 0.005 OCl by depleting free volume and increasing tortuosity. Maximizing amorphous continuity is crucial for high conductivity.

title: A coordination chemistry strategy of ammonia molecular pillaring for structurally reinforced Prussian blue cathodes in sodium-ion batteries
abstract: We report a coordination chemistry strategy where labile crystal water in Prussian blue is substituted with ammonia. The thermodynamically favored NH 3 coordination effectively stabilizes the open framework, unlocking superior cycling durability.

title: From kinetics to cathode layer design: establishing targets for cathodes in anion-exchange membrane electrolyzers
abstract: Broad design principles for AEM cathode catalysts and catalyst layers elucidated through kinetic analysis.

title: Improved piezo-catalysis through integration of dual-active semiconductors in a NaNbO 3 /MoS 2 heterojunction for degradation of rhodamine B dye and ofloxacin antibiotic, and Cr( vi ) reduction
abstract: Piezocatalysis has emerged as a promising and sustainable method for degrading hazardous contaminants in aqueous environments.

title: Retraction: Convolutional neural network prediction of the photocurrent–voltage curve directly from scanning electron microscopy images
abstract: Retraction of ‘Convolutional neural network prediction of the photocurrent–voltage curve directly from scanning electron microscopy images’ by Yuta Hayashi et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2023, 11 , 22522–22532, https://doi.org/10.1039/D3TA05282F.

title: Bio-inspired spiral-structured poly(vinyl alcohol)/liquid metal/carbon nanofiber composites for efficient thermal management
abstract: Liquid metals (LMs) have garnered significant interest for developing LM-filled flexible composites due to their high thermal conductivity and fluidity.

title: Acrylamide-based hydrogels for rechargeable zinc-based batteries: properties, synthesis and applications
abstract: We present the fundamental molecular structure, characteristics, optimization strategy for physicochemical properties, synthesis methods and applications of acrylamide-based hydrogels in zinc-based batteries.

title: Fe/Fe 3 O 4 heterostructures supported on N,O-doped hollow carbon spheres with high catalytic performance for oxygen reduction and zinc–air batteries
abstract: The oxygen reduction reaction (ORR) is a crucial half-reaction in energy conversion and storage systems, playing a significant role in new energy devices such as fuel cells and metal–air batteries.

title: High-temperature variant of oxygen-rich covalent triazine frameworks as multifunctional electrocatalysts
abstract: Hetero-structured, oxygen-rich covalent triazine frameworks synthesized via high-temperature ionothermal routes with optimized heteroatom content and defect density, serve as multifunctional electrocatalysts, rivalling commercial Pt/C and RuO 2 .

title: Orbital overlap-stabilized amorphous metal phosphate frameworks for industrial current density electrocatalysis
abstract: A durable, low-cost catalyst for hydrogen production is achieved by introducing trace Ru into amorphous metal phosphate. This stabilizes structure, improves charge transport, and enables efficient water splitting at industrial current densities.

title: A phase-tuned layered 1T W-MoS 2 /PEDOT:PSS hybrid: functional engineering towards self-binding supercapacitor cathodes
abstract: The substitutional doping of W into PEDOT:PSS integrated MoS 2 , leads to increased interlayer spacing between the MoS 2 layers. This results in enhanced performance when utilized as self-binding cathode by fabricating coin cell device.

title: A plant-stabilized and self-initiated liquid metal hydrogel for high-performance multifunctional sensing and infrared camouflage
abstract: A dual-level interface stabilization strategy enables a green one-pot synthesis of EGaIn-reinforced hydrogels with excellent performance for real-time motion monitoring and infrared camouflage.

title: Entropy-driven mechanisms in P2-type layered oxide cathodes for sodium-ion batteries: new insights from first-principles and electrochemical analysis
abstract: Configurational entropy stabilizes P2-type Na x MO 2 layered cathodes by suppressing TM migration and P2–O2 gliding. High-entropy compositions distribute redox activity, improving structural reversibility and cycling stability in Na-ion batteries.

title: An acid–base dual-immune Cu electrocatalyst via quaternized chitosan buffering for pH universal nitrate reduction to ammonia
abstract: Quaternized chitosan enhances NO 3 RR activity of Cu catalyst by enriching reactants, generating active sites, and enabling pH-universal stability. The process is integrated with adipic acid production, achieving a high combined electron efficiency.

title: Multi-capture site design in a bifunctional Zr-MOF for broad-spectrum and efficient removal of short- and long-chain PFASs
abstract: Bifunctional MOF-808 captures short/long-chain PFAS via amino/fluorinated groups. A unique pore effect reduces C5 adsorption. Effective and reusable in wastewater, it offers a new PFAS-removal strategy.

title: A highly efficient antibacterial solar-thermal evaporator based on waste tea leaves for water evaporation and thermoelectric generation
abstract: We developed a strategy for preparing a highly efficient antibacterial solar-thermal evaporator by waste tea, which mitigates microbial contamination, extends the lifespan of evaporators, and improves the water quality of evaporated water.

title: Photosynthesis-inspired dual-mode self-healing coatings: leveraging peony-like ZnO for corrosion interception and energy harvesting
abstract: Inspired by photosynthesis, a dual-mode self-healing coating was developed: it enables rapid repair via photothermal conversion and long-term anti-corrosion protection via ‘multilayered trapping effect’ of peony-like ZnO heterostructure.

title: Polymerized tungstate-molybdenum sulfide electrocatalysts for the hydrogen evolution reaction under acidic conditions
abstract: Polyoxometalate-based electrocatalysts represent a promising class of earth-abundant electrocatalysts for hydrogen production; however, their large-scale synthesis remains challenging due to the multi-step procedures.

title: A self-adaptive ternary NiCoMo-carbon composite as a bifunctional electrocatalyst for high-current-density water splitting
abstract: A self-adaptive carbon-embedded ternary NiCoMo composite via electrodeposition enables surface reconfiguration, achieving efficient overall water splitting at 1000 mA cm −2 .

title: Electron trap engineering in g-C 3 N 4 with molecular Mo 3 S 4 clusters for visible-light-driven photocatalysis
abstract: [Mo 3 S 4 Cl 3 (en) 3 ]Cl clusters modulate g-C 3 N 4 electronically, creating trap states that enhance charge separation and visible-light activity, unveiling a new route to tailor photocatalysts for efficient ROS generation and pollutant degradation.

title: Unveiling wavelength-dependent roles of light in dynamic structures for photothermal toluene oxidation over atomically dispersed Pd/TiO 2
abstract: Visible light-driven hot electron transfer from Pd to TiO 2 via Pd–O–Ti creates oxygen vacancies and enhances ˙O 2 − for efficient toluene oxidation, whereas UV-induced reverse transfer yields negligible structural change and catalytic improvement.

title: Modulating catalyst surface wettability to boost electrochemical ammonia synthesis under ambient conditions
abstract: Engineered hydrophobic–hydrophilic regions tune catalyst wettability to control the triple-phase boundary, promoting N 2 adsorption while limiting proton transport. This enhances NRR selectivity and suppresses competing HER.

title: Electrochemical-assisted triple oxidation strategy to efficiently extract lithium from spent LiFePO 4
abstract: Electrochemical-assisted triple oxidation strategy to efficiently extract lithium from spent LiFePO 4 using Ti 4 O 7 electrode as the anode at ambient conditions.

title: Superhydrophilic electrodes with defect-trapped Pt nanoparticles boost the acidic hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: The hydrogen evolution reaction (HER) is a critical electrochemical process in water electrolysis.

title: Synergistic bulk and interface modification via lanthanization enhances structural stability and ion kinetics in O3-NaNi 1/3 Fe 1/3 Mn 1/3 O 2 cathodes
abstract: This work simultaneously constructs a lanthanide-based surface layer and achieves gradient La 3+ doping in NaNi 1/3 Fe 1/3 Mn 1/3 O 2 (NFM), leading to remarkable improvements in both rate capability and cycling stability.

title: Ultrasmall FeNiP x nanoparticles embedded into amorphous FeNiO x nanosheets for industrial-level high current density water oxidation
abstract: A highly active and durable electrocatalyst, made up of dense ultrasmall crystalline FeNiP x nanoparticles embedded into amorphous FeNiO x nanosheets, is designed and demonstrated for ampere-level OER current density.

title: Machine-learning-assisted high-throughput screening for accelerating the discovery of diatomic catalysts in N 2 electroreduction
abstract: An ML–DFT integrated high-throughput screening framework for diatomic catalysts toward the nitrogen reduction reaction (NRR).

title: Red light-active porphyrin-based Zr-MOF nanorods enable highly selective aerobic oxidation of sulfides and amines
abstract: The strategic utilization of red-light-responsive photocatalysts presents a unique opportunity to perform organic reactions under mild conditions, thereby minimizing side reactions and broadening the scope of viable substrates.

title: Synergistic Cr-doping and crystalline/amorphous heterointerface engineering to promote surface reconstruction of FeCo LDH for efficient oxygen evolution
abstract: Synergistic Cr-doping and amorphous NiS x heterointerface engineering to promote surface reconstruction of FeCo LDH for efficient oxygen evolution.

title: Chloride modification of vacuum-assisted blade-coated perovskite solar cells and mini-modules in ambient environment
abstract: In situ PL reveals that vacuum quenching a perovskite composition with PbCl 2 additive slows the crystallization and ultimately suppresses non-radiative recombination in perovskites.

title: Phase engineering of two-dimensional MoS 2 : from structural evolution to tribological applications
abstract: Precise control of MoS 2 phase transitions optimizes its lubrication performance. The tribological efficiency is influenced by environmental conditions and phase state. Nonlinear friction regulation techniques are crucial for phase evolution.

title: Structural transformations with Li + -deintercalation in cathode materials Li 3 VSc(PO 4 ) 3 with anti-NASICON and NASICON structures
abstract: Although vanadium-containing phosphates with the NASICON-related structure are promising cathode materials for lithium-ion batteries, the issues of structural degradation associated with the complete oxidation of vanadium remain unclear.

title: Advanced modification strategies of CdS-based photocatalysts for enhanced green hydrogen production
abstract: Solar-driven photocatalytic water-splitting offers a promising route toward sustainable hydrogen (H 2 ) production and decarbonization.

title: A novel 1,1,6,6-tetracyano hexatriene linked conjugated microporous polymer for high photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: A TCHT linked 2D-polymer was synthesized via [2 + 2] CA–RE reaction. It shows NIR absorption, radical character, and high photocatalytic H 2 production due to strong D–A interactions and nitrile groups improving hydrophilicity and charge separation.

title: Exploring the potential of transition metal tungstates for photoelectrochemical water oxidation: a combined experimental and computational approach
abstract: DFT theoretical and experimental insights into transition metal tungstates reveal electronic structure–property relationships controlling photoanode activity, which enable selecting CuWO 4 and FeWO 4 as promising electrode materials.

title: Entropy-assisted nitrogen-doped carbon-anchored high-entropy alloy composites for efficient and stable universal photovoltaic electrodes
abstract: HEA NPs@NC accelerates interfacial charge separation and transport with its abundant active sites and precise energy-level alignment, enhancing the photovoltaic performance of dye sensitized solar cells and HTL-free C-perovskite solar cells.

title: Rational design of an organic–anionic electrolyte additive for zinc-ion batteries
abstract: The Zn‖Zn symmetric cell with an acrylamide and sodium p -aminobenzenesulfonate electrolyte additive can be stably charged and discharged for over 1600 h at 2 mA cm −2 .

title: Precise diameter control derived intermetallic PtNiCo nanowires: a simple two-step synthesis and operando XAS insight
abstract: Developing efficient, high-performance intermetallic PtNiCo nanowire catalysts with precise diameter control is crucial for enabling the commercial deployment of proton exchange membrane fuel cells (PEMFCs).

title: Morphology and facet effects on the charge and discharge mechanisms in FeSe 2 -based lithium-ion storage
abstract: FeSe 2 spheres, stars and bars as anodes for lithium storage exhibit distinct stages of capacity rise and fall. Synchrotron X-ray diffraction analysis revealed a unique anode composition change during charging and discharging.

title: A photo-thermo-electric conversion system based on a photothermal organic cocrystal composite film for outdoor self-powered supply
abstract: An excellent photothermal cocrystal facilitates power generation in solar-driven thermoelectric devices, paving the way for outdoor portable self-powered systems.

title: Constructing a multi-dimensional hierarchical structure to improve the cycling stability of silicon nanowires
abstract: This work's 0D‑1D‑3D SiNWs hierarchy tackles Si anode expansion and poor conductivity via three-level synergy: 0D nanoparticles buffer stress, 1D nanowires conduct electrons, and the 3D network stabilizes the structure.

title: Exploring the electrocatalytic performance of PdIrSnZnMo high entropy alloy (HEA) towards the hydrogen evolution reaction in an acidic medium: a theoretically supported approach
abstract: The present study highlights the HER electrocatalytic performance of PdIrSnZnMo HEA with the delivery of a low overpotential of 17 mV to reach the benchmark current density (−10 mA cm −2 ) and excellent stability for 100 h in an acidic medium.

title: Emerging M 5 X 4 MXenes: from atomic structure to applications
abstract: A systematic discussion of the expansion of M 5 X 4 MXenes, their properties, and their applications has been presented. Further, critical future research directions for the M 5 X 4 MXene family have been provided towards broader applications.

title: The role of oxygen excess on fluoride intercalation in Ruddlesden–Popper electrodes for fluoride ion batteries: the case of LaSrMnO 4
abstract: LaSrMnO 4 is an intercalation material for fluoride ion batteries. Oxygen over-stoichiometry in the sample affects fluoride intercalation mechanisms and energetics, unveiling new migration pathways that hinder fluoride mobility.

title: Effectively controlling Ni photodeposition on polyhedron SrTiO 3 via double-doped Al and Co
abstract: The image shows that the Al/Co double-doped polyhedron SrTiO 3 with nanoclusters and satellite single atoms of Co offers a highly efficient photoreduction of Ni 2+ to Ni 0 . Most of the Ni elements are enriched in the (112) facet.

title: Silver single atom in polymeric carbon nitride as a stable and selective oxygen reduction electrocatalyst towards hydrogen peroxide synthesis
abstract: Single atom Ag catalyst is stabilized in polymeric carbon nitride (PCN) (a) and catalyzes H 2 O 2 electrosynthesis. *OOH adsorbs on Ag coordinated in the heptazine ring (b). Ag–PCN decreases adsorption free energy of *OOH intermediate (c).

title: Suppressing the dissolution of organic components to construct a low-solubility SEI in sodium-ion batteries: the critical role of electrolyte additive TMSPi
abstract: The solid electrolyte interphase (SEI) formed during repeated cycling plays crucial roles in determining the cycling stability of sodium-ion batteries (SIBs).

title: Recent advances in MOF-derived materials for supercapacitors: modification strategies and electrochemical properties
abstract: MOF-derived materials constitute a versatile family with diverse morphological features, and these derivatives have exhibited promising electrochemical performance when applied in supercapacitors.

title: Stable N , O -acetal covalent adaptable networks: bio-based, highly dynamic and fully recyclable materials
abstract: A novel covalent adaptable network based on highly dynamic N , O -acetal exchangeable functions is described: 92% C biobased, mechanically reprocessable and fully chemically recyclable.

title: Oxygen vacancy-rich In 2 O 3 -ZrO 2 catalysts synthesized via DBD plasma for enhanced CO 2 -to-CO conversion
abstract: DBD-engineered In-Zr catalysts with abundant oxygen vacancies exhibit strong CO 2 adsorption and electron-driven activation, delivering efficient and selective CO 2 -to-CO conversion under plasma conditions.

title: Multiphase NiMo with spatially separated active sites enabled by data-assisted synthesis for spillover-driven alkaline hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: Efficient hydrogen evolution in alkaline media requires electrocatalysts that couple rapid water dissociation with facile hydrogen desorption.

title: Engineering MOFs for thin-film and nanofilm nanocomposite membranes for CO 2 separation
abstract: This paper critically reviews strategies for molecularly engineering crystalline MOFs to enhance their distribution within polymers and to improve CO 2 /gas separation properties in the resulting thin-film and nanofilm nanocomposite membranes.

title: Data-Driven Design Strategies for Chemically Stabilizing Cathode-Coating Interfaces Employing Interpretable Machine Learning
abstract: The chemical stability of cathode–coating interfaces plays a critical role in enabling high-energy, high-voltage battery systems; however, the key descriptors governing interfacial compatibility remain poorly understood. In this study, we...

title: Symmetry-broken FeN 3 –O on a negatively charged carbon host: dual modulation of electronic structure and interfacial field for robust oxygen reduction
abstract: A negatively charged carbon host creates an interfacial electric field to facilitate O 2 activation, synergizing with a symmetry-broken FeN 3 –O site to achieve superior ORR kinetics.

title: Ether-functionalized asymmetric lithium imide salts: molecular design principles for low-melting molten salt electrolytes
abstract: Controlled ether functionalization enables low-melting molten lithium salts with clustered transport and near-unity Li + transference numbers.

title: A vinylene-linked covalent organic framework with optimized functional sites for efficient C 2 H 2 /C 2 H 4 separation
abstract: A novel nitrogen-rich vinylene-linked COF denoted as FZU-98 was prepared via aldol condensation. More abundant nitrogen adsorption sites endow FZU-98 with much higher adsorption toward C 2 H 2 (4.9) in the C 2 H 2 /C 2 H 4 mixture, as compared to COF-DFB.

title: Mesoporous cobalt difluoride synchronizes aluminum oxidation and perchlorate decomposition for enhanced oxygen transfer in energetic composites
abstract: Mesoporous CoF 2 synchronizes oxygen release from KClO 4 and consumption by Al via dual functions: etching Al 2 O 3 and catalyzing perchlorate decomposition. The Al/KClO 4 /CoF 2 composite achieves ultrahigh heat release and prolonged stable combustion.
