title: Spin polarized oxygen evolution reaction in chiral amorphous manganese-doped cobalt oxide: Enhancing electrocatalytic activity via lattice oxygen participation
abstract: Chiral-induced spin selectivity (CISS) in chiral inorganic materials has emerged as a promising strategy for enhancing water electrolysis by generating spin-polarized hydroxyl radicals with parallel spins, which favor the formation...

title: Cu( ii )/Fe( ii ) electron-deficient pairs as bifunctional catalysts for efficient PMS activation and solar-driven water evaporation
abstract: Addressing the dual challenges of organic wastewater purification and freshwater regeneration requires innovative materials capable of multifunctional integration.

title: Au-decorated hollow CuO with modulated electronic structure for efficient electrocatalytic glucose oxidation
abstract: Hollow microspherical Au/N-CuO heterojunction is constructed. Au decoration boosts electron transfer and enhances glucose adsorption. Hollow architecture offers abundant accessible active sites.

title: Tailoring Pt–support interactions via Mn–N x coordination for enhanced phosphoric acid tolerance in HT-PEMFC cathodes
abstract: Mn–N x moieties induce electron transfer from Pt NPs, downshifting the d-band center to weaken PA adsorption and alleviate poisoning.

title: Hierarchical pore-engineered carbon cathodes for high-loading, lean-electrolyte lithium–sulfur cells
abstract: Scalable immersion-precipitation phase-inversion strategy constructs hierarchical carbon cathodes with interconnected large–middle–small pores, enabling high sulfur loadings (up to 10 mg cm −2 ) and lean-electrolyte operation (down to 4 μL mg −1 ).

title: Ultra-robust zinc-ion batteries with Co-solvent water-in-salt electrolytes for enhanced stability and capacity
abstract: A concept of hybrid co-solvent water-in-salt-based aqueous electrolytes to enhance the stability of zinc-ion batteries.

title: Tuning Co-Ni Electrochemical Active Sites via Iron Incorporation in Carbonate Hydroxide Frameworks for High-Performance Supercapacitors
abstract: Transition metal incorporation is widely recognised as an efficient approach to enhance charge storage capability and ion transport behaviour in electrochemically active materials. In the present work, Fe-doped CoNi carbonate...

title: Universal asymmetric single-halogenation of the central unit in non-fullerene acceptors enables high-performance organic solar cells with efficiencies approaching 20%
abstract: Universal asymmetric single-halogenation of the central unit in non-fullerene acceptors enables high-performance organic solar cells with efficiencies approaching 20%.

title: Machine-learning-driven integrated probing of oxygen-vacancy distribution and ionomer morphology in an iridium oxide catalyst–ionomer nanocomposite electrode for water electrolyzers
abstract: A machine-learning-driven EELS method simultaneously maps oxygen-vacancy distribution and ionomer morphology in IrO 2− x electrodes, revealing that ionomers preferentially adsorb onto vacancy-enriched catalyst surfaces.

title: Quasi-low-dimensional perovskites with enhanced stability against lattice strain and desired band edge by A-site design
abstract: The integration of optimal CPAC spacers in quasi-low-dimensional perovskites can be an effective design strategy to enhance structural stability against lattice strain while fine-tuning electronic structures for making durable optoelectronic devices.

title: Impact of connectivity on the electronic structure of 3D carbazole-bridged conjugated systems
abstract: Distinct molecular connectivity in 3D non-fullerene acceptors regulates excited-state behavior and photovoltaic performance. Comparable bandgaps and nonlinear optical responses are retained despite topology-dependent electronic differences.

title: Stabilization of planar heterojunction solar cells using a dimethyl sulfoxide treated hole transport layer
abstract: DMSO-modified PEDOT:PSS enhances the stability of heterojunction solar cells, retaining performance after 72 h. This reduction in degradation is due to improved phase separation, conductivity, and suppressed charge recombination.

title: Pareto-optimization of sustainable high-entropy alloys: balancing hardness and sustainability via machine learning
abstract: Random Forest and Pareto optimization screen 50 000 virtual HEAs, identifying champions with 80–85% lower embodied energy than conventional superalloys.

title: Asymmetric fibre supercapacitors for sustainable energy storage in next-generation soft textile wearables
abstract: Asymmetric fibre supercapacitors (AFSCs) offer higher voltages and energy densities than symmetric designs. This review discusses electrode materials, fabrication technologies, device designs, and future outlooks of AFSCs for soft textile wearables.

title: Electron-beam irradiation enables concurrent thermoelectric and mechanical enhancement in commercial p-type Bi 0.5 Sb 1.5 Te 3
abstract: Electron-beam irradiation enables simultaneous thermoelectric and mechanical enhancement in commercial p-type Bi 0.5 Sb 1.5 Te 3 through defect induction and annealing effect.

title: Carbon confinement engineering in high-density single-atom catalysts: boosting efficient electrochemical energy conversion
abstract: This review summarizes the catalytic mechanisms, confined synthesis strategies, and structure–activity relationships of HDSACs in key electrochemical energy conversion reactions, aiming to guide the rational design of high-performance HDSACs.

title: Ligand-engineered Cu–Zn–In–Se quantum dots for flexible laminated luminescent solar concentrators
abstract: Photo of the flexible laminated luminoscent solar concentrators.

title: Design and fabrication of NPI/γ-Fe 2 O 3 composite films and investigation of the synergistic mechanism for enhanced photothermal conversion efficiency
abstract: NPI films were designed via molecular simulation for efficient fabrication. NPI/γ-Fe 2 O 3 films were prepared by in situ polymerization. Synergy between NPI and γ-Fe 2 O 3 enhanced photothermal performance. Photothermal efficiency improved 6-fold.

title: Structure–property–performance relationship of a series of CO 2 RR-active N-doped mesoporous carbon frameworks
abstract: Structure-controlled mesoporous carbons reveal a structure–property–performance relationship where pyridinic-N sites govern CO 2 -to-CO selectivity, whereas graphitic-N leads to nearly 100% HER.

title: Benzotriazole-based ionic COFs embedded in biologic aerogels for stable photocatalytic seawater splitting
abstract: A benzotriazole-based ionic COF, synthesized via post-modification, was embedded into a nanocellulose-supported aerogel, which shows efficient photocatalytic seawater splitting performance with HER of 87 mmol m −2 h −1 and AQY of 11.6% at 420 nm.

title: Efficient construction of hard carbon through hydrothermal carbon coating engineering toward enhanced sodium–ion storage performance
abstract: Using glucose as the coating agent, porous carbon (PC) is coated via direct pyrolysis and hydrothermal-assisted strategies. The polyfuran polymer formed by hydrothermal reaction of glucose enables the conversion of PC into hard carbon effectively.

title: Unravelling the Impact of Buried Interface Voids on Hysteresis in Perovskite Solar Cells via Opto-Electro-Ionic Simulation
abstract: Understanding the microscopic origins of performance hysteresis in perovskite solar cells is essential for achieving high performance and long-term stability. Herein, we develop an opto-electro-ionic model to systematically investigate the...

title: Tailored charge distribution modulated by cyclic carbonates enabling the development of high-performance PEO-based solid electrolytes
abstract: Constructing an efficient SEI/CEI layer through cyclic carbonate regulation strategies, the steric hindrance of VEC side chains controls uniform charge distribution to achieve high-performance PEO-based solid-state lithium metal batteries.

title: Magnetic field-induced surface reconstruction of Fe-CoP nanostructures for enhanced oxygen evolution catalytic performance
abstract: High-activity Fe-CoP nanoclusters were made via magnetic electrodeposition. Static magnetic field regulation of the OER was studied. In 1.0 M KOH, FeCoP-600 had a 315 mV overpotential whilst a field of 800 mT reduced it to 300 mV.

title: Nanostructural design of flexible mesoporous hydrogel electrodes via colloidal electrochemical deposition for highly efficient oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: Electrochemical deposition of colloidal hybrid cobalt hydroxide nanosheets enables nanostructural design of flexible mesoporous hydrogel electrodes with both a large surface area and efficient mass transport properties.

title: High-stability aqueous Ca-ion batteries featuring hydrated eutectic electrolytes and small-molecule conjugated anodes
abstract: A hydrated eutectic electrolyte based on Ca(ClO 4 ) 2 ·4H 2 O and sulfolane enables highly reversible Ca 2+ storage in aqueous Ca-ion batteries, delivering excellent cycling durability and high coulombic efficiency.

title: Shape memory superamphiphobic sponge: a tunable platform for oil aerosol filtration
abstract: A structurally adaptive 3D superamphiphobic sponge filter using shape memory polymers, achieving highly efficient (95%) and low-resistance (0.054 kPa) oil aerosol capture with tunable architectures.

title: Stabilisation of lanthanum nickelate electrocatalysts via Pt doping for high-current-density rechargeable zinc–air batteries
abstract: Pt- and Pd-doped LaNiO 3 are synthesised by the conventional sol–gel combustion method and tested in zinc–air batteries (ZABs) at a high current density of 60 mA cm −2 .

title: Zero-dimensional lead-free halide perovskites: from structural design to optoelectronic applications
abstract: This review summarizes recent progress in zero-dimensional lead-free double perovskites, highlighting their crystal structure, optical properties, and potential applications.

title: Carbon nitride shielded Co–W–S interfaces for ultra-stable hydrogen evolution in harsh acidic media
abstract: A CN-coated Co–W–S heterostructure (CN@2CoS/WS 2 /CP) delivers acid-proof, precious-metal-free HER, with the CN shell protecting and stabilizing active sites. It achieves low overpotentials and long-term 500 mA cm –2 operation with zero metal leaching.

title: Ex situ and operando investigations on (FeCoCrMnZn) 3 O 4 high entropy oxide as an anode for Li-ion batteries
abstract: (FeCoCrMnZn) 3 O 4 high entropy oxide samples were prepared through a solid-state synthesis route involving two major steps: ball milling and annealing.

title: Synchronously introducing and encasing non-stoichiometric defects in TiO 2 nanowires by NEG coating and interface reconstruction for sustainable photoelectrochemical water oxidation
abstract: Ti NEG coating enables surface passivation and interface reconstruction of TiO 2 nanowire array photoanodes toward sustainable solar-driven PEC hydrogen production.

title: Sparse Au nanoparticle arrays modulate Zn nucleation pathways and ion transport: a mechanistic approach to dendrite-free aqueous battery cycling
abstract: Sparse Au nanoparticle arrays define a new interface paradigm, where engineered sparsity simultaneously controls nucleation, electric fields, ion transport, and side reactions, enabling dendrite-free, ultra-long-life Zn cycling with minimal Au use.

title: A direct Z -scheme heterojunction of CsPbBr 3 /SnS 2 nanosheets for visible light-driven photocatalytic CO 2 reduction
abstract: CsPbBr 3 /SnS 2 Z-scheme heterojunctions were subtly constructed for visible-light-driven CO 2 -to-CO conversion. The atomic-scale proximity in the 2D/2D architecture ensures efficient interfacial charge transfer.

title: Vacancy-induced asymmetric coordination of single-atom bismuth for selective photoelectrochemical oxidation of glycerol to dihydroxyacetone
abstract: A WO 3 photoanode with Bi single atoms and O-vacancy-induced N–Bi–O asymmetric coordination is constructed. It shows 1.21 mA cm −2 at 1.2 V vs. RHE, 118.2 mmol m −2 h −1 glycerol conversion, and 71.3% DHA selectivity.

title: Dual-precursor coating strategy for one-step upgrade of LiFePO 4 to LiMn 0.6 Fe 0.4 PO 4 cathodes with atomic-level Mn/Fe uniformity and enhanced electrochemical performance
abstract: Lithium manganese iron phosphate (LMFP) has emerged as an ideal alternative to lithium iron phosphate (LFP) due to its higher operating voltage and resultant energy density.

title: Understanding the reversible charging/discharging mechanism of Li 3 BO 3 for thermochemical energy storage: an experimental and computational study
abstract: As the first attempt made on Li 3 BO 3 + CO 2 system for the TCES application, this work demonstrated the promise of Li 3 BO 3 , in terms of CO 2 -carrying capacity, thermochemical heat storage density, and long-term heat charging/discharging stability.

title: Ultrasonic levitation construction of palladium doped near-infrared carbon dots as nanoprobes for carbon monoxide sensing and imaging
abstract: NIR CDs were constructed through an ultrasonic levitation method, which resulted in redshifts of the emission over 150 nm and enhanced Pd 2+ content with improved sensitivity. The CDs can serve as promising probes for the sensing and imaging of CO.

title: Targeting interstitial atoms in n-type Mg 3 (Bi, Sb) 2 single crystals for robust ambient energy conversion
abstract: Strategic interstitial Mn doping in Mg 3 (Bi, Sb) 2 single crystal simultaneously achieves high thermoelectric performance and intrinsic chemical stability, enabling robust module operation for over 300 hours in ambient air.

title: How improper use of activated carbon leads to biased data interpretation in post-Li battery systems
abstract: Improper use of activated carbon counter electrodes can severely bias electrochemical data in post-lithium batteries. This work identifies key pitfalls and provides practical guidelines for their reliable use.

title: From lithium to proton mobility in garnet electrolytes: an NMR and conductivity study of H 5.2 Li 1.3 La 3 Zr 1.5 Ta 0.5 O 12
abstract: Protonation of polycrystalline garnet H 5.2 Li 1.3 La 3 Zr 1.5 Ta 0.5 O 12 alters local Li environments and dominates ionic transport, while Li + mobility remains largely frozen, highlighting how Li + /H + exchange affects conductivity in garnet-type electrolytes.

title: Efficient bubble removal in superhydrophilic gas diffusion electrodes via poly(vinyl alcohol) coating for high-current anion-exchange membrane water electrolysis
abstract: Bubble-induced performance degradation caused by gas bubble accumulation, particularly at high current density, significantly hinders the performance of anion-exchange membrane water electrolysis (AEMWE).

title: Effects of inorganic salt types and concentrations on the catalytic performance of molybdenum disulfide in naphthalene hydrogenation
abstract: Inorganic salts influence the catalysis of naphthalene hydrogenation by regulating crystal phase evolution, interlayer structure and active sites.

title: Densely packed high-entropy spinel nanoparticles derived from concentration-driven MOF gels for lithium-ion hybrid capacitors
abstract: Lowering the MOF gel precursor concentration slows gelation kinetics, forming uniform fine-grained networks. Calcination converts these into densely packed porous spinel HEOs with optimized performance in lithium-ion hybrid capacitors.

title: π-plane size-dependent photocatalytic activity of radical-doped diimide covalent organic frameworks
abstract: Three diimide-based COFs, PDI-COF, NDI-COF, and PBI-COF, with successively larger π-plane sizes, were constructed, which exhibit π-plane size-dependent photocatalytic activity for C–C and C–P coupling of tetrahydroisoquinoline.

title: Integrating polysulfide adsorption and electrocatalysis on a mineral-based separator for high-rate and long-life Li–S batteries
abstract: The ESrp@CoP composite separator, integrating CoP nanoparticles on etched serpentine, enables a synergistic “adsorption-catalysis” mechanism for Li–S batteries. This design achieves remarkable long-term cycling stability, far outperforming controls.

title: Precursor-based synthesis and electrochemical nitrogen reduction reaction (eNRR) activity of compositionally complex early transition metal carbides and (carbo)nitrides
abstract: Compositionally complex carbides and carbonitrides, incorporating multiple transition metals into a single-phase lattice, have emerged as promising candidates for advanced applications, including ultra-high-temperature ceramics, hard coatings, and heterogeneous catalysis. However,...

title: A dual-template MOF/ZIF approach for structurally optimized metal oxide@CNT and porous carbon electrodes in supercapacitors
abstract: Dual-template-assisted MOF/ZIF-derived materials are engineered to construct high-performance asymmetric supercapacitors. This pairing in device yields superior electrochemical peformance highlighting its promise for next-generation energy storage.

title: Arch-shaped triboelectric nanogenerator with a Ni/Cu bimetallic MOF-engineered intermediate layer for self-powered electrochemical clean hydrogen production and environmental remediation
abstract: An arch-shaped triboelectric nanogenerator with a Ni/Cu bimetallic MOF-engineered intermediate layer, coupled with an electrochemical process, facilitates self-powered clean hydrogen production and simultaneous environmental remediation.

title: Positionally heterogeneous non-fully conjugated dimeric acceptors with unilateral conjugated π-bridges enable highly efficient and stable organic solar cells
abstract: In this study, two non-fully conjugated dimeric acceptors, DYTC6T-V-out and DYTC6T-V-in, were designed and synthesized via a unilateral conjugated π-bridge strategy, introducing positional isomerism at the π-bridge site.

title: Analysing a Pd metallosurfactant derived single atom catalyst for biofouling mitigation and the oxygen reduction reaction in microbial fuel cells
abstract: Pd NC @Pd-SAC having Pd single atoms/nanoclusters on N-doped mesoporous carbon made via metallosurfactant-assisted pyrolysis show 4e − ORR pathway, reduce biofouling, and boost SCMFC power by 1700% due to synergistic electronic/spillover effects.

title: Electrocatalyst-induced kinetic modulation of anion-based redox mediators in aqueous iron–chromium redox flow batteries
abstract: Dual catalytic and anticatalytic effects enable selective kinetic control in iron–chromium redox flow batteries. Bismuth nanoparticles accelerate [Cr(CN) 6 ] 3−/4− redox kinetics, suppress hydrogen evolution, and deliver high efficiency and stability.

title: Thermally activated chain reconfiguration enabled by selenium incorporation for efficient carrier transport in antimony sulfide thin-film solar cells
abstract: Energetic Se introduced by magnetron sputtering and thermal reconfiguration reconstructs the Sb 2 S 3 lattice, passivates defects, suppresses self-trapping and nonradiative recombination, enabling stable device with 7.82% efficiency.

title: Asymmetric microdome structured flexible and skin-mountable pressure sensors monitored with computational analysis
abstract: A novel solution employing an asymmetric microdome structure with varying radius sizes was prepared and supported by FEM analysis. Also, large-sized reduced graphene oxide was utilized to facilitate electron transfer within its large basal plane.

title: Crystal structure driven electrical properties and mobile ion-dynamics of the layered compounds: A 2 M 2 TeO 6 (A = Li/Na and M = Cu/Ni) family
abstract: We present a comprehensive investigation of the ionic conduction mechanisms and their correlation with the crystal structure by employing a combined approach of impedance spectroscopy and neutron scattering.

title: Cobalt–niobium co-doping of indium oxide: structural and adsorption/catalytic properties for CO 2 valorization
abstract: The co-doping of the indium oxide structure with cobalt and niobium is attempted using a microemulsion method to produce high-surface-area, mesoporous, functional materials.

title: Revisiting MnP 4 as a negative electrode for Li-ion batteries: mechanism and performance
abstract: From disordered MnP 4 (prepared by ball milling) neither well-crystallized Li 7 MnP 4 nor crystalline MnP4 in cycling is formed, which favours improved electrochemical performance.

title: Comparative study of oxygen source doping effects on the multidimensional stability of Li 5.5 PS 4.5 Cl 1.5 solid electrolytes
abstract: P 2 O 5 doping in O-doped Li 5.5 PS 4.5 Cl 1.5 argyrodite stabilizes oxygen at 4a/4d sites, improving uniformity, bandgap and thermal stability. Li 2 O disrupts 16e sites, causing element dispersion and bond weakening.

title: High capacity metal-rich copper sulfide as an intercalation-type cathode material for all solid-state batteries
abstract: We report an intercalation-type Cu 1.8 S cathode material with porous spherical microparticles for all-solid-state batteries (ASSBs), which achevies superior cycling stability and high energy density by suppressing interfacial side reactions.

title: Entropy-stabilized perovskite oxide Ba 0.1 Ca 0.1 La 0.05 Sr 0.75 Ti 0.85 Nb 0.15 O 3 with improved thermoelectric performance for high-temperature waste heat recovery
abstract: An entropy-stabilized perovskite oxide of composition Ba 0.1 Ca 0.1 La 0.05 Sr 0.75 Ti 0.85 Nb 0.15 O 3 exhibiting enhanced power factor, generating power output of 95.5 mW from an all n-type four-leg module.

title: Enhancing the electrical properties and surface uniformity of a copper-coated carbon nanotube fiber by optimizing copper electrodeposition
abstract: Two-step copper electrodeposition mitigates porosity and surface non-uniformity in direct-spun CNT fibers, boosting electrical performance for lightweight conductor applications.

title: Enhanced thermal reliability of p-type PbTe thermoelectric devices through interfacial design with an FeCoNiCr multicomponent diffusion barrier
abstract: FeCoNiCr is introduced as a robust diffusion barrier for p-type PbTe. An in situ formed Cr 3 Te 4 interlayer ensures suppressed interdiffusion, ultralow contact resistivity, and a high device efficiency with outstanding thermal stability.

title: Polar heterocyclic molecule-assisted co-assembly of monolayers at the NiO x /perovskite interface for high-efficiency and stable inverted perovskite solar cells
abstract: Multifunctional BBOA in a Co-SAM layer simultaneously addresses interface coverage, defect passivation, and energy level alignment, leading to a high-performance PSC with a champion PCE of 25.08%.

title: Synergistic defect engineering and interfacial regulation in amphiphilic cerium-based metal–organic frameworks for efficient heavy oil upgrading
abstract: Defect-engineered amphiphilic Ce/MOF-SDG enables mild, in situ heavy-oil upgrading by oxygen-vacancy activation and oil–water interfacial regulation.

title: Nickel ion and single-atom solution addition and electrolyzer retrofitting to enhance CO 2 reduction to methane
abstract: By introducing functional additives into the electrolyte and optimising the structure of the electrolyser, it is possible to precisely direct the CO 2 reduction pathway, thereby promoting the efficient production of methane.

title: Hierarchical aerogel-confined deep eutectic electrolytes with complete water immobilization for high-performance, freeze-tolerant supercapacitors
abstract: Deep eutectic solvents (DESs) have emerged as sustainable electrolyte candidates for advanced electrochemical energy storage owing to their wide electrochemical stability windows, low volatility, and environmental compatibility.

title: Nanoengineering 2D ceria-perovskite monolayers on SrTiO 3 nanocubes: structure-redox property relationship
abstract: Epitaxial growth of a 2D ceria-perovskite nano-overlayer on SrTiO 3 nanocubes enables 100% cerium redox utilization at low temperatures, providing a robust platform for the design of high-efficiency, lanthanide-lean catalysts.

title: Proton-conducting hydrogen-bonded nanochannels encapsulating perylene-based photocatalysts for solar hydrogen evolution
abstract: Hydrogen-bonded proton channels in HOFs enable rapid proton transport and improve photocatalytic H 2 generation.

title: Fundamental understanding of carbon evolution in polycarbosilane derived SiC ceramics: a ReaxFF molecular dynamics study
abstract: Large-scale reactive molecular dynamics simulations were performed for the evolution of carbon phase in pyrolysis of polycarbosilane, providing guideline for design of polymer-derived SiC ceramics.

title: Nanoarchitectured Pt–Pd foams as novel hydrogen reservoirs through Pt–H bonding
abstract: Hydrogen is a promising candidate to be the main renewable energy source in the future, but there has been limited development of hydrogen storage methods. Pt–Pd nanofoams are synthesized with a Pt-rich core and a Pd-rich for this application.

title: Synergistic valorization of spent lithium-ion battery electrodes: anode graphite derived lamellar graphene oxide membranes for selective lithium recovery from cathode leachate
abstract: This novel strategy of fabricating a GO-PEI membrane from spent graphite offers a green process for recovering lithium from the leachate of spent lithium-ion battery cathode materials.

title: Unraveling supercapacitive cobalt manganese sulfide nanoflakes: a standalone binder-free electrode material for a solid-state prototype device
abstract: A 2D bimetallic chalcogenide—cobalt manganese sulfide (CoMn 2 S 4 ) nanoflakes—designed using density functional theory (DFT) calculations, works as a binder free standalone electrode material for asymmetric supercapacitor devices.

title: Passive multistage solar desalination enables durable high-efficiency freshwater production
abstract: We present a bioinspired surface enabling durable reverse multistage evaporation by enhancing heat transfer and suppressing salt crystallization, achieving 410% solar-to-steam efficiency and 6.25 kg m −2 h −1 evaporation rate under one sun illumination.

title: Interstitial anionic electrons as spin–charge reservoirs for sterically tuned single-atom catalysis in 2D electrides
abstract: The steric effects of single-atom dopant dictate spin and charge redistribution of IAEs in vdW electrides, enabling controllable catalytic activities.

title: Architecture-Driven Porous Copper Nanofiber Networks for High-Rate CO₂ Electroreduction to Ethylene
abstract: The electrochemical reduction of CO₂ to multi-carbon products offers a promising route for carbon-neutral energy conversion, yet achieving high selectivity toward ethylene (C₂H₄) remains challenging. While CuO-derived catalysts are widely...

title: High voltage hybrid Zn-MnO 2 /air batteries via decoupled electrolyte
abstract: A decoupled electrolyte hybrid Zn-MnO 2 /air battery enables a dual high-voltage plateau via MnO 2 dissolution and ORR. This design ensures the device achieves both high voltage and high capacity simultaneously.

title: Enhancing NO electroreduction on N/O co-coordinated single-atom catalysts via d-band center modulation
abstract: DFT screening identifies four Cu-based N/O co-coordinated SACs with excellent NORR activity. Oxygen coordination upshifts the Cu d-band center to strengthen NO adsorption, while ML interprets the key factors governing NO adsorption energies.

title: Engineered titanium dioxide-based photocatalysts for NO x abatement: navigating design strategies and structure activity relationships for practical air purification
abstract: The integration of structural design and mechanism-driven optimization in engineered TiO 2 photocatalysts enhances NO x abatement for air purification.

title: A review on strategies toward high-mass-loading aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: This review summarizes recent progress in developing high-mass-loading cathodes for aqueous zinc-ion batteries and highlights key design principles and emerging strategies toward practical, high-energy-density systems.

title: Engineered cellulose-supported photocatalysts for clean energy and environmental remediation: progress and prospects
abstract: The growing global demand for sustainable energy and environmental remediation has accelerated interest in efficient cellulose-supported photocatalysts.

title: Recent advances in MXene-based self-powered electrochemical sensors
abstract: This review evaluates MXene structural integration and performance mechanisms in self-powered electrochemical sensors (SPECSs), focusing on active component function, maximum power output, long-term stability, and analytical sensitivity.

title: Controlled synthesis and interfacial properties of polyvinylidene fluoride based metal-fluoride surface treatments for high-nickel NCM cathodes
abstract: A surface-localized MF x -rich layer generated by PVDF-derived fluorination stabilizes high-Ni NCM cathodes by suppressing surface reconstruction, preserving Li + transport kinetics, and improving long-term cycling performance.

title: Molecularly modulated π-conjugated carbon nitride heterojunctions for efficient photocatalytic C–C bond cleavage in lignin models
abstract: A molecularly modulated carbon nitride-based heterojunction catalyst (TFPT/g-C 3 N 4 ) was synthesized, which enables the photocatalytic cleavage of C–C bonds in lignin models.

title: A stability-directed dual-filter strategy for MOF electrolytes to achieve durable high-power PEM water electrolysis under dynamic operation
abstract: A dual-filter strategy for MOF electrolytes: (1) function filter (hierarchical porosity + proton sites) and (2) stability filter (hard Lewis acidic metals like Zr 4+ ). IM-UiO-66-AS achieves 10.71 A cm −2 and 3× lower decay vs. baseline.

title: Hybrid carbonate and redox systems for thermochemical energy storage
abstract: Hybrid CaCO 3 /CuO composites for TCES integrate carbonate and redox cycles, enhancing multicycle stability, energy density, thermal conductivity, and solar absorptance.

title: Giant electric-field-induced strain associated with defect dipoles in Fe-doped barium titanate single crystals activated by an electric field
abstract: A rapid AC-field-induced aging process (<1 h) in Fe-BTO single crystals achieves a giant strain of ∼1% via defect dipole alignment. This defect dipole-mediated 90° domain switching offers high-performance potential for low-frequency actuators.

title: Thermopolymerized flame-retardant polymer interphase toward highly stable gel electrolytes for thermal safety of lithium metal batteries
abstract: An in situ thermally polymerized PVA/PAM-TFEP protective layer enables uniform Li + flux, dendrite-free Li deposition, and high interfacial stability. The TFEP-containing flexible polymer network further enhances flame retardancy and battery safety.

title: Significantly improved breakdown strength and energy density in high-temperature polymer dielectric enabled by bilayered nanocoatings
abstract: Bilayer nanocoated polymer dielectrics were constructed via the synergistic regulation of charge blocking and charge dissipation, combined with plasma treatment, achieving enhanced high-temperature energy storage performance.

title: Surface engineered sulvanite integrated with layered double hydroxide for asymmetric hybrid supercapacitor
abstract: Surface engineered sulvanites are integrated with Ce( iii )––Mg( ii )––Y( iii ) LDH for supercapacitor applications. The optimized material was used to SLASH device to assess the practical application for next generation energy storage devices.

title: Counterion-assisted adsorption strategy for a high-thermopower thermo-electrochemical cell
abstract: For thermo-electrochemical cells utilizing hydrophobic interactions to build concentration gradients, guanidinium counterions strengthen I 3 − adsorption in PNIPAM domains, thereby amplifying gradients and substantially enhancing thermopower.

title: Naphthalenediimide-based donor–acceptor COFs with low exciton binding energy toward sunlight-driven synthesis of multisubstituted olefins and sulfoxides
abstract: Two imine-linked two-dimensional donor–acceptor COFs with different linker lengths have been successfully synthesized, which exhibit low exciton binding energy and sunlight-driven photocatalytic synthesis of multisubstituted olefins and sulfoxides.

title: Rational design of single-atom catalysts stabilized within the interlayer of covalent organic frameworks for the oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: DFT simulations and machine learning algorithms are applied to screen single-atom catalysts intercalated within COFs for the electrocatalytic oxygen evolution reaction, revealing the key descriptors of stability and catalytic activity.

title: Ultrawide bandgap CaHfO 3 as a linear dielectric filler for superior energy storage in 0.7Bi 0.5 Na 0.5 TiO 3 -0.3Sr 0.7 Nd 0.2 TiO 3 ceramics
abstract: High-power pulsed electronics and compact capacitive storage demand lead-free dielectrics that simultaneously offer high breakdown strength (BDS), energy storage density ( W rec ), energy efficiency ( η ) and stability.

title: Deciphering the Role of Sulfonated Side-Chain Length in Modulating Proton Transport in TpBd-COFs
abstract: Covalent organic frameworks (COFs) functionalized with sulfonated side chains have emerged as promising materials for proton exchange membranes due to their ordered nanoporous structures and tunable ionic functionalities. Here, we...

title: Ultra-trace polyvinyl alcohol induced interfacial solvation regulation for stable zinc metal anodes
abstract: An ultra-trace (ppb) polyvinyl alcohol additive regulates interfacial Zn 2+ solvation, simultaneously suppressing dendrites, corrosion and hydrogen evolution, and extending the cycle stability of aqueous zinc-ion batteries.

title: Unveiling the effect of crystallinity on rapid sodium storage of digallium trisulfide/carbon nanowires
abstract: Low-crystallinity Ga 2 S 3 /C nanowires possess more active sites and lower Na + diffusion barrier, thus delivering better sodium storage performance in specific capacity and rate capability than its high-crystallinity counterpart.

title: Solvent-mediated surface modification of electron transport layers for efficient PbS quantum dot solar cells
abstract: Solvent mediated surface modification of the ZnMgO ETL reduces hydroxyl related defects, improves film wettability and uniformity, enhances charge extraction, and boosts the efficiency of PbS quantum dot solar cells.

title: Monolayer LDH nanosheet / quaternized polymer composite membranes with enhanced anion conductivity and alkaline durability for water electrolysis
abstract: The incorporation of 2D LDH nanosheets into partially fluorinated quaternized polymers (QPAF-4) facilitated fast hydroxide transport via hydrogen-bonding networks and enhanced alkaline durability by mitigating quaternary ammonium degradation.

title: Dopant-controlled transition-metal ordering in high-voltage spinel cathodes
abstract: Neutron PDF analysis reveals that Fe doping in high-voltage spinel LiMn 1.5 Ni 0.5 O 4 drives site-selective cation redistribution across length scales, directly tuning redox behaviour and battery performance.

title: Synergistic role of self-induced CdIn 2 S 4 nanopyramid sacrificial layer for the enhanced photocatalytic activity of TiO 2 nanotube-based chalcogenide heterostructures
abstract: (1) Synthesis steps for electrode fabrication, (2) electron transfer kinetics during the photo-catalytic reaction.

title: Optimizing high-temperature energy storage capability by regulating interchain spacing with a tailored pendant group in self-crosslinkable polyetherimides
abstract: Tailored crosslinking restricts β-relaxation by introducing deep traps. This strategy of breaking the conjugated structure of PEIs provides fresh insights for investigations on high-temperature film capacitors.

title: Accelerated synthesis of titanium-Beta zeolites using N -methyl-2-pyrrolidone and Beta seeds
abstract: Synthesis of Ti-Beta can be accelerated from 8 days down to 20 h with combined use of Beta seeds and N -methyl-2-pyrrolidone.

title: Atomic/molecular layer deposition of p-type conducting copper–sulfur–organic coordination polymer thin films for thermoelectric applications
abstract: A three-precursor ALD/MLD process based on Cu-for-Li trans-metalation reaction allows the fabrication of p-type thermoelectric poly[Cu-ETT] coordination polymer thin films with appreciably high Seebeck coefficient.

title: Nanocomposite ruthenium oxide electrocatalysts for the low-pH oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: Ruthenium oxides are effective anode catalysts for proton-exchange water electrolysers, but are unstable in operation. This study investigates a method to stabilise ruthenium oxides through combination with various stabilising metal oxide matrices.

title: Ferroelectricity-tunable MXene-family photocatalysts for overall water splitting
abstract: Tunable photocatalytic water splitting on MXene by a ferroelectric switch.

title: Regenerable oxygen-deficient Ni/γ-Al 2 O 3 catalyst for efficient glycerol aqueous phase reforming
abstract: Spent Ni/γ-Al 2 O 3 regeneration increased oxygen vacancies and crystallinity of γ-Al 2 O 3 , enhancing catalytic performance in glycerol aqueous phase reforming.

title: Magnetic-field-induced vertical alignment of carbon nanotubes in Na 3 V 2 (PO 4 ) 2 F 3 with bulk dual-doping for high-rate sodium-ion batteries
abstract: We proposed a stepwise engineering strategy. Construct vertically aligned CNTs combined with dual-element bulk doping, exhibiting excellent electrochemical performance.

title: Concomitant polymorphism in oxime-bridged pyrazole–tetrazole energetic materials
abstract: An oxime-bridged nitropyrazole–tetrazole gives two concomitant polymorphs (3-α, and 3-β) whose oxime-directed conformational differences dictate packing, thermal stability, and RDX-like detonation performance.

title: Electrolyte-as-binder strategy using in situ -formed PDOL gel for binder-free cathodes in flexible quasi-solid-state Li–S batteries
abstract: A binder-free cathode integrated with a PDOL-based quasi-solid-state (QSS) electrolyte was fabricated via in situ polymerization. The binder-free QSS Li–S batteries show improved cyclability, flexibility, and safety.

title: Negative-to-positive electrode soluble species crossover induced accelerated degradation in lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Thermally soluble solid electrolyte interphase on negative electrodes triggers alkyl fluorophosphate-driven negative-to-positive electrode crosstalk, accelerating positive electrode interface and cracking-based degradation in lithium-ion batteries.

title: Unveiling the irreversible structural evolution upon rehydration of manganese-based Prussian white: an in situ X-ray diffraction study
abstract: Manganese-based Prussian White (PW) is a promising cathode material for sodium-ion batteries, due to the variety of its composition, its intercalation properties, and its good electrochemical performance.

title: Shedding light on cerium( iii / iv ), the unsung hero, and mitigating its chemical inertness with Ni
abstract: Enhancement of the electrochemical energy storage profile of chemically inert cerium( iv ) by introducing a transition metal (Ni) via 3d–2p–4f orbital coupling engineering and amplifying oxygen vacancies.

title: Seed-mediated construction of bimetallic FeCo clusters on N-doped mesoporous carbon for bifunctional electrocatalysis in rechargeable Zn–air batteries
abstract: A Co-single-atom seeded growth strategy, employing ammonia-mediated pyrolysis, is developed for the precise growth of FeCo clusters on nitrogen-doped mesoporous carbon (FeCo/SMC) as bifunctional electrocatalysts in rechargeable Zn–air batteries.

title: Sustainable approaches to polyvinyl alcohol: synthesis, degradation, recycling and upcycling
abstract: An overarching review of the current state of research and technology related to sustainable production, use, and post-treatment of polyvinyl alcohol, evaluating key advancements and identifying areas and opportunities for further development.

title: Stabilizing electrode–electrolyte interphases using soluble metal–organic polyhedra for high-performance lithium metal batteries
abstract: A new class of multifunctional electrolyte additives based on soluble metal–organic polyhedra (MOP) enables stable CEI/SEI formation with film-forming and anion-regulating properties, boosting interfacial stability in high-voltage Li-metal batteries.

title: Lithium dendrite holey strategy for enabling a high-rate and wide-temperature all-solid-state Ti 3 C 2 T x fiber supercapacitor
abstract: Lithium dendrite holey strategy for enabling a high-rate all-solid-state Ti 3 C 2 T x fiber supercapacitor.

title: Consecutive intra-particle phase transitions in the LiFePO 4 battery electrode material
abstract: When the asymmetry of the thermodynamic free energy of LiFePO 4 is accounted for, delithiation occurs through a solid solution pathway, thereby challenging the prevailing notion that this phase is only kinetically stabilized at the nano-scale.

title: An efficient and durable electrocatalyst for electrochemical water splitting: WCuBP micro-leaf-clusters
abstract: The WCuBP micro-leaf cluster (MLC) exhibited high OER activity compared to the benchmark electrode RuO 2 . Moreover, the bifunctional and hybrid configurations demonstrated lower cell voltages of 2.27 and 2.03 V at 1000 mA cm −2 in 1 M KOH electrolyte.

title: Defect-engineered VO x cathode and novel polyvinyl alcohol@polyaniline hydrogel separator for ultra-stable fiber Zn-ion batteries
abstract: The flexible fiber zinc-ion battery using the VO@450 integrating nitrogen atoms and oxygen vacancies as cathode and the novel PVA@PANI hydrogel as separator demonstrates superior specific capacity, high flexibility and ultra-long cycling stability.

title: A novel bipolar phthalocyanine-based composite cathode for advanced Zn-organic batteries
abstract: A specially designed CuPc-DPTZ-CMPs/CNT composite cathode, with its multi-active-site architecture and bipolar framework, demonstrates high discharge voltage in zinc-organic batteries.

title: Molecular engineering of multi-polar groups enables long-term stable Zn anodes at high current density
abstract: The multi-polar d -pantothenic acid additive coordinates solvation structure, interface, and H-bond network, inhibiting Zn dendrites and concentration polarization, enabling stable Zn anode cycling at high current density.

title: Standalone photo-rechargeable capacitor integrating DSSCs with a Bi//NiCo 2 S 4 asymmetric supercapacitor for high-efficiency indoor and outdoor energy storage
abstract: Monolithic photo-capacitor integrating two series DSSCs with a Bi//NiCo 2 S 4 supercapacitor delivers bias-free photocharging, >1.2 V photovoltage, 170.4 mF cm −2 capacitance, and >7% low-light storage efficiency for indoor/outdoor IoT.

title: Boosting BiVO 4 photoanode performance via organic–inorganic cocatalyst synergy for efficient water splitting
abstract: We fabricated BiVO 4 /TDA/NiFe 2 O 4 photoanodes by immersion and spin-coating, achieving a photocurrent density of 6.5 mA cm −2 at 1.23 V vs. RHE with 50 hours stability under AM 1.5G illumination.

title: Synergistically engineered NiCoMn-LDH@carbon nanofibers for boosted electrochemical charge storage and high-energy supercapacitors
abstract: Synergistic material design and electrochemical performance of NiCoMn-LDH@CNF.

title: Cu/Fe 3 O 4 embedded polyethersulfone beads: a scalable and magnetically separable catalyst for hydrogen evolution from ammonia borane and selective hydrogenation of cinnamaldehyde
abstract: In this work, we developed magnetically separable Cu@Fe 3 O 4 :PES based catalyst beads that enable both efficient hydrogen generation from ammonia borane (AB) and tandem reduction of organic compounds under mild and environmentally benign conditions.

title: Sub-10 nm metal-dispersed crystalline C 3 N 5 for photocatalytic CO 2 reduction to produce formic acid and acetic acid
abstract: This work presents the synthesis of Ag-dispersed on crystalline C 3 N 5 (CCN) through photodeposition to achieve a surface plasmon resonance effect between Ag particle and CCN for photocatalytic CO 2 reduction.

title: High-performance polydiacetylene organic cathode for zinc-ion batteries
abstract: Organic cathodes are considered promising alternatives to metal-based electrodes in energy storage devices due to their versatile structural and electrochemical properties and benign environmental impact.

title: Hierarchical RuO 2 /Co 3 O 4 heterostructures enable efficient bromine-mediated electrochemical ethylene epoxidation via electronic and structural synergy
abstract: Hierarchical RuO 2 /Co 3 O 4 heterostructures enable efficient bromine-mediated electrochemical ethylene epoxidation via interfacial electronic modulation, which inhibits the anodic reconstruction of cobalt oxides and ensures long-term stability.

title: Fluorescent Carbon Quantum Dot–Based Nano-Surfactants for Multifunctional Interfacial Modulation and Improved Oil Recovery in Low-Permeability Reservoirs
abstract: Carbon quantum dot-based cationic surfactants integrate nanomaterial transport advantages with interfacial activity, enabling simultaneous interfacial tension reduction, wettability alteration, and deep reservoir penetration, thus offering a fundamentally new strategy for...

title: Emerging strategies for designing MoSe 2 -based electrocatalysts for renewable hydrogen technologies
abstract: This review article highlights the engineering of MoSe 2 -based catalysts for the hydrogen evolution reaction.

title: Reimagining interface processing in solid-state batteries via electrochemical flash sintering
abstract: Electrochemical control of flash sintering in solid-state battery materials: structural evolution and hotspot formation revealed by total scattering.

title: Regulating molecular packing and crystallization kinetics via trifluoromethyl-functionalized solid additives for efficient organic solar cells
abstract: A trifluoromethyl-functionalized volatile solid additive was developed to optimize the crystallization and vertical phase distribution of blend films, thereby boosting device performance in organic solar cells across multiple systems.

title: Disentangling the Role of Al, Co, and Mn Dopants in LiNiO2 Cathodes via Synchrotron-Based Probes
abstract: High-nickel layered oxide cathodes like LiNiO2 (LNO) offer high energy density but suffer from rapid structural degradation during cycling, limiting their practical application. This study systematically investigates the dopant-dependent structural...

title: Control of ion exchange capacity in block copolymer binders enables high hydroxide conductivity at low swelling and improves catalyst activity for AEM water electrolysis
abstract: Varying the hydrophilic/hydrophobic ratio in block copolymer binders for AEMWE allows to control ion exchange capacity, swelling and ionic conductivity, leading to superior single-cell performance with current densities of 3800 mA cm −2 at 2 V.

title: Enhancing energy-storage characteristics of BNT-based ceramic via quadruple perovskite (AA′ 3 B 4 O 12 ) modification
abstract: Lead-free dielectric ceramics are of interest for pulsed power devices, but achieving high recoverable energy at low/moderate electric fields is still challenging.

title: Electrochemical nitrate reduction to ammonia by non-noble metal single-atom catalysts: bridging waste remediation and sustainable hydrogen storage
abstract: This review summarizes the principles of electrochemical nitrate reduction and highlights the design, characterization, mechanisms, advances, challenges, and perspectives of non-noble metal single-atom catalysts for nitrate-to-ammonia conversion.

title: Highly selective electrochemical reduction of nitrite to ammonia over CuWO 4 nanoparticles
abstract: CuWO 4 nanoparticles serve as a highly efficient electrocatalyst for the selective reduction of NO 2 − to NH 3 . In neutral solution, CuWO 4 achieves an NH 3 production rate of 27.46 mg h −1 cm −2 and a faradaic efficiency of 95.09% at −1.0 V.

title: Solvation-engineered two-electron viologen and TEMPO for neutral aqueous organic redox flow batteries
abstract: Ether-linked quaternized side-chain engineering enables highly soluble viologen and TEMPO redox couples for neutral aqueous organic redox flow batteries with high voltage and long cycling stability.

title: A low-crystallinity polymer donor enables high-efficiency semitransparent organic solar cells under substantial donor dilution
abstract: Low-crystallinity polymer donor PL2 enables efficient semitransparent organic solar cells under substantial donor dilution by maintaining donor–acceptor interfacial area and balanced charge transport.

title: Simultaneous engineering of cation disorder and morphology of molecular-ink derived AgBiS 2 photocathodes for solar water splitting
abstract: This work demonstrates the first AgBiS 2 -based photocathode achieved using urea as a Lewis base additive to improve film morphology and simultaneously control cation disorder through dithiocarbamate-based coordination chemistry.

title: Coordination engineering of nickel sites in Ni-MOF for synergistic optimization of electronic structure and interfacial water toward efficient alkaline hydrogen evolution
abstract: By altering the local coordination environment of Ni sites, an interfacial microenvironment and electron-rich state favorable to water adsorption are constructed, tuning the morphology and electronic structure of Ni-MOF and promoting the Volmer step.

title: Synergistic electronic modulation of Ru sites via N-doped CeO 2 and Cs promotion for high efficiency H 2 generation from ammonia decomposition
abstract: N-doped CeO 2 enhances Ru dispersion and oxygen vacancies, optimizing charge transfer and reaction kinetics to facilitate efficient N 2 desorption, significantly boosting NH 3 decomposition catalytic performance.

title: A review of methanol-to-olefins conversion over SAPO-34: catalyst design, mechanisms, and kinetics
abstract: This review highlights recent advancements in the methanol-to-olefins (MTO) process over SAPO-34 as a sustainable route for light olefin production.

title: Carbon vs. carbon: rational host selection for Zn–I 2 cathodes
abstract: Aqueous Zn–I 2 batteries are limited by the formation and migration of soluble polyiodides, making carbon important not only as a conductive framework, but also as the host that defines the...

title: Solvent-free dual in situ interfacial engineering of polyester composites for enhanced capacitive energy storage
abstract: Molecular-level interfacial engineering has emerged as a key strategy to simultaneously enhance the dielectric constant and breakdown strength of polymer composites for capacitive energy storage.

title: Ultra-broadband and high-efficiency hierarchical metasurface solar absorber designed by a dual-population co-evolution genetic algorithm
abstract: The DCD-GA-based inverse-designed TiN/TiO 2 /Ti MIM metasurface demonstrates ultrabroadband absorption, high-temperature stability, and polarization/wide-angle insensitivity for solar energy conversion.

title: Mechanistic mapping of alkali-ion storage in micro-spherical closed-pore hard carbon: electrochemical, ex situ , and DFT approaches
abstract: Investigating porous closed-pore hard carbon (HC) anodes is crucial for advancing alkali-ion batteries.

title: High energy storage performance of random norbornene copolymers containing oligothiophene as a side-chain for dielectric capacitor applications
abstract: New dielectric polymers featuring oligothiophene grafted onto a bicyclic norbornene framework were reported. The high polarizability of sulfur atoms, increased β relaxation and the introduction of trap sites deliver exceptional performance at 150 °C.

title: Exploring the surface reconstruction of AgPdIr nanoalloys with a defined IrO 2 –AgPd heterostructure for exceptional formate oxidation
abstract: The reconstruction of oxide species on nanoalloy surfaces is ubiquitous and can profoundly influence the formate oxidation reaction (FOR) process, though the reconstruction often remains elusive yet critically important to unravel.

title: A nanoparticle-decorated bismuth- and nickel-doped Sr 2 Fe 1.5 Mo 0.5 O 6− δ cathode for enhanced CO 2 reduction in solid oxide electrolysis cells
abstract: This study presents a new bismuth- and nickel-modified SFM perovskite cathode (BiSFMNi) with exsolved Fe–Ni nanoparticles, achieving improved performance for high-temperature CO 2 electrolysis.

title: B-site engineered medium-entropy perovskite as a dual-purpose material enabling piezoelectric energy harvester and supercapacitor electrode applications
abstract: Non-equimolar B-site-engineered La(TiMnFeCo)O 3 medium-entropy perovskites outperform equimolar variants, enabling high performance for supercapacitor electrodes and piezoelectric energy harvesting (PENG).

title: Elucidating the fundamental governing principles of CO 2 reduction on single-atom catalysts through interpretable machine learning
abstract: A universal atomic descriptor is proposed to accurately predict CO 2 RR thermodynamics on BC 2 -supported single-atom catalysts.

title: Plasma lattice-matched interfacial engineering enables boosted photocatalytic O 2 activation for antibiotic degradation
abstract: Tuning oxygen-species activity in S-scheme heterojunctions is key for antibiotic degradation, yet random interfacial orientation causes lattice mismatch, impairing O 2 adsorption/dissociation and reducing catalytic performance.

title: Urazole-decorated multifunctional mesoporous polymer networks for efficient capture of uranium( vi ) from aqueous matrices
abstract: This work presents the first example of urazole-decorated multifunctional porous networks synthesized via TAD–indole reaction, which exhibits outstanding uranium uptake performance across a wide range of wastewater matrices, including seawater.

title: Thiol-assisted aerosol synthesis of mesoporous Cu–SiO 2 catalysts for effective and stable ethanol dehydrogenation
abstract: Mesoporous Cu–SiO 2 catalysts are prepared via aerosol-assisted sol–gel; upon addition of (3-mercaptopropyl)-trimethoxysilane (MPTMS), we observe enhanced Cu dispersion and time-on-stream stability during ethanol dehydrogenation to acetaldehyde.

title: Engineering intermolecular charge transfer in covalent organic frameworks for photocatalytic hydrogen peroxide generation
abstract: In this manuscript, we have demonstrated the engineering of intermolecular charge transfer in covalent organic frameworks (COFs) for photocatalytic hydrogen peroxide generation.

title: Increasing the energy storage of an ultrathin biaxially oriented polypropylene film via soft-photon-induced crosslinks implanted at industrial scale using roll-to-roll processing
abstract: A competitive energy density of 2.4 µm BOPP with an efficiency of >95% at 125 °C is demonstrated via the industrial scale implantation of soft-photon-induced crosslinks.

title: Enhancing interfacial solar-driven evaporation and hydrovoltaic electricity generation by porous carbon nanoparticles from waste polyester
abstract: We report the facile construction of porous carbon nanoparticle (PCN)-based dual-functional evaporators using waste PET as a precursor to realize high-performance freshwater and electricity co-generation.

title: Heteroatom-tuned σ–π orbital reorganization enables efficient oxygen reduction and evolution of electrocatalysis beyond noble-metal catalysts
abstract: Heteroatom tuning of σ–π orbital interactions at TM–N 4 sites modulates adsorption chemistry, breaks ORR/OER scaling relations, and enables efficient bifunctional electrocatalysis using earth-abundant metals.

title: Ion-pair and solvation engineering for high-potential organic catholytes in aqueous flow batteries
abstract: A high-potential aqueous organic catholyte based on 4-nitrophenethylamine hydrobromide delivers 1.24 V vs. SHE. Bromide-mediated solvent-separated ion pairs enhance solvation and transport, enabling reversible acidic flow batteries.

title: Harvesting waste heat with molecular precision: the role of metal organic coordination polymers for sustainable organic thermoelectrics
abstract: Coordination chemistry enables descriptor-driven control of electronic and thermal transport in metal–organic coordination polymers, closing the theory – experiment gap in organic thermoelectrics.

title: A micro-sized bimodal meso/microporous Si anode made from a modified Al–Si melt for use in lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Micro-sized porous Si with bimodal meso/micropores (µSPSi) is constructed via Al–Si melt modification. Owing to the synergistic stress-buffering effect of the hierarchical structure, the µSPSi@C anode delivers enhanced electrochemical performance.

title: Efficient piezo-photocatalysis via a g-C 3 N 4 /Ti 3 C 2 T x heterojunction: synergy of piezoelectric and built-in fields
abstract: The g-C 3 N 4 /Ti 3 C 2 T x heterojunction, synthesized via electrostatic self-assembly, exhibits superior piezo-photocatalytic activity under visible light and mechanical stirring through synergistic coupling of piezoelectric and built-in fields. Graphical abstract image: John White Photos/Getty Cr…

title: Correction: Ultra-stable solid-state lithium metal batteries with ferroelectric oxide-enhanced PVDF-based hybrid solid electrolytes
abstract: Correction for “Ultra-stable solid-state lithium metal batteries with ferroelectric oxide-enhanced PVDF-based hybrid solid electrolytes” by Jie Zhao et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2025, 13 , 9347–9356, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4TA08724K.

title: Role of catalyst transformations, defect reconstruction, and seawater splitting under a broad solar spectrum using a MOF-derived (Cu)TiO 2 model system
abstract: Defect-rich to defect-free: The story of CuO x /TiO₂ proves that phase and electronic (in)stability are key factors governing the catalytic stability of a photocatalyst.

title: ZIF@MOF core–shell functional structure synthesized via controlled ligand-exchange for enhanced CO 2 separation in mixed matrix-membranes
abstract: A ZIF-67@Co-MOF-74 core–shell structure was synthesized via controlled ligand exchange and used to prepare MMMs for CO 2 separation. The core–shell design enhanced filler–polymer compatibility and led to improved CO 2 adsorption-driven permeability

title: Dynamic thiourea networks enable self-healing chromic films for visual detection of light and acidic vapors
abstract: Smart chromic materials that respond to light and acid are promising for environmental sensing, wearables, and safety monitoring.

title: Nitrogen-doped WSe 2 few-layer nanotubes for electrocatalytic nitrate reduction via Se vacancies
abstract: N-doped WSe 2 few-layer nanotubes (NTs) were prepared and synchronously modulated by controlling the N content. Significant Se vacancies produced in N-WSe 2 NTs enhance the electrocatalytic activity of N-WSe 2 NTs for nitrate reduction reaction.

title: Size-Controlled Hydrothermal Synthesis of Crystalline High-entropy Spinel Oxide Nanoparticles for Oxygen Evolution Electrocatalysts
abstract: High-entropy oxides offer a promising platform for oxygen evolution reaction catalysis owing to their entropy-stabilized structures and flexible design of cation metal elements. However, conventional high-temperature synthesis leads to extensive...

title: Impact of surface vacancies on the dynamics of metal atoms on reducible oxides: an enhanced sampling study with machine-learned potentials for Pt 1 /TiO 2
abstract: This work uses molecular dynamics and metadynamics driven by machine learned potentials to probe the evolution of platinum atoms on defect-containing rutile titania. Free energy surface characteristics are distinct from potential energy surfaces.

title: Enhanced piezoelectric performance of lead-based ceramics via multi-B site ion design and local structural disorder
abstract: The high-entropy concept has emerged as a promising strategy for designing ferroelectric ceramics with superior piezoelectric properties.

title: Synergistic chiral mediation and Ga 3+ doping introduces lattice distortion and defect states to construct an intensified built-in electric field and abundant active sites for enhanced BiOBr photoelectrocatalytic performance
abstract: This paper employs chiral mediation coupled with Ga 3+ doping to introduce lattice distortion and defect states into BiOBr, thereby enhancing the built-in electric field and expanding active sites for improved photoelectrocatalytic performance.

title: Phase transition model of ferroelectric 2D hybrid perovskites
abstract: A Monte Carlo model captures MA/BA cation ordering in ferroelectric 2D hybrid perovskites reproducing observed phase transitions and long-range cation order.

title: Wide-temperature flexible polyurethane network reinforced PVDF-HFP electrolytes for solid-state lithium metal batteries operated under extreme conditions
abstract: A solid polymer electrolyte was prepared from cross-linked polyurethane and PVDF-HFP, featuring high ionic conductivity and superior interfacial stability.

title: S-scheme-mediated Ce-NSO/Ce-gCN heterostructure for enhanced photocatalytic hydrogen evolution via sea water splitting
abstract: The Ce-NSO/Ce-gCN S-scheme-based heterojunction developed in this study is capable of sustaining high HER in seawater under visible-light irradiation.

title: Protocol-aware standardization of relaxation-induced kinetic masking in incremental capacity fingerprints of lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Incremental capacity analysis (ICA) is widely used to probe lithium-ion battery degradation because it preserves material-related information in the voltage response. Its mechanistic value, however, depends on whether the measured...

title: Liquid crystal elastomers for solar, mechanical, thermal, and electrochemical energy applications
abstract: Liquid crystal elastomers provide a multifunctional platform for solar, mechanical, thermal, and electrochemical energy applications.

title: Interfacial and electronic modulation of FeP–CoP on reduced graphene aerogel boosts bifunctional catalysis for overall water splitting
abstract: Sustainable H 2 production demands efficient bifunctional water-splitting catalysts.

title: Why tail states matter? Impact of defect types on the electrochemical kinetics of CF x cathodes
abstract: Featuring carbon clusters as primary defects, fluorinated hard carbon surpasses fluorinated graphite in electrical conductivity, Li + kinetics, and electrochemical coupling.

title: Electro-chemo-mechanical failure in cobalt-free high-nickel cathodes: common degradation of polycrystalline and single-crystal morphologies in halide-based all-solid-state batteries
abstract: Cobalt-free high-nickel LiNi 0.9 Mn 0.1 O 2 cathodes show a common failure mode in halide ASSBs regardless of morphology. H2–H3 lattice contraction induces interfacial voids, severing ionic pathways and overshadowing the benefits of single-crystal design.

title: Modulating molecular aggregates via nonconjugated bridges for enhanced photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: Through bridge engineering of dyes, the carrier lifetimes and hydrogen production performance were significantly enhanced by suppression of dye aggregation.

title: A machine learning-enabled planar interdigitated thermogalvanic hydrogel for synergistic thermal and strain sensing
abstract: This study demonstrates a strain–thermal coupling mechanism in an interdigitated thermogalvanic hydrogel, enabling self-powered thermal and strain sensing with high-accuracy machine learning recognition.

title: Two dimensional PdRuMoFeCo multi-component metallene for rechargeable Zn–air batteries with high performance
abstract: A two-dimensional PdRuMoFeCo multi-component alloy metallene is developed as a highly efficient bifunctional ORR/OER electrocatalyst, enabling superior performance in rechargeable Zn–air batteries.

title: Engineering high-valent Ni 3+ –O active sites through Pd incorporation in NiCo-MOF/Ni-foam for enhanced 5-hydroxymethylfurfural electrooxidation
abstract: A carbon-free, scalable Pd/NiCo-MOF/NF electrode is fabricated; its in situ electro-reconstruction generates high-density Ni/Co oxyhydroxides, and Pd–NiOOH coupling optimizes HMFOR performance for efficient FDCA production.

title: Tailoring the electronic structure of FeRu-NC for efficient electrochemical oxidative dehydrogenation of l -ascorbic acid
abstract: FeRu-NC achieves high-efficiency l -ascorbic acid electrooxidation by modulating the electronic structure of Fe sites through Ru doping, regulating adsorption of intermediates.

title: Atomically precise Au 42 and Cu-doped Au 42 nanorods for CO 2 reduction: the critical role of ligand removal
abstract: Ligand removal profoundly alters Au 42 nanorod activity and selectivity. Cu doping enhances CO 2 reduction in pristine Au 42 but suppresses activity in the –SR removed cluster by shifting the potential-determining step.

title: Defect-rich ZnFeCo spinel oxide for enhancing the performance of various electrocatalytic reactions and producing high value-added chemicals
abstract: Reasonable design and efficient synthesis of multifunctional electrocatalysts are crucial for promoting energy conversion, green manufacturing, and pollutant resourcing.

title: Boosting CO 2 methanation over Ni-based catalysts via La–Al mixed oxide synergy
abstract: Ni/LaAlO 3 catalysts boost CO 2 methanation via oxide synergy, moderate basicity, and Ni 0 surface area. Operando FT-IR reveals an H-assisted associative pathway involving monodentate carbonates and Ni 0 –CO intermediates.

title: Amphoteric molecular engineering of the buried SnO 2 /perovskite interface toward efficient and durable solar cells
abstract: The amphoteric small molecule dichloro(diethylamino)phosphine (DDP) effectively enhances the crystallinity of perovskite films and passivates defects at the buried interfaces, achieving an efficiency of 25.54% with impressive thermal stability.

title: Low-toxicity light-absorbing materials for indoor photovoltaic applications
abstract: Recent research progress in low-toxicity light-absorbing materials for indoor photovoltaic applications is discussed, with a particular focus on emerging lead-free halide perovskites.

title: 3D dual network MOF-derived magnetic Co/Ni@C-GnP@PDMS composites for absorption-dominant EMI shielding with enhanced thermal conductivity
abstract: A fully interconnected 3D dual-network integrating conductive GnP and MOF-derived Co/Ni@C composite enables absorption-dominant EMI shielding (71.5 dB) with improved through-plane thermal conductivity (1.37 W m −1 K −1 ).

title: Engineering fibrous-interconnected potassium bis(dioxovanadium) phosphate frameworks for fast-charging and high-rate sodium-ion supercapacitors
abstract: High-performance sodium-ion storage systems are essential for next-generation energy technologies.

title: Tailoring isolated Cu–N 4 and Fe–N 4 atomic sites for efficient selective electroreduction of CO 2 to CO
abstract: The electrochemical reduction of CO 2 to CO represents a critical pathway for carbon-neutral energy cycles, yet designing catalysts that simultaneously achieve high activity, selectivity, and durability remains challenging.

title: Chain-length-selective adsorption governs diffusion-limited dendrite growth mode in battery electrodes
abstract: Growth of classical dendrites in metal anodes represents a far-from-equilibrium, diffusion-limited crystallization process that critically impacts the safety and performance of energy storage systems.

title: Integrating singlet oxygen-driven photocatalytic H 2 O 2 synthesis with uranium( vi ) sequestration by a pyrene-based porous aromatic framework
abstract: Pyrene-integrated BINOL-based porous aromatic framework facilitates sacrificial-agent-free H 2 O 2 production with SCC efficiency of 1% via 1 O 2 and water-oxidation paths, enabling photoimmobilization of uranium( vi ) up to 627.5 mg g −1 .

title: Ligand engineering via the hard–soft-acid–base theory: enabling the design of MOFs for efficient electrocatalytic oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: A layered MOF Co-Tdp-Bpy was designed based on the Hard–Soft-Acid–Base (HSAB) theory, and exhibited excellent activity and stability in the OER.

title: Indium-filled skutterudite/PEDOT:PSS-derived sulfur-amorphous carbon hybrids: an organic–inorganic interface design strategy for phonon-charge decoupling and enhanced thermoelectric performance
abstract: Hierarchical S-AC interphases derived from in situ PEDOT:PSS carbonization simultaneously scatter phonons and facilitate electron transport in In-filled skutterudites, enabling zT = 1.47 at 700 K.

title: Interlayer polymerization enables cross-plane conjugation in COFs for efficient photocatalytic H 2 O 2 production
abstract: A novel interlayer polymerization enhances charge transport in covalent organic frameworks (COFs). Polymer-bridged PPy-COF and PAn-COF improve photocatalytic H 2 O 2 production, operating via pathway a singlet-oxygen-mediated reduction pathway.

title: Ultrafast Zn 2+ diffusion and exceptional stability in self-assembled 2D VS 2 /Ti 3 C 2 T X heterostructures for advanced zinc-ion batteries
abstract: Heterostructure of Vanadium disulfide (VS 2 ) and MXene has emerged as a promising cathode material for aqueous zinc-ion batteries (AZIBs) owing to synergistic effect of layered structure with high theoretical capacity of VS 2 and MXene.

title: Lithium–sulfur battery cathode with sulfurized bacterial cellulose and metal-coordinated polymers to strengthen stress and promote sulfur utilization
abstract: A highly strengthened Li–S battery cathode is prepared by sulfurization of bacterial cellulose (BC) followed by coating with BTA–Ni, wherein the BC skeleton maintains structural stability and the BTA–Ni alleviates the shuttle of polysulfides.

title: A Sn/ZnO nano-island hybrid catalyst for neutral H 2 O 2 electrosynthesis from O 2 or air
abstract: A nano-island hybrid catalyst enables highly efficient and selective H 2 O 2 production via the 2e-ORR in neutral media using either O 2 or ambient air.

title: Dynamic regulation of thermoelectric transport properties in GeTe via phase transition of VO 2
abstract: Here, VO 2 with insulator-to-metal transition, is employed to enhance the thermoelectric performance of Ge 0.92 Sb 0.08 Te compounds. The optimized sample achieved a remarkable ZT value of ≈2.0 at 603 K and an average ZT avg ≈ 1.2 between 303 and 723 K.

title: Electronic structure engineering of bimetallic NiFe-MOF via one-dimensional tubular assembly for enhanced oxygen evolution
abstract: 1D tubular bimetallic NiFe-MOF assemblies were constructed and achieved enhanced conductivity, stability, and OER activity via electronic synergy and mesoporous architecture, enabling efficient water splitting and AEM electrolysis.

title: Magnesium chloride salt-assisted synthesis of porous N-doped carbons from molten polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons for high-performance supercapacitors
abstract: A molten-state synthesis of new nitrogen-doped carbons (NdCs) is reported with tailored surface and electrochemical properties. They exhibit state-of-the-art activity (energy and power densities and cycling performance) as supercapacitor materials.

title: Synergistic passivation of perovskite surfaces using a multi-functional four-end zwitterionic amino acid
abstract: Through NH 3 + occupying A-sites and COO − passivating B-sites, ZAA forms a stable “quadrupedal anchoring” mode on the perovskite surface. ZAA allH and ZAA CF could passivate undercoordinated Pb, reduce surface states, and resist water invasion.

title: B-site co-doping-induced symmetry breaking for enhanced optoelectronic properties in vacancy-ordered double perovskites
abstract: Vacancy-ordered double perovskites ( i.e. , Cs 2 SnBr 6 ) have attracted considerable attention due to their intrinsic stability and lead-free composition.

title: A green and efficient strategy for fabricating supercapacitor electrodes using a mechanochemically synthesised Cu-HHTP MOF
abstract: Following structural elucidation, a mechanochemically synthesised copper-tricatecholate MOF is used as an electrode material in coin-cell supercapacitors employing an aqueous electrolyte.

title: Atomic layer deposition of NiO x : harnessing the potential of new precursor combinations for photoelectrochemical water oxidation
abstract: This work shows the critical effect of both nickel precursors and oxygen sources on the photoelectrochemical response towards water oxidation of NiO thin films grown by atomic layer deposition.

title: In situ nanocrystallization on the perovskite film surface for enhanced light emission
abstract: The in situ nanocrystallization induced by spin-coating phosphine oxides on the surface of the perovskite film remarkably improves the light emission owing to the spatial carrier confinement effect.

title: Fabrication of robust eutectic gel polymer electrolytes for high-performance zinc-ion batteries using a solvent-exchange strategy
abstract: A solvent-based exchange strategy using deep eutectic solvents enables achieving robust eutectic gel polymer electrolytes, suppressing dendrites and side reactions while delivering zinc ion batteries with stable cycling performance.

title: Switching CO 2 electroreduction on copper between C 2 products and HCOOH by ionic surfactants
abstract: A strategy for regulating the wetting property of catalysts and changing the selectivity of electrochemical reduction products of CO 2 by using ionic type surfactants.

title: Nonlinear conduction and multifunctional integration enabled by segregated network modulation in PET@Ni composites
abstract: PET@Ni composites with engineered segregated networks exhibit tunable conductivity, enhanced capacitive sensing, and strong EMI shielding (63.77 dB). Theory and simulations reveal the roles of surface conduction and interparticle tunneling.

title: Promising thermoelectric performance in earth abundant SnS: local Sn distortion and modular misfit nanostructures
abstract: Sn off-centering and vacancy tuning in SnS result in elevated phonon scattering and high thermoelectric device performance.

title: Zwitterionic nanomicelle self-assembly reconstructs interfacial chemistry toward durable zinc anode
abstract: SPE, a zwitterionic surfactant functional additive, forms nanomicelles in aqueous ZnSO 4 electrolyte. The micelle-regulated interface enables uniform Zn 2+ flux and suppressed water activity, yielding a dendrite-free Zn anode stable over 3900 h.

title: Graph neural network-based multi-objective Bayesian optimization for enhanced screening of metal–organic frameworks with optimal separation performance
abstract: Metal–organic frameworks (MOFs) are porous crystalline materials with applications in gas capture, drug delivery, and molecular separations.

title: Engineering multi-layered yolk–shell CuO@Cu x Sn 1− x O 2 ( x = 0, 0.1, 0.2, and 0.3) heterojunction for photocatalytic H 2 generation from atmospheric moisture
abstract: A multi-layered CuO@Cu x Sn 1− x O 2 yolk–shell heterojunction is engineered for atmospheric moisture splitting. Synergistic Cu–Sn electronic modulation and hierarchical architecture corroboratively enhance H 2 production directly from ambient vapor.

title: Engineering ultrafine Cu nanoparticles supported on zeolites via solvent-free inter-zeolite transformation for bioethanol dehydrogenation
abstract: Aggregated Cu-NPs supported on FAU were successfully redispersed into small clusters (∼2 nm) during the simultaneous conversion of FAU to BEA by IZT process. Significantly high acetaldehyde selectivity (>70%) was achieved using transformed catalyst.

title: Trifunctional cobalt single-atom catalyst with axial chloride coordination to achieve efficient methylamine electrosynthesis: a constant-potential DFT study
abstract: A single-atom Co metal catalyst to achieve efficient methylamine electrosynthesis via the CO–*NH 2 coupling pathway.

title: Inert nickel doping unlocking aqueous proton storage by hydrated copper hexacyanoferrate as a competitive cathode for proton pseudocapacitors
abstract: An inert hetero-atomic Ni-doping strategy is purposefully designed to optimize copper hexacyanoferrate toward producing asymmetric proton pseudocapacitors as competitive and reliable cathode platforms.

title: Poly-cyclodextrin modified graphene oxide for PFAS removal from drinking water
abstract: This study reports the synthesis of poly-β-cyclodextrin functionalized graphene oxide as an efficient sorbent for PFAS removal from water, offering mechanistic insights into the interactions governing the adsorption.

title: In situ ammonia synthesis and energy generation via aqueous Zn–NO 3 − batteries
abstract: Zn–NO 3 − battery is a “one stone-three birds” strategy enabling energy generation, NH 3 synthesis & nitrate remediation. FeCu(1 : 2)S x catalyst enhances selectivity via Cu adsorption & Fe hydrogenation, powering 62 LEDs for 70 h.

title: Lattice engineering in MnO 2 via V 5+ doping for high-performance aqueous Te–MnO 2 batteries
abstract: An aqueous tellurium–manganese dioxide (Te–MnO 2 ) battery, which employs carbon-supported tellurium (C@Te) as a replacement for the Zn metal anode coupled with a vanadium-doped MnO 2 (Mn 1− x V x O 2 ) cathode, has been developed.

title: Unraveling the dynamic phase transition behavior of LiMn 0.7 Fe 0.3 PO 4 nanoparticle electrodes for lithium-ion batteries
abstract: The rate-dependent structural transition of LMFP is clarified: increasing current enlarges the solid-solution region, while the two-phase region is delayed. At 20C, LMFP shows a short-memory behavior and delivers a hysteretic phase transition.

title: First-principles study on screening diatomic catalysts for electrocatalytic NO reduction on single-layer BAs
abstract: The catalytic performance and reduction mechanism of NH 3 synthesis by NO reduction on TM 2 C 4 @BAs were studied in detail, and the formation mechanisms of by-products under high concentration coverage and competitive reactions were compared.

title: In situ self-assembly of amine-rich carbon supraparticles for rapid CO 2 capture
abstract: In situ self-assembly covalently grafts amines into carbon supraparticles, enabling uniform dispersion in a hierarchical pore network, faster CO 2 uptake, 2.4-fold higher capacity (2.87 mmol g −1 ), and 49% amine efficiency vs. 22% for post-loading.

title: Boron-driven energy technologies: borophene and its derivatives in supercapacitors
abstract: This review integrates borophene, metal borides, and MBenes into a unified boron-based platform for high-performance supercapacitors, linking multicenter bonding, electronic structure, and charge-storage mechanisms to guide durable energy storage.

title: Effect of externally applied pressure on rechargeable alkaline zinc batteries at limited depth of discharge
abstract: Externally applied pressure can affect charge transfer resistance and cycling stability of alkaline Zn–MnO 2 batteries.

title: Engineering a Pt-nanoparticle modified nanoporous boron-doped diamond electrode for efficient electrochemical ozone production
abstract: A Pt nanoparticle-modified porous boron-doped diamond (Pt/PBDD) electrode is developed for efficient electrochemical ozone production (EOP).

title: Chlorine-mediated construction of monometallic Ru–RuO 2 with abundant interfaces for efficient pH-universal hydrogen evolution catalysis
