abstract: A chlorine-mediated strategy synthesizes a unique Ru/RuO₂ heterostructure catalyst, achieving exceptional hydrogen evolution reaction (HER) activity across the entire pH range.

title: Enhancing electronic metal–support interaction in Cu–ZnO/N-carbon catalysts by generating more pyridinic nitrogen species for efficient CO 2 hydrogenation to methanol
abstract: Pyridinic nitrogen in NOMC strengthens Cu–ZnO electronic metal–support interaction, enriching Cu active centers for efficient CO 2 hydrogenation to methanol.

title: Dual-Plasmonic Au and CoNiO2 co-sensitized ZnO Membrane: Photothermal Effect Promotes Charge Separation for Enhanced Visible-Light-Infrared-Driven CO2 Reduction
abstract: Photocatalytic CO2 reduction to CO is promising but limited by narrow light absorption, inefficient charge separation, and high energy barriers. Herein, we construct Au-plasmon-enhanced ZnO/CoNiO2 S-scheme heterojunctions (ZAC-X) on Zn...

title: MOF-structure-dependent mechanical and tribological properties of polyimide composite coatings: synergistic experimental and computational insights
abstract: MOF-5/ZIF-8: coordination bond breakage during friction. MOF-5′s carboxylic acid ligand forms chelates tribochemically, enhancing transfer film adhesion. UiO-66: undecomposed.

title: Robust flexible Janus composites for absorption-dominated EMI shielding
abstract: Developing electromagnetic interference (EMI) shielding materials with high absorption capability is an effective strategy to mitigate electromagnetic radiation and the associated secondary electromagnetic pollution.

title: Elucidating the stabilizing effect of copper substitution in high voltage P2-type layered oxides for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: P2-type sodium transition-metal oxides are promising high-energy cathodes for sodium-ion batteries but suffer from structural degradation and irreversible redox reactions particularly in the high-voltage region, resulting in rapid capacity fade.

title: Selenium-doped VO 2 cathode with enhanced kinetics towards stable zinc–vanadium pouch batteries
abstract: Selenium-doped VO₂ cathodes with enhanced electron conductivity and Zn²⁺ diffusion enable ultra-stable zinc–vanadium pouch batteries with 99% capacity retention after 450 cycles.

title: Buffering internal thermal surges: the role of precursor specific heat capacity in the microstructural integrity of LiNiO 2 cathodes
abstract: High precursor specific heat capacity buffers thermal surges, promoting uniform LiNiO 2 grains. This intrinsic property yields robust cathodes with superior electrochemical stability and performance, emphasizing thermophysics over geometry.

title: Highly adhesive, fluorine-free binders enabled by chemical anchoring for durable high-nickel cathodes in lithium-ion batteries
abstract: A fluorine-free polynorbornene binder featuring covalent chemical anchoring significantly enhances the mechanical adhesion and interfacial stability of high-nickel cathodes for high-energy-density lithium-ion batteries.

title: Porosity-driven electrochemical divergence in structurally polymorphic 2D metal–organic frameworks for lithium-ion storage
abstract: Cu-based 2D MOFs (p-Cu-THQ and d-Cu-THQ) reveal how porosity affects Li-ion storage, with dense d-Cu-THQ showing higher capacity and rate due to pseudocapacitive behaviour while porous p-Cu THQ offers better cycling stability via improved diffusion.

title: A functional separator enabling safe and high-performance sodium metal batteries under elevated temperature
abstract: The N‑Us@PI separator, prepared by integrating Na 3 Zr 2 Si 2 PO 12 @UiO-SO 3 Na (N‑Us) onto a polyimide (PI) substrate, regulates Na + transport, deposition, and stripping at high temperatures, enhancing the stability and lifespan of sodium metal batteries.

title: Adsorption of conducting polymer to high-surface-area nanoengineered cellulose fibers to facilitate rapid fabrication of highly conductive papers
abstract: Paper is an attractive substrate for sustainable and scalable organic electronics; however, its intrinsically insulating nature, the absence of continuous electronic pathways, and the lack of control over mixed ionic-electronic...

title: Ordered VO 2 nanoflowers with amorphous hybrid interfaces induced by iodide ion doping for superior zinc-ion storage
abstract: A method was developed for controllable I − doping of VO 2 , and the doping induced the formation of ordered 3D nanoflowers, which expanded the ion tunnels, created oxygen vacancies, and significantly enhanced Zn-ion storage performance.

title: HyNA-LDI-MS: a real-time mass spectrometric approach for probing interfacial charge transfer
abstract: A hybrid nanoarray enhanced laser desorption/ionization mass spectrometry (HyNA-LDI MS) approach enables monitoring of photoelectron and hot carrier transfer at semiconductor–metal interfaces.

title: Structurally engineered bifunctional oxygen electrocatalysts derived from a cobalt-based LDH-MOF architecture
abstract: Engineering a Co-LDH-MOF architecture via ultrasonication-assisted self-assembly produces a strongly coupled core–shell electrocatalyst, endowing rechargeable Zn–air batteries with a stable voltage gap and exceptional durability over 1000 h.

title: Research progress of catalysts for catalytic methane combustion
abstract: This article reviews various factors influencing the performance of noble-metal and non-noble-metal catalysts in catalytic methane combustion and their reaction mechanisms.

title: Recent advances in the industrialization of direct recycling for retired lithium-ion batteries
abstract: This review focuses on two crucial core components: pretreatment processes and direct regeneration methods. It highlights recent technological advancements, identifies industrial bottlenecks, and outlines critical directions for future development.

title: From direct regeneration to upcycling: recycling of spent LiFePO 4 cathodes
abstract: Upcycling facilitates a paradigm shift in the regeneration of spent LiFePO 4 from mere performance recovery to upgrading through strategies like elemental doping or coating, offering a new perspective for the development of regeneration technologies.
